## §2.257 — FF Scope: Dates & Duration Tab Re-anchoring (Phase 1)

> Date: 2026-05-28  
> Status: Phase 1 research complete (no implementation in this section)

### 2.257-A: Scope confirmation

Operator instruction (RUN_ACE) requests:
1. Focus specifically on **edit project detail page** (`pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`).
2. Move **Revision Orders** from Progress tab back to its original location: **Dates & Duration** tab.
3. Improve **Dates & Duration UI readability**.

### 2.257-B: Current-state trace (edit page only)

- `v-window-item value="schedule"` currently renders only Primary Project Dates card.
- `CiRevisionOrdersTable` is currently rendered under `v-window-item value="progress"` (first block in that tab).
- Progress tab currently contains `Revision Orders → Progress Reports → Project Milestones → Timelogs`.

### 2.257-C: Gap and impact

| Requirement | Current | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| Revision Orders under Dates & Duration | In Progress tab | **Major** |
| Dates & Duration readability uplift | Compact single-card presentation | **Moderate** |
| Keep Progress tab focused on accomplishment flow | Mixed with schedule-change governance block | **Major** |

### 2.257-D: Research decision locks

1. **Component reuse lock:** keep `CiRevisionOrdersTable` as the single revision-order surface (no duplicate implementation).
2. **Placement lock:** mount `CiRevisionOrdersTable` inside `schedule` tab, below Primary Project Dates.
3. **Progress-tab purity lock:** remove revision-order block from `progress` tab; keep `Progress Reports → Project Milestones → Timelogs`.
4. **Readability lock:** improve Dates & Duration information hierarchy (section title/subtitle density, spacing, and visual grouping) without changing API contracts.

---

## ARCHITECTURE REFERENCE

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Backend | NestJS + MikroORM (hybrid) + PostgreSQL via `pg` driver |
| Frontend | Nuxt 3 + Vue 3 Composition API + Vuetify 3 + ApexCharts (`vue3-apexcharts`) |
| Auth | JWT + RBAC; Google OAuth; OpenLDAP (deployment-gated) |
| File Uploads | Multer via `FileInterceptor('file', { limits: { fileSize: 10MB } })` |
| MCP | Figma official server `https://mcp.figma.com/mcp` (assistive only) |

---

### MikroORM Hybrid Data Access Model (ENFORCED PATTERN)

The backend uses a **hybrid** data access model â€” NEVER introduce new `DatabaseService` usage.

| Use Case | Method |
|---|---|
| CRUD (create/update/delete/list) | `em.find()`, `em.findOne()`, `em.persistAndFlush()`, `em.flush()` |
| Analytics / complex joins / GROUP BY | `em.getConnection().execute(sql, params)` |
| Auth user lookups (bypass soft-delete) | `em.findOne(User, { email: { $ilike: email }, deletedAt: null }, { filters: false })` |
| Legacy health only | `DatabaseService` â€” retained ONLY in `health.service.ts`, NEVER extend |

**Raw SQL parameter binding:**
- Use `?` placeholders (NOT `$1`, `$2`) â€” MikroORM PostgreSQL driver maps `?` â†’ `$N`
- Array parameters: `WHERE campus = ANY(?)` with params `[['MAIN', 'CABADBARAN']]` (array as single `?`)
- Never use string-interpolated SQL â€” always parameterized

**Paginated response:** `createPaginatedResponse(data, total, page, limit)` â†’ `{ data: T[], meta: { total, page, limit, totalPages } }`
- ALL consumers must unwrap `.data` â€” never assume plain array from paginated endpoints

**Global soft-delete filter:** `@Filter({ name: 'notDeleted', cond: { deletedAt: null }, default: false })` on entities.
- Applied via `em.addFilter('notDeleted')` in `AppModule` scope
- NEVER use `default: true` â€” causes auth login 500 (Phase HT root cause)
- Auth lookups pass `{ filters: false }` to bypass

---

### Authentication Architecture

- **JWT flow:** `Authorization: Bearer <token>` â†’ `JwtStrategy` validates â†’ `@CurrentUser()` injects `JwtPayload { sub, email, role, campus }`
- **Guards:** `@UseGuards(JwtAuthGuard, RolesGuard)` at controller class level + `@Roles(...)` per route
- **Public routes:** `@Public()` decorator bypasses global guard
- **Google OAuth:** `GET /api/auth/google` â†’ callback â†’ JWT issued
- **OpenLDAP:** `ldap.strategy.ts` â€” deployment-gated; Phase JD fix applied â€” uses `em.findOne` (no DatabaseService)
- **Auth module:** Does NOT import `DatabaseModule` (Phase JD)

---

### RBAC Architecture

- Permission keys (immutable in `pmo-frontend/middleware/permission.ts`): `uo`, `coi`
- Roles: `SuperAdmin` â†’ `Admin` â†’ `Staff` â†’ `Viewer`
- Campus scoping: non-admin users see own-campus records + all PUBLISHED + own-created/assigned
- Frontend composable: `usePermissions()` â†’ `canAdd(module)`, `canEdit(module)`, `canDelete(module)`, `isAdmin`, `isStaff`, `isSuperAdmin`

---

### Frontend Coding Patterns

- **API calls:** `api.get<T>()`, `api.post()`, `api.patch()`, `api.del()` â€” NOT `api.delete()`
- **Tab content (Vuetify 3):** MUST use `<v-tabs v-model>` + `<v-window v-model>` + `<v-window-item value="">` pattern. Using raw `v-if` on template blocks causes Vuetify to set model to internal index instead of string value (Phase JF root cause fix).
- **ApexCharts typing:** `type: 'donut' as const`, `position: 'bottom' as const` â€” literal type assertions required to satisfy `ApexOptions`
- **Lazy tab fetch:** `watch(activeTab, (tab) => { if (tab === 'target' && !data.value) fetchData() })`
- **Error state:** Always track `error` ref alongside `loading` ref â€” show visible retry button, not silent fail
- **DELETE:** `api.del()` (not `api.delete()`) â€” established in CLAUDE.md Directive 81

---

### COI Module Architecture

See Section 2.115 below for full COI analysis. Summary:

| Component | Location | Status |
|-----------|----------|--------|
| Backend controller | `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.controller.ts` | âœ… Full CRUD + workflow + analytics |
| Backend service | `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.service.ts` | âœ… Includes analytics methods |
| Public controller | `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/public-construction.controller.ts` | âœ… Unauthenticated PUBLISHED-only |
| Module | `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/construction-projects.module.ts` | âœ… Registers both controllers |
| Admin index | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/index.vue` | âœ… List + KPI + filter + analytics tab |
| Admin detail | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` | âœ… Full detail + gallery |
| Admin edit/new | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`, `new.vue` | âœ… Stable |
| Public index | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/index.vue` | âœ… Public project cards |
| Public detail | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue` | âœ… Read-only detail |
| Adapters | `pmo-frontend/utils/adapters.ts` | âœ… BackendMilestone, BackendFinancial, BackendGalleryItem corrected |

**COI entities (5):** `ConstructionProject`, `ConstructionMilestone`, `ConstructionProjectFinancial`, `ConstructionGallery`, `ConstructionSubcategory`

**COI publication workflow:** `DRAFT â†’ PENDING_REVIEW â†’ PUBLISHED / REJECTED`

**COI RBAC:** Permission key `coi`. Admin = full control. Staff = own/assigned records. Viewer = read-only (PUBLISHED only for non-admin).

| Client | Config | Status |
|--------|--------|--------|
| Claude Code CLI | `.mcp.json` â†’ `https://mcp.figma.com/mcp` | âœ… Connected + authenticated |
| VS Code Copilot | `.vscode/mcp.json` â†’ `https://mcp.figma.com/mcp` | âœ… Connected + authenticated |

### OpenLDAP Status

â¸ **Deployment-gated.** Not activated during development. Activates when operator provisions LDAP server at first deployment (~1â€“2 day scope).

### University Operations Module (Reference Pattern)

The UO module is the architectural reference for all data-entry modules:
- Physical Accomplishment: `pages/university-operations/physical/index.vue`
- Financial Accomplishment: `pages/university-operations/financial/index.vue`
- Analytics Dashboard: `pages/university-operations/index.vue`
- Backend: `pmo-backend/src/university-operations/university-operations.service.ts`
- Analytics: 91 raw SQL CTEs via `em.getConnection().execute()` â€” intentional per BAR reports requirement
- 8 EntityRepositories: UO, Indicator, Financial, QR, QRS, FY, Taxonomy, OrgInfo

### 2.132-D: Top 5 Gaps (Figma vs Current Codebase)

| # | Gap | Affects | Priority |
|---|-----|---------|----------|
| 1 | Facilities Assessment module entirely missing | Both | High but large â€” defer to dedicated phase |
| 2 | COI Project Detail 7-tab system (Overview/Documents/Gallery/POW/Team/Timeline/Analytics) | Admin | Medium â€” already partially addressed by Phase JR's 6-tab restructure |
| 3 | Funding-source-segmented project list pages (GAA / Locally / Special Grants) | Admin | Medium â€” defer until funding-source data model finalized |
| 4 | Repairs campus segmentation + project detail | Both | Medium â€” defer to dedicated phase |
| 5 | Public client surface beyond COI (HomePage, HeroSection, CategoryShowcase, StatsOverview, accessibility controls) | Client | **High â€” natural next step, dual-side per operator directive** |

**Selection rationale for Phase JS:** Operator directive specifies "admin AND client side prototype integration." Gap #5 is the client-side foundation that ties existing modules into a navigable public site. Pairing it with an admin dashboard refresh (Figma `DashboardEnhanced`) gives dual-side coverage in one phase without scope creep into module rebuilds (gaps #1, #3, #4 are larger and merit their own phases). A shared design token extraction sub-phase establishes the visual foundation for all future Figma-driven phases.

### 2.132-E: Proposed Phase JS Sub-Phase Decomposition

| Sub-phase | Side | Scope |
|-----------|------|-------|
| **JS-A** | Both (foundation) | Extract Figma design tokens (color palette, typography scale, spacing) from client `globals.css` + `default_theme.css`. Reflect in Vuetify theme â€” namespaced under new design tokens (do NOT regress existing pages). |
| **JS-B** | Client | Build public landing page at `pages/index.vue` for unauthenticated visitors. Mirror Figma client `HomePage` layout: Hero + Category cards (COI / UniOps / GAD) + Public stats overview. Authenticated users continue to redirect to `/dashboard` (preserve existing behavior). |
| **JS-C** | Admin | Refresh `pages/dashboard.vue` to align with Figma admin `DashboardEnhanced`. Add KPI stat cards row (Total Projects, Pending Reviews, Active Contractors, Quarterly Compliance), preserve existing UO summary section additively. |

### 2.132-F: Backend Dependencies

| Need | Existing Endpoint | Decision |
|------|-------------------|----------|
| Public stats counters (Hero + StatsOverview) | `/api/construction-projects` (works for unauthenticated public list per `coi/public/index.vue`) | Reuse via client-side count. NO new aggregate endpoint (YAGNI). |
| Pending reviews count for KPI card | `/api/construction-projects?publication_status=PENDING_REVIEW` (filter on existing list endpoint) | Verify filter param exists; if not, count client-side. |
| Active contractors count | `/api/contractors` | Reuse. |
| Quarterly compliance | UO summary endpoint already in `dashboard.vue` (`/api/university-operations/analytics/pillar-summary`) | Reuse â€” derive compliance % from existing payload. |

**No new backend work required for Phase JS.** All sub-phases are frontend-only.

### 2.132-G: Risk Assessment

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| Vuetify theme change regresses existing pages | JS-D3: tokens scoped to NEW components only; existing `primary` palette unchanged. |
| Public landing breaks auth redirect for logged-in users | JS-D2: Preserve `if (authenticated) navigateTo('/dashboard')` guard; only render Hero block for anonymous. |
| Figma Make snapshots lack inline source | JS-D5: Use targeted resource-link `get_design_context` calls per component (HomePage, HeroSection separately) rather than top-level. Adapt as REFERENCE per MCP server instructions, not 1:1 port. |
| Scope creep into module rebuilds | JS bounded to 3 sub-phases; gaps #1, #3, #4 explicitly deferred. |
| Admin dashboard regression | JS-D4: KPI row ADDITIVE above existing UO summary; existing analytics preserved verbatim. |

## Section 2.131 â€” Phase JR: Profile Page Redesign + External Link Generalization + Create/Edit/Profile Consistency (2026-05-05)

### 2.131-A: Profile Page (`detail-[id].vue`) Current State Audit

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` â€” 1143 lines
**Layout:** Flat scrollable layout â€” no tabs. Left col `md=8`, right col `md=4`.

**Left column content:**
| Card / Section | Notes |
|----------------|-------|
| Status & Progress header card | flex row: Status chip + Publication chip + Campus chip + Physical progress bar + Financial progress bar + Contract Amount. Not a standalone card header â€” embedded in `v-card-text`. |
| Project Information `v-card` | Ideal image, description, dates, duration, contract number, engineer, manager, geolocation, objectives, key features. No `v-icon` in title. |
| Milestones `v-card` + `v-data-table` | Columns: Milestone, Description, Target Date, Actual Date, Status, Remarks. No icon in title. |
| Gallery (MOV) `v-card` | Image grid with Upload button (admin-gated). Separate from the Attachments card â€” NOT grouped with documents. No icon in title. |
| Documents & Attachments `v-card` | Lists docs (files + Drive links). Upload File btn + Add Drive Link btn (admin-gated). Icon `mdi-file-multiple` in title. |

**Right column content:**
| Card / Section | Notes |
|----------------|-------|
| Building Details `v-list` | Building Type, Floor Area, Floors, Subcategory. No icon in title. |
| Contractor & Funding `v-list` | Contractor, Fund Source. No icon in title. |
| Assigned Staff/Personnel `v-card` | Shows ONLY `project.delegatedToName` â€” misses `project.assignedUsers[]` multi-user list. Hidden if `delegatedToName` is falsy. |
| Financial Records `v-data-table` | FY, Appropriation, Obligation, Disbursement, Util Rate, Disb Rate. No icon in title. |
| Approval History `v-timeline` | 3 stages: Created â†’ Submitted â†’ Reviewed. Correct. |

**Identified gaps for JR:**

| Gap | Current | Target | Priority |
|-----|---------|--------|----------|
| G1 | Card title "Documents & Attachments" | "Attachments" | High |
| G2 | "Add Google Drive Link" dialog â€” validates only `drive.google.com` / `docs.google.com` | Support any external URL (https://) | High |
| G3 | Drive link button label: "Add Drive Link" (mdi-google-drive) | "Add Link" (mdi-link) | High |
| G4 | DRIVE_URL_REGEX restricts to Google domains | General URL regex: `/^https?:\/\/.+/i` | High |
| G5 | Document delete missing (no delete button on document list items) | Delete button â†’ `DELETE :id/documents/:docId` (same endpoint as JQ-C) | High |
| G6 | Assigned Staff shows only `delegatedToName` | Show full `assignedUsers[]` list + delegatedToName (if different) | Medium |
| G7 | Gallery (MOV) and Documents are separate sections â€” fragmented | Group both under a unified "Attachments" section with sub-tabs or dividers | Medium |
| G8 | Flat layout â€” no tab navigation for a 1143-line page | Tab-based layout mirroring Create/Edit 6-tab structure | High (JR-B) |
| G9 | No icons in most `v-card-title` elements | Consistent `v-icon` prefix + `d-flex align-center ga-2` class pattern | Low |

### 2.131-C: External Link Generalization Analysis

**Scope:** All three COI pages â€” the operator scope for JR explicitly says "Any external links (not limited to Google Drive)."

**`new.vue` current state (link input):**
From JQ research (Section 2.130-A): The Documents tab uses "External Links" as the section label. The hint text contains Google Drive instructions. Validation in the input: needs verification â€” likely still Drive-specific hint text.

**`edit-[id].vue` current state:**
Same "External Links" label (JQ-B target), but underlying input hint likely Drive-specific.

**`detail-[id].vue` current state (confirmed from code):**
- `DRIVE_URL_REGEX = /^https?:\/\/(drive|docs)\.google\.com\//i` â€” line 293
- Dialog title: "Add Google Drive Link" (line 866)
- Button label: "Add Drive Link" with `mdi-google-drive` icon (line 764â€“769)
- Placeholder: `https://drive.google.com/file/d/...`
- Description text: "For files larger than 20MB, paste a shareable Google Drive link here."
- Error message: "Must be a Google Drive URL"

**Target for all 3 files:**
- Regex: `(v: string) => /^https?:\/\/.+/i.test(v) || 'Must be a valid URL (https://...)'`
- Dialog title: "Add External Link"
- Button label/icon: "Add Link" / `mdi-link`
- Placeholder: `https://...`
- Hint/description: "Add a link to any external resource (Drive, SharePoint, website, etc.)"

**Backend confirmation:** `POST :id/documents` with `documentType: 'link'` accepts any string in `externalLink` â€” no server-side domain restriction. Change is frontend-only.

---

### 2.131-D: Profile Page Tab Redesign Analysis

**Rationale:** The current flat layout (1143 lines) mirrors no consistent navigation pattern. Create/Edit uses a 6-tab structure. The profile page should use the same 6 logical groups to ensure visual consistency and reduce cognitive load.

**Proposed profile tab structure (mirrors Create/Edit):**

| Tab # | Tab Label | Content |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| 1 | Overview | Status/progress header card (current top card) + Project Information (description, image, objectives, key features) |
| 2 | Location & Physical | Campus chip, Geolocation, Building Details card (type, floor area, floors, subcategory), Beneficiaries |
| 3 | Contractor & Funding | Contractor & Funding card (existing right col content) |
| 4 | Timeline & Milestones | Milestones data-table |
| 5 | Personnel & Scope | Assigned Staff/Personnel card (fixed to show all assignedUsers[]) + Project Engineer/Manager |
| 6 | Attachments | Unified section: Gallery (with upload if canEdit) + Files (with delete if canEdit) + Links (with add/delete if canEdit) |

**Sidebar vs tab decision:** Keep the Financials and Approval History as a persistent sidebar strip on Tab 1 (Overview), OR promote them to separate tabs. Since they are read-only and frequently referenced, keeping Financials on Overview tab sidebar and Approval History at the bottom of Overview is the cleanest approach without over-engineering.

**Constraint:** Profile page does NOT use `tabRequired` gating (read-only tabs, no form completion logic). Tab navigation is free-form.

---

### 2.131-E: Priority and Execution Order

| Sub-Phase | Scope | Risk | Justification |
|-----------|-------|------|---------------|
| **JQ-A** (pending) | Tab + card label rename â€” Create + Edit | Very Low | Should complete first; unblocks consistency audit |
| **JQ-B** (pending) | Divider consistency + overlines â€” Create + Edit | Low | Depends on JQ-A labels being finalized |
| **JR-A** | Profile page: Attachments rename + external link generalize + assigned users fix | Lowâ€“Medium | Atomic fixes; no structural change; link generalization needed before JR-B |
| **JQ-C** | Backend removeDocument + frontend delete buttons (Edit form) | Medium | Backend before frontend; Postman gate required |
| **JR-B** | Profile page: Tab-based structural redesign | Mediumâ€“High | Largest scope; depends on JR-A fixes being stable; profile delete reuses JQ-C endpoint |
| **JR-C** | External link generalization â€” Create + Edit forms | Low | After JR-A confirms general pattern; template-only in new.vue + edit-[id].vue |

**Complete recommended order:** JQ-A â†’ JQ-B â†’ JR-A â†’ JQ-C (backend + Postman) â†’ JQ-C frontend (Edit) â†’ JR-B (Profile tab redesign) â†’ JR-C (Create/Edit link generalization)

## Section 2.129 â€” Phase JP: Deployment-Resilient Conflict + Attachment UX + Edit Restructure (2026-05-02)

**Status:** Phase 1 Research Complete
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE â€” JO-A SQL fix (`ON CONFLICT (project_code) WHERE (deleted_at IS NULL)`) is correct in source but NOT in compiled `dist/`. Runtime log shows stale SQL. This is the THIRD instance of "source-vs-runtime divergence" in this development cycle (first JJ-A, then JO-A, now post-JO). Pattern indicates the development workflow itself is fragile â€” relying on ON CONFLICT inference of a partial index makes every code change brittle.
**Continuity:** Builds on Sections 2.125â€“2.128. Does not invalidate prior work â€” validates the AS-DESIGNED behavior of partial index + predicate match. The decision now is to **eliminate the dependency on inference altogether** so the fix is invariant to compile/restart correctness.

---

### 2.129-A: STEP 1 â€” Backend Constraint Root Cause (Three-Layer Confirmation)

**Layer 1 â€” Database state (verified via direct query):**
```
projects unique constraints: (none â€” projects_project_code_key dropped in JN-A)
projects unique indexes:    projects_project_code_active_idx
                             ON projects (project_code) WHERE (deleted_at IS NULL)
```

**Layer 2 â€” Source code (`src/.../service.ts:375`):**
```sql
ON CONFLICT (project_code) WHERE (deleted_at IS NULL) DO NOTHING
```
âœ… JO-A applied correctly. Predicate matches partial index. Would work IF this code reached runtime.

**Layer 3 â€” Compiled output (`dist/src/.../service.js:260`):**
```sql
ON CONFLICT (project_code) DO NOTHING
```
âŒ Stale (pre-JO-A). The runtime log error matches this byte-for-byte.

**Mechanism: nest start --watch fails to recompile certain edits on Windows.** Documented in Section 2.124-A. Same defect, third occurrence. The `chokidar` file watcher in NestJS CLI does not reliably observe edits made by Claude Code's Write/Edit tool on Windows NTFS. The compiled `dist/` becomes a frozen snapshot of whichever build last succeeded.

**Why the transaction rolls back:** PostgreSQL parses the bare `ON CONFLICT (project_code)` and tries to infer an arbiter index. Finding NO unrestricted unique index/constraint on `project_code` (only the partial one with predicate), it raises `42P10` "no unique or exclusion constraint matching the ON CONFLICT specification". This is a STATEMENT-LEVEL error in PostgreSQL's planner â€” the implicit transaction aborts, rollback fires, no rows inserted.

**Layer responsible: Build/deployment pipeline (recurring) + code design (fragile dependency).**

The build/deployment issue can recur indefinitely. The CODE design choice â€” `ON CONFLICT` with predicate â€” is what makes it possible. A different code design that does NOT depend on inference would eliminate the dependency entirely.

---

### 2.129-B: Code Design Alternatives

| Approach | Pros | Cons | Resilient to stale dist? |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Current (JO-A): ON CONFLICT (col) WHERE (predicate)** | Atomic, single round-trip | Requires source = compiled SQL exactly | âŒ NO â€” stale compile breaks it |
| Drop partial index, restore plain UNIQUE | Brings back the JL-1 bare ON CONFLICT working | Re-introduces soft-delete blocking issue (JN root cause) | âŒ NO â€” same fragility |
| **Try/catch on 23505 unique_violation** | Atomic via DB constraint, no inference, works with ANY unique index shape (partial or plain) | Slightly more code (try block + error code check) | âœ… **YES â€” invariant to constraint shape** |
| SELECT-then-INSERT (no ON CONFLICT) | Simple, classic pattern | Race window between SELECT and INSERT | âœ… YES â€” but loses atomicity |

**Decision: Try/catch on `23505` (unique_violation) PostgreSQL error code.**

Rationale:
1. **Deployment-resilient:** works whether the unique index is plain, partial, or named. Whether dist/ is stale or fresh. Whether the migration ran or didn't.
2. **Atomic at DB level:** the unique index does the work; we just translate the error.
3. **Already established pattern:** the JJ-A version of `service.ts` had a similar try/catch (later removed in JL-1 in favor of ON CONFLICT). Restoring it is a step backward in elegance but a step forward in robustness.
4. **No SQL inference dependency:** removes the entire class of "predicate must match" failure modes.

**New SQL (JP-A):**
```sql
INSERT INTO projects (...) VALUES (...) RETURNING id;
-- No ON CONFLICT clause. The unique index does the work.
```

**New error handling:**
```typescript
try {
  const inserted = await conn.execute(`INSERT INTO projects ... RETURNING id`, [...]);
  // success
} catch (err: any) {
  // PostgreSQL error code 23505 = unique_violation
  if (err?.code === '23505') {
    throw new ConflictException(`Project code ${dto.project_code} is already in use`);
  }
  throw err;
}
```

This works with the JN-A partial index (which still enforces uniqueness for non-deleted rows â€” that's exactly what we want). When a duplicate code is INSERTed, PostgreSQL raises `23505` before the row is committed; we catch it and translate to a 409.

---

### 2.129-C: STEP 2 â€” Attachment System UX Gap Analysis

**Current state (post-JO-D, JO-E):**
- Create form documents tab (`new.vue`): 3 picker rows (file / image / Drive link), staged upload list, no inline guidance
- Edit form documents card (`edit-[id].vue`): existing file list + 3 inline pickers, no inline guidance

**Operator-identified UX gaps:**

| Gap | Current State | Required |
|---|---|---|
| Lack of upfront instructions | No help text explaining the 3 upload paths | Header alert: "Upload files (â‰¤20MB), images (â‰¤10MB), or paste a Google Drive link for files exceeding limits" |
| Drive link constraints unclear | Just a text field labeled "Google Drive URL" | Tooltip / hint: "Must be drive.google.com or docs.google.com URL. Set sharing to 'Anyone with the link can view' before pasting." |
| Size limits invisible until validation fails | Only the field labels mention limits, not the consequence | Inline note: "Files >20MB will be rejected. Use a Drive link for those." |
| Upload type choices unlabeled | "Type" dropdown values are technical (`mov`, `attachment`, etc.) | Add brief description chip alongside each type choice |
| No visual distinction between upload categories | All 3 picker rows look similar | Section dividers + icon headers: `<v-icon>mdi-paperclip</v-icon> Documents`, `<v-icon>mdi-image</v-icon> Images`, `<v-icon>mdi-google-drive</v-icon> External Links` |

**Backend support audit:** âœ… all required endpoints exist (JN-D). No backend changes needed. Pure frontend UX enhancement.

---

## Section 2.133 â€” Phase JT: COI Navigation Fix, Auth System Design, Activity Logging, Profile Refactor (2026-05-10)

**Status:** Phase 1 Research Complete
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE â€” four issues identified post-JR: (A) navigation bug where `router.back()` from edit page leaves detail page frozen; (B) Attachments tab UX gap (empty state + permission inconsistency); (C) authorization system gaps (no Auditor role, no activity logging); (D) project details page Figma alignment. No implementation authorized.
**Continuity:** Extends Sections 2.131â€“2.132. JQ/JR verified complete.

---

### 2.133-A: Navigation Bug â€” Root Cause (`edit-[id].vue` â†’ `detail-[id].vue`)

**Symptom:** From `/coi/edit-{id}` (any tab), clicking the back arrow updates the URL to `/coi/detail-{id}` but the page appears frozen â€” stale data or skeleton shows indefinitely. Hard refresh resolves it.

**Code path analyzed:**

`pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 305â€“306:
```typescript
function goBack() {
  router.back()  // â† ROOT CAUSE
}
```

`pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` lines 414â€“420:
```typescript
// ACE-R15 Tier 3: Simple onMounted (no watchEffect complexity)
onMounted(() => {
  console.log('[COI Detail] Mounted with ID:', projectId)
  fetchProject()
  fetchGallery()
  fetchDocuments()
})
```

`detail-[id].vue` line 23:
```typescript
const projectId = route.params.id as string  // static, not reactive
```

**Root cause â€” three contributing factors:**

| Layer | Issue |
|-------|-------|
| Navigation call | `router.back()` uses `History.go(-1)` (browser back). In Nuxt 3 SPA mode, this pops the history stack and triggers `popstate`. Vue Router's popstate handler may skip the full component lifecycle if the destination was previously cached in Vue Router's internal component pool. |
| Component lifecycle | `onMounted` fires only on TRUE mount. If the detail page component is restored from Vue Router's history cache (not destroyed/re-created), `onMounted` does NOT fire. Result: no `fetchProject()` call. |
| Static ID extraction | `projectId = route.params.id as string` at module scope â€” not reactive. Even if `route.params.id` updates, no watcher exists to re-trigger data fetches. This is intentional per ACE-R15 Tier 3 but creates a single-mount dependency. |

**Contrast with save flow (working):** `edit-[id].vue` line 296:
```typescript
router.push(`/coi/detail-${projectId}`)  // deterministic push â€” always creates fresh navigation entry
```
`router.push()` always triggers a full Nuxt 3 navigation lifecycle â†’ component destroy + remount â†’ `onMounted` fires.

**Fix direction:** Replace `router.back()` in `goBack()` with `router.push('/coi/detail-' + projectId)`. This is consistent with the save handler and eliminates the SPA history-restoration ambiguity. No watcher needed.

**Why NOT add a `watch` on route params:** Adding `watch(() => route.params.id, fetchAll)` would break ACE-R15 Tier 3 directive and adds unnecessary reactivity. The deterministic `router.push()` fix is simpler and addresses the root cause.

**Detail page `middleware` gap (secondary finding):** `detail-[id].vue` uses `middleware: 'auth'` only (line 4â€“6). All other COI pages (`new.vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `index.vue`) use `middleware: ['auth', 'permission']`. The detail page should add `'permission'` middleware for consistency and to enforce view-access checks via `canView('coi')`.

---

### 2.133-B: Attachments Tab â€” UX Gap Analysis

**Finding:** No code defect in the `<v-window-item value="attachments">` (confirmed at `detail-[id].vue` line 1014 â€” element IS present). The "blank white screen" is a UX perception issue.

**What the tab actually renders when project has no attachments and user lacks edit rights:**

1. **Gallery card** (lines 1015â€“1061): `"No images uploaded"` (grey text, centered)
2. **Attachments card** (lines 1063â€“1168): Icon + `"No attachments yet."` + caption (grey text)
3. **Upload buttons hidden**: `v-if="canEdit('coi')"` â€” hidden for Viewer-role users
4. **Gallery Upload button hidden**: `v-if="canEditCurrentProject"` â€” entity-level check

â†’ A Viewer with no data sees two small grey text blocks on white background â€” appears blank.

**Permission inconsistency found:**

| Button | Guard used | Correct guard |
|--------|-----------|--------------|
| Gallery "Upload" button | `canEditCurrentProject` (entity-level) | âœ… Correct |
| Attachments "Upload File" | `canEdit('coi')` (module-level) | âŒ Should be `canEditCurrentProject` |
| Attachments "Add Link" | `canEdit('coi')` (module-level) | âŒ Should be `canEditCurrentProject` |
| Gallery delete buttons | `canEditCurrentProject` | âœ… Correct |
| Document delete buttons | `canEditCurrentProject` | âœ… Correct |

Module-level `canEdit('coi')` means ANY Staff+ user can see upload buttons, even if they're not assigned to the project. Entity-level `canEditCurrentProject` checks ownership/assignment. The upload buttons should use the same guard as delete buttons.

**Secondary finding â€” `isDriveLink` mimeType check:**

```typescript
function isDriveLink(doc: DocumentItem): boolean {
  return doc.mimeType === 'application/x-google-drive-link'
}
```

This check was designed for the OLD Drive-only link system. New external links added via `submitDriveLink()` are posted with `documentType: 'link'` to the backend. The backend's `uploadDocument()` service determines `mimeType`. If the backend assigns `mimeType = 'application/x-google-drive-link'` for ALL external links (even non-Drive), this filter is accurate. If the backend now uses a different mimeType for generic links (e.g., `application/x-external-link`), `documentLinks` computed would be empty even with links present.

**Verification needed:** Inspect `construction-projects.service.ts` `uploadDocument()` to confirm mimeType assigned for `documentType: 'link'` entries.

**Fix direction:** 
1. Replace `canEdit('coi')` with `canEditCurrentProject` on attachment upload buttons
2. Improve empty state: add contextual message ("Contact the project owner to add attachments") for non-editors
3. Verify/fix `isDriveLink` mimeType check based on backend behavior

---

### 2.133-C: Authorization System Gaps

**Current state (verified):**

| Layer | State |
|-------|-------|
| Backend roles | `Admin`, `Staff`, `Viewer` (string-based, `@Roles()` decorator on endpoints) |
| Frontend permission model | `usePermissions()` composable â€” module-level CRUD (`canView`, `canAdd`, `canEdit`, `canDelete`) |
| Per-tab permissions | None â€” all tabs accessible to anyone who can edit the module |
| Activity logging | **NOT IMPLEMENTED** â€” confirmed by grep across entire `pmo-backend/src/` |
| Module override system | `moduleOverrides` per-user in JWT â€” `{ [moduleKey]: boolean }` â€” currently used for UO sub-modules |

**Required additions (from operator prompt):**

| Role | Mapping |
|------|---------|
| Full Oversight | Read-only + activity log access â€” maps to new `Auditor` role (NOT an existing role) |
| Full Access | Full CRUD â€” already covered by `Admin` / `SuperAdmin` |
| View-Only | Read-only â€” already covered by `Viewer` |
| Restricted Editor | Edit specific sections only â€” new concept, requires per-tab override extension |

**Auditor role design:**
- New role name: `Auditor`
- Permissions: `{ canView: true, canAdd: false, canEdit: false, canDelete: false }` (same as Viewer)
- UNIQUE capability: `canViewActivityLog` â€” allowed to read `/api/activity-logs`
- Implementation: extend `ROLE_PERMISSIONS` in `usePermissions.ts` + add `Auditor` to backend `RolesGuard` checks + new `canViewActivityLog()` permission function

**Restricted Editor design:**
- Approach: extend `moduleOverrides` system with section-level keys
- Key format: `coi:contract`, `coi:progress`, `coi:documents` â†’ `true` (allowed) or `false` (denied)
- Frontend: check `moduleOverrides['coi:contract']` before rendering/enabling contract tab actions
- Backend: no direct change needed (backend enforces endpoint-level, not tab-level)
- Risk: HIGH complexity â€” deferred as a separate sub-phase (JT-D) unless operator prioritizes

**Activity Logging System Design:**

New MikroORM entity `ActivityLog`:

```typescript
@Entity({ tableName: 'activity_logs' })
export class ActivityLog {
  @PrimaryKey({ type: 'uuid', defaultRaw: 'gen_random_uuid()' })
  id!: string

  @ManyToOne(() => User, { nullable: false })
  user!: User

  @Property()
  userEmail!: string  // denormalized for performance (no join on read)

  @Property()
  userName!: string

  @Enum(() => ActivityAction)
  action!: ActivityAction  // CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE, SUBMIT, PUBLISH, REJECT, WITHDRAW, UPLOAD, REMOVE_ATTACHMENT

  @Property()
  entityType!: string  // 'CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT', 'REPAIR', etc.

  @Property()
  entityId!: string

  @Property({ type: 'json', nullable: true })
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>  // e.g., { changedFields: [...], previousStatus: '...' }

  @Property({ defaultRaw: 'now()' })
  createdAt!: Date
}
```

Migration: `activity_logs` table with indexed `(entity_type, entity_id)` and `user_id`.

Service interface:
```typescript
// ActivityLogService
async logAction(
  user: { id: string; email: string; name: string },
  action: ActivityAction,
  entityType: string,
  entityId: string,
  metadata?: Record<string, unknown>
): Promise<void>

async findLogs(filter: { entityType?: string; entityId?: string; userId?: string; limit?: number; page?: number }): Promise<PaginatedResponse<ActivityLog>>
```

Integration points in `construction-projects.service.ts`:
- After `create()` success â†’ `logAction(user, CREATE, 'CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT', id)`
- After `update()` success â†’ `logAction(user, UPDATE, 'CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT', id, { changedFields })`
- After `remove()` success â†’ `logAction(user, DELETE, 'CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT', id)`
- After `submitForReview()` â†’ `logAction(user, SUBMIT, ...)`
- After `publish()` â†’ `logAction(user, PUBLISH, ...)`
- After `reject()` â†’ `logAction(user, REJECT, ..., { notes })`
- After `withdraw()` â†’ `logAction(user, WITHDRAW, ...)`
- After `uploadDocument()` â†’ `logAction(user, UPLOAD, ..., { fileName, documentType })`
- After `removeDocument()` â†’ `logAction(user, REMOVE_ATTACHMENT, ..., { docId })`

Read endpoint: `GET /api/activity-logs` â€” `@Roles('Admin', 'SuperAdmin', 'Auditor')`, paginated.

**Migration sequence:** Next SQL migration after current last (checked: migrations go up to at least `043`).

### 2.133-D: Project Details Figma Alignment Gaps

**Current detail page structure (`detail-[id].vue`):**
- 6 tabs: Overview / Location & Physical / Contractor & Funding / Timeline / Personnel / Attachments
- Tab structure mirrors Create/Edit form âœ…
- JR-B implementation complete âœ…

**Identified gaps (without live Figma access at time of research):**

| Gap | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| Overview tab information density | Current: status chip + publication chip side-by-side. Figma typically shows more structured card with progress bars, financial summary inline |
| Financial display | Current: financials listed in Overview tab as v-list-items. Figma may use a dedicated card with bar charts or utilization summary |
| Timeline tab | Current: v-data-table of milestones. Figma shows Gantt or card-per-phase layout |
| Missing `permission` middleware | `detail-[id].vue` lacks `permission` middleware â€” Viewer-role user with COI module override=false can still access this page |

**Priority assessment:** Figma alignment is ENHANCEMENT tier (not blocking). Navigation bug (JT-A) and permission middleware fix (sub-item of JT-C) are CRITICAL and block this phase.

**Execution dependency:** JT-C (permission system) must be designed before JT-E (Figma alignment) to ensure new layout respects correct permission guards.

---

### 2.133-E: Implementation Priority Order

| Priority | Sub-phase | Reason |
|----------|-----------|--------|
| 1 | JT-A (Navigation fix) | BLOCKING â€” broken UX on every editâ†’back flow; one-line fix |
| 2 | JT-B (Attachments UX) | BLOCKING for editors â€” wrong permission guard on upload buttons |
| 2 | JT-C (Add `permission` middleware to detail page) | Security gap â€” Viewer override not enforced on detail |
| 3 | JT-D (Activity Logging) | New feature â€” infrastructure phase; database migration required |
| 4 | JT-E (Auth Auditor role + Restricted Editor) | Complex; deferred until logging is in place |
| 5 | JT-F (Figma alignment) | Enhancement â€” defer until JT-A/B/C stable |

---

### 2.133-F: Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|
| `router.push()` fix changes cancel behavior (goes to detail instead of history-back) | LOW | Consistent with save handler; acceptable UX |
| Activity log writes slow down CUD operations | LOW | Async fire-and-forget pattern; log failures non-fatal |
| `activity_logs` table migration | LOW | Additive, no existing table touched |
| Auditor role added to backend | LOW | New entry in enum/string check; no existing role changed |
| `canEditCurrentProject` on upload buttons | NONE | Same permission already used on delete buttons â€” identical semantics |

### 2.134-B: Prototype vs Implementation â€” Comprehensive Comparison

#### Admin Prototype File Inventory

**Location:** `prototype/Admin Side/src/components/construction-infrastructure/`

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `project-detail/tabs/OverviewTab.tsx` | Section A (description, ideal image, accomplishment records) + Section B (actual accomplishment, %, dates, contractor) |
| `project-detail/tabs/TimelineTabEnhanced.tsx` | Milestone management: title, description, startDate, endDate, actualStartDate, actualEndDate, status, progress% |
| `project-detail/tabs/GalleryTab.tsx` | Photo gallery with M&E category tagging |
| `project-detail/tabs/DocumentsTab.tsx` | Documents with category (report/form/assessment/other) |
| `project-detail/tabs/TeamTab.tsx` | Personnel with assignmentPeriod, isActive |
| `project-detail/tabs/DataAnalyticsTab.tsx` | Read-only computed analytics |
| `project-detail/tabs/IndividualPOWTabEnhanced.tsx` | **Plan of Work (BOQ)** â€” individual work items with quantity, unit, costs, progress, M&E entries |
| `dialogs/TimelineDialogs.tsx` | Add/Edit/View dialogs for milestones |
| `dialogs/POWDialogs.tsx` | Add/Edit dialogs for POW items |
| `admin/AssignedPersonnelManager.tsx` | Personnel assignment management |

#### Client Prototype Tab Structure

**Location:** `prototype/General User Side/src/components/client/construction/ProjectDetailPage.tsx`

Client tabs observed: Overview / (Financial + Physical sections inline) / Gallery / Documents / Team / Timeline

Client `Project` interface key fields:
- `overview`: `{ summary, objectives[], scope, keyFeatures[] }`
- `financialAllocation: FinancialAllocation[]` â€” `{ category, allocated, utilized, remaining, percentage }` (category-based, e.g. Civil Works / Equipment / Contingency)
- `physicalAccomplishment: PhysicalAccomplishment[]` â€” `{ phase, description, targetCompletion, actualCompletion%, status }`
- `facilities: string[]` â€” facilities list (e.g. "24 Smart Classrooms")
- `beneficiaries: number` â€” integer count
- `team: TeamMember[]`, `gallery: GalleryImage[]`, `documents: ProjectDocument[]`
- `timeline: TimelineEntry[]` â€” `{ type: daily|weekly|monthly|quarterly, date, remarks, attachments, phase, contractor }`

| Contractor & Funding | OverviewTab Section B (contractor, dates) | Project Details card | Aligned |
| Timeline | TimelineTabEnhanced | Timeline tab | **Gap: milestone entity missing startDate, progress%, category** |
| Personnel | TeamTab | Team tab | Aligned |
| Attachments | DocumentsTab + GalleryTab | Gallery + Documents | Aligned after JT-B fix |
| MISSING | IndividualPOWTabEnhanced | â€” | **Work Program / POW tab not implemented** |
| MISSING | DataAnalyticsTab | â€” | Analytics deferred (YAGNI) |

### 2.134-C: Data Model Gap Analysis

#### 2.134-C1: `ConstructionMilestone` Entity Gaps

**Current entity** (`construction_milestones` table):
```
id, projectId, title, description, targetDate, actualDate, status, remarks, createdAt
```

**Admin prototype `TimelineMilestone` type** (`TimelineDialogs.tsx`):
```typescript
id, title, description,
startDate,     // â† MISSING in entity
endDate,       // maps to targetDate
actualStartDate,  // â† MISSING
actualEndDate,    // maps to actualDate
status, progress,  // progress% â† MISSING
remarks, notes?
```

**Required additions to `construction_milestones`:**
| Column | Type | Default |
|--------|------|---------|
| `start_date` | DATE | NULL |
| `actual_start_date` | DATE | NULL |
| `progress` | DECIMAL(5,2) | 0.00 |
| `category` | VARCHAR(50) | NULL (optional grouping) |

**Migration:** Additive columns â€” backward compatible (all nullable / with defaults).

#### 2.134-C2: No `construction_pow_items` Table

**Admin prototype `POWItem` type** (`POWDialogs.tsx`):
```typescript
id, description, progress%, quantity, unit,
estimatedMaterialCost, estimatedLaborCost, estimatedProjectCost,
unitCost, variance, startDate, endDate, projectDuration,
status, category
```

**Current status:** No entity, no table, no DTO, no endpoints, no frontend tab.

**Decision:** Implement as new `construction_pow_items` entity. This is the Plan of Work / Bill of Quantities â€” core to COI project tracking. SCOPE IN for JU.

**Required new table `construction_pow_items`:**
| Column | Type |
|--------|------|
| `id` | UUID PK |
| `project_id` | UUID NOT NULL |
| `description` | TEXT NOT NULL |
| `progress` | DECIMAL(5,2) DEFAULT 0 |
| `quantity` | DECIMAL(10,2) |
| `unit` | VARCHAR(50) |
| `unit_cost` | DECIMAL(15,2) |
| `estimated_material_cost` | DECIMAL(15,2) |
| `estimated_labor_cost` | DECIMAL(15,2) |
| `estimated_project_cost` | DECIMAL(15,2) |
| `variance` | DECIMAL(15,2) |
| `start_date` | DATE |
| `end_date` | DATE |
| `project_duration` | VARCHAR(100) |
| `status` | VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'NOT_STARTED' |
| `category` | VARCHAR(100) |
| `sort_order` | INTEGER DEFAULT 0 |
| `created_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() |
| `updated_at` | TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW() |

#### 2.134-C3: `beneficiaries` Field Type Mismatch

**Entity:** `beneficiaries` is `VARCHAR(255)` (text)
**Prototype:** `beneficiaries: number` (integer count)
**Frontend form:** `beneficiaries: ''` (text, never validated as number)

**Decision:** Add `beneficiaries_count` integer field to entity alongside existing text field. Existing text field preserved for backward compat (no migration risk). Form shows `beneficiaries_count` as numeric input; old `beneficiaries` text field deprecated (still accepted for legacy data).

Actually: the simpler approach is to just note this as a documentation gap. The `beneficiaries` text field is flexible enough (can store "3200" and cast on display). The form just needs a numeric input bound to the same field. No schema change required. Flag for UI-only fix.

#### 2.134-C4: `facilities` Field Not in Entity

**Prototype:** `Project.facilities: string[]` â€” e.g., "24 Smart Classrooms", "8 Laboratories"

**Current entity:** No `facilities` column.

**Resolution:** Use existing `key_features: JSONB` column â€” already semantically closest. Display `key_features` as "Key Features / Facilities" in the Overview tab. No schema change needed. Form already captures `key_features` as a text field that gets split into arrays.

#### 2.134-C5: `ConstructionProjectFinancial` Alignment with Prototype

**Current entity** (per-fiscal-year model): `appropriation, obligation, disbursement`

**Client prototype `FinancialAllocation[]`**: category-based (Civil Works, Equipment, Contingency) with `allocated, utilized, remaining, percentage`

**These serve different purposes:**
- Current model: DBM BAR reporting (appropriation/obligation/disbursement per year) âœ… Correct for MIS compliance
- Prototype model: Internal budget breakdown by category

**Decision:** Current model is authoritative for MIS/DBM compliance. Category-based breakdown is a cosmetic presentation layer â€” can be computed client-side from `contract_amount` or added as `metadata JSONB` on the financial record. No schema change required. **Prototype's category view is ASPIRATIONAL** â€” not a blocking gap.

#### 2.134-C6: `PhysicalAccomplishment` Phases (Client Prototype)

**Client prototype `PhysicalAccomplishment[]`**: `{ phase, description, targetCompletion, actualCompletion%, status }`

**Current implementation:** `physical_progress` (overall %) + `construction_milestones` (discrete milestones)

**Resolution:** Milestones already serve as physical accomplishment phases once enhanced with `progress%` (JU-A). The client prototype's "phases" map to milestones. No new entity needed.

### 2.134-E: Codebase Structure Analysis

#### Current structure

```
pmo-frontend/
â”œâ”€â”€ pages/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ coi/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ index.vue          â† admin COI list + KPI
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ new.vue            â† create project (1800+ lines estimated)
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ edit-[id].vue      â† edit project (1300+ lines)
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ detail-[id].vue    â† project detail profile (1200+ lines)
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ public/
â”‚   â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ index.vue      â† public list
â”‚   â”‚       â””â”€â”€ detail-[id].vue â† public detail (read-only)
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ ...
â”œâ”€â”€ components/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ ... (no coi/ subdirectory)
â””â”€â”€ composables/
    â””â”€â”€ usePermissions.ts

pmo-backend/
â”œâ”€â”€ src/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ construction-projects/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ construction-projects.controller.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ construction-projects.service.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ construction-projects.module.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ public-construction.controller.ts
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ dto/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ activity-logs/         â† NEW (JT-D)
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ database/entities/
```

#### Identified issues

1. **`new.vue` and `edit-[id].vue` are very similar** â€” both have the 6-tab form structure, the same dropdown options, the same validation rules. Estimated duplication: 40-60% of form field code. DRY violation.

2. **No `components/coi/` directory** â€” shared COI sub-components (milestone row, attachment panel, POW item row) are all inline in page files. This makes pages extremely long and hard to maintain.

3. **`detail-[id].vue` will grow** â€” adding a POW tab will push it past 1500 lines inline. Should extract tab panels to components.

4. **Backend structure is clean** â€” single `construction-projects` module with clear service separation. No restructuring needed. `activity-logs/` correctly isolated.

#### Recommended codebase improvements (JU-D)

Create `pmo-frontend/components/coi/` with:
- `CiMilestoneTable.vue` â€” milestone list used in Timeline tab (extracted from `detail-[id].vue`)
- `CiAttachmentsPanel.vue` â€” gallery + docs (extracted from `detail-[id].vue` Attachments tab)
- `CiPowItemTable.vue` â€” POW items table (new, for Work Program tab)
- `CiFormBasics.vue` â€” shared project form fields (project code, title, description, status, campus) used by both `new.vue` and `edit-[id].vue`

**Priority:** Lower than data model and UI fixes. Apply AFTER JU-A/B/C are stable.

### 2.134-F: Priority Decision and Execution Order

| Priority | Sub-phase | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|-----------|
| 0 (prerequisite) | JT-A, JT-B, JT-C | Navigation fix + permission fixes are blocking UX. Must complete before JU implementation. |
| 1 | JU-A (Milestone enhancements) | Foundation for Timeline tab. Additive migration, no regression. Enables correct physical accomplishment display. |
| 2 | JU-B (POW Items module) | Core admin feature. Requires new entity + migration + endpoints + frontend tab. Independent of JU-A. |
| 3 | JU-C (Overview tab progress enrichment) | No new data model. Uses existing `physical_progress` / `financial_progress` fields. Pure frontend. |
| 4 | JU-D (Component extraction + DRY) | Maintainability. Only after features are stable. No behavioral change. |

**What is NOT in JU scope (YAGNI):**
- Category-based financial allocation breakdown (cosmetic, no MIS requirement)
- Timeline diary entries (daily/weekly/monthly progress diary â€” separate from milestones)
- Data Analytics tab (already partially covered by `coi/index.vue` stats)
- Figma token extraction + public landing page (Phase JS, separate pending phase)

|---|----------------------------|---------------------------|--------|
| 1 | Overview | **Overview** | Expand: absorb Location & Physical + Contractor & Funding data as collapsible info cards within Overview tab |
| 2 | Location & Physical | *(absorbed into Overview)* | REMOVE as separate tab |
| 3 | Contractor & Funding | *(absorbed into Overview)* | REMOVE as separate tab |
| 4 | Timeline | **Timeline** | Reorder to pos 2 |
| 5 | Personnel | **Team** | Rename + reorder to pos 6 |
| 6 | Attachments | *Split into Gallery + Documents* | SPLIT into two tabs |
| â€” | (new) | **POW (Program of Works)** | ADD at pos 3 |
| â€” | (new) | **Gallery** | Extracted from Attachments, pos 4 |
| â€” | (new) | **Documents** | Extracted from Attachments (files + links), pos 5 |

**Final target tab structure for `detail-[id].vue` (6 tabs):**

| Pos | value | Label | Content |
|-----|-------|-------|---------|
| 1 | `overview` | Overview | Summary + progress bars + key features chips + project info cards (location, contractor, funding) |
| 2 | `timeline` | Timeline | Milestones with startDate, progress%, category (JU-A enhanced) |
| 3 | `pow` | POW (Program of Works) | POW items table â€” new (JU-B) |
| 4 | `gallery` | Gallery | Photo gallery â€” split from Attachments |
| 5 | `documents` | Documents | Documents + external links â€” split from Attachments |
| 6 | `team` | Team | Personnel list (renamed from "Personnel") |

---

#### Tab Content Redistribution Logic

**Overview tab absorbs from Location & Physical and Contractor & Funding:**
- Location & Physical data: `campus`, `building_type`, `floor_area`, `number_of_floors`, `latitude/longitude`, `beneficiaries` â†’ rendered as a "Project Details" info card within Overview
- Contractor & Funding data: `contractor`, `funding_source`, `contract_amount`, `contract_number`, `start_date`, `target_completion_date`, `actual_completion_date`, `project_duration` â†’ rendered as a "Contract Information" info card within Overview
- Progress bars (`physical_progress`, `financial_progress`) â†’ remain at Overview top (JU-C)
- ALL existing data bindings preserved â€” content is reorganized spatially, not removed

**Attachments tab split:**
- Gallery images (`construction_gallery` table) â†’ Gallery tab
- Documents (`documents` table, type FILE) â†’ Documents tab
- External links (`documents` table, type LINK) â†’ Documents tab (as a separate "Links" sub-section within Documents)
- Upload button permission guards follow JT-B fix (`canEditCurrentProject`)

---

#### `edit-[id].vue` Form Tab Assessment

The edit form (`edit-[id].vue`) uses different tab structure (data entry form, not display view):
`basic | contract | schedule | progress | personnel | documents`

**Decision:** Edit form tabs do NOT need to mirror the detail view tabs â€” they serve a different purpose (editing vs viewing). The Figma admin prototype uses inline dialogs for editing, but our implementation correctly uses a dedicated edit page.

**No change to `edit-[id].vue` tab names/order.** Only the POW tab (JU-B-7) will be added.

---

#### Risk Update for JU-E (Tab Restructure)

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|
| Merging 3 tabs â†’ 1 Overview tab requires coordinating data from multiple fetch calls | LOW | All data already fetched in the same `fetchProject()` call; only layout changes |
| Splitting Attachments tab into Gallery + Documents | LOW | Gallery items and documents are already separate arrays in the project response; just display them in separate `v-window-item` blocks |
| Tab value rename (e.g., `documents` â†’ `pow`, `personnel` â†’ `team`) affects deep-link URLs | LOW | No external deep-links; `activeTab` defaults to `overview` on load; no router param for tab |
| Data Analytics tab removal (was never implemented) | NONE | The tab was never built; only prototype has it |

---

## Section 2.135 â€” Phase JV: Timeline/Milestone Reintegration + Contextual Viewâ†’Edit Navigation (2026-05-06)

**Status:** Phase 1 Research Complete
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE â€” Phase 3 implementation of JU-A-4 Part 2 BLOCKED on plan-vs-reality gap. Plan instructed "add fields to the Add/Edit milestone form in `edit-[id].vue` Timeline tab" â€” but neither the Timeline tab nor any milestone form exists in `edit-[id].vue`. Operator authorized return to Phase 1+2 to revise the plan.
**Continuity:** Extends Sections 2.133 + 2.134. Does NOT duplicate JU-A-1/2/3 (backend) work â€” those remain valid. JU-A-4 Part 1 (detail-page Timeline display) also remains valid. Only the Edit-page surface needs replanning.

---

### 2.135-A: Root Cause â€” Missing Timeline Tab + Milestone Form in `edit-[id].vue`

#### Evidence (current state on disk)

| Surface | Tab structure | Milestone form? |
|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| `pages/coi/new.vue` (Create) | `basic, contract, schedule, progress, personnel, documents` (6 tabs) | âŒ No milestone fields, no Timeline tab. `grep -i milestone` returns 0 hits. |
| `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (Edit) | `basic, contract, schedule, progress, personnel, documents` (6 tabs, identical to `new.vue`) | âŒ No milestone fields, no Timeline tab. `grep -i milestone` returns 0 hits. |
| `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` (Profile) | `overview, location, contractor, timeline, personnel, attachments` (6 tabs) | âœ… **Read-only** display only. `v-list-item v-for="milestone in project.milestones"` at line 961. No Add/Edit/Delete UI. No "Add Milestone" button. |
| Backend (`construction-projects.controller.ts:191â€“228`) | â€” | âœ… Full CRUD: `GET/POST :id/milestones`, `PATCH/DELETE :id/milestones/:milestoneId` â€” **endpoints exist but no frontend caller.** |

`grep -rn "addMilestone\|createMilestone\|milestoneDialog\|/milestones" pmo-frontend/` â†’ 0 matches.

#### Why it is missing â€” three concurrent factors

1. **Plan assumption error (JU-A-4 plan.md:3880â€“3882):** The JU-A plan was scoped as "Milestone Entity Enhancement" â€” backend column/DTO additions plus a frontend display update. The line "Add `start_date`, `progress`, `category` fields to the Add/Edit milestone form" silently presupposed an existing Add/Edit form. No such form was ever built; the COI module was always a backend-CRUD-only milestone surface.
2. **Lifecycle progression mismatch:** Project creation (`new.vue`) does not capture milestones â€” milestones are post-creation operational artifacts (planning phase outputs). The Edit page mirrors `new.vue` tab structure verbatim, so it inherits the same omission.
3. **Detail page is the only milestone surface:** `detail-[id].vue` was given a read-only Timeline tab (Phase JR-B), but no Add button. The implicit user model was "milestones are seeded externally / via API" â€” never validated against actual operator workflow. JT-F partial milestone enhancement (JU-A-4 Part 1) extended the **display** further without addressing the absent input surface.

#### Status of omission classification

| Classification | Verdict |
|---|---|
| Intentional | âŒ No. Activity-log infrastructure (JT-D) and prototype reference (`TimelineDialogs.tsx`) both presume editable milestones. |
| Incomplete implementation | âœ… Primary cause. Backend CRUD complete; frontend write-path never built. |
| Lost during refactor | âŒ No. Git history search (`git log -- pmo-frontend/pages/coi/`) shows no removed milestone form â€” it was never authored. |

---

### 2.135-B: Required Milestone Data Model (Re-confirmation)

Already established by JU-A-1/2/3 (Section 2.134-C1) and **already implemented** on backend:

**Effective `construction_milestones` schema (post-migration 046):**
| Column | Type | Required | Notes |
|--------|------|----------|-------|
| `title` | VARCHAR(255) | YES | Existing |
| `description` | TEXT | NO | Existing |
| `target_date` | DATE | NO | Existing â€” milestone end / due |
| `actual_date` | DATE | NO | Existing â€” milestone actual end |
| `start_date` | DATE | NO | **JU-A-1 added** |
| `actual_start_date` | DATE | NO | **JU-A-1 added** |
| `progress` | DECIMAL(5,2) | NO (DEFAULT 0) | **JU-A-1 added** â€” 0â€“100 |
| `status` | VARCHAR(50) | NO (DEFAULT 'PENDING') | Existing |
| `category` | VARCHAR(50) | NO | **JU-A-1 added** |
| `remarks` | TEXT | NO | Existing |

**Authoritative DTO (`CreateMilestoneDto`):** All 4 new fields are `@IsOptional()` with class-validator constraints. `progress` is bounded `@Min(0) @Max(100)`. Backward compatible.

**No further entity/DTO/migration work needed** â€” JV scope is purely frontend write-path UI.

---

### 2.135-C: Missing Components and Dependencies (for Edit-page Timeline)

| Concern | Status | Required for JV |
|---------|--------|-----------------|
| Backend milestone CRUD endpoints | âœ… Exists (controller.ts:191â€“228) | Reuse â€” no change |
| `CreateMilestoneDto` | âœ… Exists with all 4 new fields | Reuse â€” no change |
| Activity logging on milestone CUD | âœ… Wired in `construction-projects.service.ts` (JT-D) | Reuse â€” no change |
| `BackendMilestone` + `UIMilestone` adapter | âœ… Updated in JU-A | Reuse |
| `useApi()` composable supporting `post/patch/del` | âœ… Existing | Reuse |
| `useToast()` for success/error feedback | âœ… Existing | Reuse |
| `usePermissions` `canEditCurrentProject` | âœ… Existing | Gate Add/Edit/Delete UI |
| **Add/Edit milestone dialog component** | âŒ **MISSING â€” JV scope** | Build |
| **Milestone list/table with row-level Edit & Delete** | âŒ **MISSING â€” JV scope** | Build |
| **Delete confirm dialog** | âŒ **MISSING â€” JV scope** | Build (or reuse pattern from existing delete flows) |
| **Timeline tab `v-window-item` in `edit-[id].vue`** | âŒ **MISSING â€” JV scope** | Build |
| **`tabOrder` extension** | âŒ Current array length 6; needs +1 | Update |
| **Tab navigation footer (Previous/Next) flow** | âš ï¸ Currently hard-codes `'documents'` as last tab | Update conditional in template |

---

### 2.135-D: Risks Caused by Missing Milestone Tracking

| # | Risk | Severity | Manifestation |
|---|------|----------|---------------|
| R1 | No operator path to create/edit milestones via UI | HIGH | Milestones can only be created via direct API call. Operators cannot manage them. Timeline tab in detail page will always show "No milestones recorded" until the missing UI exists. |
| R2 | Progress tracking incomplete | HIGH | Project-level `physical_progress` is detached from milestone-level `progress`. Without milestone CRUD UI, the planned per-milestone progress visualization (JU-A-4 Part 1) is decorative â€” values can never be set by users. |
| R3 | Reporting/analytics gap | MEDIUM | Future analytics (JU-C and beyond) are designed to aggregate milestone progress. Empty milestone tables produce empty analytics. |
| R4 | Cross-module workflow inconsistency | MEDIUM | UO module captures targets/actuals via dedicated forms (BAR1/BAR2). COI lacks the equivalent for physical accomplishment phases (= milestones). MIS reporting parity is broken until milestone capture UI lands. |
| R5 | Audit trail blind spot | LOW | JT-D's milestone log hooks (`fireLog('CREATE'/'UPDATE'/'DELETE', 'CONSTRUCTION_MILESTONE', ...)`) never fire because no UI calls the endpoints. Auditor role views on activity log will show 0 milestone entries despite the feature being "complete." |
| R6 | Plan integrity erosion | LOW | Marking JU-A "complete" without milestone UI introduces a silent regression: subsequent phases (JU-D-2 component extraction of `CiMilestoneTable.vue`) presume the table exists to extract. JV must close this loop before JU-D can run. |

---

### 2.135-E: Contextual Viewâ†’Edit Navigation â€” Current State

#### Existing routing/navigation structure

**Edit entry point â€” single, non-contextual:**
- `detail-[id].vue:90` â€” `function editProject() { router.push(\`/coi/edit-${projectId}\`) }`
- `detail-[id].vue:494` â€” `<v-btn ... @click="editProject">Edit Project</v-btn>` â€” **page-level button only**
- No section-level Edit buttons. No tab-level Edit buttons.

**Tab activation in Edit page (`edit-[id].vue`):**
- Line 89: `const activeTab = ref('basic')` â€” hard-coded default
- No `route.query` consumption. No deep-linking. Always lands on `basic` tab regardless of view origin.

**Detail page tab values:** `overview, location, contractor, timeline, personnel, attachments` (line 509â€“527)

**Edit page tab values:** `basic, contract, schedule, progress, personnel, documents` (line 90)

#### The mismatch problem

| Detail tab (origin) | Closest Edit tab (destination) |
|---------------------|--------------------------------|
| `overview` | `basic` (description, title, status) |
| `location` | `progress` (building details + lat/lng nested in Progress tab in current Edit form) |
| `contractor` | `contract` |
| `timeline` | **(no tab today â€” JV adds `timeline`)** |
| `personnel` | `personnel` |
| `attachments` | `documents` |

The detail tab values do **not** match Edit tab values. Direct passthrough (`router.push(\`/coi/edit-${id}?tab=${activeTab.value}\`)`) would land on a non-existent tab in many cases. A **mapping layer** is required.

#### Codebase precedent for query-based deep-linking

`grep -rn "route.query" pmo-frontend/pages/` confirms established pattern:
- `university-operations/physical/index.vue:110` â€” `(route.query.quarter as string) && ['Q1','Q2','Q3','Q4'].includes(...)`
- `university-operations/financial/index.vue:89` â€” `(route.query.pillar as string) && PILLARS.some(p => p.id === route.query.pillar)`
- `university-operations/new.vue:15` â€” `route.query.pillar as string`
- `login.vue:64` â€” `route.query.redirect as string`
- `pages/auth/callback.vue:15` â€” `route.query.token`

**Established convention:** Use `route.query.<key>` with explicit allow-list validation. This is the path of least surprise.

---

### 2.135-F: Required Contextual Navigation Strategy

**Mechanism:** URL query parameter `?tab=<value>` consumed by `edit-[id].vue` `onMounted`.

**Strategy:**

1. **Source of truth (detail page):** Each section card in `detail-[id].vue` gets a small "Edit" icon button (visible only when `canEditCurrentProject`). The button calls `editSection(targetTab)` which routes to `/coi/edit-${id}?tab=${targetTab}`.
2. **Sink (edit page):** `edit-[id].vue` reads `route.query.tab`, validates against `tabOrder` allow-list, sets `activeTab.value` accordingly. Falls back to `'basic'` if absent or invalid.
3. **Mapping table** (detail tab â†’ edit tab):
   ```
   overview      â†’ basic
   location      â†’ progress     (building & location fields live here)
   contractor    â†’ contract
   timeline      â†’ timeline     (NEW â€” added by JV)
   personnel     â†’ personnel
   attachments   â†’ documents
   ```
4. **Page-level Edit button (`detail-[id].vue:494`)** continues to route without `?tab=` â†’ lands on default `basic` (preserves existing behavior for the global Edit button).

**No external deep-link breakage** â€” the query param is additive. Existing `/coi/edit-{id}` URLs continue to work unchanged.

#### UI/UX consistency requirements

| Requirement | Implementation |
|-------------|----------------|
| Section-level Edit affordance | Small `mdi-pencil` icon button in each Detail tab section card header (top-right). Tooltip: "Edit this section". |
| Permission gating | All section Edit buttons reuse `canEditCurrentProject` (same as page-level Edit). Hidden for Viewer role. |
| Predictable activation | Edit page must `activeTab` switch synchronously before any field is rendered (avoid tab flicker). Use `onBeforeMount` or set `activeTab` from query param at script-setup-top, before `onMounted`. |
| Preserve back-navigation | `goBack()` in edit page already pushes to `/coi/detail-${id}` (Phase JT-A). No change. The `?tab=` round-trip is one-way: Viewâ†’Edit only. After save, return to detail page with no tab preservation (current behavior). |
| Form focus | NOT in scope for JV. Tab activation is sufficient; field-level focus is YAGNI for v1. |

#### Risks in current navigation structure

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|------|----------|------------|
| N1 | Invalid `?tab=` value (typo, stale link) | LOW | Allow-list validation falls back to `basic`. Toast not needed â€” silent fallback is the standard pattern (see `physical/index.vue:110`). |
| N2 | Detail-page tab and Edit-page tab don't share value names | MEDIUM | Mapping table inside `detail-[id].vue editSection()` resolves once per click. Single source of truth. |
| N3 | Save handler currently hard-codes redirect to detail page | LOW | After save, redirect remains `/coi/detail-${id}` (no tab preservation). Acceptable for v1. Future enhancement: round-trip `?tab=` through save handler. |
| N4 | Adding new `timeline` tab shifts `tabOrder` array index | LOW | `nextTab/prevTab` use `indexOf` lookup â€” stable across reorderings. |

---

### 2.135-G: Timeline / Milestone System Validation

#### Relationship matrix

| Concept | Source | Sink | Coupled to milestone? |
|---------|--------|------|----------------------|
| Project-level `physical_progress` | Manual entry (Edit form Progress tab) | Detail page Overview, dashboards, analytics | LOOSELY â€” independent value today; could be derived from milestones in future, but not required |
| Project-level `target_physical_progress` | Manual entry | Same | NO |
| Milestone `progress` (per-milestone) | Will be entered via JV milestone dialog | Detail page Timeline tab (visualization only) | YES â€” **the new write surface JV builds** |
| Milestone `status` | Same | Detail page Timeline tab + future dashboards | YES |
| `construction_pow_items.progress` (JU-B) | POW form (separate) | Detail page POW tab | NO (independent) |
| `university-operations` BAR1 indicators | Separate module | UO dashboards | NO (different module) |

#### Impact on analytics, dashboards, reporting

- **Today (no JV):** Milestone progress is not displayed in any aggregate dashboard. Detail-page Timeline tab shows raw values per row only.
- **After JV:** No new aggregation surface is added. JV is a **write-path** for milestones; analytics consumption is deferred to future phases.
- **Dashboards (`pages/dashboard.vue`):** Currently reads project-level `physical_progress` only. Unaffected by JV.
- **Reporting:** No DBM/MIS report currently includes milestone-level data. Unaffected by JV.

**Conclusion:** JV is **scoped tightly to the missing CRUD surface**. It does not rewire analytics, dashboards, or reporting. It restores operator capability to populate the data that Phase JU and the prototype already presume to exist.

#### Alignment with future client-side visibility

Client prototype (`prototype/General User Side/.../ProjectDetailPage.tsx`) already has a Timeline tab consuming `timeline: TimelineEntry[]`. JV restores the input surface required for that data to ever exist. JV does NOT touch the client-facing module â€” that's a future phase post-JU.

---

### 2.135-H: Continuity Audit (No Regression)

| Existing feature | JV impact | Verification |
|------------------|-----------|--------------|
| JT-A navigation fix (`router.push` instead of `router.back()`) | NONE | `goBack()` unchanged |
| JT-B attachment permission (`canEditCurrentProject`) | NONE | Reused pattern, no permission rewrite |
| JT-C `permission` middleware on detail page | NONE | Already in place |
| JT-D activity logging | EXTENDED | New milestone CUD operations call existing `fireLog()` hooks â€” no new hook code |
| JT-E Auditor role + `canViewActivityLog()` | NONE | Read-only role, JV adds write surface for editors only |
| JT-F overview enhancements | NONE | Detail page Overview untouched |
| JU-A-1 migration 046 | NONE | Already applied; JV consumes columns it created |
| JU-A-2 entity update | NONE | Already applied |
| JU-A-3 DTO update | NONE | Already applied; JV form binds to these fields |
| JU-A-4 Part 1 detail Timeline display | NONE | Display logic unchanged; values now populatable via JV form |
| JU-B POW module (pending) | INDEPENDENT | POW table is a different concept (per JU-D2). JV touches only milestone CRUD. |
| JU-C overview progress enrichment (pending) | INDEPENDENT | Project-level progress fields, not milestone-level |
| JU-D component extraction (pending) | DEPENDENCY ADDED | `CiMilestoneTable.vue` extraction (JU-D-2) now extracts the JV-built table |
| JU-E tab restructure (pending) | INDEPENDENT | Detail page tab order, not edit page |

**Net regression risk: LOW.** JV is purely additive to `edit-[id].vue` and adds 1 query-param read + 6 small section-Edit buttons to `detail-[id].vue`. No existing code path is rewritten.

---

### 2.135-I: Priority Decision

**Three options presented in operator brief:**
1. Reinstate timeline/milestone system first
2. Implement contextual edit navigation first
3. Refactor Edit Project structure first

**Recommendation: Option 1 first, Option 2 immediately after, in a single Phase JV with two sub-phases.**

#### Justification

| Criterion | Option 1 (Timeline first) | Option 2 (Nav first) | Option 3 (Refactor first) |
|-----------|---------------------------|----------------------|---------------------------|
| Workflow dependency | Restores blocked operator capability today | Adds nav to existing tabs only â€” does not unblock milestone capture | Refactor is YAGNI without a clear destination form |
| System stability | Additive; backend already proven | Additive; small surface | High risk â€” touches form lifecycle, validation, save handler |
| User operational impact | HIGH â€” operators currently cannot create/edit milestones | LOW â€” convenience improvement | NONE direct; preparatory only |
| Plan continuity | Closes the JU-A-4 Part 2 gap that blocked Phase 3 | Enables JV-A surface but doesn't justify on its own | Out of scope for the operator's stated issue |
| Risk of independent execution | LOW (pure additive UI) | LOW (pure additive routing) | HIGH (form refactor without proven need) |

**Sub-phase ordering inside Phase JV:**
- **JV-A: Timeline Tab + Milestone CRUD UI in `edit-[id].vue`** (executes first; closes JU-A-4 Part 2)
- **JV-B: Contextual Viewâ†’Edit `?tab=` query routing** (executes second; consumes the now-existing `timeline` tab value)

Option 3 (Edit page refactor) is **rejected for JV** â€” no scope-justifying need. If extraction is needed, JU-D already covers it as a follow-up phase.

---

### 2.135-J: Constraints and Assumptions Carried Into Phase 2

1. **Plan-frozen scope:** Phase JV adds Timeline tab + milestone CRUD + contextual nav ONLY. No POW, no overview enrichment, no tab restructure (those remain JU-B/C/D/E).
2. **Backward compatibility:** All new milestone fields are optional. Existing milestones (created via direct API or empty) display correctly with default values.
3. **Permission model:** Reuse `canEditCurrentProject` exactly as JT-B. No new permission types. No new role checks.
4. **Activity logging:** Reuse existing `fireLog()` hooks in `construction-projects.service.ts`. No new entity types.
5. **No backend changes:** Migration 046 stands. `CreateMilestoneDto` stands. Controller endpoints stand. JV is frontend-only.
6. **No prototype port:** Do not import `TimelineDialogs.tsx` directly. Vuetify-native dialog implementation. Match prototype semantics, not React/shadcn structure.
7. **Tab order in `edit-[id].vue`:** Insert `timeline` between `schedule` and `progress` (logical flow: schedule â†’ timeline â†’ progress). 7 tabs total. `nextTab/prevTab` continue to work via `indexOf`.

---

#### Classification Summary

| Classification | Count | % | Description |
|---------------|-------|---|-------------|
| G1 â€” Fully covered | 25 | 37% | Direct DB + UI coverage; join-only items included |
| G2 â€” Shape mismatch | 16 | 24% | DB exists; naming, type, enum, or model gap |
| G3 â€” DB exists, UI missing | 1 | 1% | `beneficiaries` (DTO exists, Create form omits field) |
| G4 â€” Full gap (DB + UI missing) | 22 | 32% | No DB column AND no form field |
| G5 â€” Derived/computed | 4 | 6% | No capture needed; compute at query time |
| **Total** | **68** | **100%** | |

**G4 breakdown (22 fields):**
- `overview.summary`, `overview.scope`, `facilities` (3 â€” project-level narrative gaps)
- `financial.category` (1 â€” financial model architecture gap)
- `timeline.id/type/date/title/content/author/attachments` (7 â€” entire diary system missing)
- `team.role`, `team.department`, `team.phone` (3 â€” per-project assignment metadata)
- `gallery.status` (1 â€” lifecycle field missing on gallery entity)
- Financial write surface (3 â€” no admin UI for creating/editing `construction_project_financials` records)
- Additional `record_assignments` metadata (4)
