| E8 | `team.role` (per-project) | `<v-text-field>` per assignment row | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue` + `edit-[id].vue` | "Personnel" tab â†’ assignment table | Optional; max 100 chars | Admin | âœ… | âœ… |
| E9 | `team.department` | `<v-text-field>` or auto from user profile | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue` + `edit-[id].vue` | "Personnel" tab â†’ assignment table | Optional | Admin | âœ… | âœ… |
| E10 | `team.phone` | `<v-text-field>` | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue` + `edit-[id].vue` | "Personnel" tab â†’ assignment table | Optional; format hint | Admin | âœ… | âœ… |
| E11 | `gallery.status` | `<v-select>` (active / archived) | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` | Gallery section â†’ upload/edit dialog | Required; default 'active' | Admin / Staff | âœ… | âœ… |
| E12 | `doc.status` (lifecycle) | `<v-select>` (active / archived / draft) | `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` | Attachments tab â†’ doc/link upload dialog | Required; default 'active' | Admin / Staff | âœ… | âœ… |

---

### 2.136-F â€” Foundation Risk Register

| # | Risk | Severity | Rationale | Client Impact | Workflow Coupling |
|---|------|----------|-----------|---------------|------------------|
| R1 | Timeline diary system entirely missing | CRITICAL | 7 fields have no DB foundation; `timelineData JSONB` is a dead stub | Client cannot render Timeline tab | New normalized table, entity, DTO, service, and admin form required |
| R2 | Financial model mismatch (per-year vs per-category) | HIGH | Client's `FinancialAllocation` is a categorized breakdown; DB stores per-fiscal-year aggregates | Financial breakdown tab renders empty or incorrect | Architectural decision â€” changing model may affect BAR integration |
| R3 | Financial write surface missing | HIGH | `construction_project_financials` records exist but no admin UI can create or edit them | Client sees stale or zero financial data | Blocked until financial CRUD dialog added to `edit-[id].vue` |
| R4 | `team.role/department/phone` missing | MEDIUM | Client renders per-member role badge (emerald), department, phone; none exist in `record_assignments` | Team tab shows members without roles â€” visually incomplete | `record_assignments` schema change required |
| R5 | Gallery category enum mismatch | MEDIUM | Admin: PROGRESS/BEFORE/AFTER; Client: Before/In Progress/Completed/Documentation | Gallery category filter breaks | Requires enum migration or display-layer mapping |
| R6 | Document status semantic mismatch | MEDIUM | Admin `status` tracks processing pipeline; Client expects lifecycle (active/archived/draft) | Document filter tabs fail | Requires second status column or vocabulary replacement |
| R7 | `overview.summary` and `scope` missing | MEDIUM | Two prominent Overview tab text blocks have no DB foundation | Overview tab partially empty | New columns on `construction_projects` required |
| R8 | `facilities` list missing | LOWâ€“MEDIUM | Client renders Facilities as bullet list; no DB field | Overview tab missing section | New column or JSONB field required |
| R9 | `beneficiaries` in DTO but not in Create form | LOW | Field in schema but inaccessible via UI | Beneficiary count always null | Admin form template update only (no migration needed) |
| R10 | `building_type` vs `category` naming | LOW | Client reads `category`; DB stores `building_type` | Category badge shows empty unless API maps it | Serializer alias or response transform fix |
| R11 | `key_features/objectives` TEXT vs string[] | LOW | Client renders bullet lists; admin stores as single TEXT; newline-split is fragile | May render correctly initially; breaks on edge cases | Define newline-delimited contract or migrate to JSON array |
| R12 | `phases[]` indirect mapping via milestones | LOW | No first-class phase entity; milestones used as proxies by category | Phase chips may not appear or show incorrect data | Milestone category convention must be enforced |
| R13 | No public/client-role read endpoint for COI | MEDIUM | All COI endpoints require JWT auth; client prototype assumes accessible API | Client-side integration blocked at API layer | Requires new public or client-role endpoint with field whitelist |
| R14 | `location` text vs lat/lng split | LOW | Admin stores lat/lng + campus separately; client renders single location string | Location shows empty or incorrect format | Composite field or API serialization mapping required |

---

### 2.136-G â€” Cross-Cutting Foundation Concerns

#### G.1 â€” Public Read Endpoint Readiness

**Finding:** All existing COI backend endpoints (`/api/construction-projects/*`) are protected by JWT `AuthGuard`. No public or client-role accessible read endpoint exists. The client prototype assumes a read-accessible API returning project data.

**Gap:** Before client-side integration, a separate read-only endpoint path must be designed that: (a) returns only publishable project records, (b) excludes sensitive operational fields, and (c) supports pagination/filtering/search. This endpoint does not currently exist in `pmo-backend/src/construction-projects/`.

#### G.2 â€” Sensitive Field Exposure Risks

**Finding:** The `construction_projects` table contains fields inappropriate for public exposure: `latitude/longitude` (precise geolocation), `contract_amount` (financial contract value), `project_engineer`/`project_manager` (internal staff names), `contractor_id` (internal FK).

**Gap:** A field whitelist serializer or separate public DTO must be defined before a public endpoint is created. Returning the full entity to unauthenticated clients is a security risk.

#### G.3 â€” File Access Strategy

**Finding:** Gallery images and documents are stored with `file_path` values (local path or storage key). No public URL generation strategy is defined for client access.

**Gap:** Client-side file access requires one of: public CDN bucket, presigned URL generation, or a proxy endpoint. This strategy is undocumented and unimplemented. File access for the client prototype is currently blocked.

#### G.4 â€” Snapshot vs Live-Read Concerns

**Finding:** Progress bars, financial breakdowns, and team assignments on the client are live-read (current state). No snapshot or version-locking mechanism exists in the COI module â€” contrast with `university-operations` which uses `quarterly_reports` with a DRAFT â†’ PUBLISHED lifecycle.

**Gap:** A decision is needed on whether COI client data is: (a) always live, (b) snapshotted periodically, or (c) locked to a review/publication cycle. This affects the data model for timeline entries and financial records.

#### G.5 â€” Slug / Canonical URL Strategy

**Finding:** Client prototype navigation uses UUID in routes. No human-readable slug is implemented.

**Gap:** A slug field (e.g., `csu-science-building-2025`) or deterministic URL strategy should be defined before client-side launch. No slug column exists in the current schema.

#### G.6 â€” Localization / i18n Concerns

**Finding:** All project fields are single-language text strings. No i18n framework is configured. CSU public-facing content may require Filipino or regional language support.

**Gap:** Not an immediate blocker. Documented for future-proofing. If required, a multi-language content strategy must be designed before the client layer is built.

---

### 2.136-H â€” Coverage Audit Summary

**Audit Date:** 2026-05-07
**Phase:** JV â€” COI Admin â†” Client Data-Capture Coverage Audit
**Total fields audited:** 68 across 8 client-visible interfaces

| Classification | Count | % |
|---------------|-------|---|
| G1 â€” Fully covered | 25 | 37% |
| G2 â€” Shape mismatch | 16 | 24% |
| G3 â€” DB exists, UI gap | 1 | 1% |
| G4 â€” Full gap (DB + UI missing) | 22 | 32% |
| G5 â€” Derived/computed | 4 | 6% |
| **Total** | **68** | **100%** |

**Priority gap areas:**
1. **Timeline diary system** (G4 Ã— 7) â€” requires new normalized `construction_timeline_entries` table; largest single gap
2. **Financial model architecture** (G4 Ã— 1, G2 Ã— 2) â€” per-year vs per-category mismatch; architectural decision required
3. **Team assignment metadata** (G4 Ã— 3) â€” role, department, phone missing from `record_assignments`
4. **Project narrative fields** (G4 Ã— 2) â€” summary, scope not in DB or any form
5. **Gallery + document semantic gaps** (G2 Ã— 2) â€” enum and status vocabulary mismatches
6. **No public read endpoint** (R13) â€” client integration blocked at API layer

The system's core project data (title, status, dates, contractor, funding, progress, gallery upload, document upload) is well-covered at 37% G1. Foundation gaps are concentrated in extended narrative content, the timeline diary subsystem, per-project team metadata, and the financial categorization model.

---

*Phase 1 complete â€” awaiting operator Phase 2 authorization.*

## Section 2.137 â€” COI Configuration Error Analysis and Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

> **Phase:** JX Research (Phase 1 only)
> **Date:** 2026-05-07
> **Trigger:** Vite build error blocking COI Project Details and Edit pages

---

### 2.137-A â€” Import Resolution Failure: Root Cause

**Error reported:**
```
[plugin:vite:import-analysis]
Failed to resolve import "~/utils/api"
from: components/coi/CiPowItemTable.vue
```

**File analyzed:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiPowItemTable.vue`

**Line 3 (the broken import):**
```typescript
import { api } from '~/utils/api'
```

**Root cause classification:** STALE / INCORRECT IMPORT PATH â€” not an alias misconfiguration.

**Analysis:**

1. **`~/utils/api` does not exist.**
   The only file in `pmo-frontend/utils/` is `adapters.ts`. No `api.ts` or `api/index.ts` is present at that path.

2. **The correct API surface is `~/composables/useApi.ts`.**
   `useApi()` is a Nuxt 3 composable that returns `{ get, post, put, patch, del, upload, loading, error }`. It is **auto-imported by Nuxt** â€” no explicit import statement is needed in any `.vue` file.

3. **All other COI pages use the correct pattern:**
   ```typescript
   // pages/coi/edit-[id].vue line 11
   const api = useApi()
   // pages/coi/detail-[id].vue line 11
   const api = useApi()
   // pages/coi/index.vue, new.vue â€” same pattern
   ```
   No explicit `import` is needed; Nuxt auto-import resolves `useApi` from `composables/`.

4. **`CiPowItemTable.vue` was written with a singleton import pattern** (`{ api }`) assuming a module-level export that never existed in this codebase. This was likely carried over from a different project or written without running the dev server to validate.

5. **Nuxt alias is correctly configured.** `nuxt.config.ts` has no custom alias definitions; `~` is the default Nuxt alias pointing to the frontend project root (`pmo-frontend/`). The alias itself is not the issue.

**Exact fix required (1 line change):**
```diff
- import { api } from '~/utils/api'
+ // (removed â€” useApi is auto-imported by Nuxt)
```
Then add inside `<script setup>`:
```typescript
const api = useApi()
```

**Severity:** ðŸ”´ BUILD-BLOCKING â€” `CiPowItemTable.vue` cannot compile, which cascades to all importers.

---

### 2.137-B â€” Impact Scope: Affected Pages

Both pages that import `CiPowItemTable.vue` are blocked:

| File | Import line | Impact |
|------|------------|--------|
| `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` | line 3 | Edit Project page fails to render |
| `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` | line 3 | Project Details page fails to render |

No other files import `CiPowItemTable.vue`. Grep confirms exactly 2 importers.

**Unaffected pages:**
- `pages/coi/index.vue` â€” no `CiPowItemTable` import; works
- `pages/coi/new.vue` â€” no `CiPowItemTable` import; works
- `pages/coi/public/` â€” no `CiPowItemTable` import; works

---

### 2.137-C â€” Build System Alignment Audit

| Check | Finding |
|-------|---------|
| `nuxt.config.ts` has custom alias definitions | âŒ None â€” default Nuxt `~` alias only |
| `~/utils/api` file exists | âŒ Does not exist |
| `~/utils/adapters.ts` exists | âœ… Exists â€” used correctly by all pages |
| `~/composables/useApi.ts` exists | âœ… Exists and auto-imported by Nuxt |
| `useApi()` auto-import configured | âœ… Nuxt default â€” composables in `~/composables/` are auto-imported |
| Any `tsconfig.json` path alias issues | N/A â€” issue is a missing file, not a path alias |

**Conclusion:** Zero infrastructure changes required. The fix is a 1-line removal + 1-line addition inside `CiPowItemTable.vue`.

---

### 2.137-D â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

#### D1 â€” Tab Structure Comparison

| Tab | `new.vue` | `edit-[id].vue` | `detail-[id].vue` |
|-----|-----------|----------------|-------------------|
| Basic Info | `basic` âœ… | `basic` âœ… | â€” (Overview) |
| Contract | `contract` âœ… | `contract` âœ… | â€” (in Overview) |
| Schedule | `schedule` âœ… | `schedule` âœ… | â€” (in Overview) |
| Timeline | âŒ Not present | `timeline` âœ… | `timeline` âœ… |
| POW | âŒ Not present | `pow` âœ… | `pow` âœ… |
| Progress & Details | `progress` âœ… | `progress` âœ… | â€” (in Overview) |
| Financial | âŒ Not present | `financial` âœ… | âŒ Not in tab bar |
| Personnel / Team | `personnel` âœ… | `personnel` âœ… | `team` âš ï¸ |
| Documents / Attachments | `documents` âœ… | `documents` âœ… | `documents` âœ… |
| Gallery | âŒ Not present | âŒ Not present | `gallery` âœ… |
| Overview | âŒ N/A | âŒ N/A | `overview` âœ… |

**Gap: `personnel` vs `team`**
- `edit-[id].vue` v-tab value: `"personnel"`, label: "Personnel"
- `detail-[id].vue` v-tab value: `"team"`, label: "Team"
- These represent the same concept (project team / assigned personnel)
- The JV contextual navigation uses `?tab=` query params â€” `detail-[id].vue` edit buttons route to `edit-[id].vue?tab=personnel`. If a user clicks the Team section's edit button on `detail-[id].vue`, it must route to `?tab=personnel` (not `?tab=team`), because the edit page uses `personnel` as the tab value. This requires careful mapping when JV-B is implemented or already implemented.

**Intentional omissions (not bugs):**
- `new.vue` lacks timeline/pow/financial â€” CORRECT. These require an existing `projectId` (the project must first be created via POST). These tabs are edit/detail-only.
- `detail-[id].vue` lacks Financial as a dedicated tab â€” Financial data is rendered inside the Overview tab inline. This is a DESIGN CHOICE, not a missing feature. Financial write surface (JW-F) will be in `edit-[id].vue`.
- `new.vue`/`edit-[id].vue` lack Gallery â€” Gallery management lives in `detail-[id].vue`. This is intentional separation.

#### D2 â€” Orphaned Tab Content in `detail-[id].vue`

Line 958 of `detail-[id].vue` contains a comment:
```
<!-- ============= LOCATION & PHYSICAL TAB (orphaned by JU-E; retained for safety, no v-tab triggers it) ============= -->
```

This is dead template content that Vuetify will never render (no matching `v-tab` with its value). It poses no runtime risk but is dead code left from Phase JU-E tab restructure.

**Risk:** Low. No user-visible impact. May cause confusion during future maintenance.

#### D3 â€” `canEditCurrentProject` Permission Guard Consistency

All three pages apply permission checks:
- `new.vue` â€” implicit (staff/admin can create)
- `edit-[id].vue` â€” uses `canEditCurrentProject` from `usePermissions()` to show/hide fields and buttons
- `detail-[id].vue` â€” uses `canEditCurrentProject` for section Edit buttons

`CiPowItemTable` receives `canEdit` as a prop:
- `edit-[id].vue` passes `:can-edit="true"` â€” HARDCODED. This means ALL authenticated users who can reach the edit page can add/edit/delete POW items regardless of their assignment status.
- `detail-[id].vue` passes `:can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"` â€” CORRECT, uses permission guard.

This is a continuity gap: the edit page bypasses the permission guard for POW items.

#### D4 â€” `useToast` Usage in `CiPowItemTable.vue`

The component imports `useToast` correctly:
```typescript
import { useToast } from '~/composables/useToast'
```
This is an **explicit import** â€” not using Nuxt auto-import. All other pages omit the import (using auto-import). While functionally correct, it is slightly inconsistent with the codebase convention. **Not a bug.**

---

### 2.137-E â€” Shared Component Architecture Assessment

| Aspect | Finding |
|--------|---------|
| `components/coi/` directory | 1 file only: `CiPowItemTable.vue` |
| Duplication of POW table logic | None â€” only one implementation |
| API wrapper fragmentation | Composable `useApi.ts` is single-source-of-truth; no fragmentation |
| Adapter functions | `utils/adapters.ts` is single-source; used consistently |
| Toast utility | `composables/useToast.ts` â€” mostly auto-imported; `CiPowItemTable.vue` explicitly imports it (minor inconsistency) |
| Shared permission composable | `composables/usePermissions.ts` â€” used in detail/edit pages consistently |

**Assessment:** Architecture is clean. The single anomaly is `CiPowItemTable.vue`'s stale import path. No duplicate utilities, no fragmented API wrappers.

---

### 2.137-F â€” Priority Decision

| Priority | Action | Justification |
|----------|--------|---------------|
| 1 (BLOCKER) | Fix `CiPowItemTable.vue` import | Both `edit-[id].vue` and `detail-[id].vue` cannot render until this is resolved. All other continuity work is blocked. |
| 2 (LOW) | Fix POW `canEdit` hardcoded in `edit-[id].vue` | Permission bypass â€” `"true"` should be `canEditCurrentProject`. Minor security gap. |
| 3 (LOW, cosmetic) | Remove orphaned Location & Physical tab content from `detail-[id].vue` | Dead code cleanup â€” no runtime impact. |
| 4 (DEFER) | `personnel` vs `team` tab name reconciliation | Only matters when JV-B contextual navigation is verified. JV-B edit buttons must route to `?tab=personnel` (not `?tab=team`) since `edit-[id].vue` uses `personnel`. |

---

### 2.137-G â€” Risks and Constraints

| Risk | Severity | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Both major admin COI flows blocked | ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | Edit and Detail pages cannot render. All JU/JV/JW development blocked until fixed. |
| `canEdit="true"` hardcoded | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | Any authenticated user reaching `edit-[id].vue` can mutate POW items regardless of project assignment |
| Orphaned dead template code | ðŸŸ¢ LOW | No runtime risk; maintenance confusion only |
| `personnel`/`team` naming gap | ðŸŸ¢ LOW | Deferred to JV-B verification â€” contextual edit buttons must map correctly |

---

## Section 2.138 â€” COI Milestone and POW Schema Drift Analysis

> **Phase:** JY Research (Phase 1 only)
> **Date:** 2026-05-13
> **Trigger:** Runtime HTTP 500 errors on milestone POST and POW GET/POST due to missing DB columns

---

### 2.138-A â€” Milestone Schema Drift: Root Cause

**Errors observed:**
```
POST /api/construction-projects/{id}/milestones
â†’ column "start_date" of relation "construction_milestones" does not exist
```

**Layer audit:**

| Layer | start_date | actual_start_date | progress | category |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|----------|----------|
| `construction_milestones` DB table (live) | âŒ MISSING | âŒ MISSING | âŒ MISSING | âŒ MISSING |
| `database/migrations/046_enhance_construction_milestones.sql` | âœ… Defines ADD COLUMN | âœ… Defines ADD COLUMN | âœ… Defines ADD COLUMN | âœ… Defines ADD COLUMN |
| `ConstructionMilestone` entity | âœ… `startDate` | âœ… `actualStartDate` | âœ… `progress` | âœ… `category` |
| `CreateMilestoneDto` | âœ… `start_date` | âœ… `actual_start_date` | âœ… `progress` | âœ… `category` |
| Service `createMilestone()` | âœ… maps to entity | âœ… maps to entity | âœ… maps to entity | âœ… maps to entity |

**Root cause:** Migration `046_enhance_construction_milestones.sql` was authored correctly (Phase JU-A-1) and defines all four `ALTER TABLE ... ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` statements. The migration was **never executed** against the live database. The entity, DTO, and service are all ahead of the database schema.

**Fix required:** Execute migration 046 only â€” no code changes needed.

**Migration 046 content:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE construction_milestones
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS start_date DATE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS actual_start_date DATE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS progress DECIMAL(5,2) NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.00,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS category VARCHAR(50);
```

---

### 2.138-B â€” POW Schema Drift: Root Cause

**Errors observed:**
```
GET /api/construction-projects/{id}/pow-items
â†’ column c0.sort_order does not exist

POST /api/construction-projects/{id}/pow-items
â†’ column "progress" of relation "construction_pow_items" does not exist
```

**Baseline DB schema (from `database/database draft/2026_01_12/pmo_schema_pg.sql`):**

The `construction_pow_items` table was bootstrapped from a draft schema file and contains:
`id, project_id, item_number, description, unit, quantity, estimated_material_cost, estimated_labor_cost, unit_cost, total_cost, is_unit_cost_overridden, date_entry, status, remarks, category, phase, is_variation_order, parent_item_id, metadata, created_at`

**Entity `ConstructionPowItem` defines (Phase JU-B additions):**

| Entity field | DB column | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `description` | `description` | âœ… exists |
| `quantity` | `quantity` | âœ… exists (type: DB DECIMAL(15,2) NOT NULL vs entity DECIMAL(10,3) nullable â€” safe implicit cast) |
| `unit` | `unit` | âœ… exists |
| `unitCost` | `unit_cost` | âœ… exists |
| `estimatedMaterialCost` | `estimated_material_cost` | âœ… exists |
| `estimatedLaborCost` | `estimated_labor_cost` | âœ… exists |
| `estimatedProjectCost` | `estimated_project_cost` | âŒ MISSING |
| `progress` | `progress` | âŒ MISSING |
| `variance` | `variance` | âŒ MISSING |
| `startDate` | `start_date` | âŒ MISSING |
| `endDate` | `end_date` | âŒ MISSING |
| `projectDuration` | `project_duration` | âŒ MISSING |
| `status` | `status` | âœ… exists (DB default `'Active'`, entity default `'NOT_STARTED'`) |
| `category` | `category` | âœ… exists |
| `sortOrder` | `sort_order` | âŒ MISSING |
| `createdAt` | `created_at` | âœ… exists |
| `updatedAt` | `updated_at` | âŒ MISSING |

**Columns present in DB draft but NOT in entity (orphaned from JU-B redesign):**
`item_number`, `total_cost`, `is_unit_cost_overridden`, `date_entry`, `remarks`, `phase`, `is_variation_order`, `parent_item_id`, `metadata`

**Root cause:** During Phase JU-B, the `ConstructionPowItem` entity was redesigned from the original schema into a leaner work-breakdown-structure model. New columns were added to the entity (`progress`, `sort_order`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `project_duration`, `variance`, `estimated_project_cost`, `updated_at`) but **no numbered `ALTER TABLE` migration was ever written**. The live DB still has the old column set from the draft schema bootstrap.

---

### 2.138-C â€” Migration Governance Audit

**Pattern:**

1. COI tables were bootstrapped from `database/database draft/` files â€” no numbered migration creates them.
2. Numbered migrations (002â€“046) only add columns to pre-existing tables via `ALTER TABLE`.
3. The breakdown at Phase JU-B: entity and DTO were updated, service was updated, but the corresponding `ALTER TABLE` migration was not authored.
4. Migration 046 (milestones) was authored correctly but never executed â€” an operator deployment oversight.

**Governance gaps:**

| Gap | Impact |
|-----|--------|
| No migration authored when entity columns were added in JU-B | POW columns silently missing in DB |
| Migration 046 authored but not applied | Milestone columns missing in DB |
| Draft schema files serve as implicit DB baseline, not numbered migrations | No single source of truth |
| No schema-execution verification checkpoint per deployment | Migrations accumulate unexecuted |

---

### 2.138-D â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

| Flow | Milestone impact | POW impact |
|------|-----------------|------------|
| Create Project (`new.vue`) | No milestone entry on create â€” no impact | No POW entry on create â€” no impact |
| Edit Project (`edit-[id].vue`) | Timeline tab: Add Milestone POSTs `start_date`/`progress`/`category` â†’ **500** | POW tab (`CiPowItemTable`): GET crashes (`sort_order`), POST crashes (`progress`) |
| Detail View (`detail-[id].vue`) | ORM `find()` selects all entity columns â†’ missing columns returned as undefined; no crash on read, but data incomplete | ORM `find()` with `orderBy: { sortOrder: 'asc' }` â†’ **SELECT crash** |

**Frontend and backend code layers require NO changes.** Both the DTO and service already handle all fields correctly. The fix is exclusively in the database schema.

---

### 2.138-E â€” Severity and Priority

| Issue | Severity | Fix |
|-------|---------|-----|
| Milestone POST: `start_date` missing | ðŸ”´ BLOCKER | Execute migration 046 (already written) |
| POW GET: `sort_order` missing | ðŸ”´ BLOCKER | Write + execute new migration 047 |
| POW POST: `progress` missing | ðŸ”´ BLOCKER | Same migration 047 |
| POW `quantity` type mismatch (DECIMAL(15,2) vs DECIMAL(10,3)) | ðŸŸ¡ LOW | Implicit PostgreSQL cast; no errors expected; can normalize in 047 |
| Orphaned POW columns in DB (`total_cost`, `item_number`, etc.) | ðŸŸ¢ COSMETIC | Leave in place â€” no query impact |

**Priority:**
1. Execute migration 046 â†’ unblocks Timeline tab (zero code changes)
2. Write and execute migration 047 â†’ unblocks POW tab (zero code changes)
3. Orphaned column cleanup â€” deferred (YAGNI)

---

*Phase 1 complete â€” awaiting operator Phase 2 authorization.*

## Section 2.139 â€” COI Milestone Display and POW `item_number` Constraint Analysis

> **Phase:** JZ
> **Date:** 2026-05-07
> **Status:** Phase 1 complete
> **Precondition:** Phase JY migrations executed (JY-A + JY-B). Milestone POST succeeds. POW GET succeeds. Two new runtime failures observed.

---

### 2.139-A â€” Milestone Display Failure in `edit-[id].vue` (Response Shape Mismatch)

**Observed behavior:** Milestone submission succeeds (HTTP 201). Query log confirms DB insertion and retrieval (`1 result`). Despite this, the Timeline tab renders "No milestones recorded."

**Root cause â€” confirmed:**

`fetchMilestones()` (`edit-[id].vue` lines 623â€“629) calls:
```typescript
const res = await api.get<{ data: EditMilestoneItem[] }>(
  `/api/construction-projects/${projectId}/milestones`
)
existingMilestones.value = (res.data || []).map(m => ({ ... }))
```

The controller (`construction-projects.controller.ts:199`) returns:
```typescript
return this.service.findMilestones(id);
```

`findMilestones()` returns `Promise<ConstructionMilestone[]>` â€” a plain array. NestJS serializes this as a JSON array `[{...}]`.

`useApi.get()` (`composables/useApi.ts:66`) does `return await response.json()` â€” no wrapping. So `res` IS the array.

On a JavaScript array, `.data` is `undefined`. Therefore:
- `res.data` â†’ `undefined`
- `(res.data || []).map(...)` â†’ `[].map(...)` â†’ `[]`
- `existingMilestones.value` stays empty every fetch

**Evidence:** DB query log shows `[took 1 ms, 1 result]` for `construction_milestones` select. Frontend renders zero items.

**Contrast:** `CiPowItemTable.vue:112` already uses the correct defensive pattern:
```typescript
const list = Array.isArray(res) ? res : (res?.data || [])
```

**Scope:** `fetchMilestones()` only. `fetchGallery()` returns `PaginatedResponse<>` (has `.data`). `fetchDocs()` controller explicitly returns `{ data }` wrapper. Both unaffected.

---

### 2.139-A2 â€” Milestone Title Not Rendering in `detail-[id].vue` (Field Name Mismatch)

**Observed behavior:** Milestone cards visible in `detail-[id].vue` (data loads via `findOne()` embed), but milestone titles are blank.

**Root cause â€” confirmed:**

`detail-[id].vue:1027` renders:
```html
<span class="font-weight-medium">{{ milestone.name }}</span>
```

The `ConstructionMilestone` entity defines the field as `title` (`construction-milestone.entity.ts:12`):
```typescript
@Property({ length: 255 })
title!: string;
```

`findOne()` embeds milestones via ORM `milestoneRepo.find()` â€” result has camelCase field names per MikroORM convention. The property is `title`, not `name`. Therefore `milestone.name` is always `undefined`.

All other milestone field references in `detail-[id].vue` are correct: `milestone.status`, `milestone.category`, `milestone.startDate`, `milestone.targetDate`, `milestone.actualDate`, `milestone.progress`, `milestone.description`, `milestone.remarks`.

---

### 2.139-B â€” POW `item_number` NOT NULL Constraint Violation

**Observed error:**
```
null value in column "item_number" of relation "construction_pow_items" violates not-null constraint
Failing row contains (69803f9f-..., 1d3656e1-..., null, Tenetur rem quo amet, ...)
```

**Root cause â€” confirmed:**

The `construction_pow_items` table was bootstrapped from the draft schema (`database/database draft/2026_01_12/pmo_schema_pg.sql`) which defined:
```sql
item_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL
```

Phase JU-B redesigned the `ConstructionPowItem` entity. `item_number` was intentionally removed â€” no such property exists in `construction-pow-item.entity.ts` or any DTO.

Phase JY-B migration (`Migration20260507190000_EnhanceConstructionPowItems.ts`) added the 8 missing JU-B columns using `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`, but did **not** address the orphaned `item_number NOT NULL` constraint.

Therefore every ORM INSERT omits `item_number` â†’ PostgreSQL rejects with NOT NULL violation.

**Column position in failing row:** slot 3 (id, project_id, **item_number**, descriptionâ€¦) = `null`

**Recommended fix:** `ALTER TABLE construction_pow_items ALTER COLUMN item_number DROP NOT NULL` â€” column becomes nullable, no default needed since no code reads it.

---

### 2.139-C â€” Migration Governance Gap

| Gap | Root cause | Impact |
|-----|-----------|--------|
| JU-B removed `item_number` from entity but wrote no migration to reconcile the DB column | No governance checkpoint requiring "entity field removed â†’ write ALTER COLUMN or DROP COLUMN migration" | Subsequent JY-B migration added new columns but skipped the unresolved constraint |
| JY-B noted orphaned columns as YAGNI | Correct for nullable orphaned columns; incorrect for NOT NULL columns â€” those block INSERTs | All POW inserts fail despite JY-B migration appearing complete |

**Rule going forward:** When an entity field is removed AND the DB column carries a NOT NULL constraint, a migration to `ALTER COLUMN â€¦ DROP NOT NULL` (or `DROP COLUMN`) is **mandatory**, not optional.

---

### 2.139-D â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Impact

| Flow | Milestone | POW |
|------|-----------|-----|
| `edit-[id].vue` Timeline tab | Data fetched from DB, rendered as empty (2.139-A). Add/Edit/Delete operations work (POST/PATCH/DELETE succeed). | INSERT blocked by `item_number` NOT NULL (2.139-B) |
| `detail-[id].vue` Milestones card | Data embedded in `findOne()` response; milestone list visible but titles blank (2.139-A2) | Read-only list â€” not affected by 2.139-B |
| `create/new.vue` | No milestone or POW entry on creation â€” no impact | No impact |

---

### 2.139-E â€” Severity and Priority

| Issue | Severity | Fix location |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `fetchMilestones()` response shape mismatch (2.139-A) | ðŸ”´ BLOCKER â€” milestones never visible in edit page | `edit-[id].vue:626` â€” 1-line change |
| `milestone.name` field mismatch (2.139-A2) | ðŸŸ¡ HIGH â€” titles blank in detail view | `detail-[id].vue:1027` â€” 1-word change |
| POW `item_number` NOT NULL (2.139-B) | ðŸ”´ BLOCKER â€” all POW inserts fail | New MikroORM migration |

**Execution order:**
1. JZ-B: DB migration (remove constraint) â€” no code dependency; unblocks POW POST immediately
2. JZ-A: Fix `fetchMilestones()` in `edit-[id].vue` â€” 1-line frontend fix
3. JZ-A2: Fix `milestone.name` â†’ `milestone.title` in `detail-[id].vue` â€” 1-word fix

---

*Section 2.139 â€” Phase 1 complete. Phase JZ plan follows in plan.md.*

---

## Section 2.140 â€” POW `construction_pow_items` Full Orphaned NOT NULL Column Audit

> **Phase:** KA
> **Date:** 2026-05-07
> **Status:** Phase 1 complete
> **Trigger:** After JZ-B migration executed (`item_number DROP NOT NULL`), POW POST immediately fails on next orphaned NOT NULL column: `total_cost`. Pattern identified â€” systematic audit required.

---

### 2.140-A â€” Audit Methodology

Cross-reference the draft schema bootstrap (`database/database draft/2026_01_12/pmo_schema_pg.sql` lines 598â€“623) against the current `ConstructionPowItem` entity (`construction-pow-item.entity.ts`) to identify every NOT NULL column whose corresponding entity field is either:
- **Absent** (removed during JU-B redesign, or never added), OR
- **Nullable in the entity** (entity allows NULL; DB does not)

Columns with `NOT NULL DEFAULT <value>` are safe â€” PostgreSQL populates them automatically even when the ORM omits them. Only bare `NOT NULL` without a DEFAULT are blockers.

---

### 2.140-B â€” Complete Column-by-Column Analysis

| Draft schema column | DB constraint | Entity property | Entity nullable? | Result |
|---------------------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------|--------|
| `id` | `PRIMARY KEY` | `id` | No | âœ… Generated by DB |
| `project_id` | `NOT NULL` | `projectId` | No | âœ… Always provided |
| `item_number VARCHAR(50)` | **`NOT NULL`** | *(none â€” removed JU-B)* | â€” | âœ… **Fixed by JZ-B** |
| `description TEXT` | `NOT NULL` | `description` | No | âœ… Always provided |
| `unit VARCHAR(20)` | **`NOT NULL`** | `unit` | **Yes** | ðŸ”´ **Orphaned NOT NULL** â€” entity nullable |
| `quantity DECIMAL(15,2)` | **`NOT NULL`** | `quantity` | **Yes** | ðŸ”´ **Orphaned NOT NULL** â€” entity nullable |
| `estimated_material_cost` | nullable | `estimatedMaterialCost` | Yes | âœ… |
| `estimated_labor_cost` | nullable | `estimatedLaborCost` | Yes | âœ… |
| `unit_cost DECIMAL(15,2)` | **`NOT NULL`** | `unitCost` | **Yes** | ðŸ”´ **Orphaned NOT NULL** â€” entity nullable |
| `total_cost DECIMAL(15,2)` | **`NOT NULL`** | *(none â€” removed JU-B)* | â€” | ðŸ”´ **Orphaned NOT NULL** â€” no entity property (current blocker) |
| `is_unit_cost_overridden` | `DEFAULT FALSE` | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… DEFAULT prevents NULL |
| `date_entry DATE` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |
| `status VARCHAR(50)` | `DEFAULT 'Active'` | `status` (default `'NOT_STARTED'`) | No | âœ… Entity always provides value |
| `remarks TEXT` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |
| `category VARCHAR(100)` | nullable | `category` | Yes | âœ… |
| `phase VARCHAR(100)` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |
| `is_variation_order` | `NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE` | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… DEFAULT prevents NULL |
| `parent_item_id` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |
| `metadata JSONB` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |
| `created_at` | `NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()` | `createdAt` | No | âœ… Entity provides default |
| `updated_at` | `NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()` | `updatedAt` | No | âœ… Entity provides default |
| `deleted_at` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |
| `deleted_by` | nullable | *(none)* | â€” | âœ… |

**JY-B additions** (appended after draft-schema columns):
`progress`, `sort_order`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `project_duration`, `variance`, `estimated_project_cost` â€” all have defaults or are nullable per JY-B migration âœ…

---

### 2.140-C â€” Orphaned NOT NULL Columns Requiring Remediation

| Column | Reason blocked | Current status |
|--------|---------------|----------------|
| `item_number` | Removed from entity (JU-B); no DEFAULT | âœ… Fixed by JZ-B |
| `total_cost` | Removed from entity (JU-B); replaced by `estimatedProjectCost`; no DEFAULT | ðŸ”´ **Current runtime blocker** |
| `unit` | Entity marks as nullable; draft schema has NOT NULL; no DEFAULT | ðŸŸ¡ At-risk (form always provides today; will fail if omitted) |
| `quantity` | Same pattern as `unit` | ðŸŸ¡ At-risk |
| `unit_cost` | Same pattern as `unit` | ðŸŸ¡ At-risk |

**Total needing remediation in this phase:** 4 columns (`total_cost`, `unit`, `quantity`, `unit_cost`)

---

### 2.140-D â€” Why `unit`, `quantity`, `unit_cost` Are At Risk

The JU-B entity redesigned these as **optional** (`nullable: true`) to support partial POW entries (e.g., description-only items without cost data). The draft schema had them as `NOT NULL` because the original design assumed all cost fields were mandatory.

Currently the form populates all three, so they don't fail today. However:
- The entity allows NULL
- The frontend form fields are not hard-required at the HTML level
- A future empty submission would crash with the same NOT NULL violation pattern
- Leaving them NOT NULL contradicts the entity contract and creates a silent time-bomb

---

### 2.140-E â€” Root Cause: Incremental Migration vs. Full Schema Reconciliation

| Migration | Action | Columns addressed |
|-----------|--------|-------------------|
| JY-B (Migration20260507190000) | ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS (8 new columns) | Added new columns; did not audit existing NOT NULL constraints |
| JZ-B (Migration20260507200000) | DROP NOT NULL on `item_number` | Fixed one column â€” triggered discovery of `total_cost` |
| **KA-A (this phase)** | DROP NOT NULL on all 4 remaining orphaned columns | Complete reconciliation |

**Root cause:** Each fix was reactive (fixing the column that caused the most recent crash) rather than proactive (auditing all NOT NULL columns before any fix). This phase closes the gap with a complete, one-shot reconciliation.

---

### 2.140-F â€” Scope and Constraints

- **No entity changes** â€” entity already models these columns correctly (absent or nullable)
- **No DTO changes** â€” DTOs already match entity
- **No service changes** â€” service does not reference `total_cost`
- **No frontend changes** â€” form already sends the data the entity expects
- **Database-only** â€” single `ALTER TABLE` statement
- **Idempotent** â€” `DROP NOT NULL` on an already-nullable column is a no-op in PostgreSQL; safe to re-run

---

*Section 2.140 â€” Phase 1 complete. Phase KA plan follows in plan.md.*

---

## Section 2.141 â€” COI Operational Governance Refinement: POW, Financials, Documents, Authorization, and Codebase Structure

> **Audit Date:** 2026-05-13
> **Continuity:** Extends Sections 2.136â€“2.140. Does not duplicate validated findings.
> **Scope:** Aâ€“G: POW governance, financial domain, document compliance, authorization/logging, codebase structure, cross-flow continuity, priority decision.

---

### 2.141-A â€” POW Tab Governance and Field Validation

#### A.1 â€” Required Field Inventory vs Current Entity

| Prompt-Required Field | DB Column | Entity Property | DTO Field | Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Description | `description TEXT NOT NULL` | `description` | `description` | âœ… |
| Quantity | `quantity DECIMAL(10,3)` | `quantity` (nullable) | `quantity` | âœ… nullable |
| Unit | `unit VARCHAR(50)` | `unit` (nullable) | `unit` | âœ… nullable |
| Material Cost | `estimated_material_cost DECIMAL(15,2)` | `estimatedMaterialCost` | `estimated_material_cost` | âœ… nullable |
| Labor Cost | `estimated_labor_cost DECIMAL(15,2)` | `estimatedLaborCost` | `estimated_labor_cost` | âœ… nullable |
| Project Cost | `estimated_project_cost DECIMAL(15,2)` | `estimatedProjectCost` | `estimated_project_cost` | âœ… nullable |
| Unit Cost | `unit_cost DECIMAL(15,2)` | `unitCost` (nullable) | `unit_cost` | âœ… nullable |
| Date of Entry | absent | absent | absent | **GAP** â€” `startDate` = work start; `createdAt` = auto-set; no user-settable entry date |
| Status | `status VARCHAR(50)` | `status` | `status` | âœ… (CHECK: NOT_STARTED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/BLOCKED) |
| Remarks | absent | absent | absent | **GAP** â€” `description` serves different semantic purpose |
| Toggle Columns | N/A | N/A | N/A | UI-only â€” no DB change needed |

**Orphaned DB columns (not in entity or DTO):**
- `total_cost` â€” replaced by `estimatedProjectCost`; KA migration drops NOT NULL constraint
- `item_number` â€” draft-schema artifact; JZ-B migration drops NOT NULL; column exists silently

#### A.2 â€” Nullability Governance

| Classification | Fields |
|---|---|
| Required (NOT NULL) | `description`, `status`, `sort_order`, `progress` |
| Optional + Nullable | `quantity`, `unit`, `unit_cost`, `estimated_material_cost`, `estimated_labor_cost`, `estimated_project_cost`, `variance`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `project_duration`, `category` |
| Missing (need addition) | `remarks` (administrative justification), `date_of_entry` (user-set audit date) |
| Orphaned in DB (nullable after KA) | `total_cost`, `item_number` |

#### A.3 â€” Workflow Capability Assessment

| Capability | Supported | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| Contractor cost tracking | âœ… via cost fields | No contractor-specific reference per item |
| Accomplishment monitoring | Progress % only | No qualitative remarks or narrative justification |
| Cost tracking | âœ… material/labor/project cost | No computed total shown in UI |
| Operational reporting | Partial | No `date_of_entry`, no `remarks` for audit trail |
| Auditing | Partial | ActivityLog fires but no field-delta recording |

#### A.4 â€” Table/UI Scalability Risks

- Toggle columns: not yet implemented â€” all columns shown always; needed for mobile and information-density control
- Large dataset handling: `v-table` without pagination â€” degrades at >100 items
- Sort order: `sort_order INTEGER` exists but no drag-to-reorder UI
- No computed total row in UI (sum of all estimated_project_cost per project)

#### A.5 â€” POW Governance Risk Register

| Risk | Severity |
|---|---|
| Missing `remarks` blocks audit justification | MEDIUM |
| Missing `date_of_entry` prevents chronological record-keeping | MEDIUM |
| Orphaned DB columns create schema noise | LOW |
| No toggle-column mechanism in UI | LOW |
| No pagination on large POW tables | LOW (current volume) |
| No computed total surface | LOW |

---

### 2.141-B â€” Financial Tab: Domain Purpose and Display Failure Analysis

#### B.1 â€” Domain Purpose Validation

The Financial tab serves infrastructure project financial monitoring aligned with Philippine COA reporting standards:

| Component | Standard Meaning |
|---|---|
| Appropriation | Budget allocated per GAA / project appropriation |
| Obligation | Amount committed through contracts/purchase orders |
| Disbursement | Actual cash payments released |
| Utilization Rate | Obligation Ã· Appropriation (computed server-side) |
| Disbursement Rate | Disbursement Ã· Obligation (computed server-side) |

**Verdict:** The per-fiscal-year appropriation/obligation/disbursement model is correct for government infrastructure monitoring (matches BAR No. 2 pattern already used in University Operations module). No domain-level redesign required.

**Missing operational fields for full traceability:**

| Missing Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `activity_title` | Links financial record to a specific work activity or contract item |
| `transaction_category` | Classification (Civil Works, Materials, Labor, Equipment, etc.) |
| `remarks` | Justification or audit annotation |
| `payment_reference` | Billing/JEV/transaction reference number |
| `status` | Workflow state (ALLOCATED / OBLIGATED / DISBURSED / LIQUIDATED) |

These represent Phase 2 enhancements. Current simple model is sufficient for initial deployment.

#### B.2 â€” Display Failure Root Cause (Confirmed)

`findFinancials()` service method returns `ConstructionProjectFinancial[]` (plain array). Controller passes this directly â€” no `{ data: [...] }` wrapper.

Frontend `fetchFinancials()` in `edit-[id].vue`:
```
const res = await api.get<{ data: any[] }>('.../financials')
existingFinancials.value = (res.data || []).map(...)
```

When `res` is a plain array, `res.data` is `undefined`. Result: financial list always renders empty on the Edit page regardless of saved records.

**Correct pattern (as in CiPowItemTable.vue):**
```
const list = Array.isArray(res) ? res : (res?.data || [])
```

**Scope of same bug pattern:** Any frontend component that fetches from a list endpoint returning a plain array and accesses `.data` will silently show empty. Milestone fetch (`fetchMilestones`) in `edit-[id].vue` uses the same `res.data` pattern â€” probable same failure.

#### B.3 â€” Schema/Display Inconsistency Map

| Layer | State |
|---|---|
| DB table `construction_project_financials` | Correct â€” stores fiscal_year, appropriation, obligation, disbursement, metadata |
| Entity `ConstructionProjectFinancial` | Correct â€” maps all columns |
| Service `findFinancials()` | Returns plain array (no wrapper) |
| Controller `GET :id/financials` | Returns plain array (no data wrapper) |
| Frontend `fetchFinancials()` | Reads `res.data` â€” always undefined â†’ display failure |
| Detail page financials | Populated from `findOne` via `cp.*` sub-query â€” renders correctly |

#### B.4 â€” Government Infrastructure Financial Context

Standard requirements not yet captured (Phase 2+):
- Per-activity billing tracking tied to STE/SWA document submissions
- Contractor payment schedules linked to milestone completion
- Retention money tracking (standard Philippine contract retention)
- Variation order financial impact recording
- Cash flow projection (planned vs actual disbursement per period)

---

### 2.141-C â€” Document Governance System: CPES/Infrastructure Compliance Analysis

#### C.1 â€” Infrastructure/CPES Document Inventory

**Group 1: Contract Foundation**
1. Contract of Agreement and Bill of Quantities (BOQ)
2. Notice to Proceed (NTP)
3. Approved Building Plans
4. Program of Works (POW)
5. CPM Work Schedule / Bar Chart / Manpower and Equipment Schedule
6. Organizational Chart and Financial Chart
7. General Layout of Project Facilities

**Group 2: Contractor Qualifications**
8. Contractor's Valid License and JV Agreement
9. Materials Engineers Accreditation
10. List of Subcontractors or Suppliers
11. Original and Revised Quality Control Program (QCP)

**Group 3: Accomplishment Reports**
12. Approved Statement of Time Elapsed (STE)
13. Approved Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA)
14. Materials Test Reports/Results

**Group 4: Change Orders and Time Management**
15. Approved Variation Orders
16. Approved Time Extensions
17. Approved Time Suspension/Resumption Orders and Monthly Suspension Reports

**Group 5: Completion**
18. Final Completion Inspection Report
19. Certificate of Completion
20. Straight Line Diagram
21. As-Built Plan

**Group 6: Extended/Custom**
22. Project Profile / Feasibility Studies
23. Procurement Documents
24. Safety Compliance Documents
25. COA/Government Agency Requirements
26. Progress Reports / Billing Documents
27. User-defined (expandable)

#### C.2 â€” Checklist Governance Model

Current system has no checklist capability. Existing `documents` table is a flat upload store. Required checklist model fields:

| Field | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `document_category_group` | Group 1â€“6 classification |
| `document_type_code` | Unique identifier per document type |
| `document_type_label` | Human-readable name |
| `is_required` | Mandatory vs optional per project type |
| `submission_status` | NOT_SUBMITTED / SUBMITTED / UNDER_REVIEW / APPROVED / REJECTED |
| `submitted_by` | FK to users |
| `submitted_at` | Timestamp |
| `reviewed_by` | FK to users |
| `reviewed_at` | Timestamp |
| `review_notes` | Rejection/approval notes |
| `current_version` | Latest version number |
| `expiry_date` | For time-limited documents (licenses, accreditation) |
| `linked_document_id` | FK to `documents.id` (the actual uploaded file) |

**Architecture:** A seeded `construction_document_types` reference table + per-project `construction_document_checklist_items` instance table with submission/approval state. Decoupled from the existing flat `documents` table (which remains for raw uploads).

#### C.3 â€” Document Lifecycle Analysis

| Stage | Description | Current Support |
|---|---|---|
| Required | Document category must be submitted | No checklist |
| Submitted | File uploaded, awaiting review | No review queue |
| Under Review | Reviewer assigned and reviewing | No reviewer assignment |
| Approved | Formally accepted by reviewer | No approval action |
| Rejected | Returned for revision with notes | No rejection workflow |
| Superseded | Replaced by newer version | No version chain |
| Expired | Time-limited document past validity | No expiry tracking |

`lifecycle_status` (ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/DRAFT) from JW-D models storage state, not compliance state â€” insufficient for CPES checklist requirements.

#### C.4 â€” Flexible Document Architecture Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Hardcoding 27 document types in frontend enums | HIGH | Use seeded DB reference table with admin-extensible categories |
| Flat upload model unable to represent compliance tracking | HIGH | Separate checklist entity from upload entity |
| No versioning â€” replacing a file loses history | MEDIUM | Version chain via `linked_document_id` on checklist items |
| No expiry tracking for licenses/accreditations | MEDIUM | `expiry_date` column on checklist items |
| `documentType` free-text too coarse for 27 categories | HIGH | Category group + type code hierarchy |

#### C.5 â€” Multi-View UI Strategy

| View | Suitability | Use Case |
|---|---|---|
| Checklist view (tabular with status badges) | Best for compliance monitoring | "Is this document submitted and approved?" |
| Accordion / grouped sections | Good for browsing large document sets | Groups 1â€“6 organized sections |
| Timeline / history view | Good for audit trail | Submission/review history per document |
| Table/grid view (current) | File library only | Not compliance-aware |

**Recommendation:** Checklist view as primary compliance surface. Current file library table retained as secondary upload management. Accordion for group navigation.

---

### 2.141-D â€” Authorization and Activity Logging Governance

#### D.1 â€” Role-Permission Matrix (Current vs Required)

| Action | SuperAdmin | Admin | Staff (assigned) | Auditor | Client (future) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Create project | âœ… | âœ… | âœ… | âŒ | âŒ |
| Edit all project fields | âœ… | âœ… | owner/assigned only | âŒ | âŒ |
| Edit POW items | âœ… | âœ… | module canEdit check | **missing** | âŒ |
| Edit financials | âœ… | âœ… | partial | **missing** | âŒ |
| Upload documents | âœ… | âœ… | assigned only | âŒ | âŒ |
| Approve documents | âœ… | âœ… | âŒ | âŒ | âŒ |
| View all projects | âœ… | âœ… | own/assigned | **needs** | published only |
| View activity logs | âœ… | âœ… | âŒ | **needs** | âŒ |
| Delete records | âœ… | Admin only | âŒ | âŒ | âŒ |

**Confirmed gaps:**
- `Auditor` role seeded in DB (migration 045) but referenced in zero `@Roles()` decorators â€” completely inactive
- No tab-level authorization â€” any authorized Staff can reach all Edit tabs
- No restricted-editor role (Staff limited to specific tabs)
- Client read access: no public endpoint (R13 from Section 2.136-G still open)

#### D.2 â€” Activity Logging Architecture Gaps

**Current ActivityLog entity captures:**
- `action` (enum: CREATE/UPDATE/DELETE/SUBMIT/PUBLISH/REJECT/WITHDRAW/UPLOAD/REMOVE_ATTACHMENT)
- `entityType`, `entityId`, `userEmail`, `userName`, `metadata` (JSON), `createdAt`

**Missing for full audit-trail compliance:**

| Missing Field | Impact on Auditability |
|---|---|
| `changedFields` â€” list of field names modified | Cannot answer "what field was changed?" |
| `previousValues` â€” JSON snapshot of before-state | Cannot reconstruct prior state |
| `newValues` â€” JSON snapshot of after-state | Cannot verify what was set |
| `ipAddress` | COA cybersecurity traceability requirement |
| `tabContext` â€” which UI tab triggered the action | Debugging and audit context |

`metadata` JSON is available and could carry these fields, but no convention is enforced today across service calls â€” each fireLog() call passes arbitrary ad-hoc data.

#### D.3 â€” Audit-Trail Requirements

1. Every CUD on a project record: user, timestamp, entity, affected fields with before/after values
2. Financial records: who created/modified each fiscal-year row
3. Document submissions: submitter, reviewer, timestamps, disposition
4. POW item changes: progress old/new values explicitly captured
5. Approval/rejection: reviewer identity and justification text required in log

---

### 2.141-E â€” Codebase Structure Governance

#### E.1 â€” Current Structure Assessment

**Backend:** Single monolithic `construction-projects.service.ts` (~1,200+ lines) handles all sub-modules: CRUD, milestones, POW, timeline entries, financials, gallery, documents, assignments, publication workflow. Single controller. 12 entities in one module.

**Frontend:** State management entirely inline in 4 page files. `components/coi/` has only `CiPowItemTable.vue`. No extracted composables for COI data fetching. Duplication of fetch patterns across pages.

#### E.2 â€” Modular Boundary Analysis

| Sub-module | Approx. Lines | Extraction Candidate |
|---|---|---|
| Milestones | ~75 | `milestones.service.ts` |
| POW Items | ~120 | `pow-items.service.ts` |
| Timeline Entries | ~80 | `timeline-entries.service.ts` |
| Financials | ~40 | `financials.service.ts` |
| Gallery | ~60 | `gallery.service.ts` |
| Documents | ~80 | `documents.service.ts` |
| Assignments | ~50 | `assignments.service.ts` |
| Publication Workflow | ~100 | `publication.service.ts` |
| Core CRUD + search | ~400 | `construction-projects.service.ts` (retained core) |

#### E.3 â€” Admin vs Client Separation Requirements

| Concern | Current | Required |
|---|---|---|
| Admin pages | `pages/coi/` | Keep â€” admin path |
| Client/public endpoint | Not yet built | `src/construction-projects/public-construction.controller.ts` exists but has limited scope |
| Shared components | `components/coi/Ci*.vue` | Correct namespace â€” continue |
| Data-fetching composables | Inline in each page | Extract to `composables/useCOI*.ts` |
| Auth boundary | Per-page middleware | Per-tab guards for complex authorization |

#### E.4 â€” Scalability Risks

| Risk | Severity |
|---|---|
| 1,200-line monolithic service â€” merge conflicts, hard to test | HIGH |
| Data-fetching logic duplicated across 4 pages | MEDIUM |
| No public endpoint for client integration (R13) | HIGH |
| No API abstraction layer â€” URL strings scattered | MEDIUM |
| components/coi/ has 1 component â€” most logic still inline | MEDIUM |

---

### 2.141-F â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

#### F.1 â€” Confirmed Inconsistencies

| Flow | Issue | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| Financial display (edit page) | `fetchFinancials()` reads `res.data` on plain-array response â€” always empty | BLOCKER |
| Milestone display (edit page) | Same `res.data` pattern on `findMilestones` plain array â€” probable failure | PROBABLE BLOCKER |
| Tab routing | TAB_MAP: `team â†’ personnel` corrects mismatch | RESOLVED |
| POW schema drift | KA migration closes remaining NOT NULL orphans | PENDING KA |
| Document dual-status columns | `lifecycle_status` vs pipeline `status` â€” frontend must choose correctly | MEDIUM |
| CiPowItemTable import | JX-A resolved auto-import | RESOLVED |
| Orphaned window-items | JX-C removed location/contractor blocks | RESOLVED |

#### F.2 â€” Create â†’ Edit â†’ Detail Synchronization Map

| Data Area | Create | Edit | Detail | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Narrative fields (summary/scope/facilities) | âœ… sends | âœ… pre-populates | âœ… renders | None |
| Beneficiaries (numeric) | âœ… sends | âœ… pre-populates | âœ… renders | None |
| Assignment metadata (role/dept/phone) | âœ… via assignments[] | âœ… pre-populates | âœ… renders | None |
| Financial records | N/A | Saves but renders empty (display bug) | Renders via findOne | Edit-page display |
| Milestones | N/A | Saves OK; list may be empty (probable bug) | Renders via findOne sub-query | Edit-page display |
| POW items | N/A | âœ… via CiPowItemTable | âœ… via CiPowItemTable | None |
| Gallery uploads | N/A | âœ… immediate upload | âœ… renders | None |
| Document uploads | âœ… lifecycle_status | âœ… lifecycle_status saved | No filter by status | Status filter |

| Priority | Area | Justification |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Financial + Milestone display fix (F/B) | P0 runtime blocker â€” users cannot verify saves on edit page |
| 2 | POW `remarks` + `date_of_entry` fields (A) | Governance gap â€” needed for audit compliance |
| 3 | Auditor role activation (D) | Compliance risk â€” role seeded but completely inactive |
| 4 | Activity log field-delta convention (D) | Audit-trail hardening â€” enforce `changedFields`/`previousValues`/`newValues` in metadata |
| 5 | Document governance system (C) | Largest new feature â€” CPES checklist requires new tables, service, and UI |
| 6 | Financial traceability fields (B) | Enhancement â€” `activity_title`, `remarks`, `status` workflow |
| 7 | Codebase structure refactor (E) | Tech debt â€” no functional impact; improves maintainability |

*Section 2.141 â€” Phase 1 complete. Phase KB plan follows in plan.md.*
