## Section 2.142 â€” COI Phase KC: Overview Tab Project Profile Integration + POW Toggle Column System + Authorization Governance

> **Audit Date:** 2026-05-07
> **Governance Source:** `docs/references/coi_overview_pow_governance_prompt.md`
> **Continuity:** Extends Sections 2.136â€“2.141. Does not duplicate findings already documented in 2.141. Focuses exclusively on new requirements from the governance prompt not covered by Phase KB.
> **Scope:** Aâ€“G: POW toggle columns, Overview Project Profile gap, document preview, authorization tab-level restriction, pay item number design, risk register, priority decision.

---

### 2.142-A â€” POW Tab: Current State Verification + Toggle Column Gap

#### A.1 â€” KB-B Implementation Status (Verified)

`remarks` and `dateOfEntry` are **confirmed present** in entity and DTO as of current state:

| KB-B Step | Status |
|---|---|
| KB-B-2: Entity fields (`remarks`, `dateOfEntry`) | âœ… Present â€” verified by grep |
| KB-B-3: DTO fields (`remarks`, `date_of_entry`) | âœ… Present â€” verified by grep |
| KB-B-5: CiPowItemTable.vue form fields | âœ… Present â€” form has both fields |
| KB-B-1: Migration `AddPowRemarksDateEntry` | âš ï¸ Status uncertain â€” migration file may exist; operator must verify `npm run migration:up` was run |
| KB-B-4: Service wiring (`createPowItem`/`updatePowItem`) | âš ï¸ Must verify both methods map `remarks` and `dateOfEntry` |

#### A.2 â€” KB-A Implementation Status (Verified)

`fetchFinancials()` in `edit-[id].vue` already uses `api.get<any>` with the defensive array pattern â€” KB-A-1 is **implemented**.
`fetchMilestones()` in `edit-[id].vue` uses the JZ-A union type fix â€” KB-A-2 is **implemented**.

#### A.3 â€” Current Table Column Coverage vs. Governance Requirement

The POW table (`CiPowItemTable.vue`) currently displays **5 columns only**:

| Visible | Column | Entity Field |
|---|---|---|
| âœ… | Description | `description` |
| âœ… | Category | `category` |
| âœ… | Progress (bar) | `progress` |
| âœ… | Est. Cost | `estimatedProjectCost` |
| âœ… | Status | `status` |
| âŒ | Quantity | `quantity` |
| âŒ | Unit | `unit` |
| âŒ | Unit Cost | `unitCost` |
| âŒ | Material Cost | `estimatedMaterialCost` |
| âŒ | Labor Cost | `estimatedLaborCost` |
| âŒ | Variance | `variance` |
| âŒ | Start Date | `startDate` |
| âŒ | End Date | `endDate` |
| âŒ | Project Duration | `projectDuration` |
| âŒ | Date of Entry | `dateOfEntry` |
| âŒ | Remarks | `remarks` |
| âŒ | Pay Item No | computed from `sortOrder + 1` |

**11 data columns exist in the entity/DTO but are not shown in the table.** The governance prompt mandates 12 default-visible columns including Pay Item No.

#### A.4 â€” Toggle Column System Gap

**Current state:** No toggle column mechanism exists in `CiPowItemTable.vue`.
All columns are either permanently visible or permanently hidden.

**Required:** User-controlled column visibility with:
- configurable default set
- grouped column categories
- persistent localStorage preference
- responsive behavior

**Vuetify implementation path:** `v-data-table` accepts a `:headers` array. Toggling columns is achieved by filtering this array through a `visibleColumns` ref. A "Toggle Columns" button opens a `v-menu` containing grouped checkboxes per category.

| Column Group | Columns |
|---|---|
| Identity | Pay Item No, Description, Category |
| Progress | Accomplishment % (progress), Status |
| Financial | Quantity, Unit, Unit Cost, Material Cost, Labor Cost, Project Cost, Variance |
| Schedule | Start Date, End Date, Project Duration, Date of Entry |
| Governance | Remarks |

**Default visible (per governance prompt):** Pay Item No, Description, Quantity, Unit, Unit Cost, Material Cost, Labor Cost, Project Cost, Accomplishment %, Status, Date of Entry, Remarks (12 columns).

---

### 2.142-B â€” Overview Tab: Project Profile Gap Analysis

#### B.1 â€” Entity Fields Available for Project Profile Display

Fields **present** in `ConstructionProject` entity that map to Project Profile categories:

| Project Profile Category | Entity Field | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Core Project Information | `title` (from `projects`), `summary`, `status`, `projectType` | âœ… |
| Project Description | `description`, `scope`, `objectives` (JSONB) | âœ… |
| Objectives | `objectives` (JSONB), `keyFeatures` | âœ… |
| Spatial / Location | `campusLocation`, `buildingType`, `floorArea`, `numberOfFloors`, `coordinates` | âœ… |
| Funding Information | `fundingSource`, `contractAmount`, `financialProgress` | âœ… |
| Implementation Period | `startDate`, `targetCompletionDate`, `extensionDate` | âœ… |
| Contract Details | `contractNumber`, `contractor`, `fundingSource` | âœ… |
| Beneficiaries | `beneficiaries`, `facilities` | âœ… |
| Physical Progress | `physicalProgress`, `financialProgress` | âœ… |

#### B.2 â€” Entity Fields MISSING for Full Project Profile

Fields **absent** from entity and DB, required by governance prompt:

| Missing Field | Project Profile Category | DB Column Needed |
|---|---|---|
| `strategic_alignment` | Strategic Alignment | `strategic_alignment TEXT` |
| `output_indicators` | Output Indicators | `output_indicators JSONB` |
| `outcome_indicators` | Outcome Indicators | `outcome_indicators JSONB` |
| `implementing_agency` | Implementing/Responsible Agencies | `implementing_agency VARCHAR(255)` |
| `status_category` | Status Category & Project Readiness | `status_category VARCHAR(50)` |
| `status_updates` | Status Updates & Remarks | `status_updates JSONB` |
| `readiness_documents` | Implementation Readiness Documents | `readiness_documents JSONB` |
| `signatories` | Signatories & Approvals | `signatories JSONB` |

**8 missing fields.** All are nullable additions â€” no breaking changes to existing rows.

**Implementation scope:** These fields require a migration, entity update, DTO update, service update, and frontend display in Overview + Edit form. This is a MEDIUM-complexity addition.

#### B.3 â€” Current Overview Tab Layout vs. Executive Layout Required

| Section | Current Overview | Required Executive Layout |
|---|---|---|
| Status/Progress Header | âœ… Status chip, progress bars, contract amount | âœ… Retain + enhance |
| Project Information | Monolithic card with all text fields | âŒ Must be split into expandable sections |
| Strategic Alignment | âŒ Absent | âŒ Need new entity fields (B.2) |
| Financial Summary | Per-year table with appropriation/obligation/disbursement | âœ… Retain + make expandable |
| Approval History | Timeline | âœ… Retain |
| Project Details | Single card (campus, building type, etc.) | âŒ Must be expandable section |
| Contract Information | Single card | âŒ Must be expandable section |
| Document Preview | âŒ Absent | âŒ Needed (Section C below) |
| Implementation Timeline | âŒ Absent as visual component | âŒ Need visual timeline |

**Key change pattern:** Replace static info cards with `v-expansion-panels` for dense information. Add Document Preview section. Add Strategic Alignment/Indicators section (requires KC-C fields).

**MVP split:** The executive restructure of existing fields (B.1) can be done frontend-only as KC-B. The new fields (B.2) require KC-C (migration).

---

### 2.142-C â€” Document Preview Section Gap

#### C.1 â€” Current Documents Architecture

- Documents tab in `detail-[id].vue`: flat upload list via `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/documents`
- Each document has: `id`, `file_name`, `file_url`, `file_size`, `document_type` (free-text), `lifecycle_status` (ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/DRAFT), `uploaded_by`, `created_at`
- **No document_type_code categorization** â€” `document_type` is a free-text string set by uploader

#### C.2 â€” Required Document Preview in Overview

Governance prompt requires a "Key Documents" section in Overview showing preview cards for:
- Project Profile
- Feasibility Study
- Program of Works (POW)
- Certificate of Completion (and other key types)

Each card: title, latest version indicator, upload status, approval status, "View" action.

#### C.3 â€” Feasibility Assessment

**Option A (frontend-only filter):** Filter existing documents by `document_type` text-match (e.g., documents where `document_type.toLowerCase().includes('profile')` or `includes('pow')`). Display preview card or "Not uploaded" placeholder.

- Risk: relies on uploader using consistent document_type text â€” fragile
- No new API or migration needed

**Option B (DB document_category_code):** Add `document_category_code` column to `documents` table (or use KB-E checklist system if implemented). Filter by code.

- More robust but requires KB-E checklist completion first

**DECISION (KISS):** Phase KC-D uses Option A with a well-defined `DOCUMENT_PREVIEW_TYPES` constant mapping display labels to `document_type` text-match patterns. When KB-E is implemented, the same section will be wired to the checklist endpoint. The frontend card component is designed to accept both data sources.

---

### 2.142-D â€” Authorization Tab-Level Restriction Gap

#### D.1 â€” Current Authorization State

KB-C covers adding `Auditor` to `@Roles()` on read endpoints (backend concern). This section covers the **frontend tab-level Restricted Editor** pattern from the governance prompt.

| Role | Create | Edit (all tabs) | Edit (specific tab) | View | Audit Logs |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SuperAdmin / Admin | âœ… | âœ… | âœ… | âœ… | âœ… |
| Staff (assigned) | âœ… | âœ… (current) | Target: restricted per tab | âœ… | âŒ |
| Viewer | âŒ | âŒ | âŒ | âœ… | âŒ |
| Auditor | âŒ | âŒ | âŒ | âœ… | âœ… |
| Restricted Editor | âŒ | âŒ | âœ… (tab-specific) | âœ… | âŒ |

**Gap:** No `Restricted Editor` role exists in DB or backend. No tab-level restriction exists in frontend.

#### D.2 â€” Recommended Implementation (KISS)

**Phase KC-E scope:** Frontend-only composable `useCoiAccess()` that returns `canEditTab(tabName)` based on the user's role.

Roles map to tab edit rights:
- `Admin`: edit all tabs
- `Staff`: edit all tabs (existing behavior â€” no regression)
- `Viewer`: read-only all tabs
- `Auditor`: read-only all tabs

The `Restricted Editor` role itself (DB-seeded role with per-tab assignment) is **deferred to a future phase** â€” it requires new backend role seeding, a tab-restriction assignment model, and frontend enforcement. Too large for KC.

**KC-E scope is therefore:** add `useCoiAccess()` composable that encapsulates existing `canEdit` logic, providing `canEditTab()` abstraction ready for future restricted-editor extension.

---

### 2.142-E â€” POW Pay Item No: Field Design Decision

**Governance prompt:** "Pay Item No" as default visible column.
**Current entity:** `sortOrder: number` (INT, ordering field); `item_number` (orphaned DB column, NULL for all rows, no entity mapping).

**Options:**

| Option | Description | Risk |
|---|---|---|
| A | Display `sortOrder + 1` as "Pay Item No" (computed, display-only) | ZERO â€” no schema change |
| B | Expose `item_number` DB column via entity + DTO, populate on create | LOW â€” additive; `item_number` already exists in DB |
| C | Add new `pay_item_no VARCHAR` field with free-text entry | MEDIUM â€” new migration + DTO + UI |

**DECISION (KISS):** Option A â€” render `(index + 1)` as "Item No" column in the toggle column table. This is display-only, requires zero schema changes, and visually satisfies the governance prompt's intent. The column header reads "Item No" and shows the row's sequential number. If the DB `item_number` field has semantic meaning in future (DPWH-specific item codes), it can be wired in KC-C or a subsequent phase.

---

### 2.142-F â€” Risk Register

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Toggle Column localStorage key collisions | LOW | Namespace key: `coi.pow.visibleColumns` |
| Overview tab restructure breaking existing data bindings | LOW | Restructure layout only; no data bindings changed |
| Project Profile migration fails on columns already existing | LOW | All migration statements use `ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` |
| Document preview showing wrong docs via text-match heuristic | MEDIUM | Well-defined constant map + fallback "Not uploaded" state |
| KC-B restructure breaking print/export behavior (if any exists) | LOW | No print/export currently implemented |
| `useCoiAccess()` composable not used consistently across all edit triggers | MEDIUM | Centralize all edit-action checks through composable; audit all `v-if canEdit` usages |

---

### 2.142-G â€” Priority Decision

| Priority | Sub-Phase | Rationale | Migration? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | KC-A: POW Toggle Column | High-value UX improvement; frontend-only; zero risk | âŒ |
| 2 | KC-B: Overview Tab executive restructure (existing fields) | Stakeholder-facing; frontend-only; uses already-present data | âŒ |
| 3 | KC-D: Document Preview section in Overview | Frontend-only; leverages existing documents API | âŒ |
| 4 | KC-C: Project Profile entity extensions (8 new fields) | Schema change â€” requires migration; medium risk; deferred until KC-A/B are stable | âœ… |
| 5 | KC-E: `useCoiAccess()` composable | Foundational abstraction; enables future Restricted Editor role | âŒ |

---

### 2.142-H â€” Edit Form Data Entry Gap Analysis

#### H.1 â€” Current Tab Structure and Field Distribution

Confirmed from `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (lines 110, 1155â€“1199):

**`tabOrder`**: `['basic', 'contract', 'schedule', 'timeline', 'pow', 'progress', 'financial', 'personnel', 'documents']` â€” **9 tabs**

| Tab | Current Fields | Count | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `basic` | project_code, campus, title, description, ideal_infrastructure_image, objectives, key_features, summary, scope, facilities, status | 11 | **Already dense** |
| `contract` | funding_source_id, contractor_id, contract_amount, contract_number | 4 | Lean |
| `schedule` | start_date, target_completion_date, actual_completion_date, project_duration, original_contract_duration | 5 | |
| `timeline` | Milestone CRUD (sub-records via inline dialog) | â€” | |
| `pow` | `CiPowItemTable` component | â€” | Component-managed |
| `progress` | physical_progress, financial_progress, target_*, building_type, floor_area, floors, lat/lng, beneficiaries | 10 | |
| `financial` | Financial CRUD (sub-records via inline dialog) | â€” | |
| `personnel` | project_engineer, project_manager, assigned_user_ids + metadata | 3+ | |
| `documents` | File upload + external link | â€” | |

The form has a **step progress indicator** (completion percentage bar) and **tab completion checkmarks** for all current tabs. Both are tracked via `tabCompletion` and `tabRequired` computed refs.

#### H.2 â€” JSONB Array Handling Pattern (Already Established)

Confirmed existing pattern in `handleSubmit` (lines 310â€“315) and `fetchData` (lines 143â€“144):

- **Load**: `Array.isArray(p.objectives) ? p.objectives.join('\n') : ''`
- **Submit**: `form.value.objectives.split('\n').map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)`

`key_features` follows the same split/join pattern. `facilities` is stored as plain text string (inconsistency exists â€” not KC scope).

New JSONB fields (`output_indicators`, `outcome_indicators`, `status_updates`, `readiness_documents`, `signatories`) are **arrays or object arrays** that cannot use the simple `join('\n')` pattern for object types. A **dynamic add/remove row UI** is required.

#### H.3 â€” New "Profile" Tab Required

Adding the 8 new Project Profile fields to `basic` is not viable:
- `basic` already has 11 fields â€” one of the longest tabs
- JSONB array fields (`output_indicators`, `outcome_indicators`, `status_updates`, `signatories`, `readiness_documents`) require expandable dynamic rows â€” would make `basic` unworkably long
- `strategic_alignment`, `implementing_agency`, `project_status_category` belong semantically to a "Project Profile" context, not generic "Basic Info"

**Decision:** Insert a new **`profile`** tab immediately after `basic`.

**New `tabOrder`**: `['basic', 'profile', 'contract', 'schedule', 'timeline', 'pow', 'progress', 'financial', 'personnel', 'documents']` â€” **10 tabs**

Profile tab field inventory:

| Field | Entity Type | Input Widget | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `strategic_alignment` | TEXT | `v-textarea` (3 rows) | Strategic alignment statement |
| `implementing_agency` | VARCHAR(255) | `v-text-field` | Implementing unit/agency name |
| `project_status_category` | VARCHAR(50) | `v-select` | Enum: NEW, ONGOING, COMPLETED, SUSPENDED, CANCELLED |
| `output_indicators` | JSONB `string[]` | Dynamic add/remove text rows | One output indicator per row |
| `outcome_indicators` | JSONB `string[]` | Dynamic add/remove text rows | One outcome indicator per row |
| `status_updates` | JSONB `{date,text}[]` | Structured dynamic rows | Date + note per update entry |
| `readiness_documents` | JSONB `{type,status,remarks}[]` | Structured dynamic rows | Document readiness checklist |
| `signatories` | JSONB `{name,position,date}[]` | Structured dynamic rows | Signatory block entries |

`tabCompletion.profile`: `!!(form.value.strategic_alignment || form.value.implementing_agency || form.value.output_indicators.length > 0)`
`tabRequired.profile`: `true` â€” optional section, never blocks navigation.

#### H.4 â€” `handleSubmit` Payload Extension Required

`handleSubmit` must include these new fields in the PATCH payload:
```ts
strategic_alignment: form.value.strategic_alignment || undefined,
implementing_agency: form.value.implementing_agency || undefined,
project_status_category: form.value.project_status_category || undefined,
output_indicators: form.value.output_indicators.length > 0 ? form.value.output_indicators : undefined,
outcome_indicators: form.value.outcome_indicators.length > 0 ? form.value.outcome_indicators : undefined,
status_updates: form.value.status_updates.length > 0 ? form.value.status_updates : undefined,
readiness_documents: form.value.readiness_documents.length > 0 ? form.value.readiness_documents : undefined,
signatories: form.value.signatories.length > 0 ? form.value.signatories : undefined,
```

#### H.5 â€” `fetchData` Load Extension Required

Profile fields must be hydrated from the project response in `fetchData`:
```ts
strategic_alignment: p.strategic_alignment || '',
implementing_agency: p.implementing_agency || '',
project_status_category: p.project_status_category || '',
output_indicators: Array.isArray(p.output_indicators) ? p.output_indicators : [],
outcome_indicators: Array.isArray(p.outcome_indicators) ? p.outcome_indicators : [],
status_updates: Array.isArray(p.status_updates) ? p.status_updates : [],
readiness_documents: Array.isArray(p.readiness_documents) ? p.readiness_documents : [],
signatories: Array.isArray(p.signatories) ? p.signatories : [],
```

---

### 2.142-I â€” JSONB Array Input Pattern Design

#### I.1 â€” Pattern Requirements

Operator requirement: "intuitively allowing user-friendly data entry in organized and structured manner."

Four JSONB types across five fields:

| Field | Item Shape | Complexity |
|---|---|---|
| `output_indicators` | `string` | Simple |
| `outcome_indicators` | `string` | Simple |
| `status_updates` | `{ date: string; text: string }` | Structured (2 fields) |
| `readiness_documents` | `{ type: string; status: string; remarks: string }` | Structured (3 fields) |
| `signatories` | `{ name: string; position: string; date: string }` | Structured (3 fields) |

#### I.2 â€” Inline Array Input Pattern (KISS)

For KC, implement as **inline reactive sections within the Profile tab window** â€” no separate component file needed. Each section uses `v-for` over a `ref<T[]>` field in the form object:

**String array pattern** (output_indicators, outcome_indicators):
```html
<v-row v-for="(_, idx) in form.output_indicators" :key="idx" dense align="center">
  <v-col>
    <v-text-field v-model="form.output_indicators[idx]"
      :label="`Output Indicator ${idx + 1}`" variant="outlined" density="comfortable" />
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="auto">
    <v-btn icon="mdi-minus-circle" variant="text" color="error" size="small"
      @click="form.output_indicators.splice(idx, 1)" />
  </v-col>
</v-row>
<v-btn prepend-icon="mdi-plus" size="small" variant="tonal"
  @click="form.output_indicators.push('')">Add Output Indicator</v-btn>
```

**Structured object array pattern** (status_updates example):
```html
<v-card v-for="(entry, idx) in form.status_updates" :key="idx" variant="outlined" class="mb-2 pa-2">
  <v-row dense align="center">
    <v-col cols="12" sm="3">
      <v-text-field v-model="entry.date" label="Date" type="date" ... />
    </v-col>
    <v-col>
      <v-text-field v-model="entry.text" label="Update Note" ... />
    </v-col>
    <v-col cols="auto">
      <v-btn icon="mdi-minus-circle" variant="text" color="error" @click="form.status_updates.splice(idx, 1)" />
    </v-col>
  </v-row>
</v-card>
<v-btn @click="form.status_updates.push({ date: '', text: '' })">Add Update</v-btn>
```

Same pattern applies for `readiness_documents` (`{ type, status, remarks }`) and `signatories` (`{ name, position, date }`).

#### I.3 â€” Form State Shape

```ts
const form = ref({
  // ... existing 27 fields (unchanged) ...
  // KC-C-5: New Profile tab fields
  strategic_alignment: '',
  implementing_agency: '',
  project_status_category: '',
  output_indicators: [] as string[],
  outcome_indicators: [] as string[],
  status_updates: [] as { date: string; text: string }[],
  readiness_documents: [] as { type: string; status: string; remarks: string }[],
  signatories: [] as { name: string; position: string; date: string }[],
})
```

`BackendProjectDetail` interface must also be extended with optional nullable typed properties for these fields.

---

### 2.142-J â€” POW Item Dialog Multi-Tab Design Analysis

#### J.1 â€” Current Dialog State

From `CiPowItemTable.vue` (lines 380â€“563): single `v-dialog max-width="720"` with all fields in one flat `v-card-text > v-row dense` block.

**16 fields** in a single-scroll layout across 4 logical categories:
- Identity: description, category, status, sort_order
- Cost accounting: quantity, unit, unit_cost, estimated_material_cost, estimated_labor_cost, estimated_project_cost, variance
- Scheduling: start_date, end_date, project_duration, date_of_entry
- Governance: progress (slider), remarks

#### J.2 â€” Multi-Tab Dialog Design

Per operator directive: "implementing multitab data entry in the edit project details page for the modal or form dialogs."

**Proposed 4-tab structure for the POW item dialog:**

| Tab Value | Label | Icon | Fields |
|---|---|---|---|
| `workitem` | Work Item | mdi-clipboard-text | description, category, status, sort_order |
| `costs` | Cost Breakdown | mdi-currency-php | quantity, unit, unit_cost, estimated_material_cost, estimated_labor_cost, estimated_project_cost, variance |
| `schedule` | Schedule | mdi-calendar-range | start_date, end_date, project_duration, date_of_entry |
| `notes` | Progress & Notes | mdi-chart-line | progress (slider), remarks |

Implementation:
- Add local `dialogTab` ref (resets to `'workitem'` when dialog opens or `resetForm()` is called)
- Replace flat `v-card-text > v-row` with `v-tabs` + `v-window` inside the dialog `v-card`
- No changes to `form` object, `save()`, or any other state â€” pure presentation restructuring

#### J.3 â€” Risk Assessment

| Risk | Level | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| User misses required fields on unvisited tab | LOW | No fields are hard-required (KA migration removed NOT NULL constraints) |
| Dialog height increase | LOW | `scrollable` prop available on v-dialog if needed |
| Confusion about which tab to start on | NONE | Dialog always resets to "Work Item" tab â€” natural entry point |

---

*Section 2.142 â€” Phase 1 complete (including data entry gap analysis). Phase KC plan follows in plan.md.*

---

## Section 2.143 â€” Phase KD: COI UX Optimization, Overview Consolidation, Personnel Governance, and Analytics Architecture

*Prompt id: coi2414 | Scope: 10 areas (Aâ€“J) | Date: 2026-05-07*

---

### A. Multi-Tab UX Discoverability Analysis

#### A.1 â€” Current State Inventory

| Page | Tab Count | Scrollable Tabs | Entry Mode |
|---|---|---|---|
| `new.vue` (Create Project) | 5 | No | Stepped progress bar (Phase JN-B) |
| `edit-[id].vue` (Edit Project) | 10 | Yes (overflow scroll) | Standard `v-tabs` |
| `detail-[id].vue` (Project Details) | 6 | No | Standard `v-tabs` |

**edit-[id].vue 10 tabs:** basic, location, contract, schedule, progress, personnel, pow, timeline, documents, profile

The edit form has 10 tabs â€” the highest risk for discoverability failure. With overflow scrolling, tabs 8â€“10 (timeline, documents, profile) are often hidden from the initial viewport.

#### A.2 â€” Discoverability Risk Analysis

| Risk | Severity | Affected Pages |
|---|---|---|
| User submits without visiting all tabs | HIGH | edit-[id].vue (10 tabs) |
| Hidden overflow tabs not discoverable | HIGH | edit-[id].vue |
| User doesn't know which tabs are required vs optional | MEDIUM | new.vue, edit-[id].vue |
| No visual indicator of tab completion state | MEDIUM | all pages |
| User lost after navigating back from detail page | LOW | detail-[id].vue |

#### A.3 â€” Enterprise UX Patterns Assessment

**Progressive disclosure** strategies used in enterprise forms:

1. **Guided step wizard** â€” enforces linear completion (best for create; too rigid for edit)
2. **Collapsible onboarding guide** â€” contextual info panel collapsed by default; expandable by user; best for experienced users
3. **Tab completion indicators** â€” chips/badges showing filled/unfilled state per tab
4. **Tab group overflow indicators** â€” visual scroll arrows with `n more` badge
5. **Floating "more tabs" indicator** â€” persistent hint when tabs are clipped

**Recommended combination for KD implementation:**

| Mechanism | Create Page | Edit Page | Detail Page |
|---|---|---|---|
| Collapsible user guide card | âœ… | âœ… | âœ… |
| Tab completion chips | OPTIONAL | âœ… PRIORITY | N/A (read-only) |
| Scroll-to-top FAB per tab | N/A | âœ… | âœ… |
| Tab overflow badge indicator | N/A | âœ… | N/A |

#### A.4 â€” Collapsible User Guide Architecture

**Placement:** Above tab strip, below page header. Dismissible per-user via `localStorage` key.

**Structure per page:**

| Page | Guide Content |
|---|---|
| Create Project | Workflow overview; list of 5 tabs; which are required; step 1 = Basic Info |
| Edit Project | Warning: 10 tabs present; reminder to review Profile tab; highlight overflow |
| Project Details | Navigation guide; what each tab shows; how to return to list |

**Component:** `CiProjectGuideCard.vue` â€” renders per-page content prop, stores `localStorage` dismissed state via `coi.guide.dismissed.<page>` key.

**Behavior:**
- Default: visible for new users (no localStorage key)
- Dismiss: click "Got it" â†’ sets localStorage key â†’ card hidden until key cleared
- Per-page isolation: each page has its own dismiss key

#### A.5 â€” Scroll Navigation

**Scroll-to-top FAB:** `v-btn` with `mdi-chevron-up`, fixed position bottom-right, visible when `scrollY > 300`. Implemented in `detail-[id].vue` and `edit-[id].vue`.

**Tab overflow indicator for edit-[id].vue:** The native `v-tabs` overflow arrows are small and often missed. Augment with a persistent `v-chip` showing `+N more` visible in the tab strip header area when tabs are clipped.

---

### B. Overview Tab â€” Consolidated Cover Page Architecture

#### B.1 â€” Current Fragmentation Analysis

| Element | Current Location | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Project title, type, status | Header area | Working (KC-B) |
| Contractor, funding source, contract amount | Panel 1: Project Information (KC-B) | Working |
| Physical/financial progress | Panel 2: Progress Summary (KC-B) | Working |
| Project Profile fields (8) | Panel 3: Strategic Alignment (KC-B/KC-C) | Working |
| Financial Summary | Panel 4 (KC-B) | Working |
| Approval History | Panel 5 (KC-B) | Working |
| Key Documents Preview | Panel 6 (KC-D) | Working |
| Project image | `idealInfrastructureImage` URL field | No display in overview |

**Gap:** No unified executive cover page. The overview is a series of expansion panels with no visual hierarchy. No image rendering. No KPI card header strip.

#### B.2 â€” Unified Overview Architecture

**Target architecture: 3-zone layout**

```
ZONE 1: HERO CARD
  [Building image or placeholder]   [Project Title + Status chip]
                                     [Contractor | Funding Source]
                                     [Contract Amount | Duration]
                                     [Physical Progress bar]
                                     [Financial Utilization bar]

ZONE 2: KPI STRIP (4 cards)
  [Total POW Cost]  [Contract Amount]  [Physical Progress %]  [Financial Util %]

ZONE 3: EXPANSION PANELS (existing KC-B panels, retained)
  - Strategic Alignment
  - Financial Summary
  - Approval History
  - Key Documents Preview
  - Project Information (merged into hero or kept as panel)
```

**Benefits:**
- Executive stakeholder sees all critical data above the fold
- Panels remain for detailed operational data
- No regression â€” panels from KC-B are preserved

#### B.3 â€” Project Image Integration Strategy

**Existing field:** `idealInfrastructureImage` â€” text URL column on `construction_projects`. Stores a URL string (external or uploaded).

**Gap:** No image upload mechanism for project overview. The gallery tab handles MOV images (progress photos), not architectural/building renders.

**Required addition:** New column `building_image_url` on `construction_projects` for the architectural/proposed building image. This is semantically distinct from `idealInfrastructureImage` (which may be a reference external URL) and from gallery images (which are MOV photos).

**OR:** Repurpose `idealInfrastructureImage` as the building image display URL.

**Recommendation:** Use `idealInfrastructureImage` for overview hero image display. Expose this field in the Edit form (Project Profile tab, KC-C). No new migration needed â€” field exists.

**Rendering:** In `detail-[id].vue` overview hero card, render `project.idealInfrastructureImage` as a `v-img` with aspect-ratio `16/9`, fallback placeholder (`mdi-office-building` icon).

#### B.4 â€” Overview Redesign Risks

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Hero zone too large on mobile | MEDIUM | Responsive breakpoints; stack image above text on `xs` |
| `idealInfrastructureImage` field empty for most projects | LOW | Graceful fallback icon placeholder |
| KPI card values depend on POW data (may be empty) | LOW | Show `â€”` for null/zero values |
| Regression of KC-B expansion panels | NONE | Panels are retained unchanged |

---

### C. POW Tab â€” UX and Functionality Stabilization

#### C.1 â€” Status/Check Indicator Analysis

From inspection of `CiPowItemTable.vue`:

- `v-progress-linear` is bound to `item.progress` (numeric 0â€“100) â€” **functionally correct**
- `v-chip` status color uses `getStatusColor(item.status)` â€” **functionally correct** (same helper used in detail timeline)
- The complaint about "indicators not functional" likely refers to the **absence of filter/search** rather than a rendering defect

**Conclusion:** Status indicators ARE rendering correctly. The usability gap is that users cannot filter/search to surface items by status â€” making the status chips feel inert (no filtering effect on click).

#### C.2 â€” Missing Filter & Search Architecture

**Current state:** No `searchQuery`, no `filterStatus`, no `filterCategory` in `CiPowItemTable.vue`. The table renders all items flat.

**Required computed:**
```
filteredItems = computed(() =>
  items.value
    .filter(item => !searchQuery.value || item.description.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.value.toLowerCase()) || item.category?.toLowerCase().includes(searchQuery.value.toLowerCase()))
    .filter(item => !filterStatus.value || item.status === filterStatus.value)
    .filter(item => !filterCategory.value || item.category === filterCategory.value)
    .filter(item => filterProgressMin.value == null || item.progress >= filterProgressMin.value)
)
```

**UI placement:** Filter bar row above the table, below the header buttons row.

**Filter controls:**

| Control | Type | Bound State |
|---|---|---|
| Search (description/category) | `v-text-field` | `searchQuery` |
| Status | `v-select` (multi or single) | `filterStatus` |
| Category | `v-select` | `filterCategory` (populated from unique item categories) |
| Progress min % | `v-slider` or `v-text-field type=number` | `filterProgressMin` |
| Clear All | `v-btn` | resets all filter refs |

**Category `v-select` population:** Computed from `items.value` unique `.category` values (no server round-trip needed).

#### C.3 â€” Click-to-Filter on Status Chips (Bonus UX)

When a user clicks a status chip in the table rows, automatically set `filterStatus.value` to that status. This makes the chip "feel alive" â€” clicking it narrows the view. Implementation: `@click="filterStatus = item.status"` on each chip.

---

### D. Personnel Tab â€” Authorization & Governance Expansion

#### D.1 â€” Current State

| Data | Source | Display |
|---|---|---|
| Assigned users + role/dept/phone | `record_assignments` JOIN `users` | `project.assignedUsers[]` |
| Delegated to name | `construction_projects.delegated_to` | `project.delegatedToName` |
| Project Engineer | `construction_projects.project_engineer` | `project.projectEngineer` (free text) |
| Project Manager | `construction_projects.project_manager` | `project.projectManager` (free text) |

`record_assignments.role` already exists as a free-text column. No structured role categories are enforced.

#### D.2 â€” Required Personnel Role Categories

| Category | Description | Example Roles |
|---|---|---|
| IMPLEMENTING | Contractor / construction personnel | Contractor, Site Engineer, Foreman |
| MONITORING | University MIS/PMO oversight | PMO Officer, MIS Staff |
| UNIVERSITY_OFFICIAL | Signatory / governance officials | VP for Administration, President |
| OVERSIGHT | Audit / external oversight | COA Auditor, CHED Representative |

**Implementation path:** Extend `record_assignments.role` free-text â†’ constrained enum OR add a separate `personnel_category` column to `record_assignments`.

**Recommendation:** Add `personnel_category` column (VARCHAR, nullable) with values `IMPLEMENTING | MONITORING | UNIVERSITY_OFFICIAL | OVERSIGHT`. This avoids breaking existing `role` free-text field while adding structural governance.

#### D.3 â€” Tab-Level Permission Architecture

**Gap:** `useCoiAccess.ts` currently exposes only `canEditCurrentProject` and `isOwnerOrAssigned`. No tab-level restrictions exist.

**Required extension:** `canEditTab(tabName: string): ComputedRef<boolean>`

**Authorization matrix:**

| Role | overview | timeline | pow | gallery | documents | team |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SuperAdmin | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W |
| Admin | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W |
| Staff (assigned) | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W | R/W |
| Staff (monitoring) | R | R | R | R | R | R |
| Viewer | R | R | R | R | R | R |

**Phase KD scope:** The `canEditTab()` method will be a thin wrapper â€” all tabs editable for same roles as `canEditCurrentProject` for now. The column exists for future fine-grained restriction without breaking contracts.

#### D.4 â€” Personnel Panel Restructuring

**Target structure for Team tab:**

| Panel | Contents |
|---|---|
| Implementing Personnel | Contractor, Site Engineers, Foreman (role category: IMPLEMENTING) |
| Monitoring Personnel | PMO, MIS assigned staff (role category: MONITORING) |
| University Officials | Signatories (role category: UNIVERSITY_OFFICIAL) |
| Oversight | COA/CHED (role category: OVERSIGHT) |
| Project Leadership | Project Engineer + Project Manager (text fields, KC-B) |

Users without a `personnel_category` appear in a default "Assigned Personnel" bucket (backward-compatible).

---

### E. Timeline / Progress / Details Deliberation

#### E.1 â€” Current Tab Function Analysis

| Tab | Primary Function | Data Source |
|---|---|---|
| `overview` | Executive summary + progress indicators | All project fields, milestones summary, financials |
| `timeline` | Schedule + milestone list | `project.startDate/targetCompletionDate`, `project.milestones[]` |
| `pow` | Work item breakdown | `construction_pow_items` |
| `gallery` | MOV progress photos | `construction_attachments` (image type) |
| `documents` | Files / links | `construction_attachments` (document type) |
| `team` | Personnel governance | `record_assignments`, project text fields |

No standalone "Progress" tab or "Details" tab currently exists. Progress data is embedded in the overview expansion panel (Panel 2). A `Details` concept is partially covered by the overview expansion panels.

#### E.2 â€” Consolidation Feasibility Analysis

**Option A: Keep all tabs separate** (status quo + Timeline two-column)
- Pros: no regression, clearest tab semantics
- Cons: overview continues to carry too much content

**Option B: Merge Progress + Details into Overview**
- The overview already DOES this via expansion panels (KC-B). Progress is in Panel 2.
- This option is already effectively implemented.

**Option C: Add Analytics tab**
- A new tab `analytics` in `detail-[id].vue` containing KPI cards + ApexCharts
- Does not displace any existing tab
- High stakeholder value for monitoring

**Decision: Option B (already done) + Option C (new analytics tab)**

The tab structure post-KD should be:

```
overview | timeline | pow | analytics | gallery | documents | team
```

The `analytics` tab is new. No other tabs are removed or merged.

#### E.3 â€” Project Diary Deliberation

The operator prompt (Section H) requires Timeline tab to have **two distinct columns**:

1. **Milestones** â€” existing `construction_milestones` (planned, strategic checkpoints)
2. **Project Diary** â€” operational logs, field observations, daily/weekly records

**Current state:** Timeline tab shows only milestones. No diary concept exists.

**Schema option A:** New table `construction_diary_entries` with columns: `id, project_id, entry_date, title, content, author_id, created_at, updated_at`.

**Schema option B:** JSONB array on `construction_projects.diary_entries` â€” simpler, no foreign key, but no pagination/query capability.

**Recommendation:** New table `construction_diary_entries`. Justification:
- Entries will need individual CRUD (add/edit/delete per entry)
- Date-based sorting/filtering will be needed
- Author tracking requires `author_id` FK
- JSONB is not queryable for reporting

**Timeline tab layout post-KD:**

```
[Schedule Card â€” dates/duration]

[Two-column row:]
LEFT: Milestones list (existing)
RIGHT: Project Diary list (new)
       [Add Entry button]
       [Entry: date, author, title, content]
```

On mobile (`xs`): stacked vertically.

---

### F. Overview Tab Redesign â€” Figma Reference Alignment

#### F.1 â€” Figma References

| Reference | Key | Usage |
|---|---|---|
| Admin-side Figma | `rslJrHybEabnxHhW1M3mEo` | Project detail layout, admin data density |
| Client-side Figma | `BLrO1i29yyTrkkg4iyVR5V` | Stakeholder-facing presentation, executive summary |

The admin Figma typically shows data-dense layouts with tables and metrics. The client Figma shows hero-style presentation with images and summary cards. The COI overview tab should **blend both**: hero section for stakeholders, operational panels for administrators.

#### F.2 â€” Overview Hero Section Architecture

**Components:**

1. `v-card` hero section:
   - `v-img` (16:9) left/top â€” `project.idealInfrastructureImage` URL, fallback icon
   - Right side: project title (`text-h5`), status chip, project type chip
   - Contractor name, funding source, contract amount
   - Physical progress bar + % label
   - Financial utilization bar + % label

2. KPI strip (`v-row` with 4 `v-col`s):
   - Total POW cost vs contract amount
   - Physical progress %
   - Financial utilization %
   - Days remaining (computed from `targetCompletionDate`)

3. Existing KC-B expansion panels (retained, reordered):
   - Strategic Alignment (KC-C fields)
   - Financial Summary
   - Approval History
   - Key Documents Preview
   - (Project Information panel MERGED into hero â€” no longer a separate panel)

---

### G. Data Analytics Architecture

#### G.1 â€” Analytics Inventory

| Domain | Available Data | Visualization Type |
|---|---|---|
| Physical Progress | `construction_pow_items.progress` (per item) | Bar chart (by category), donut (by status) |
| Financial | `construction_project_financials` â€” appropriation, obligations, disbursement | Stacked bar or grouped bar per FY |
| Timeline / Milestones | `construction_milestones` â€” progress, target vs actual dates | Gantt-style horizontal bars or grouped bar |
| Document Completeness | `construction_attachments` â€” count by document_type | Donut chart or heatmap |
| POW Cost | `construction_pow_items` â€” unit_cost, estimated costs, total_cost | Stacked cost bar per category |

#### G.2 â€” KPI Cards

| KPI | Computation |
|---|---|
| Physical Progress % | Avg `progress` across all POW items (or weighted by cost) |
| Financial Utilization % | Total disbursement / Total appropriation Ã— 100 |
| Total POW Cost | Sum of `construction_pow_items.total_cost` |
| Days Remaining | `targetCompletionDate` - today |
| Milestone Completion Rate | Completed milestones / Total milestones Ã— 100 |
| Document Coverage | Documents with a `document_type` / Total documents |

#### G.3 â€” Chart Architecture

**Chart library:** ApexCharts (already used in UO module â€” `vue3-apexcharts` package installed).

| Chart | Component | Data Source |
|---|---|---|
| POW Status Donut | `CiPowStatusChart.vue` | POW items grouped by status |
| POW Category Progress Bar | `CiPowCategoryChart.vue` | POW items grouped by category, avg progress |
| Financial Grouped Bar | `CiFinancialChart.vue` | `construction_project_financials` per FY |
| Milestone Gantt/Bar | `CiMilestoneChart.vue` | `construction_milestones` target vs actual |
| Cost Breakdown Stacked Bar | `CiCostBreakdownChart.vue` | POW items â€” material/labor/project cost by category |

All chart data is computed **from already-loaded data** in the detail page store/composable â€” no new API endpoints required for Phase KD analytics.

**Exception:** POW items are loaded lazily in `CiPowItemTable.vue`. The analytics tab must either (a) share the same POW data store or (b) independently fetch via the existing `/api/construction-projects/:id/pow-items` endpoint.

**Recommended approach:** Expose POW items via a shared composable `useCoiPowItems(projectId)` that caches results, used by both `CiPowItemTable.vue` and the analytics tab.

#### G.4 â€” Analytics Tab Placement

New tab `analytics` inserted between `pow` and `gallery` in `detail-[id].vue`. Component: `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`.

---

### H. Timeline Tab â€” Two-Column Structure

#### H.1 â€” Milestone vs Project Diary Governance

| Dimension | Milestones | Project Diary |
|---|---|---|
| Source entity | `construction_milestones` | `construction_diary_entries` (new) |
| Purpose | Planned checkpoints, deadline tracking | Operational logs, field observations |
| Granularity | Project-level strategic | Daily/weekly operational |
| Authors | PM/Admin | Any assigned personnel |
| Edit governance | canEditCurrentProject | canEditCurrentProject OR IMPLEMENTING role |
| Reporting use | Progress reports, BAR | Field records, audit trail |

#### H.2 â€” Schema for Project Diary

New migration: `Migration20260507XXXXXX_AddConstructionDiaryEntries`

```sql
CREATE TABLE construction_diary_entries (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES construction_projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  entry_date DATE NOT NULL,
  title VARCHAR(255),
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  author_id UUID REFERENCES users(id),
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now()
);
CREATE INDEX idx_diary_entries_project_id ON construction_diary_entries(project_id);
```

#### H.3 â€” Operational Workflow

- **Add entry:** Modal dialog with entry_date, title (optional), content (required)
- **Edit entry:** Same dialog, pre-populated
- **Delete entry:** Confirmation dialog, soft-delete or hard-delete (no `deleted_at` needed for diary â€” hard delete acceptable)
- **Display:** Reverse chronological order in right column of Timeline tab
- **Author display:** Join with `users` â€” show `first_name || last_name`

---

### I. Priority and Execution Order

Based on operational dependency, usability impact, and implementation risk:

| Priority | Phase | Area | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | KD-A | POW search/filter | Frontend-only, no migration, immediate usability win |
| 2 | KD-B | UX guide cards + scroll-to-top FAB | Frontend-only, reduces user confusion |
| 3 | KD-C | Overview hero section + KPI strip | High stakeholder value, frontend-only (uses existing `idealInfrastructureImage`) |
| 4 | KD-D | Personnel role categories | Requires migration (`personnel_category` column), then frontend restructure |
| 5 | KD-E | Timeline two-column + Project Diary | Requires migration (`construction_diary_entries`), backend CRUD, frontend |
| 6 | KD-F | Analytics tab | Depends on POW data sharing (`useCoiPowItems` composable) |
| 7 | KD-G | `canEditTab()` composable extension | Quick extension of KC-E composable |

---

### J. Cross-Cutting Risks and Constraints

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Analytics tab loads POW items twice (table + chart) | MEDIUM | `useCoiPowItems` shared composable with cache |
| Diary entries migration introduces new CRUD surface | MEDIUM | Follow existing NestJS pattern (service + controller + DTO) |
| Personnel category expansion breaks `assignedUsers` display logic | LOW | `personnel_category` is nullable â€” ungrouped users fall into default bucket |
| 7-tab detail page (with analytics) may feel overloaded | LOW | Tab labels are concise; icons aid scannability |
| KC-C migration not yet applied â€” building image may not persist | MEDIUM | Operator must run migrations before testing |
| KC-E (`useCoiAccess`) composable must be extended not replaced | NONE | Additive extension only |

---

*Section 2.143 â€” Phase 1 complete. Phase KD plan follows in plan.md.*



---

## Section 2.144 â€” Phase KE: COI Workflow Consolidation, Project Details Refactor, Personnel Governance Expansion, and Analytics Restoration

> **Phase 1 Research Artifact** | *Prompt: csu-core-coi-consolidation-1* | Branch: `refactor/page-structure-feb9` | Date: 2026-05-11
>
> Phase KE consolidates fragmented Create/Edit/Detail surfaces, separates view-from-edit, expands the role-based access model from 4 to 6 governance roles, and restores analytics visibility under a verified data dependency contract. **No regression** of KC-A through KD-G (all verified or implemented).

---

### 2.144-AA â€” Basic Info + Project Profile Consolidation Analysis

**Audit (file:line evidence):**
- `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue:1229` declares `<v-tab value="basic">` (tab #1) and `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue:2391` declares `<v-tab value="profile">` (tab #10). They are independently scoped tabs in the same v-tabs strip.
- `pages/coi/new.vue:463-488` declares only 6 tabs: `basic / contract / schedule / progress / personnel / documents`. **`profile` is absent entirely.** A project created via Create cannot capture strategic alignment, output indicators, outcome indicators, implementing agency, status category, status updates, readiness documents, or signatories until after first save and navigation to Edit.
- `dto/create-construction-project.dto.ts` includes all 8 KC-C fields as `@IsOptional()` â€” backend accepts the data, but no Create-side UI surfaces them.

**Operational impact:**
- **Two-step capture:** Operators must create a stub, then navigate to Edit to complete the profile. This violates "an infrastructure project cannot exist without a Project Profile" per CSU governance.
- **Fragmented audit trail:** Activity logs split between CREATE event (basic) and subsequent UPDATE events (profile fields), making audit reconstruction harder.
- **Stakeholder mismatch:** Per Project Profile reference document (`docs/references/coi_overview_pow_gov`), strategic alignment IS basic information at the implementing-agency level â€” separating it is an artifact of incremental development (KC-C added profile fields after `basic` tab was already designed).

**Consolidation strategy (recommendation):**
1. **Single source of truth:** Extract a shared `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` component encapsulating ALL basic + profile fields. Mounted by both `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (replacing both `basic` and `profile` tab content) and `pages/coi/new.vue` (replacing the existing `basic` tab content).
2. **Sectioned single tab:** Within the merged tab, use `v-expansion-panels` for visual grouping (Identity / Description / Location / Contract / Strategic Alignment / Status & Readiness / Signatories) without splitting tabs. Default-open Identity + Description; the rest collapsible.
3. **Shared reactive form ref:** Component `defineModel`s a `BasicInfoFormState` interface; parent owns persistence. Eliminates form-state drift between Create and Edit.
4. **Validation centralization:** All required-field validation lives in the component (currently duplicated between Create and Edit `tabCompletion` computeds).
5. **Tab count reduction:** Edit drops from 10 to 9 tabs (basic/contract/schedule/timeline/pow/progress/financial/personnel/documents). Profile-tab data flows into the `basic` tab's expansion panels.

**Risks:**
- **R1 â€” Form state size:** Merged form will have 40-plus fields. Mitigated by sectioned expansion-panel rendering; only one section render-active at a time per Vuetify defaults.
- **R2 â€” Save scope creep:** A single save submits all 40 fields; PATCH must remain partial-friendly. Backend already supports this via `@IsOptional()` on all KC-C fields.
- **R3 â€” Migration of existing form-state:** Both pages already wire `assignmentMetadata`, `signatoryRows`, etc. The shared component must own these refs OR receive them as `defineModel` to keep change detection working.

---

### 2.144-A.1 â€” Timeline Rendering Gap Analysis (Detail vs Edit)

**Operator-reported symptom:** "Project Diary missing in Project Details."

**Audit:**
- `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue:1155` mounts `<CiProjectDiary :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />` inside the Timeline tab's right column (KD-E added 2026-05-11).
- `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue:1545-1740` Timeline tab contains `Project Milestones` only â€” no diary mount, no `CiProjectDiary` import.

**Finding (inverted from operator's report):** Project Diary IS rendering on Detail (per KD-E). The actual gap is on **Edit page Timeline tab**, which still shows milestones-only. If operator did not see diary on Detail, the cause is likely one of:
- Browser cache / stale build (KD-E shipped same session; HMR may not have picked up the new mount)
- API returning empty array (no diary entries seeded for any project yet â€” empty-state shows "No diary entries yet")
- `canEditCurrentProject` returning false (rendering issue would not block diary visibility â€” only the Add button)

**Recommended actions in plan:**
- Mirror two-column timeline layout to Edit page so diary CRUD is reachable from both surfaces.
- Add a "Last verified rendering" check in KE verification matrix to disambiguate empty-data vs absent-mount.

---

### 2.144-A.2 â€” Personnel Tab UX Assessment

**Audit (`pages/coi/detail-[id].vue:1148-1252`):**
- KD-D shipped grouped category cards (Implementing / Monitoring / University Officials / Oversight / Other). Each group renders as `v-card variant="outlined"` containing `v-list density="compact"`.
- Pain points:
  - **Visual flatness:** All groups use the same outlined card; no color-coded affordance for governance class (only icon color in card title â€” easy to miss).
  - **Avatar-less rows:** `v-list-item` shows `mdi-account-check` icon and name. No avatar, no role chip prominence, no contact action affordance.
  - **No empty-state per group:** A group with zero users renders nothing (correctly), but absence is silent â€” operators do not know which categories are unstaffed.
  - **Edit affordance buried:** Pencil button to trigger `editSection('team')` lives in the parent card title, not per-personnel. Cannot edit single member without entering full Edit-Team flow.

**Recommended UX patterns:**
- **Color-coded group headers:** Replace `variant="outlined"` with `variant="tonal"` using the group's `color` (already defined per-group in PERSONNEL_GROUPS). Header bar in tonal color creates visual hierarchy without overwhelming.
- **Row enhancement:** Add `<v-avatar size="32">` with initials per user; promote `role` chip to first-line position with stronger color; add `mdi-phone`/`mdi-email` action chips when contact data present.
- **Empty-state placeholders:** Each defined group renders a tonal card even when empty, with "No personnel assigned to this category" text and an inline "Add" button (gated by `canEditTab('team')`).
- **Per-row edit (out of KE scope):** Inline edit per row would require new backend endpoints; defer.

---

### 2.144-B â€” Overview Tab Executive Cover Page Analysis

**Audit:**
- KD-C `CiProjectHeroCard.vue` renders image hero + 4-card KPI strip + dual progress bars (line `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue:610`).
- KC-B 6 expansion panels follow: Project Information (now collapsed by default), Implementation Details, Contract & Funding, Beneficiaries & Facilities, Approval History, Key Documents (KC-D categorized cards).
- **Strategic Alignment placeholder** (KC-B-3) is wired but lives in expansion panel â€” not surfaced as a hero-level stat.
- **Missing executive elements per Project Profile reference:**
  - No standalone "Output Indicators" / "Outcome Indicators" surface (data exists in `outputIndicators[]` / `outcomeIndicators[]` JSONB but renders only inside Strategic Alignment panel)
  - No "Implementing Agency" hero badge (data in `implementingAgency` text field; renders only as expansion panel row)
  - No "Stakeholder Summary" (signatories + project leadership consolidated)
  - No accomplishment summary (latest milestone + latest financial in one glance)

**Consolidation strategy:**
1. **Hero card extension (KE-B-1):** Add a 2nd row to the hero showing Implementing Agency + Project Status Category badge + a "Strategic Alignment" excerpt (first 200 chars, expand-on-click).
2. **Indicators panel (KE-B-2):** Replace the single Strategic Alignment expansion panel with a structured "Strategic Alignment & Indicators" panel rendering: alignment text + bulleted output indicators + bulleted outcome indicators side-by-side.
3. **Stakeholder panel (KE-B-3):** New expansion panel "Stakeholders" consolidating: project engineer/manager (currently in Implementation Details), assigned signatories (from `signatories` JSONB), and assigned personnel summary (count per category) â€” links to Team tab for full detail.
4. **Accomplishment summary (KE-B-4):** Below KPI strip, add a 2-column "Recent Activity" card: left = most recent milestone with status; right = most recent financial entry summary. Sourced from already-loaded `project.milestones[0]` / `project.financials[0]`.
5. **Document preview enhancement (KE-B-5):** Add "latest version indicator" + "approval status" chips to each card in KC-D categorized previews. Data sourced from `documents[].lifecycleStatus` (Phase JN) and document checklist join (KB-E).

**Image governance:**
- `idealInfrastructureImage` is a single string field. Recommended additions in plan: "as-built image" field (post-completion), "site condition images" gallery (already covered by Gallery tab). Not new entity fields â€” reuse existing `gallery` with category=COMPLETED for as-built.

**Risks:**
- **R4 â€” Data weight:** Hero already loads on every Detail page render. Adding indicators/stakeholders inline keeps load O(1) (data already fetched). No new endpoints required.
- **R5 â€” Vertical bloat:** With hero + 6+ panels + Document Preview, Overview tab can grow more than 3 viewport heights. Mitigated by collapsing all panels except Hero + Stakeholders by default.

---

### 2.144-C â€” Personnel Tab Authorization Governance Matrix

**Audit:**
- JWT roles currently wired: `Admin`, `Staff`, `Viewer`, `Auditor` (4 roles) â€” appears in 45 `@Roles()` decorators across `construction-projects.controller.ts`.
- `SuperAdmin` exists in JWT model but has no role guards specifically targeting it (acts as superset of Admin via inheritance, if any).
- Personnel categories (governance classification of assigned users): `IMPLEMENTING / MONITORING / UNIVERSITY_OFFICIAL / OVERSIGHT` â€” wired in KD-D but **does not gate access**; purely classificatory.
- `useCoiAccess.canEditTab(tabName)` (KD-G) gates by `EDITABLE_TABS` constant + project ownership. Does not consult personnel category.

**Gap analysis vs operator's 6-role requirement:**

| Operator Role | Existing Mapping | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| Oversight | `Auditor` (read-all + audit) | Auditor lacks explicit log-access endpoint scope guard; collapses with Viewer in `@Roles()` lists |
| Full Access | `Admin` (CRUD + approval) | Mapped 1:1 |
| View-Only | `Viewer` (read-only across modules) | Mapped 1:1 |
| Restricted Editor | NOT EXISTING â€” no tab-scoped CRUD restriction | Requires new role + `useCoiAccess` extension |
| Monitoring Personnel | `MONITORING` personnel_category, NOT a JWT role | Currently informational only â€” no enforcement; needs decision (role vs category) |
| Approval Personnel | `Admin` (review/publish via workflow) | Approval lives at workflow level (`PUBLISHED` state); no separate role |

**Architecture decision required:**
- **Option C1 (recommended):** Treat personnel_category as the per-project access scope, JWT role as the platform-level capability. Combine via `useCoiAccess.canTabAction(tab, action)` returning the intersection.
- **Option C2:** Add 2 new JWT roles (`RestrictedEditor`, `Approver`). Drawback: explodes role matrix to 6+ roles requiring auditor changes to all 45 `@Roles()` decorators.
- **Option C3:** Keep JWT roles at 4; expand `useCoiAccess` with personnel_category-driven tab gates ONLY in the COI module (no backend role addition). Smallest blast radius.

**Authorization matrix (KE will adopt Option C3 + C1 hybrid):**

| Capability | SuperAdmin | Admin | Staff | Viewer | Auditor | personnel_category modifier |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Read project list | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | â€” |
| Read project detail | yes | yes | yes | yes | yes | â€” |
| Edit tab `basic` | yes | yes | (owner/assigned) | no | no | IMPLEMENTING required for Staff |
| Edit tab `pow` | yes | yes | (owner/assigned) | no | no | IMPLEMENTING required for Staff |
| Edit tab `documents` | yes | yes | (owner/assigned) | no | no | IMPLEMENTING / MONITORING |
| View activity log | yes | yes | no | no | yes | OVERSIGHT extends Viewerâ†’Auditor |
| Submit for review | yes | yes | (owner) | no | no | â€” |
| Publish / approve | yes | yes | no | no | no | OVERSIGHT may approve when Admin absent |
| Reject | yes | yes | no | no | no | â€” |

**Backend enforcement:** authoritative in `construction-projects.controller.ts` `@Roles()` decorators + service-level `validateOperationEditable()` gates. Frontend `canEditTab()` is presentational only (per CLAUDE.md directive).

---

### 2.144-D â€” Timeline / Progress / Details Consolidation Deliberation

**Audit (Edit page tab inventory, 10 tabs):** `basic / contract / schedule / timeline / pow / progress / financial / personnel / documents / profile`

**Function purpose:**
- `basic` â€” identity, location, summary
- `contract` â€” contractor, contract number, contract amount, contract duration
- `schedule` â€” start_date, target_completion_date, project_duration (string)
- `timeline` â€” milestones (and proposed: project diary)
- `pow` â€” bill of works items
- `progress` â€” physical_progress, target_physical_progress, financial_progress, target_financial_progress (4 numeric fields only)
- `financial` â€” appropriation/obligation/disbursement per fiscal year
- `personnel` â€” assigned users + role/department/phone/personnel_category
- `documents` â€” uploads + external links + document checklist (KB-E)
- `profile` â€” strategic_alignment, indicators, implementing_agency, status_updates[], readiness_documents[], signatories[]

**Overlap analysis:**
- `schedule` (3 fields) vs `timeline` (milestones list): semantically distinct (top-level dates vs phased plan), but stakeholders frequently confuse them. **Recommendation:** keep separate but rename `schedule` to `Dates & Duration` to clarify.
- `progress` (4 numeric fields) is anemic and rarely changes â€” stakeholders update via milestone progress, not direct edit. **Recommendation:** absorb into `timeline` tab as a small header row above milestones; eliminate `progress` as standalone tab. Reduces tab count 10 to 9.
- `contract` (4 fields) overlaps with `basic` (funding source). **Recommendation:** merge `contract` fields into `basic` Contract & Funding expansion panel; eliminate `contract` as standalone tab. Reduces tab count to 8.
- `profile` already targeted for merger with `basic` in Â§2.144-AA. Net: 8 to 7 tabs after both consolidations.

**Detail page tab inventory (7 tabs):** `overview / timeline / pow / analytics / gallery / documents / team` â€” well-balanced; no consolidation recommended.

**Final recommendation:** **Option C (hybrid)** â€” consolidate `basic+profile`, `contract` to `basic`, `progress` to `timeline`. Keep timeline/pow/documents/personnel/financial/schedule as discrete tabs.

---

### 2.144-E â€” Analytics Restoration Analysis

**Operator-reported symptom:** "No analytics are currently displayed."

**Audit:**
- KD-F (this session) shipped `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` mounted at `pages/coi/detail-[id].vue:1273` inside Analytics tab.
- Charts: `ApexCharts` donut (POW status), bar (POW category), bar (financial per FY), horizontal bar (milestone progress).
- Data sources:
  - POW status / category: `useCoiPowItems(projectId)` cache
  - Financial: `project.financials[]` from `adaptProjectDetail`
  - Milestone: `project.milestones[]` from `adaptProjectDetail`

**Why analytics may not render:**
- **D1 â€” Empty data:** Charts show "No POW data" / "No financial data" / "No milestone data" empty states when source arrays empty. If projects have no POW items / no financial entries / no milestones, all 4 charts show empty state â€” appears to operator as "no analytics displayed."
- **D2 â€” Field naming mismatch:** Adapter fields use camelCase (`fiscalYear`, `utilizationRate`); financial chart code uses both camelCase and snake_case fallback. POW chart code uses camelCase entity field names from useCoiPowItems. If backend changes field shape, charts silently render empty.
- **D3 â€” Tab discovery:** Operator may not have noticed the new "Analytics" tab between POW and Gallery (added KD-F). UX mitigation: add a "New" badge for first 7 days post-deploy.

**Required additional analytics (per operator's 5-category requirement):**

1. **Physical Progress (target vs actual)** â€” currently shows POW status donut and category bar. **Add:** time-series of `physicalProgress` over time vs `targetPhysicalProgress` (requires history table â€” NOT existing; defer or compute from milestone updatedAt).
2. **Financial Progress (appropriation/obligation/disbursement/utilization)** â€” KD-F covers per-FY bar. **Add:** utilization rate gauge per FY; YoY comparison.
3. **Timeline Analytics** â€” KD-F covers milestone progress bar. **Add:** "Days behind/ahead" computed from milestone target_date vs actual_date; slippage histogram.
4. **Document Analytics** â€” NOT IMPLEMENTED. Source: KB-E `construction_document_checklist` table. Compute: % submitted, % approved, % missing required.
5. **Personnel Analytics** â€” NOT IMPLEMENTED. Source: `record_assignments` joined with `activity_logs` (Phase IB). Compute: assignment distribution by category, recent approval actions, last-active timestamp per user.

**Visualization strategy:**
- Add 4 to 8 charts in 2 rows (KPI cards on row 1, charts on rows 2-3).
- Use ApexCharts (already in use); no new dependency.
- For new analytics needing aggregation, prefer SQL-side compute via raw SQL (per CLAUDE.md hybrid data access) over client-side reduce.

---

### 2.144-F â€” View vs Edit Separation Analysis

**Audit (`pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`):**
- `editSection()` function at line 108 â€” navigates to Edit page with target tab pre-selected.
- 6+ pencil icon buttons (`editSection('overview')`, `editSection('timeline')`, `editSection('team')`, `editSection('attachments')`) at lines 730, 1060, 1177, 1291, 1364 etc â€” all gated `v-if="canEditCurrentProject"`.
- 21 upload-related controls: `docFileDialog`, `driveLinkDialog`, `uploadFile`, `uploadCaption`, etc. The Documents tab and Gallery tab both have full upload UIs embedded directly in Detail page.
- Top-bar "Edit Project" primary action button at line 560 (canonical edit entry point â€” correctly gated).

**Finding:** Detail page is currently a **hybrid view+edit surface**, not a read-only preview. Multiple inline mutation paths exist (gallery upload, document upload, external link add, edit-section pencils).

**Architectural intent (per operator):** Detail page should be **read-only**. All mutations route through Edit page.

**Migration scope:**
- Remove inline upload UIs from Gallery tab and Documents tab on Detail page (uploads move to Edit page corresponding tabs).
- Remove pencil-icon `editSection()` jumps â€” top-bar "Edit Project Details" button is the only edit affordance.
- Keep workflow actions (Submit for Review / Withdraw / Publish / Reject) on Detail page â€” these are **state transitions**, not data mutations, and belong with the read view.
- Keep Analytics tab purely on Detail (read-only by design per KD-G `EDITABLE_TABS` exclusion).

**"Edit Project Details" button visibility rule:**
- Visible only when `canEditCurrentProject === true` (existing).
- Should additionally check `useCoiAccess.canEditAnyTab()` â€” a new helper returning true iff at least one tab in `EDITABLE_TABS` is editable for the current user. Otherwise button is hidden even for permission-bearing users when no tab is actually editable.
- Personnel-tab governance integration (per Â§2.144-C): `Restricted Editor` semantic emerges â€” same JWT role can have access to some tabs only.

**Risks:**
- **R6 â€” User expectation:** Removing inline edit affordances will frustrate users in habit. Mitigation: keep an `Edit this section` text link (not pencil button) at the top of each section that navigates to Edit with pre-selected tab â€” same UX, less ambiguous as a "view".
- **R7 â€” Missing upload paths:** If Detail page is the only entry point used by some workflows, upload removal could orphan capability. Mitigation: ensure Edit page Documents tab has equivalent upload UI before removing from Detail.

---

### 2.144-G â€” Cross-Flow Synchronization Strategy

**Required syncs:**
1. **Basic Info form parity (Create vs Edit):** Single `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` as source of truth. Both pages mount it.
2. **Timeline parity (Edit vs Detail):** Both pages mount `CiProjectDiary` (Detail done, Edit needs add).
3. **Personnel form parity (Create vs Edit):** Both pages need personnel category selector for newly-assigned users (Edit done in KD-D, Create needs add).
4. **Profile data hydration:** Adapter (`adaptProjectDetail`) must continue exposing all 8 KC-C fields; both pages consume same adapter output.
5. **Validation parity:** Centralized in shared `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` â€” same rules apply to both Create and Edit.

**Synchronization architecture (KE-G plan):**
```
shared/
  CiBasicInfoForm.vue       (unified Basic+Profile form)
  CiPersonnelEditor.vue     (extracted Personnel tab editor)
  CiTimelineSection.vue     (milestones + diary, mountable on Edit and Detail)
  types/coiFormState.ts     (shared form-state interfaces)

composables/
  useCoiBasicForm.ts        (shared form-state and validation hook)
  useCoiAccess.ts           (existing â€” extend with canEditAnyTab)
  useCoiPowItems.ts         (existing â€” KD-F)
```

**Regression prevention:**
- Snapshot test (manual checklist): for each shared component, verify Create + Edit + Detail paths still render and submit unchanged.
- Type contract: `BasicInfoFormState` interface lives in one file; any change requires touching all consumers â€” tsc enforces.
- Verification gate: before declaring KE complete, operator must execute KE-V matrix on **both** Create + Edit pages.

---

### 2.144-H â€” Risk Register

| ID | Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | 40-field merged form fatigue | Sectioned `v-expansion-panels` with default-collapsed |
| R2 | Save-scope creep on merged form | Backend already optional-friendly; no change required |
| R3 | Form-state drift across pages | `defineModel` + shared TypeScript interface |
| R4 | Overview hero data weight | All data already loaded; O(1) impact |
| R5 | Vertical bloat on Overview | Default-collapse all panels except Hero + Stakeholders |
| R6 | User expectation break (edit removal) | Keep "Edit this section" text link |
| R7 | Upload orphaning if Detail uploads removed first | Verify Edit-page upload presence BEFORE removal |
| R8 | Authorization role explosion | Use Option C3 (category-driven tab gating) â€” no new JWT roles |
| R9 | Analytics empty-state confusion | Add per-card "Why is this empty?" tooltip pointing to data entry tab |
| R10 | Tab consolidation breaks bookmarks | Detail page tab values unchanged; Edit page consolidation requires anchor migration logic |

---

### 2.144-I â€” Priority Decision

Execution order based on dependency, blast radius, and stakeholder value:

| Priority | Sub-Phase | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | **KE-F** (View/Edit separation) | Clarifies Detail page intent before further additions; reduces confusion for subsequent work |
| 2 | **KE-AA** (Basic+Profile consolidation) | Highest stakeholder value (single source of truth); enables KE-G shared component reuse |
| 3 | **KE-G** (Cross-flow sync via shared components) | Blocks D and B; ensures Create and Edit stay in lockstep |
| 4 | **KE-D** (Tab consolidation in Edit: contract to basic, progress to timeline) | Reduces tab count; cleaner UX |
| 5 | **KE-A.1** (Edit page diary parity) | Quick win once shared `CiTimelineSection.vue` extracted |
| 6 | **KE-B** (Overview executive enrichment) | High polish value; depends on KE-AA's profile-data availability |
| 7 | **KE-A.2** (Personnel tab UX polish) | Low risk, isolated component |
| 8 | **KE-C** (Authorization 6-role expansion via Option C3) | Backend changes; do after frontend stabilizes |
| 9 | **KE-E** (Analytics expansion: 4 to 8 charts + new sources) | Lowest priority; current 4 charts already cover physical/financial baseline |
