## Section 2.145 â€” Phase KF: Basic Info UX, Image Governance, Diary Visibility, Personnel Tab

**Governance:** ACE v2.4 Phase 1 (Research)
**Scope:** COI Basic Info UX refactor (AA), Project Image Governance (AB), Project Diary visibility (A), Personnel Tab UX redesign (C), Cross-flow continuity (D)
**Files analyzed:** `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`, `detail-[id].vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `adapters.ts`, `ConstructionGallery` entity, `gallery-category.enum.ts`, backend diary endpoints, Migration20260514010000

---

### 2.145-AA â€” Basic Info Tab UX Refactor (9 Expansion Panels)

**Current structure â€” 9 expansion panels in `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`:**

| # | Panel Title | Fields | Panel State |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Identity & Status | project_code, title, campus, status, project_status_category | Open by default |
| 1 | Description & Narrative | description, summary, ideal_infrastructure_image URL, objectives, key_features, scope, facilities | Closed |
| 2 | Building & Location | building_type, beneficiaries, floor_area, number_of_floors, latitude, longitude | Closed |
| 3 | Contract & Funding | funding_source_id, contractor_id, contract_number, contract_amount, original_contract_duration | Closed |
| 4 | Leadership & Agency | project_engineer, project_manager, implementing_agency | Closed |
| 5 | Strategic Alignment & Indicators | strategic_alignment, output_indicators, outcome_indicators | Closed |
| 6 | Status Updates | JSONB repeater (date, text) | Closed |
| 7 | Readiness Documents | JSONB repeater (type, status, remarks) | Closed |
| 8 | Signatories | JSONB repeater (name, position, date) | Closed |

**UX problem analysis:**

1. **Cognitive overload at entry:** User opens Basic Info tab and sees 9 collapsed panels with no context for which ones matter. Only Panel 0 is open. First-time users often miss critical panels (3, 4, 5) entirely.
2. **Excessive granularity:** Panel 4 (Leadership & Agency) contains only 3 fields â€” dedicating an entire expansion panel to 3 short fields is wasteful. It could be merged with Panel 0 (Identity) or Panel 5.
3. **JSONB panels premature:** Panels 6â€“8 (Status Updates, Readiness Documents, Signatories) are administrative workflow data, not project profile data. They burden the Basic Info form with operational complexity that most users never need at project creation time.
4. **Duplication with Personnel tab:** `project_engineer` and `project_manager` exist in Panel 4 (Basic Info) AND in the edit-[id].vue Personnel tab (lines 1870â€“1887). Users encounter these fields twice from different tabs.
5. **No progressive disclosure:** All 9 panels have equal visual weight. There is no visual hierarchy separating required fields from optional ones â€” aside from a "Required" label in Panel 0.
6. **Navigation regression:** With Panel 0 open but Panels 1â€“8 closed, the user must remember to open 8 more panels to complete the form. Vuetify `multiple` mode allows all panels to be open simultaneously but this creates extreme vertical scroll.

**Enterprise UX pattern analysis:**

Large multi-field forms in enterprise SaaS (Salesforce, ServiceNow, JIRA) typically use one of three patterns:
- **Flat sections with dividers** (e.g., GitHub issue creation): visible always, sections separated by `<v-divider>` with section headers. Best when total field count < 25 and all fields matter equally.
- **Tabbed sub-navigation** (e.g., ServiceNow record form): sub-tabs within a tab. Best for 50+ fields with clearly different audiences.
- **3-tier card layout** (e.g., Salesforce Lightning): essential fields always visible + secondary fields in an expandable card + rarely-needed fields in a "Show More" section.

For this codebase, the 3-tier card layout is the most compatible with the Vuetify component library and the existing form architecture. It minimizes regression risk compared to sub-tabs.

**Recommended restructuring â€” 4-section layout:**

| Section | Type | Fields | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Core** | Always-visible v-card (not collapsible) | project_code, title, campus, status, project_status_category, funding_source_id, contract_amount, contractor_id | ~8 fields. Required and semi-required items. Always shown. |
| **Project Details** | v-expansion-panel (open by default) | description, summary, building_type, floor_area, number_of_floors, beneficiaries, latitude, longitude, project_engineer, project_manager, implementing_agency, original_contract_duration | ~12 fields. Replaces panels 1, 2, 4. Merged for general users. |
| **Strategic Context** | v-expansion-panel (collapsed by default) | strategic_alignment, output_indicators, outcome_indicators, objectives, key_features, scope, facilities, ideal_infrastructure_image | ~8 fields. Replaces panels 1(partial) and 5. Optional context for strategic planning users. |
| **Administrative Records** | v-expansion-panel (collapsed by default) | statusUpdateRows, readinessDocRows, signatoryRows | 3 JSONB repeaters. Replaces panels 6â€“8. For admin/staff only; hidden from entry view. |

This reduces from 9 â†’ 4 visible groups (1 card + 3 expansion panels). Panel 0 (Identity) and Panels 3/4 are merged into the always-visible Core card + Project Details panel, removing the "too thin" dedicated panels.

**Leadership field duplication decision:**
- `project_engineer` and `project_manager` should remain in Basic Info (Panel "Project Details") as primary entry point.
- Personnel tab in edit-[id].vue (lines 1870â€“1887) should NOT remove these fields but can reference them as "Leadership" displayed from Basic Info data (read-only display). Alternatively, the 2 text fields in the Personnel tab can be kept for direct editing but labeled "Project Leadership (also editable from Basic Info)".
- **No structural change to Personnel tab required for this decision** â€” the duplication is acceptable given the different contexts (Basic Info = project identity, Personnel tab = team management).

**Risks:**
- **R-AA1 â€” Section boundary confusion:** Merged sections may hide fields users expect in specific places. Mitigation: use distinct section labels with icons; show field count in expansion panel title.
- **R-AA2 â€” CiBasicInfoForm sync:** Changes to `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` automatically propagate to both `new.vue` and `edit-[id].vue`. No dual-update needed.
- **R-AA3 â€” campusOptions/statusOptions duplication:** `new.vue` (lines 48â€“56) still has `campusOptions` and `statusOptions` arrays that were present before KE-AA. These are now stale (also defined in `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`). No functional bug but risk of drift. Should be removed from `new.vue`.

---

### 2.145-AB â€” Project Image Governance

**Current state:**
- Single field: `ideal_infrastructure_image` â€” `VARCHAR` URL string on `construction_projects` table, located in Panel 1 (Description & Narrative) of `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`. No file upload infrastructure for this field.
- `construction_gallery` table: full Multer upload infrastructure exists (backend service, controller, entity). Current categories: `BEFORE`, `IN_PROGRESS`, `COMPLETED`, `DOCUMENTATION` (enum in `gallery-category.enum.ts`, VARCHAR + CHECK in DB via `Migration20260507130000_AlignGalleryCategoryEnum`).
- `ConstructionGallery` entity: fields `id`, `projectId`, `imageUrl`, `caption`, `category`, `isFeatured`, `uploadedAt`.
- `isFeatured` boolean already exists â€” was presumably intended for a "featured image" use case but is currently unused in the frontend.
- **No dedicated "profile/architectural" image concept exists** beyond the URL-only `ideal_infrastructure_image` field.

**Requirement:** Max 3 project profile/architectural images, uploadable from Create/Edit pages, displayed in Overview.

**Option analysis:**

| Option | Approach | Effort | Risk | Verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A: New `project_profile_images` table | New entity, new service methods, new endpoints, new migration | High | Medium | Overkill â€” duplicates gallery infrastructure |
| B: Add `PROFILE` category to `construction_gallery` | Add `'PROFILE'` to CHECK constraint (1 migration), enforce max-3 in service, add upload UI in Edit page | Low-medium | Low | **RECOMMENDED** |
| C: JSONB image array on `construction_projects` | No Multer upload; only URL references stored in JSONB; cannot do file upload | Low | Medium | Does not support file upload requirement |
| D: Use `isFeatured` flag (up to 3 featured) | Mark up to 3 gallery items as `isFeatured=true`; no category change needed | Low | Low | Semantics are wrong â€” "featured" â‰  "profile/architectural" |

**Recommended: Option B â€” `PROFILE` category.**

**Implementation scope for Option B:**

Backend:
1. `Migration20260???_AddProfileGalleryCategory` â€” alter CHECK constraint on `construction_gallery.category` to include `'PROFILE'` alongside existing values.
2. `GalleryCategory` enum: add `PROFILE = 'PROFILE'`.
3. `ConstructionProjectsService.addGalleryItem()`: if `dto.category === 'PROFILE'`, count existing PROFILE items for this project; reject with `BadRequestException` if count >= 3.
4. No new endpoint needed â€” existing `POST /:id/gallery` handles upload; category is set in the DTO.

Frontend (Edit page):
1. Add a "Project Images" subsection to the Edit page (Documents tab or as a separate card at the top of Gallery tab).
2. Upload control: `v-file-input` + category hidden/defaulted to 'PROFILE'; show existing PROFILE images as thumbnails (up to 3); show count and "Upload" button disabled when count >= 3.
3. Display in Overview: `detail-[id].vue` Overview tab fetches gallery items with `category=PROFILE` and renders as an image strip.

Frontend (Create page `new.vue`):
- Image upload is NOT supported on Create page (project does not exist yet, no ID for gallery FK). Users upload images after project creation via Edit page.
- Display in Overview: a "No profile images uploaded" placeholder on Overview if none.
- `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL field (existing in CiBasicInfoForm Panel 1) can remain as a fallback â€” used when no uploaded PROFILE images exist.

**Fallback behavior:**
- If 0 PROFILE gallery images exist: display `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL (if present) as a single image; else show placeholder.
- If 1â€“3 PROFILE gallery images exist: display as an image carousel/strip in Overview; `ideal_infrastructure_image` field is shown as "URL fallback" with a note.

**Risks:**
- **R-AB1 â€” Migration dependency:** PROFILE category blocked by pending gallery category migration. Must run `Migration20260507130000` first (or include PROFILE in same migration if not yet applied to target DB). Since `AlignGalleryCategoryEnum` is already written and in migrations list, a new small migration for PROFILE addition is needed.
- **R-AB2 â€” Gallery tab vs Documents tab placement:** Operator has not specified where the image upload UI lives on Edit page. Gallery tab already handles progress photos; adding PROFILE images there may conflate purposes. Recommendation: add a dedicated "Project Images" card at the top of the Gallery tab with a clear "Architectural / Profile Images" label distinct from the progress gallery.
- **R-AB3 â€” No upload on Create:** New projects cannot have PROFILE images until after creation. This is acceptable given the infrastructure constraint (gallery FK requires project ID).

---

### 2.145-A â€” Project Diary Visibility

**Observed state in `detail-[id].vue`:**
- `<CiProjectDiary :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />` IS mounted at line 1017 in the right column of the Timeline tab `<v-window-item value="timeline">` (lines 905â€“1020).
- The frontend component is correctly placed. The UI structure for two-column layout is intact.

**Root cause 1 â€” Missing migration (BLOCKING):**
- `CiProjectDiary.fetchEntries()` calls `/api/construction-projects/:id/diary-entries`.
- Backend endpoint at `construction-projects.controller.ts:370` calls `service.findDiaryEntries()` which queries `construction_diary_entries` table via raw SQL.
- `Migration20260514010000_AddConstructionDiaryEntries` creates `construction_diary_entries`. This migration is **PENDING** (not applied to the target database).
- When the table doesn't exist, the backend returns a 500 error. `CiProjectDiary.fetchEntries()` catches the error and logs to console but shows the empty state â€” no toast, no visible error to the user.
- **Result: Diary panel renders as empty forever with no indication of the underlying error.**

**Root cause 2 â€” Two-diary-system architectural mismatch:**

| System | Used by | API endpoint | Table | Fields |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| JW-G timeline entries | `edit-[id].vue` | `GET/POST /:id/timeline-entries` | `construction_timeline_entries` | entry_date, entry_type, title, description, weather, manpower_count, equipment_notes |
| KD-E diary entries | `detail-[id].vue` `CiProjectDiary` | `GET/POST /:id/diary-entries` | `construction_diary_entries` | entry_date, title, content |

- These are **two completely different tables with different schemas**. Data entered in Edit page's Timeline tab (`construction_timeline_entries`) never appears in the Detail page's diary section (`construction_diary_entries`).
- The `CiProjectDiary` component on the Detail page creates diary entries directly into `construction_diary_entries`. These entries cannot be edited via Edit page.
- This creates a bifurcated workflow: operational supervisors entering rich timeline data in Edit, while detail-page users see a different (empty) diary.

**Root cause 3 â€” KE-F residual: pencil icon in Timeline tab (minor):**
- `detail-[id].vue` lines 916â€“925: edit button `@click="editSection('timeline')"` inside the Schedule card inside the Timeline tab. KE-F required read-only detail page but this button was not removed.
- Severity: low â€” the button navigates to Edit page (not an inline mutation). But it violates the KE-F directive of "Remove pencil-icon `editSection()` jumps."

**Architectural resolution options for two-diary-system:**

| Option | Description | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| **A (Merge at source):** Route Edit page diary to `/diary-entries` | `edit-[id].vue` stops using `timeline-entries` for the diary section; uses `diary-entries` instead. `construction_timeline_entries` becomes legacy-only (rich field data retained as historical). | Medium â€” Edit page diary UI needs to match `diary-entries` schema (loses weather/manpower fields) |
| **B (Surface both in Detail):** Detail page Timeline shows both timeline-entries (read-only) AND diary-entries | Detail Timeline tab shows `CiProjectDiary` (diary-entries) + a second section "Work Log" (timeline-entries, read-only). | Low regression â€” additive only |
| **C (Accept separation):** Two systems coexist with clearly differentiated names | "Project Diary" = `diary-entries` (simple notes, editable from Detail); "Work Log" = `timeline-entries` (rich operational data, editable from Edit). Detail page shows both. | Best UX clarity but UI requires redesign of Timeline tab |

**Recommendation: Option C** â€” keep both systems but surface both in Detail page Timeline:
- Left column: Milestones (existing)
- Middle column: Work Log (`construction_timeline_entries`, read-only in Detail, shown via CiWorkLogReadonly component â€” or inline list)
- Right column: Project Diary (`CiProjectDiary`, existing, needs migration applied)

Or if the 2-column layout is preferred, merge Work Log into a single column with Milestones (grouped in tabs within the column) and keep Diary in the right column.

**Immediate fix for diary visibility (minimal):**
1. Run `Migration20260514010000_AddConstructionDiaryEntries` â€” creates the table; diary entries can now be created and fetched.
2. Remove KE-F residual pencil button at lines 916â€“925 of `detail-[id].vue`.
3. No frontend code change needed â€” `<CiProjectDiary>` is already correctly mounted.

---

### 2.145-C â€” Personnel Tab UX Redesign

**Current state in `edit-[id].vue` (lines 1863â€“1984):**

**Card 1 "Personnel"** (lines 1865â€“1890):
- A plain `v-card` with title "Personnel".
- Two text fields: `project_engineer`, `project_manager` â€” no visual hierarchy, no icon, no context.

**Card 2 "Assigned Staff"** (lines 1892â€“1983):
- Autocomplete for multi-select of staff users.
- Per-assignment repeater for each selected user. Each assignment row has:
  - Container: `style="background-color: rgb(var(--v-theme-surface-variant))"` â€” this is the **root of the "dark/flat" complaint**: `surface-variant` resolves to a mid-grey in light mode and a slightly lighter grey in dark mode. In Vuetify Material You, this color creates visually indistinct blocks when multiple assignments are stacked.
  - Fields inside: Role/Position, Department, Contact Number, Personnel Category (4 fields in a dense `v-row`).
  - The `Personnel Category` select is placed last in the row â€” least prominent position â€” yet it is the most important field for governance.

**Key UX deficiencies:**

1. **Dark/flat assignment cards:** `surface-variant` container gives all assignment cards the same grey background regardless of category. No color differentiation â€” all personnel look the same regardless of whether they are IMPLEMENTING_TEAM vs MONITORING_OVERSIGHT.
2. **Category is de-emphasized:** The Personnel Category selector is the 4th field in a dense row (after Role, Department, Phone). Most users will miss it or not assign a category.
3. **No grouped view:** The repeater renders in selection order, not by category group. A project with 8 assignees shows 8 undifferentiated grey boxes.
4. **Leadership card is separate and plain:** "Personnel" card (Card 1) has just two text fields and no visual connection to the "Assigned Staff" card below it. The two cards create an artificial split.
5. **No visual feedback on assignment count per category:** There's no summary showing "3 Implementing, 2 Monitoring, 1 Oversight" before expanding the full repeater.
6. **Add/remove flow is non-obvious:** The autocomplete at the top adds a user; new metadata appears at the bottom of a potentially long list. Users may not notice the new repeater entry.

**Enterprise Personnel-Management UI patterns:**

- **Airtable, Notion:** Personnel listed as rows in a table with a column for role; color-coded by category/department.
- **Jira People Field:** Avatar chips grouped by role. Clicking expands role detail.
- **MS Project Resource Sheet:** Flat list but with a sortable "Group/Category" column always visible.

**Recommended redesign â€” 3-section layout:**

```
â”Œâ”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”
â”‚  Project Leadership    [card header]     â”‚
â”‚  â€¢ Project Engineer: [text field]        â”‚
â”‚  â€¢ Project Manager:  [text field]        â”‚
â”‚  â€¢ Implementing Agency: [text field]     â”‚
â””â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”˜

â”Œâ”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”
â”‚  Assignment Roster     [card header]     â”‚
â”‚  [autocomplete: search & add staff]      â”‚
â”‚                                          â”‚
â”‚  Category Summary chips:                 â”‚
â”‚  [Implementing: 3] [Monitoring: 2] [...]  â”‚
â”‚                                          â”‚
â”‚  â”Œâ”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”  â† tonal card  â”‚
â”‚  â”‚ ðŸ”§ IMPLEMENTING       â”‚  color=blue   â”‚
â”‚  â”‚  Juan Dela Cruz       â”‚               â”‚
â”‚  â”‚  Role: Site Engineer  â”‚               â”‚
â”‚  â”‚  Dept / Phone / [x]  â”‚               â”‚
â”‚  â””â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”˜               â”‚
â”‚  â”Œâ”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”  â† tonal card  â”‚

```

Specific changes:
1. **Replace `surface-variant` with tonal color per category** â€” same color scheme used in `detail-[id].vue` `PERSONNEL_GROUPS` constant (blue=IMPLEMENTING, orange=MONITORING, purple=OVERSIGHT, etc.). Each assignment card uses `variant="tonal" :color="categoryColor(uid)"`.
2. **Promote Personnel Category to first field** in each assignment row â€” with a colored chip preview showing the currently selected category.
3. **Category summary header** above the repeater: a row of small chips showing count per category (computed). Updates reactively as categories are assigned.
4. **Unified "Project Leadership" section** â€” merge the former Card 1 with the top of Card 2 under a single `v-card` with sectioned content.
5. **Match detail page's PERSONNEL_GROUPS** color map for visual consistency: Detail page shows grouped cards by category in read view; Edit page should use the same color scheme.

**Color map (from `detail-[id].vue` PERSONNEL_GROUPS constant):**

| Category | Icon | Color |
|---|---|---|
| IMPLEMENTING_TEAM | mdi-hard-hat | blue |
| MONITORING_OVERSIGHT | mdi-eye-check | orange |
| RESTRICTED_EDITOR | mdi-pencil-lock | purple |
| OVERSIGHT | mdi-shield-check | green |
| READ_ONLY | mdi-eye-outline | grey |
| (unset/default) | mdi-account | primary |

**Risks:**
- **R-C1 â€” Theme-specific regression:** Replacing `surface-variant` with tonal category color requires the `personnelCategoryOptions` to supply a `color` field alongside `title`/`value`. This is a small addition to the options array.
- **R-C2 â€” Unset category rendering:** If a user is assigned but `personnelCategory` is empty, the tonal card must gracefully fall back to a neutral color (e.g., `grey`) with an "Assign Category" prompt.
- **R-C3 â€” New.vue alignment:** `new.vue` also has a Personnel tab (`v-window-item value="personnel"`). Any redesign of the Personnel tab card in `edit-[id].vue` should be mirrored in `new.vue`. Both should share a `CiPersonnelEditor.vue` component (per KE-G plan â€” still pending).

### 2.145-D â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

**Create (`new.vue`) â€” Status:**

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| CiBasicInfoForm mounted | âœ… (KE-AA) |
| KC-C fields in form ref | âœ… (KE-AA: lines 50â€“56) |
| JSONB array refs present | âœ… (KE-AA: lines 58â€“61) |
| JSONB arrays in handleSubmit | âœ… (KE-AA: lines 304â€“314) |
| `campusOptions` stale local copy | âš ï¸ Duplicate of CiBasicInfoForm â€” drift risk |
| `statusOptions` stale local copy | âš ï¸ Duplicate of CiBasicInfoForm â€” drift risk |
| Personnel tab redesigned | âŒ Pending (KF-C) |
| Image upload in Basic Info | âŒ Pending (KF-AB; upload not supported on Create â€” acceptable) |

**Edit (`edit-[id].vue`) â€” Status:**

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| CiBasicInfoForm mounted | âœ… (KE-AA) |
| Profile tab replaced with redirect | âœ… (KE-AA) |
| JSONB hydration from raw backend | âœ… (lines 225â€“231: directly from `p.status_updates`, `p.readiness_documents`, `p.signatories`) |
| JSONB arrays in save payload | âœ… (lines 422â€“424) |
| Personnel tab redesigned | âŒ Pending (KF-C) |
| tabCompletion duplicate removed | âœ… (KE-AA) |
| KE-F pencil button in Timeline | âš ï¸ Lines 916â€“925: pencil still present â€” minor residual |

**Detail (`detail-[id].vue`) â€” Status:**

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| View-only (KE-F) | âœ… Mostly; pencil in Timeline (lines 916â€“925) is residual |
| CiProjectDiary mounted | âœ… (line 1017) |
| construction_diary_entries migration | âŒ PENDING â€” table missing, diary silently empty |
| Personnel grouped by category | âœ… (KE-A.2) |
| Profile image display (PROFILE gallery) | âŒ Pending (KF-AB) |

**Adapter continuity:**

The `adaptProjectDetail()` function in `adapters.ts` maps:
- `backend.status_updates` â†’ `statusUpdates`
- `backend.readiness_documents` â†’ `readinessDocuments`
- `backend.signatories` â†’ `signatories`

However, `edit-[id].vue` **does not use the adapter for hydration**. It fetches the project via raw API and reads `p.status_updates`, `p.readiness_documents`, `p.signatories` directly. The adapter mapping is only used for `detail-[id].vue` (read display).

The naming mismatch (`readinessDocuments` in adapter vs `readinessDocRows` in CiBasicInfoForm v-model) is acceptable because:
- `readinessDocRows` is the edit-page local ref, hydrated from `p.readiness_documents` (snake_case backend field).
- The adapter's `readinessDocuments` is only used in detail view context.
- No sync failure; both paths trace to `readiness_documents` column.

**Missing backend migration status:**

| Migration | Status | Impact if not applied |
|---|---|---|
| `Migration20260507210000_RelaxPowOrphanedNotNull` | PENDING | POW item creation may fail on orphaned FK constraint |
| `Migration20260513030000_AddProjectProfileFields` | PENDING | KC-C fields (strategic_alignment, etc.) not on DB â†’ 500 on save |
| `Migration20260514000000_AddPersonnelCategory` | PENDING | `personnel_category` column missing â†’ 500 on personnel save |
| `Migration20260514010000_AddConstructionDiaryEntries` | PENDING | `construction_diary_entries` table missing â†’ 500 on diary fetch |

**Critical:** If `Migration20260513030000` is not applied, the entire KE-AA form will fail to save KC-C fields silently. All 4 pending migrations must be applied before the Phase KF implementation can be fully tested.

**Residual KE-F issue:**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 916â€“925: `v-btn icon @click="editSection('timeline')"` inside Schedule card.
- KE-F rule: "Remove pencil-icon `editSection()` jumps". This was missed.
- Fix: remove the entire `<v-btn>` block at lines 916â€“925 (or replace with the canonical text link pattern: `<a @click="editSection('timeline')">Edit in Edit Project â†—</a>`).

---

### 2.145-E â€” Risk Register (Phase KF)

| ID | Risk | Area | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KF1 | Pending migrations cause silent data loss on KC-C/JSONB fields | Backend | Apply all 4 pending migrations before testing Phase KF; operator must run migrations manually |
| R-KF2 | `campusOptions`/`statusOptions` drift between `new.vue` and `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` | Frontend | Remove stale duplicates from `new.vue` â€” they are already defined in `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` |
| R-KF3 | Two-diary-system creates data bifurcation | Backend/Frontend | Implement Option C: surface both systems in Detail Timeline; clearly label each |
| R-KF4 | Personnel category color map duplication | Frontend | Extract `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP` constant to a shared file (composable or utils); import in both `edit-[id].vue` and `detail-[id].vue` |
| R-KF5 | PROFILE gallery category blocked by CHECK constraint | Backend | New migration required to add `'PROFILE'` to allowed values before frontend upload can work |
| R-KF6 | Basic Info form refactor breaks Create/Edit parity | Frontend | All changes are in `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` â€” shared component; parity is automatic |
| R-KF7 | KE-F pencil residual in Timeline tab | Frontend | Remove 7 lines at detail-[id].vue 916â€“925 |

## Section 2.146 â€” Phase KG: Attachment Governance, Analytics Fix, Create-Page Alignment, and Personnel UX

**Governance:** ACE v2.4 Phase 1 (Research)
**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** (A) Attachment/document governance gap across Create/Edit/Detail; (B) Personnel UX assessment; (C) Overview UX status; (D) Analytics restoration/bug analysis; (E) Timeline synchronization status; (F) View/Edit governance audit; (G) Cross-flow continuity (new.vue divergence)
**Files analyzed:** `new.vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `detail-[id].vue`, `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`, `CiDocumentChecklist.vue`

---

### 2.146-A â€” Attachment Tab Governance Refactor

**Context:** The three COI pages (Create, Edit, Detail) use different document/attachment workflows. The Detail page has the most advanced compliance system (`CiDocumentChecklist`), while Create and Edit are simpler.

#### A.1 â€” Create Project (`new.vue`) Documents Tab

**Current capability:**
- Staging-only: files, images, and external links are staged in browser memory (`pendingDocs`, `pendingImages`, `pendingLinks`)
- Uploaded in a second pass immediately after the project is created
- Document types available: `attachment`, `mov`, `specification`, `contract`, `other`
- Image categories: `PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `AFTER`, `OTHER`
- No `CiDocumentChecklist` integration â€” user has no visibility into CPES compliance requirements at creation time

**Gaps:**
- No compliance checklist awareness (CPES requirements invisible to creator)
- No PROFILE gallery image upload (not supported at creation time â€” acceptable; project has no ID yet)
- `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL field exists in CiBasicInfoForm â€” serves as create-time fallback

**Assessment:** Document staging is appropriate for the creation workflow. Compliance checklist is a detail/edit concern, not a creation concern. **No change needed.**

#### A.2 â€” Edit Project (`edit-[id].vue`) Documents Tab

**Current capability:**
- Upload new documents (type + description) via Multer
- Upload gallery images (`PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `AFTER`, `OTHER`)
- Upload PROFILE gallery images (max 3, KF-AB implemented)
- Manage external links
- **No `CiDocumentChecklist`** â€” compliance checklist not accessible during edit workflow

**Gap analysis:**
- Users who want to upload a required CPES document (e.g., Building Permit, Environmental Clearance) must navigate to Detail page to check compliance status, then return to Edit to upload the file.
- This two-page friction exists but is acceptable given that:
  a. `CiDocumentChecklist` is a governance-checklist, not an upload interface (uploads still go to Edit via flat file upload)
  b. Adding the full checklist to Edit would create UI duplication

**Verdict:** The gap is by design. Edit page provides the upload surface; Detail page provides the compliance tracking surface. **No change needed for KG. Document for future phase if compliance-in-edit becomes a requirement.**

#### A.3 â€” Detail/View (`detail-[id].vue`) Documents Tab

**Current capability:**
- `CiDocumentChecklist` (primary): CPES compliance checklist, full status lifecycle (NOT_SUBMITTED â†’ SUBMITTED â†’ UNDER_REVIEW â†’ APPROVED/REJECTED), grouped by category
  - Respects `:can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"` â€” edit actions gated
- File Library (secondary): view-only list of uploaded files and external links
  - **No delete buttons found** (confirmed: lines 1438â€“1480 show list items only; no delete action exposed)
  - "Manage in Edit Project Details â†—" redirect link shown when `canEditAnyTab`

**Verdict:** Detail Documents tab is correctly view-only. View/Edit governance is correct. **No change needed.**

#### A.4 â€” Cross-flow Attachment Summary

| Capability | new.vue (Create) | edit-[id].vue (Edit) | detail-[id].vue (View) |
|---|---|---|---|
| File upload | âœ… Staged | âœ… Live | âŒ Redirect-only |
| Image upload | âœ… Staged | âœ… Live (incl. PROFILE) | âŒ Redirect-only |
| External links | âœ… Staged | âœ… Live | âŒ Redirect-only |
| Compliance checklist | âŒ Not needed | âŒ By design | âœ… CiDocumentChecklist |
| Delete files | âŒ Remove from pending | âœ… Live delete | âŒ View-only |

**Conclusion:** Attachment architecture is appropriately separated. No structural change required. The previously identified gap (checklist in edit) is by design.

---

### 2.146-B â€” Personnel Tab UX Assessment

#### B.1 â€” Create (`new.vue`)

**Current state:** Simple Personnel tab with:
- `project_engineer` text field
- `project_manager` text field
- `assigned_user_ids` multi-select autocomplete (no per-assignment metadata)

**Assessment:** Minimal and appropriate for creation workflow. No category assignment, no role/department/phone metadata â€” this is acceptable because detailed assignment metadata is an Edit concern.

**Gap:** `new.vue` personnel tab has no connection to `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP`. Assigned users are added without category â€” this is acceptable (category defaults to null; category is set in Edit).

#### B.2 â€” Edit (`edit-[id].vue`)

**Current state (post KF-C):** Personnel tab has:
- "Project Leadership" card: project_engineer + project_manager
- "Assignment Roster" card: autocomplete + per-assignment color-coded tonal cards using `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP`
- Category summary chips
- Category is first field in each assignment card

**Assessment:** Post-KF-C design is well-structured. The two-card separation (Leadership vs. Roster) is clear. Color-coded categories provide good role distinction. **No further redesign needed for KG.**

#### B.3 â€” Detail (`detail-[id].vue`)

**Current state:** Two-column layout:
- Left: Personnel by Category (grouped cards via `groupedPersonnel` computed)
- Right: Project Leadership (engineer, manager)

**Assessment:** Good view-only layout. Cards use `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_MAP` for color/icon (via `PERSONNEL_GROUPS` constant from `coiFormState.ts`). **No redesign needed.**

**Conclusion:** Personnel UX across all three pages is adequate. No KG action required beyond what KF-C already implemented.

---

### 2.146-C â€” Overview Tab Enhancement Status

**Current state (post KE-B):**
- `overviewPanels` ref controls expansion state
- Hero card: status, campus, contract amount, implementing agency, status category
- Expansion panels 0â€“8:
  - Panels 0â€“5: existing (dates, description, building, geotag, milestones strip, financial summary)
  - Panel 6: Strategic Context (new in KE-B: strategic_alignment, implementingAgency, projectStatusCategory)
  - Panel 7: Project Indicators (new in KE-B: outputIndicators, outcomeIndicators as chips)
  - Panel 8: Signatories (new in KE-B: avatar list with initials/position/date)
- PROFILE images strip above expansion panels (KF-AB)

**Assessment:** Overview tab is well-enriched post KE-B and KF-AB. No further UX changes are required at this stage. The executive presentation goal is met. **No KG action required.**

### 2.146-D â€” Analytics Tab: Restoration Analysis

#### D.1 â€” Component Status

`CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` (230 lines) is fully implemented:

| Chart | Guard | Data source | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| 4 KPI cards | Always renders | `powItems`, `project.financials`, `project.milestones` | âœ… Always visible |
| POW Status Donut | `hasPowData` | `useCoiPowItems(projectId)` composable | âœ… Renders when POW exists |
| POW Category Bar | `hasPowData` | `useCoiPowItems(projectId)` composable | âœ… Renders when POW exists |
| Financial Stacked Bar | `hasFinancialData` | `props.project.financials` | âœ… Renders when financials exist |
| Milestone Progress Bar | `hasMilestoneData` | `props.project.milestones` | âš ï¸ BUG â€” renders but milestone labels broken |

#### D.2 â€” Bug Identified: Milestone Chart `yaxis.categories`

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` lines 112â€“125

**Current code:**
```typescript
options: {
  chart: { type: 'bar' },
  plotOptions: { bar: { horizontal: true } },
  xaxis: { max: 100, title: { text: 'Progress %' } },
  yaxis: { categories: ms.map(m => m.name || m.title || 'â€”') },  // WRONG
}
```

**Problem:** ApexCharts horizontal bar charts use `xaxis.categories` to define the category labels that appear on the Y-axis (left side). The `yaxis` object does not accept a `categories` property in ApexCharts â€” this silently fails. The horizontal bar renders with no labels on the Y-axis, making the chart unreadable.

**Fix:**
```typescript
xaxis: { categories: ms.map(m => m.title || m.name || 'â€”'), max: 100, title: { text: 'Progress %' } },
yaxis: {},  // no categories here
```

**Impact:** One-line fix. Milestone chart becomes readable immediately.

#### D.3 â€” "No Analytics" Root Cause

The complaint that analytics tab shows "no content" is explained by:
1. **Empty data guard messages** â€” all four charts show grey "No data" text when the underlying data arrays are empty. For a project with no POW items, no financials, no milestones â†’ four charts all show "no data" text simultaneously, giving the appearance of an empty tab.
2. **KPI cards always render** â€” the 4 KPI cards at the top render regardless of data. For empty projects they show `0%`, `â€”`, `â‚±0`, `0%`.
3. **Not a broken component** â€” the component is correctly implemented. The issue is data availability.

**Verdict:** Analytics component works correctly. Fix the milestone chart `yaxis.categories` bug. For projects with data, all charts will display. For projects without data, the empty-state text is the correct UX.

| Section | Governance | Status |

| Overview tab | View-only â€” no edit actions | âœ… |

### 2.146-G â€” Cross-Flow Continuity: CRITICAL DIVERGENCE in `new.vue`

**This is the most significant finding in Phase KG research.**

#### G.1 â€” new.vue Tab Structure vs edit-[id].vue (KE-D gap)

KE-D was applied to `edit-[id].vue` in a prior session, reducing from 10 â†’ 7 tabs. However, KE-D was **NOT applied to `new.vue`**, which still has the old 6-tab structure.

**new.vue current tabOrder (line 93):**
```typescript
const tabOrder = ['basic', 'contract', 'schedule', 'progress', 'personnel', 'documents'] as const
```

**edit-[id].vue current tabOrder (post KE-D):**
```typescript
const tabOrder = ['basic', 'schedule', 'timeline', 'pow', 'financial', 'personnel', 'documents'] as const
```

#### G.2 â€” Duplicate Contract Fields (CRITICAL)

`new.vue` Tab 2 ("Contract") renders:
- `funding_source_id` select
- `contractor_id` select
- `contract_number` text field
- `contract_amount` number field

**These same fields already exist in `CiBasicInfoForm.vue` Core card (lines ~125â€“179)**, which is mounted in Tab 1 ("Basic Info") via:
```html
<CiBasicInfoForm v-model="form" ... />
```

**Result:** Users entering the Create form encounter `funding_source_id`, `contractor_id`, `contract_number`, `contract_amount` TWICE â€” once in the Core card of Basic Info, and again in the Contract tab. The Contract tab values are the ones sent in `handleSubmit` payload, but the CiBasicInfoForm core card values are bound to the same `form` ref (same v-model keys). Any value entered in CiBasicInfoForm's core card is overwritten if the user enters different values in the Contract tab, or vice versa.

**This is a DATA INTEGRITY BUG.** Dual input for the same field with the same `form` binding means either:
- The value entered in CiBasicInfoForm Core card IS the same as Contract tab (reactive sync â€” not a problem)
- OR: users see two different places to enter the same field and are confused about which takes effect

The confusion risk is high. Users will ask "why is Funding Source in both Basic Info AND Contract?"

#### G.3 â€” Progress Tab Building Fields (Duplication)

`new.vue` Tab 4 ("Progress & Physical Details") renders:
- `target_physical_progress` (number, %)
- `target_financial_progress` (number, %)
- `building_type`, `floor_area`, `number_of_floors`, `beneficiaries`, `latitude`, `longitude` â€” **already in CiBasicInfoForm Panel 0 ("Project Details")**

The session notes confirm: "Also removed Building & Location Details which was duplicated in CiBasicInfoForm" â€” this was done for edit-[id].vue's progress tab but not for new.vue.

**Result:** Building/location fields appear in both new.vue Tab 4 AND CiBasicInfoForm Panel 0 (mounted in Tab 1). Same dual-input risk.

The `target_physical_progress` and `target_financial_progress` fields are NOT in CiBasicInfoForm â€” they are unique to Tab 4. These need a home after the progress tab is removed.

#### G.4 â€” Guide Card Inconsistency

`new.vue` guide card (line 455â€“463):
```html
:content="[
  '5 tabs to complete',
  'Basic Info is required to save',
  'You can return and edit other tabs later',
]"
```

The guide card says "5 tabs" but `tabOrder` has 6 entries. This is an incorrect user-facing count. The correct number after alignment should be 4 tabs.

#### G.5 â€” tabCompletion/tabRequired Stale Entries

`new.vue` `tabCompletion` includes entries for `contract` and `progress` tabs. The `tabRequired.contract` entry makes `funding_source_id` a blocking required field for tab navigation. After removing the contract tab:
- `funding_source_id` must still be required â†’ move requirement to `tabRequired.basic`
- `tabCompletion.basic` should include `funding_source_id` check

#### G.6 â€” Required Fix for new.vue Alignment

**Target new.vue tabOrder after KG-A:**
```typescript
const tabOrder = ['basic', 'schedule', 'personnel', 'documents'] as const
```

Note: new.vue does NOT need `timeline`, `pow`, or `financial` tabs because new projects have no milestones, POW items, or financial records yet. 4 tabs is the correct structure for project creation.

**Actions required:**
1. Remove `<v-tab value="contract">` + `<v-window-item value="contract">`
2. Remove `<v-tab value="progress">` + `<v-window-item value="progress">`
3. Add `target_physical_progress` and `target_financial_progress` fields to the Schedule window-item
4. Update `tabOrder` â†’ `['basic', 'schedule', 'personnel', 'documents']`
5. Update `tabCompletion`: remove `contract` + `progress`, add `funding_source_id` to `basic` check
6. Update `tabRequired`: remove `contract` + `progress`, add `funding_source_id` to `basic` check
7. Update guide card: "4 tabs" count
8. Update `currentStepIndex` (uses `tabOrder.indexOf` â€” computed automatically)
9. Update footer Next button condition (already correct: `!== 'documents'` or last element)

**No backend changes required.** All fields already exist in the DB, entity, DTO, and service. The `handleSubmit` payload in `new.vue` already maps all these fields correctly (lines 268â€“310).

---

### 2.146-H â€” Risk Register (Phase KG)

| ID | Risk | Area | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KG1 | Duplicate contract/building fields create user confusion and drift | `new.vue` | Remove Contract + Progress tabs entirely (KG-A) |
| R-KG2 | After removing Contract tab, `funding_source_id` loses its Required gate | `new.vue` | Move Required check to `tabRequired.basic`/`tabCompletion.basic` |
| R-KG3 | Milestone chart renders with unlabeled Y-axis | CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue | Fix `yaxis.categories` â†’ `xaxis.categories` (KG-B) |
| R-KG4 | Guide card "5 tabs" creates user confusion | `new.vue` | Update to "4 tabs" as part of KG-A |
| R-KG5 | Target progress fields lose a home after Progress tab removal | `new.vue` | Add both fields to Schedule window-item |

---

### 2.146-I â€” Priority and Execution Order

| Step | Task | Priority | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| **KG-A** | new.vue tab alignment (remove Contract + Progress tabs, align with KE-D standard) | ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | Duplicate fields = data confusion; stale 6-tab structure diverges from edit-[id].vue |
| **KG-B** | Analytics milestone chart bug fix (`yaxis.categories`) | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | One-line fix; milestone chart is unreadable without it |

**All other concerns (attachment governance, personnel UX, overview, timeline, view/edit governance) are either already addressed or require no action at this time.**

## Section 2.147 â€” COI Attachment Governance Refactor, Project Details Optimization, and Personnel Workflow Stabilization

**Phase:** KH Research (Phase 1)
**Date:** 2026-05-11
**Research Reference:** Operator prompt â€” CSU CORE: Attachment Governance Refactor, Overview Optimization, Personnel Workflow, Analytics Restoration

---

### 2.147-A â€” Attachment Tab Governance Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 1834â€“2175

**Current structure (flat, one card):**
```
v-window-item value="documents"
  v-card
    v-card-title: "Attachments"
    v-card-text:
      v-alert: info banner
      h4: "Existing files (N)"       â† flat v-list, no categories
      v-divider: Documents
      v-row: file-input + type-select + description + Upload button
      [template: gallery section]
        v-card: "Project Profile Images" (max 3)
          v-row: profile image grid
          v-row: profile image upload
        [general gallery list]
      v-divider: Upload Gallery Image
      v-row: general image upload
      v-divider: External Links
      v-row: link-url + title + desc + Add button
```

**Critical findings:**

**A.1 â€” Single shared `imageFile` ref used by two upload contexts**
- Line 2007: `v-model="imageFile"` for PROFILE image upload
- Line 2083: `v-model="imageFile"` for general gallery upload
- `imageFile` is the same reactive ref for both inputs; switching between them without clearing will cause stale file selection to be uploaded to the wrong category
- `imageUploading` is also shared â€” one boolean for two async upload paths

**A.2 â€” No structural grouping hierarchy**
- The entire tab is rendered inside one `v-card` with `v-card-text` (line 1835â€“2175 = ~340 lines)
- Documents, Gallery, and Links are three distinct entity types collapsed into one monolithic card
- No `v-card` per section â€” only `v-divider` lines act as visual separators
- Sections cannot be independently collapsed, navigated, or focused

**A.3 â€” Existing files section lacks category columns**
- `existingDocs` renders a flat `v-list` (line 1861); no grouping by `documentType`
- The type chip is shown as subtitle metadata but documents are NOT grouped by category
- No sort order â€” renders in insertion order
- No file size column on the list row

**A.4 â€” Gallery section causes rendering-adjacent freeze**
- `profileGalleryImages` and `generalGalleryImages` are computed filters over `existingGallery` ref
- All gallery items load on `fetchData()` (mount); no lazy-load or pagination
- Switching to the documents tab after a large gallery load triggers a full re-render of the entire Documents window-item, which includes the gallery grid, image previews, and all upload controls simultaneously
- The `v-img` components inside the gallery section render immediately without `v-lazy` â€” on large sets this blocks the JS thread

**A.5 â€” Page redirect instability on upload**
- `uploadEditDoc` (and `uploadEditImage`) mutate `existingDocs` / `existingGallery` reactively after each API call
- No optimistic update â€” re-fetches the full list after each upload
- If the user navigates to another tab mid-upload and then navigates back, the file input refs (`docFile`, `imageFile`) are still bound to the previous selection â€” but the reactive state has been updated, causing a mismatch between UI and state

**A.6 â€” Governance dependencies on Attachment tab**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 279â€“283: `DOCUMENT_PREVIEW_TYPES` â†’ `findDocumentPreview()` drives the Overview "Key Documents" panel
- `detail-[id].vue` line 325â€“326: `documentFiles` / `documentLinks` drive the Documents tab file/link section
- `detail-[id].vue` line 227: `profileImages` (filtered from gallery) drives the Overview profile image strip
- All three consume `documents` and `gallery` refs fetched separately â€” no shared data contract

**A.7 â€” Create form (new.vue) attachment architecture: staging only**
- `new.vue` uses a staging model (pendingDocs, pendingImages, pendingLinks arrays)
- No live upload during create â€” uploads occur post-project-creation
- Category `PROGRESS` is used for new.vue gallery (line 108) but edit uses `IN_PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `COMPLETED`, `DOCUMENTATION` â€” **inconsistent category enum between Create and Edit**

---

### 2.147-B â€” Personnel Tab Workflow Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 1694â€“1831

**Current structure:**
```
v-window-item value="personnel"
  v-card: "Personnel"                  â† project_engineer + project_manager (2 fields)
  v-card: "Assigned Staff"
    v-autocomplete: multi-select users
    [v-if assigned_user_ids.length > 0]
      div: "Per-member details"
        v-card (tonal, per user):       â† category-colored, stacked vertically
          v-row: 4 fields each
```

**Critical findings:**

**B.1 â€” Flat vertical stack for multi-user assignment cards**
- All assigned users render as a full-width stack â€” no 2-column grid even on wide screens
- On a project with 6+ assignees, this creates extreme vertical scroll within one tab
- No visual grouping by category â€” cards are rendered in assignment order, not category

**B.2 â€” Category assignment requires post-selection action**
- User must: (1) search and select from autocomplete, (2) scroll down to find the new card, (3) set category
- No ability to pre-select category before searching
- Category selection is hidden inside per-user cards that only appear after selection

**B.3 â€” Detail page vs Edit page visual inconsistency**
- `detail-[id].vue` lines 1263â€“1334: uses `PERSONNEL_GROUPS` to render tonal group headers with avatars âœ“
- `edit-[id].vue` lines 1744â€“1827: uses flat ordered cards with no group context visible until category is set
- A user switching from Detail â†’ Edit loses the grouped mental model entirely

**B.4 â€” Project Engineer/Manager fields isolated**
- `form.project_engineer` and `form.project_manager` are plain text fields in a separate card
- Not connected to the assignment system â€” no lookup from staffUsers
- On Detail page, these appear in "Project Leadership" card (line 1339â€“1362) separate from personnel groups â€” consistent, but creates a split mental model

**B.5 â€” Create page (new.vue) personnel section**
- Lines 759â€“810: identical structure â€” two text fields + autocomplete + no category cards
- Personnel metadata expansion (category, role, department, phone) is ABSENT from new.vue
- Creates a governance gap: project created without category assignments; edit required to assign categories

---

### 2.147-C â€” Overview Tab Space Utilization Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` lines 536â€“1094

**Current structure:**
```
v-window-item value="overview"
  CiProjectHeroCard                    â† hero card (KD-C)
  v-card: Profile Images (if any)
  v-card: Status + Progress bars (flat flex row)
  v-expansion-panels multiple (8 panels, all open by default at [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8])
    Panel 1: Project Information (image + summary + objectives)
    Panel 2: Project Details & Location
    Panel 3: Contract Information
    Panel 4: Financial Summary (with v-data-table)
    Panel 5: Approval History (v-timeline)
    Panel 6: Key Documents Preview (2x2 grid)
    Panel 7: Strategic Context
    Panel 8: Project Indicators
    Panel 9 (implicit): Signatories
```

**Critical findings:**

**C.1 â€” All 8 panels open by default = catastrophic scroll depth**
- `overviewPanels = ref([1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8])` means all panels render content on initial load
- A fully-populated project could produce 4,000+ px of vertical content
- Executive users expect a summary view, not a data dump

**C.2 â€” No stat/KPI card row**
- Physical progress and financial progress are buried in a flex row (lines 619â€“675) inside a card â€” not presented as dedicated KPI stat cards
- The financial summary numbers (appropriation, obligation, disbursement) are inside Panel 4 which requires scroll
- Zero KPI cards at the top level that an executive can parse in one glance

**C.3 â€” Status/progress card is a 1-column-wide flex row**
- Lines 579â€“689: flex row with Status chip + publication chip + two progress bars + contract amount
- At narrow breakpoints this wraps poorly with no `v-col` grid structure
- No `v-row`/`v-col` grid â€” pure flex with `flex-wrap` and `ga-6`

**C.4 â€” No multi-column panel layout**
- 8 panels stack vertically as single-column even on widescreen
- Content like "Project Details & Location" (a short field list) and "Contract Information" (another short list) could be side-by-side at `md` breakpoint
- "Strategic Context" (1â€“2 text fields) wastes full-width panel height

**C.5 â€” Financial data duplication**
- Panel 4 (Financial Summary) duplicates `financialSummary` computed data already shown in Status card (contract amount)
- At the same time, the Financial tab on the Edit page maintains the per-FY records
- No deduplication concern â€” but the Overview panel adds minimal value over what could be a compact 4-stat row

---

### 2.147-D â€” Analytics Tab Rendering Inventory

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`

**D.1 â€” Analytics IS rendering; charts conditionally guarded**
- `<CiProjectAnalyticsTab v-if="project" :project-id="projectId" :project="project" />` (detail line 1373)
- `hasPowData`, `hasFinancialData`, `hasMilestoneData` guards show "No X data" text correctly
- When operator reports "no analytics rendering" this is empty-data state, not a broken render

**D.2 â€” CRITICAL BUG: `f.obligations` vs `f.obligation`**
- `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` line 97: `data: sorted.map(f => Number(f.obligations ?? 0))`
- The field is named `obligation` (singular) in the entity and adapter â€” `f.obligations` always returns `undefined`
- **The Obligations series in the financial bar chart always renders as all-zeros even when obligation data exists**
- Fix: change `f.obligations ?? 0` â†’ `f.obligation ?? 0`

**D.3 â€” `financialUtilization` reads only first financial record**
- Line 41â€“46: `const f = props.project.financials?.[0]`
- Projects with multiple FY records display utilization from FY 2022 (oldest record), not the aggregate
- Should aggregate total_obligation / total_appropriation across all records

**D.4 â€” Analytics data availability matrix**

| Dataset | Source | Available in component | Empty-state handled |
|---|---|---|---|
| POW status | useCoiPowItems composable | âœ“ | âœ“ "No POW data" |
| POW category avg | useCoiPowItems composable | âœ“ | âœ“ "No POW data" |
| Financial per-FY | props.project.financials | âœ“ | âœ“ "No financial data" |
| Milestone progress | props.project.milestones | âœ“ | âœ“ "No milestone data" |
| Physical progress time series | NOT available in project prop | âœ— | N/A |
| Personnel activity | NOT fetched | âœ— | N/A |
| Document compliance rate | NOT fetched | âœ— | N/A |
| Work log entries | NOT passed to analytics | âœ— | N/A |

**D.5 â€” Chart architecture is sound; only data bugs prevent full utility**
- 4 computed chart specs (powStatusChart, powCategoryChart, financialChart, milestoneChart) are structurally correct
- KG-B fix (xaxis.categories for horizontal bar) is already applied
- Adding more charts only requires additional computed + `<VueApexCharts>` blocks â€” no architectural changes needed

---

### 2.147-E â€” Timeline Tab Synchronization Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 1336â€“1596, `detail-[id].vue` lines 1097â€“1257

**E.1 â€” Detail page: two-column layout âœ“**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1099â€“1256: `<v-row>` with `<v-col cols="12" md="6">` for milestones (left) and `<CiProjectDiary>` (right) â€” KD-E shipped and working

**E.2 â€” Edit page timeline tab: single-column, no diary**
- edit-[id].vue timeline tab starts at line 1336
- Contains: Progress Summary card + Project Milestones CRUD + [diary CRUD below milestones in same column]
- `existingDiaryEntries`, `diaryDialog`, `diaryForm`, etc. (lines 536â€“568) confirm diary CRUD state exists in edit page
- BUT the diary CiProjectDiary component is NOT mounted in the edit timeline tab â€” edit page uses raw dialog-based CRUD for diary entries
- The asymmetry: Detail shows `CiProjectDiary` component; Edit uses inline CRUD dialog pattern
- Both access the same API endpoint (`/timeline-entries`) â€” data is consistent, but UX is inconsistent

**E.3 â€” Project Diary in Create: no support**
- new.vue has no diary tab, no diary state, no diary section
- Timeline entries are keyed by `projectId` â€” diary cannot exist until after project is created
- This is architecturally correct: diary entries cannot be pre-created; no action needed for Create flow

**E.4 â€” "Progress Summary" card in Edit timeline tab**
- Lines 1337â€“1396: Progress Summary card with 4 fields (physical_progress, target_physical_progress, financial_progress, target_financial_progress)
- This card is NOT mirrored in Create (new.vue has target_* in Schedule tab per KG-A, but not actual_* fields)
- actual physical_progress / financial_progress are Edit-only (actual progress requires an existing project with real data)
- This is architecturally correct â€” no synchronization gap here

**E.5 â€” Edit diary CUD uses inline dialogs; Detail uses CiProjectDiary**
- Edit page lines 536â€“1596: `diaryDialog`, `diaryForm`, `existingDiaryEntries` drive full diary CRUD
- The CiProjectDiary component (mounted in Detail line 1254) wraps its own fetch + state + CRUD
- This means diary CUD is implemented in TWO separate places: edit page inline + CiProjectDiary component
- The component is passed `:can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"` â€” Detail page allows diary edits from the View page, which violates KE-G4 (View MUST NOT mount mutation controls)
- **AUTHORIZATION LEAKAGE: CiProjectDiary `:can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"` on Detail page allows diary write actions from the read-only view**

---

### 2.147-F â€” View vs Edit Governance Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

**F.1 â€” KE-F status: largely complete**
- `uploadDocFile()` line 312â€“313: stub comment "Removed in KE-F â€” upload only via Edit Project Details" âœ“
- `submitDriveLink()` line 316â€“318: stub âœ“
- Gallery tab (line 1376â€“1409): read-only image grid, no upload âœ“
- Documents tab (line 1411â€“1483): read-only file list with "Manage in Edit" link âœ“
- "Edit Project Details" button (line 484): gated by `canEditAnyTab` âœ“

**F.2 â€” Remaining KE-F violation: CiProjectDiary on Detail with `:can-edit`**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1254: `<CiProjectDiary :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />`
- `CiProjectDiary` with `can-edit = true` renders: Add Entry button, Edit/Delete controls per entry
- This means an authorized user on the **View/Detail** page can add, edit, and delete diary entries
- Violates KE-G4: "View page MUST NOT mount any upload, file dialog, or mutation control"
- Fix: Change to `:can-edit="false"` â€” diary writes belong in Edit page only

**F.3 â€” Residual inline edit links in Timeline tab**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1108â€“1112: Schedule card has a "Manage in Edit Project Details â†—" anchor â€” this is correct UX guidance (not an edit control), fine to keep

**F.4 â€” POW tab uses `CiPowItemTable :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject"`**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1368: `<CiPowItemTable :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />`
- `CiPowItemTable` with `can-edit` renders inline edit/delete controls for POW items
- **Same KE-G4 violation pattern as CiProjectDiary** â€” POW mutations from the read-only View page
- Fix: `:can-edit="false"` on Detail page; POW edits belong in Edit page POW tab

**F.5 â€” CiDocumentChecklist on Detail with `:can-edit`**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1414: `<CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canEditCurrentProject" />`
- Same pattern â€” checklist may render toggle/status controls if `can-edit` is true
- Fix: `:can-edit="false"` on Detail page

**Summary of KE-G4 violations remaining:**
| Component | Detail line | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `CiProjectDiary` | 1254 | `:can-edit="false"` |
| `CiPowItemTable` | 1368 | `:can-edit="false"` |
| `CiDocumentChecklist` | 1414 | `:can-edit="false"` |

---

### 2.147-G â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

**G.1 â€” Image category enum mismatch (Create vs Edit)**
- new.vue gallery upload (line 108): category options `PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `AFTER`, `OTHER`
- edit-[id].vue gallery upload (line 2096â€“2103): `IN_PROGRESS`, `BEFORE`, `COMPLETED`, `DOCUMENTATION`
- `GalleryCategory` enum likely has a canonical set; both pages use different subsets
- Risk: images uploaded from Create with category `PROGRESS` may not appear in Edit's category filter

**G.2 â€” Personnel metadata (category, role, dept, phone) absent from Create**
- new.vue personnel section has NO per-user metadata fields
- edit-[id].vue has full metadata repeater (lines 1744â€“1827)
- Projects created with no category assignments require Edit to assign categories post-creation
- This is the KE-A.2 gap: Create flow cannot assign personnel categories

**G.3 â€” Analytics chart `obligation` typo not yet fixed**
- `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` line 97: `f.obligations` â† already identified in D.2
- This is a two-character fix independent of any architecture work

**G.4 â€” KE-F violations on Detail page (F.2â€“F.5)**
- Three `can-edit` leakages remain; trivial to fix (`:can-edit="false"`)

**G.5 â€” `tabOrder` type cast in new.vue `handleSubmit`**
- new.vue `handleSubmit` (line 249): iterates `Object.keys(tabRequired.value)` expecting keys `basic, contract, schedule, progress, personnel, documents`
- After KG-A, `tabRequired.value` only has `basic, schedule, personnel, documents`
- The iteration still filters for `!tabRequired.value[k]` â€” but `contract` and `progress` keys were removed from `tabRequired`, so the loop no longer tries to navigate to them
- **Status: handled by KG-A â€” no regression** âœ“

**G.6 â€” Detail page Gallery tab `editSection('attachments')` â€” TAB_MAP gap**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1387: `editSection('attachments')` calls TAB_MAP lookup
- TAB_MAP (line 103â€“110) has: `overviewâ†’basic, timelineâ†’timeline, powâ†’pow, galleryâ†’documents, documentsâ†’documents, teamâ†’personnel`
- `'attachments'` key is NOT in TAB_MAP â€” fallback is `'basic'`
- Gallery "Manage" link navigates to Edit `basic` tab instead of `documents` tab
- Fix: change call to `editSection('gallery')` which maps to `'documents'` âœ“

---

### 2.147-H â€” Risks and Constraints

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R1 | Attachment tab shared `imageFile` ref causes wrong-category upload | HIGH | Split into `profileImageFile` + `galleryImageFile` separate refs |
| R2 | `f.obligations` typo silently shows zero obligations in analytics | HIGH | One-line fix: `f.obligation` |
| R3 | CiProjectDiary/CiPowItemTable/CiDocumentChecklist with `can-edit` on Detail allows mutations from read-only view | HIGH | Pass `:can-edit="false"` on Detail page |
| R4 | Gallery category mismatch Create vs Edit loses category context | MEDIUM | Unify to single enum; update new.vue to use same set as edit |
| R5 | All 8 overview panels open by default â€” poor executive UX | MEDIUM | Reduce default open panels to strategic subset |
| R6 | Personnel metadata not assignable during project creation | MEDIUM | Accept as architectural limitation; ensure Edit is reachable immediately post-create |
| R7 | `editSection('attachments')` navigates to wrong Edit tab | LOW | Change call to `editSection('gallery')` |
| R8 | Large gallery lists without lazy loading cause tab-switch latency | LOW | Add `v-lazy` wrapper or virtualize after data entry stabilizes |
| R9 | financialUtilization uses only first FY record | LOW | Aggregate across all records for accuracy |
| R10 | Edit page diary CRUD duplicates CiProjectDiary component logic | LOW | Long-term: consolidate via CiTimelineSection (KE-A.1 plan) |

## Section 2.148 â€” COI Overview Consolidation, Timeline Layout, and Post-KH Gap Analysis

**Phase:** KI Research (Phase 1)
**Date:** 2026-05-12
**Research Reference:** Operator directive â€” Research and plan Phase KI; continuation of KH ecosystem stabilization

### 2.148-A â€” Overview Tab: Post-KH Redundancy Analysis

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Component:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectHeroCard.vue`

**Current visual stack in Overview tab (top to bottom):**

| Layer | Component / Block | Lines |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `CiProjectHeroCard` | ~539 |
| 2 | Profile images strip (conditional, PROFILE gallery) | 542â€“575 |
| 3 | Status/Progress header card | 578â€“640 |
| 4 | KPI stat row (4 cards) | 643â€“687 |
| 5 | `v-expansion-panels` (9 panels, [3,5] open) | 691â€“1091 |

**CiProjectHeroCard contents:**
- Left col (md=4): project image placeholder
- Right col (md=8): title, status chip, subcategory chip, project code, contractor, funding source, start date, target completion, project manager, project engineer
- KPI strip (4 cards): Physical Progress %, Financial Utilization % (first FY), Contract Amount, Days Remaining
- Progress bars: Physical Progress bar + Financial Progress bar

**Status/Progress header card contents:**
- Status chip (identical to hero card status chip â†’ **DUPLICATE**)
- Publication chip (unique â€” DRAFT/PENDING_REVIEW/PUBLISHED/REJECTED + approval metadata)
- Contract Amount, right-aligned (identical to hero card KPI strip â†’ **DUPLICATE**)
- Implementing Agency (also present in Strategic Context panel â†’ semi-duplicate)
- Project Status Category (also present in Strategic Context panel â†’ semi-duplicate)

**KPI stat row contents:**
- Physical Progress % (identical to hero card KPI strip â†’ **DUPLICATE**)
- Financial Progress % (`project.financialProgress` â†’ **UNIQUE** â€” hero card shows Utilization, not Financial Progress %)
- Utilization Rate (aggregate obligation/appropriation â†’ same concept as hero card "Financial Utilization", though different calculation scope â†’ **OVERLAP**)
- Milestones completed/total (**UNIQUE** â€” not shown in hero card)

**Critical findings:**

**A.1 â€” Three-layer KPI redundancy**
- Physical Progress % appears in: hero card KPI strip + KPI stat row = 2 instances
- Contract Amount appears in: hero card KPI strip + status header card = 2 instances
- Status chip appears in: hero card right col + status header card = 2 instances
- "Utilization" concept appears in hero card ("Financial Utilization" from first FY record) + KPI row ("Utilization Rate" from aggregate) â€” different calculations, same field label ambiguity

**A.2 â€” Unique non-duplicated content per layer**

| Layer | Unique content |
|---|---|
| Hero card | Days Remaining, Progress bars, Contractor, Fund Source, Dates, PM, PE |
| Status header card | Publication status chip + approval metadata only |
| KPI stat row | Financial Progress % + Milestones count only |

**A.3 â€” Expansion panels: 9 panels with 2 open by default**
- `overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([3, 5])` â†’ Financial Summary (index 3) + Key Documents (index 5) open
- Financial Summary panel contains a full `v-data-table` â€” heavy initial render even when collapsed
- Content from closed panels (Project Information, Project Details, Contract Information) largely duplicates what the hero card already shows
- Strategic Context + Project Indicators are infrequently needed; always collapsed

**A.4 â€” Recommended consolidation strategy**
1. Remove Status/Progress header card (lines 578â€“640); relocate unique content only (publication status chip + approval metadata) to a compact chip strip below the hero card
2. Slim KPI stat row from 4 cards to 2 cards: Financial Progress % + Milestones count (remove Physical % and Utilization Rate â€” both covered by hero card)
3. Change `overviewPanels` default from `[3, 5]` to `[5]` only (Key Documents) â€” reduces initial data-table render load
4. DO NOT remove hero card or expansion panels â€” they are the correct structure

---

**KE-E implementation verified complete:**

- Hero card `financialUtilization` uses `props.project.financials?.[0]` (first FY record only)
- Analytics tab `financialChart` aggregates all FY records
- Minor inconsistency â€” `financialUtilization` in KPI card should aggregate, not use index 0
- `financialUtilization = computed(() => { const fins = ...; const totalApp = fins.reduce(...)...; return totalApp > 0 ? (totalObl / totalApp) * 100 : 0 })` â€” current code is actually correct (lines 43â€“49 of CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue)
- **Status: no action needed** â€” analytics tab is complete

### 2.148-C â€” Timeline Tab: Left-Column Density Issue

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` lines 1096â€“1254

**Current structure:**
```
<v-row>
  <v-col cols="12" md="6">       â† LEFT: 3 stacked cards
    Schedule card (start, target, actual, duration)
    Milestones card (full milestone list with progress bars)
    Work Log card (periodic work entries)
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="12" md="6">       â† RIGHT: 1 component
    <CiProjectDiary :can-edit="false" />
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

**C.1 â€” Height imbalance**
- Left column: 3 stacked cards; total height = Schedule (~100px) + Milestones (variable, typically 300â€“600px) + Work Log (variable) = very tall
- Right column: CiProjectDiary single component
- On desktop (md=6), both columns render at equal width but the left column is typically 2â€“3Ã— taller than the right diary component; creates a large empty right-side gap below the diary

**C.2 â€” Schedule card content is duplicated from hero card**
- Schedule card fields: Start Date, Target Completion, Actual Completion, Duration, Original Contract Duration
- Hero card fields (CiProjectHeroCard right col): Start Date, Target Completion
- The Schedule card duplicates two of the five hero card fields; the remaining three (Actual Completion, Duration, Original Contract Duration) are unique to the timeline tab
- Full removal would lose unique fields

**C.3 â€” Recommended fix**
- Move Schedule card to **full-width** above the `<v-row>` (mirroring edit page pattern: Progress Summary full-width â†’ Milestones + Diary 2-column)
- Left col result: Milestones card + Work Log card (still tall but matching the longer projects better)
- Right col result: CiProjectDiary (occupies full col height without empty gap)
- Schedule card at full-width above row reduces perceived height of left column and aligns with edit page layout precedent
