## Section 2.149 â€” COI Executive Overview Optimization, Timeline Governance, Analytics Restoration, and Document Architecture Refactor

**Phase:** KJ Research (Phase 1)
**Date:** 2026-05-12
**Research Reference:** Operator directive â€” Overview multi-column layout, gallery preview, analytics UX, timeline equal-height + dialogs, documents hierarchy

---

### 2.149-A â€” Overview Tab: Executive Layout Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** `<v-expansion-panels>` block, lines 664â€“1064 (9 panels)

**A.1 â€” Current expansion panel structure (post-KI)**

| Panel index | Title | Default state | Heavy render? |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Project Information | Closed | No |
| 1 | Project Details & Location | Closed | No |
| 2 | Contract Information | Closed | No |
| 3 | Financial Summary | Closed | Yes (v-data-table) |
| 4 | Approval History | Closed | No |
| 5 | Key Documents | **OPEN** (overviewPanels = [5]) | No |
| 6 | Strategic Context | Closed | No |
| 7 | Project Indicators | Closed | No |
| 8 | Signatories | Closed | No |

All 9 panels render inside a single `<v-expansion-panels multiple>` in a full-width column. On desktop (md â‰¥ 960px), the right half of the screen is entirely empty while panels occupy only the left side. This creates ~50% whitespace waste on every Overview load.

**A.2 â€” 2-Column Layout Architecture**

A `<v-row>` with two `<v-col cols="12" md="6">` containers â€” each containing its own `<v-expansion-panels>` â€” eliminates the whitespace gap by filling both halves of the viewport.

**Optimal grouping:**

| Left Column (md=6) | Panel | Content type |
|---|---|---|
| Project Information | 0 | Summary, description, scope, objectives, features, beneficiaries |
| Contract Information | 2 | Contractor, fund source, contract amount, dates, duration |
| Financial Summary | 3 | FY data table â€” totals row + v-data-table |
| Key Documents | 5 | Compliance document cards (default open) |

| Right Column (md=6) | Panel | Content type |
|---|---|---|
| Project Details & Location | 1 | Campus, building type, floor area, floors, coordinates |
| Approval History | 4 | Workflow timeline (create â†’ submit â†’ review) |
| Strategic Context | 6 | Strategic alignment, implementing agency, status category |
| Project Indicators | 7 | Output/outcome indicator chips |
| Signatories | 8 | Authorized signatories list |

Rationale: Left col groups project definition + financial info (most frequently reviewed). Right col groups operational/contextual info (less frequently opened).

**A.3 â€” Reactive ref split required**

Current: `overviewPanels = ref<number[]>([5])` â€” single array controlling all 9 panels.

Required split:
- `overviewPanelsLeft = ref<number[]>([3])` â€” Key Documents at left-array index 3 (open by default)
- `overviewPanelsRight = ref<number[]>([])` â€” none open by default on the right

Line 27 of detail-[id].vue needs to replace the single `overviewPanels` ref.

**A.4 â€” Gallery Preview Section**

Current state: `profileImages` (PROFILE category gallery items, max 3) already rendered in Overview at lines 542â€“575. The full `gallery` ref is already loaded on mount via `fetchGallery()`.

Gap: Non-PROFILE gallery images (construction photos, progress shots, MOV) are only accessible in the Gallery tab. There is no "recent gallery photos" preview on the Overview.

Required addition:
- `recentGalleryImages = computed(() => gallery.value.filter(g => g.category !== 'PROFILE').slice(0, 6))`
- Compact photo strip card below the profile images strip: 2-row grid (3 thumbs Ã— 2 rows max = 6), each thumb 1:1 aspect ratio
- Count badge showing `recentGalleryImages.length`
- "View All in Gallery" link â†’ `activeTab = 'gallery'`
- Show only when `recentGalleryImages.length > 0`
- No data fetch needed â€” `gallery` is already loaded

**A.5 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KJ-A1 | Two separate v-expansion-panels refs break `overviewPanels` usages elsewhere | LOW | Only used at line 27 (ref) and line 664 (v-model); isolated change |
| R-KJ-A2 | Key Documents at left-array index 3 (not global index 5) â€” must recalculate | LOW | Recount panel order within left col |
| R-KJ-A3 | Financial Summary v-data-table still lazy-renders on click in left col | ACCEPTABLE | Closed by default; user must open |
| R-KJ-A4 | Column height imbalance in 2-col panel layout when one col has many open panels | LOW | Use `align-start` on v-row; each col expands independently |

---

### 2.149-B â€” Data Analytics Tab: Rendering Assessment

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`

**B.1 â€” Confirmed: Analytics component IS implemented (KE-E complete)**

The `CiProjectAnalyticsTab` component contains:
- 4 KPI summary cards (Avg Physical Progress, Financial Utilization, Total POW Cost, Milestone Completion)
- Per-FY radialBar utilization gauges (conditional on `hasFinancialData`)
- 6 chart cards: POW Status donut, Avg Progress by Category bar, Financial Summary bar, Milestone Progress horizontal bar, Milestone Slippage histogram, Document Compliance donut
- Empty-state alert banner when `allEmpty` (no POW, no financials, no milestones, no checklist data)

**B.2 â€” Root cause of "no content" observation**

The component always renders the 4 KPI cards. Charts render conditionally per data type. When a project has no POW items, no financial entries, and no milestones:
- `allEmpty = true` â†’ shows info alert
- All 6 chart cards show "No [type] data" text
- 4 KPI cards show `0%` / `â€”`
- Gauges block is hidden (`v-if="hasFinancialData"`)

Result: 4 empty KPI cards + 6 placeholder cards = visually appears as "no useful content" even though the component IS rendering.

**B.3 â€” UX Gap: Cluttered empty state**

When `allEmpty = true`, the current flow shows:
1. Info alert ("Charts are empty because...") â†’ correct
2. 4 KPI cards with 0/â€” values â†’ adds confusion (are these broken?)
3. 6 chart cards each with "No [type] data" text â†’ repetitive empty messages

Improvement: When `allEmpty = true`, suppress all KPI cards and all chart cards. Replace with a single focused "Project has no analytics data yet" card + data-setup guide listing what needs to be added (POW items, financials, milestones, checklist).

**B.4 â€” Timeline/Personnel/Gallery analytics gaps**

The operator prompt also requests:
- Timeline Analytics (milestone completion, delays) â†’ `milestoneSlippageChart` ALREADY EXISTS (KE-E-3)
- Personnel Analytics â†’ NOT IMPLEMENTED â€” `project.assignedUsers` data is available in props; basic assignment counts are feasible
- Document Analytics â†’ `complianceDonutChart` ALREADY EXISTS (KE-E-4)
- Gallery Analytics â†’ NOT IMPLEMENTED â€” `gallery` data is not available in analytics component props

Finding: Personnel analytics and Gallery analytics are not implemented. They require either:
a) Passing additional props (`gallery`, `assignedUsers`) to the analytics component, OR
b) Fetching data independently inside the component

Personnel data: available via `project.assignedUsers` which is in `UIProjectDetail` passed as `:project` prop â€” accessible without new API call.
Gallery data: NOT in UIProjectDetail â€” would require a new API call or prop threading from detail-[id].vue.

**B.5 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KJ-B1 | Suppressing KPI cards when allEmpty removes visible 0 baselines | LOW | Show only the "no data" card; KPIs show 0 which misleads more than helps |
| R-KJ-B2 | Personnel analytics added while assignedUsers may be empty | LOW | Guard with `hasPersonnelData` computed |
| R-KJ-B3 | Gallery analytics require additional prop thread or API call | MEDIUM | Defer gallery analytics; out of scope for KJ |

---

### 2.149-C â€” Timeline Tab: Equal-Height Columns + Detail Dialog Architecture

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** `<v-window-item value="timeline">` â€” lines 1068â€“1272

**C.1 â€” Current state (post-KI polish)**

```
Full-width: Schedule card
<v-row>
  LEFT <v-col cols="12" md="6">:  Milestones card
  RIGHT <v-col cols="12" md="6">: Work Log card + CiProjectDiary
</v-row>
```

Left column: Milestones card â€” height determined by milestone count (variable, 0â€“âˆž)
Right column: Work Log card (variable) + CiProjectDiary (fixed internal height)

On desktop when milestone count is high (10+), left column is much taller than right. Both cols use standard `<v-col>` which stretches to fill the row height but the card content does not â€” creating a large empty gap below the diary in the right column.

**C.2 â€” Equal-height strategy**

Apply `class="d-flex flex-column"` to each `<v-col cols="12" md="6">` in the timeline row. Apply `style="flex: 1"` (or `class="flex-grow-1"`) to the **last card** in each column (the one that should expand). This makes both columns equal height by stretching the terminal card in the shorter column to fill the remaining space.

- LEFT col: `flex: 1` on Milestones card â†’ milestones expand to fill col height
- RIGHT col: `flex: 1` on CiProjectDiary wrapper â†’ diary fills remaining space after Work Log card

**C.3 â€” Full Detail Dialog â€” Milestones**

Trigger: "View All" button in Milestones card title, visible when `project.milestones?.length > 5`.

Dialog spec:
- `<v-dialog v-model="milestonesDialogOpen" max-width="860" scrollable>`
- Title: "All Milestones" + count chip
- Filter bar: status filter chips (ALL / COMPLETED / IN_PROGRESS / ON_HOLD / CANCELLED) + search text field (filters by title)
- Date range: `milestoneFilterFrom`, `milestoneFilterTo` date text inputs
- Content: same v-list pattern as the inline Milestones card
- Close button

State refs (script setup additions):
```typescript
const milestonesDialogOpen = ref(false)
const milestoneSearch = ref('')
const milestoneStatusFilter = ref('ALL')
const milestoneFilterFrom = ref('')
const milestoneFilterTo = ref('')
const filteredMilestones = computed(() => { ... })
```

**C.4 â€” Full Detail Dialog â€” Work Log**

Trigger: "View All" button in Work Log card title, visible when `workLogEntries.length > 5`.

Dialog spec:
- `<v-dialog v-model="workLogDialogOpen" max-width="860" scrollable>`
- Title: "All Work Log Entries" + count chip
- Filter bar: entry type filter chips (ALL / WORK_DAY / SUSPENSION / RESUMPTION / WEATHER_DELAY / OTHER) + search text field
- Period filter: quick-select chips (Last 7 Days / Last 30 Days / This Quarter / All Time)
- Content: same v-list pattern as the inline Work Log card
- Close button

State refs:
```typescript
const workLogDialogOpen = ref(false)
const workLogSearch = ref('')
const workLogTypeFilter = ref('ALL')
const workLogPeriodFilter = ref('ALL')
const filteredWorkLog = computed(() => { ... })
```

**C.5 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KJ-C1 | Dialog large DOM on open (100+ milestones) may stutter | LOW | `scrollable` prop + lazy render inside dialog |
| R-KJ-C2 | workLogEntries type filter must match actual enum values from backend | LOW | Use `getWorkLogEntryColor()` as reference for valid types |
| R-KJ-C3 | `flex: 1` on v-col children causes unexpected stretch on mobile | LOW | Only apply flex grow on `md+` breakpoint via class |

---

### 2.149-D â€” Documents Tab: Hierarchy Reorganization

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`
**Scope:** `<v-window-item value="documents">` â€” lines 1395â€“1467

**D.1 â€” Current order**

```
1. <CiDocumentChecklist :project-id :can-edit="false" />   â† compliance checklist accordion
2. <v-card>File Library (files + links)</v-card>           â† raw file attachments
```

**D.2 â€” Problem**

The compliance checklist (CiDocumentChecklist) is a governance tool used primarily by MIS/PMO staff to track document submission statuses. Regular users visiting the Documents tab primarily want to download actual files. Placing the checklist first forces all users to scroll past a complex accordion before reaching downloadable files.

**D.3 â€” Required order**

```
1. <v-card>File Library (files + links)</v-card>           â† PRIMARY: downloadable attachments
2. <CiDocumentChecklist :project-id :can-edit="false" />   â† SECONDARY: compliance checklist
```

No logic changes. Pure HTML block reorder. Zero regression risk.

**D.4 â€” Additional improvement**

The File Library card has a plain "Attachments" title. Upgrading to a more informative title with last-updated date would improve usability. This is a minor label enhancement, not a structural change.

---

### 2.149-E â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

**E.1 â€” Files modified in Phase KJ (projected)**

| File | Change type |
|---|---|
| `detail-[id].vue` | Primary target â€” all KJ-A, KJ-B context pass, KJ-C, KJ-D changes |
| `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` | KJ-B â€” empty-state UX + personnel analytics addition |

**E.2 â€” Files NOT modified**

| File | Status |
|---|---|
| `edit-[id].vue` | Not affected |
| `new.vue` | Not affected |
| `CiProjectHeroCard.vue` | Not affected |
| `CiDocumentChecklist.vue` | Not affected (only position in detail-[id].vue changes) |
| Backend (NestJS) | No new endpoints needed; all data already available via existing APIs |

**E.3 â€” Synchronization checks**

- `gallery` ref (line 224): loaded via `fetchGallery()` on mount â€” already available for KJ-A gallery preview; no double-fetch needed
- `workLogEntries` ref: already loaded via `useCoiWorkLog` composable or inline fetch; available for KJ-C dialog
- `project.assignedUsers`: populated in `UIProjectDetail` via adapter â€” available for KJ-B personnel analytics without new API call
- `overviewPanels` â†’ split to `overviewPanelsLeft` + `overviewPanelsRight`: no external component reads this ref; change is fully contained in detail-[id].vue

**E.4 â€” Regression risks**

| # | Risk | Phase KJ scope | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| RE-1 | `overviewPanels` ref rename breaks v-model binding | KJ-A | Replace both declaration (line 27) and usage (line 664) |
| RE-2 | 2-col v-expansion-panels loses panel index mapping for overviewPanels | KJ-A | Use separate `overviewPanelsLeft` and `overviewPanelsRight` refs |
| RE-3 | allEmpty guard in analytics suppresses KPI cards needed for context | KJ-B | Show the single empty-state card only; remove per-chart skeletons |
| RE-4 | Dialog filter state persists between openings | KJ-C | Reset filter refs when dialog is closed (`@update:modelValue="resetFilters"`) |
| RE-5 | CiDocumentChecklist order swap breaks no logic (pure template reorder) | KJ-D | Zero risk â€” verified no inter-component dependency |

| Priority | Task | Rationale |

| ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | **KJ-D: Documents tab reorder** | Zero-risk, 3-line change; immediately improves file discoverability for all users |
| ðŸŸ  HIGH | **KJ-A: Overview 2-column layout + gallery preview** | Eliminates 50% whitespace waste; highest executive UX impact |

## Section 2.150 â€” COI Executive Overview Panel Governance, Analytics Stabilization, Timeline Scalability, and Document Dual-Column Layout

**Phase:** KK Research (Phase 1)
**Files analyzed:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`
**Trigger:** Post-KJ implementation; operator prompt issued 2026-04-14

---

### 2.150-A â€” Overview Tab: Expansion Panel Default-Open Governance

**A.1 â€” Current state**

After Phase KJ-A, the Overview tab uses two independent `v-expansion-panels` instances:

```typescript
// detail-[id].vue lines 27â€“28
const overviewPanelsLeft  = ref<number[]>([])   // LEFT col â€” ALL closed by default
const overviewPanelsRight = ref<number[]>([0])  // RIGHT col â€” Key Documents open (index 0)
```

LEFT col panels (in order):
| Index | Panel |
|---|---|
| 0 | Project Information |
| 1 | Project Details & Location |
| 2 | Contract Information |
| 3 | Financial Summary |
| 4 | Approval History |

RIGHT col panels (in order):
| Index | Panel |
|---|---|
| 0 | Key Documents |
| 1 | Strategic Context |
| 2 | Project Indicators |
| 3 | Signatories |

**A.2 â€” Gap**

Operator requirement: **Project Information** (left, index 0) must also be open by default.
Currently `overviewPanelsLeft = ref([])` leaves it closed.
`overviewPanelsRight = ref([0])` is already correct (Key Documents open).

Fix: change `overviewPanelsLeft = ref<number[]>([])` â†’ `ref<number[]>([0])`.

**A.3 â€” Column balance after default-open change**

With both Project Information (left) and Key Documents (right) open, columns will be roughly balanced:
- Left: Project Info panel (compact) open + 4 panels collapsed
- Right: Key Documents panel (compact) open + 3 panels collapsed

Both opened panels are short (compact headers + minimal content) â†’ approximately equal column heights on initial load. Balance is acceptable.

**A.4 â€” Risk**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KK-A1 | Project Information has complex sub-fields that may make left col taller than right | LOW | Panel content is a compact read-only v-list; no issue in practice |
| R-KK-A2 | Change to ref([0]) is trivially reversible | NONE | 1-char change; fully contained in script setup |

---

### 2.150-B â€” Analytics Tab: Whitescreen Diagnosis and Stabilization

**B.1 â€” Package environment**

```
apexcharts:         v5.6.0
vue3-apexcharts:    v1.10.0
```

`VueApexCharts` is imported **locally** in `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` via:
```typescript
import VueApexCharts from 'vue3-apexcharts'
```

There is **no global Nuxt plugin** for ApexCharts (no `plugins/apexcharts.client.ts` exists). The `nuxt.config.ts` has no reference to `apexcharts`. This is the **primary structural issue**.

**B.2 â€” Root cause: SSR + no ClientOnly guard**

Nuxt 3 renders components server-side by default. `apexcharts` v5 accesses browser-only APIs (`window`, `document`, `SVGElement`) at module initialization time. When Nuxt tries to render `CiProjectAnalyticsTab` on the server (even transiently during hydration), it will throw or silently fail.

Additionally: `<v-window-item value="analytics">` has **no `eager` prop**. Vuetify `v-window-item` is lazy by default (content is conditionally mounted with `v-if`-equivalent logic). On first tab click, the analytics component mounts into a DOM element that is simultaneously undergoing a CSS slide/fade transition. ApexCharts `getBoundingClientRect()` at mount time may return `width: 0` or `height: 0` on a mid-transition container, causing charts to render as blank rectangles even when data is present.

**B.3 â€” Root cause: `allEmpty` during async loading**

`allEmpty = !hasPowData && !hasFinancialData && !hasMilestoneData && !hasChecklistData`

`hasPowData` depends on `useCoiPowItems(toRef(props, 'projectId'))` â€” async fetch. During the loading window: `hasPowData = false`, `hasFinancialData = false` (if no financials cached), `hasMilestoneData = false`, `hasChecklistData = false` â†’ `allEmpty = true` â†’ shows empty-state card while data loads.

When the POW fetch completes and the project has data, `allEmpty` flips to `false` and charts mount. But if ApexCharts dimension calculation already failed (due to the transition), charts remain blank. The user sees: empty-state card â†’ charts appear but are white boxes.

**B.4 â€” Chart series null-guard gap**

Several computed chart configs have patterns like:
```typescript
series: financialData.map(f => f.obligation ?? 0)
```

If `financialData` is a non-empty array but all values are `null`/`undefined`, the series becomes `[0, 0, 0]` â€” valid array, but renders a "flat" chart at 0 which can appear blank on a light background.

**B.5 â€” Recommended fix strategy**

Two-part fix:

**Fix 1 â€” ClientOnly wrapper in detail-[id].vue:**
```vue
<v-window-item value="analytics" eager>
  <ClientOnly>
    <CiProjectAnalyticsTab :project="project" :project-id="project.id" />
    <template #fallback>
      <v-skeleton-loader type="card, card" class="ma-4" />
    </template>
  </ClientOnly>
</v-window-item>
```
`eager` forces pre-mount before tab transition begins; `<ClientOnly>` ensures ApexCharts never attempts server-side rendering.

**Fix 2 â€” Series guard computed in CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue:**
Wrap each `<VueApexCharts>` with `v-if="chartSeries.length > 0 && chartSeries.some(s => s > 0)"` to suppress rendering when all values are zero.

**B.6 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KK-B1 | `eager` on analytics window-item triggers API calls on page load (before tab click) | LOW | Checklist/POW fetch is lightweight; acceptable tradeoff for rendering reliability |
| R-KK-B2 | ClientOnly wrapper introduces a skeleton flash on tab switch | LOW | Fallback skeleton provides better UX than blank white cards |
| R-KK-B3 | Series guards may suppress charts that have some-but-not-all zero values | LOW | Only suppress when ALL values are 0; mixed-value charts always render |
| R-KK-B4 | apexcharts v5 + vue3-apexcharts v1.10 may have minor API incompatibilities | LOW | Existing charts already render without errors in manual testing; no upgrade needed |

---

### 2.150-C â€” Timeline Tab: Inline List Slicing + Stat Cards

**C.1 â€” Current state after KJ-C**

```
Milestones card: v-for="milestone in project.milestones"  â†’ ALL milestones rendered inline
Work Log card:   v-for="e in workLogEntries"              â†’ ALL entries rendered inline
"View All" button: only shown when count > 5             â†’ correct trigger, incorrect behavior
```

**Gap:** "View All" button is shown when count > 5, but the inline list still renders ALL items. This defeats the purpose of the dialog â€” users see everything inline already.

**C.2 â€” Required inline slicing**

```vue
<!-- Milestones inline list: slice to first 5 -->
v-for="milestone in (project?.milestones ?? []).slice(0, 5)"

<!-- Work Log inline list: slice to first 5 -->
v-for="e in workLogEntries.slice(0, 5)"
```

"View All" button: show when count > 5 (already implemented â€” no change to trigger logic).

The dialog then provides the complete list with search + filter (already built in KJ-C).

**C.3 â€” Timeline stat cards**

Add 4 stat cards in a `<v-row>` immediately above the 2-col timeline row. These provide quick KPI visibility without opening any dialog.

Proposed stat cards:
| Card | Value | Computed |
|---|---|---|
| Total Milestones | `project?.milestones?.length ?? 0` | Simple count |
| Completed | count where `status === 'COMPLETED'` + % | Filter + format |
| In Progress | count where `status === 'IN_PROGRESS'` | Filter |
| Work Log Entries | `workLogEntries.length` | Simple count |

Stat card layout: `<v-col cols="6" md="3">` each (responsive 2-up on mobile, 4-up on desktop).

**C.4 â€” Script setup additions**

```typescript
const totalMilestones = computed(() => project.value?.milestones?.length ?? 0)
const completedMilestones = computed(() => project.value?.milestones?.filter(m => m.status === 'COMPLETED').length ?? 0)
const inProgressMilestones = computed(() => project.value?.milestones?.filter(m => m.status === 'IN_PROGRESS').length ?? 0)
const completionPct = computed(() => totalMilestones.value > 0
  ? Math.round((completedMilestones.value / totalMilestones.value) * 100)
  : 0
)
```

**C.5 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KK-C1 | Slicing inline list may confuse users who do not notice the "View All" button | LOW | Add "(showing first 5 of N)" subtitle to card when count > 5 |
| R-KK-C2 | Stat cards duplicate counts visible in dialogs | ACCEPTABLE | Stat cards are summary KPIs, not a replacement for dialogs |
| R-KK-C3 | Completed/In-Progress counts depend on backend milestone status enum matching | LOW | Existing `getMilestoneStatusColor()` references same enum values |

---

### 2.150-D â€” Documents Tab: Dual-Column Governance Layout

**D.1 â€” Current state after KJ-D**

```
Documents tab (post-KJ-D):
  1. File Library card (full width)           â† moved first by KJ-D
  2. <CiDocumentChecklist />  (full width)    â† compliance checklist accordion
```

KJ-D moved the File Library card to the top (sequential). The operator now requires a **side-by-side dual-column layout**.

**D.2 â€” Required layout**

```
Documents tab:
<v-row>
  LEFT col (md=7):   <CiDocumentChecklist />   (governance accordion groups)
  RIGHT col (md=5):  File Library card          (downloadable files + links)
</v-row>
```

Rationale:
- CiDocumentChecklist is visually complex (accordion sections, status chips, compliance indicators) â†’ wider col (7)
- File Library is a compact list of downloadable files â†’ narrower col (5)
- On mobile (cols="12" default): CiDocumentChecklist appears first â†’ governance-first on small screens

**D.3 â€” Column implementation**

No logic changes required. Only template restructuring:

```vue
<v-row>
  <v-col cols="12" md="7">
    <CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="project.id" :can-edit="canEditAnyTab" />
  </v-col>
  <v-col cols="12" md="5">
    <!-- existing File Library v-card block -->
  </v-col>
</v-row>
```

**D.4 â€” Responsive behavior**

| Breakpoint | Layout |
|---|---|
| xs / sm (mobile) | CiDocumentChecklist stacked above File Library (full width, sequential) |
| md+ (desktop/tablet) | Side-by-side, 7/5 split |

**D.5 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KK-D1 | CiDocumentChecklist has internal fixed-width elements that may overflow in 7-col | LOW | Component uses Vuetify fluid layout internally; should adapt |
| R-KK-D2 | File Library col (5) may feel narrow when many files exist | LOW | Card uses v-list with full-width items; scrollable if needed |
| R-KK-D3 | Mobile order reversal (checklist-first) may be unexpected for mobile users | LOW | Mobile users are not the primary target for governance checklist review |

---

## Section 2.151 â€” Phase KM Research: Executive Panel Optimization, Analytics Stabilization, Timeline Governance, Document Refactor & View/Edit Governance

**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Directive:** CSU CORE â€” COI Executive Panel Optimization, Timeline Governance, Analytics Stabilization, and Attachment System Refactor
**Scope:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`, `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue`

---

### 2.151-A â€” Overview Executive Panel Optimization

**A.1 â€” Current State**

`detail-[id].vue` line 27:
```js
const overviewPanelsLeft = ref<number[]>([])      // ALL left panels collapsed â† BUG
const overviewPanelsRight = ref<number[]>([0])    // Key Documents open âœ…
```

Left column panels (panel index â†’ name):

| Index | Panel Name |
|-------|-----------|
| 0 | Project Information |
| 1 | Location & Geography |
| 2 | Contract & Funding |
| 3 | Financial Summary |
| 4 | Approval Status |

Right column panels:

| Index | Panel Name |
|-------|-----------|
| 0 | Key Documents â† already default-open |
| 1 | Strategic Information |
| 2 | Key Performance Indicators |
| 3 | Signatories |

**A.2 â€” Requirement**

`overviewPanelsLeft` must default to `[0]` â†’ "Project Information" open.
Operator requirement: BOTH "Project Information" AND "Key Documents" default-open.
- Left: `ref([0])` â†’ Project Information â† 1-line change
- Right: `ref([0])` â†’ Key Documents âœ… (already correct)

**A.3 â€” Equal-Column Balance**

Vuetify `v-expansion-panels` cannot enforce programmatic equal-height across separate `v-col` elements. The most important requirement (default-open behavior) is the 1-line fix. Column height will naturally balance once Project Information (left) and Key Documents (right) are simultaneously open.

**Recommendation:** Accept natural height difference. No CSS engineering needed.

**A.4 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| R-KM-A1 | Opening Project Information may create taller left column when many fields are populated | LOW |
| R-KM-A2 | No reactive behavior change â€” `ref([0])` is purely a static initial value change | NONE |

---

### 2.151-B â€” Analytics Whitescreen Stabilization

**B.1 â€” Confirmed Root Cause**

File: `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` line 7:
```js
import VueApexCharts from 'vue3-apexcharts'
```

No `plugins/apexcharts.client.ts` exists. `VueApexCharts` is imported locally â€” no Nuxt SSR protection.

File: `detail-[id].vue` analytics window-item:
```vue
<v-window-item value="analytics">
  <CiProjectAnalyticsTab v-if="project" :project-id="projectId" :project="project" />
</v-window-item>
```

Missing: `eager` prop on `<v-window-item>`.

**Root Cause Chain:**
1. `v-window-item` without `eager` â†’ lazy-mounted during CSS tab-switch transition
2. ApexCharts calls `getBoundingClientRect()` on mount
3. During CSS transition â†’ element has `width: 0`
4. ApexCharts renders with zero-width â†’ blank/invisible chart canvases
5. No resize trigger fires after transition completes (charts remain blank)

**B.2 â€” Fix Architecture**

| Action | File | Change |
|---|---|---|
| Add `eager` | `detail-[id].vue` | `<v-window-item value="analytics" eager>` |
| Add `<ClientOnly>` | `detail-[id].vue` | Wrap `<CiProjectAnalyticsTab>` in `<ClientOnly>` |
| Fix allEmpty transient | `CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` | Add `!powLoading.value &&` guard |
| Fix emit gap | `detail-[id].vue` | Add `@go-to-edit="editSection('pow')"` to component |

**B.3 â€” Secondary: allEmpty False-Positive During POW Loading**

During POW data loading:
```js
const hasPowData = computed(() => powItems.value.length > 0)
// powLoading=true â†’ powItems=[] â†’ hasPowData=false
```
If financials/milestones are also absent â†’ `allEmpty=true` â†’ empty banner flashes briefly. Fix: gate `allEmpty` on `!powLoading.value`.

**B.4 â€” Analytics Emit Gap**

`CiProjectAnalyticsTab.vue` line 284:
```vue
<v-btn @click="$emit('go-to-edit')">Add Data via Edit Project</v-btn>
```
`detail-[id].vue` has NO `@go-to-edit` listener on `<CiProjectAnalyticsTab>` â†’ button is non-functional. Fix: `@go-to-edit="editSection('pow')"`.

---

### 2.151-C â€” Timeline Enterprise Refactor

**C.1 â€” Current Inline Rendering**

Confirmed via code reading:
- Milestones: `v-for="(milestone, idx) in project.milestones"` â€” ALL items rendered inline
- Work Log: `v-for="e in workLogEntries"` â€” ALL entries rendered inline

With 50+ milestones or 100+ work log entries â†’ long DOM, poor UX.

**C.2 â€” KJ-C Dialogs Status (Confirmed Implemented)**

- `milestonesDialogOpen` + dialog at ~lines 1558â€“1599 with search + status filter âœ…
- `workLogDialogOpen` + dialog at ~lines 1601â€“1645 with search + type filter âœ…
- "View All" buttons present (shown when > 5 items) âœ…
- Filter selects: milestone status (ALL/NOT_STARTED/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/DELAYED) + worklog type (ALL/DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY) âœ…

**C.3 â€” Stat Cards Architecture (Zero New API Calls)**

Computed from existing `project.milestones` array:

| Stat Card | Formula | Color |
|---|---|---|
| Total Milestones | `project.milestones?.length ?? 0` | primary |
| Completed | `filter(m.status === 'COMPLETED').length` | success |
| In Progress | `filter(m.status === 'IN_PROGRESS').length` | info |
| Delayed | `filter(m.status === 'DELAYED').length` | error |

**C.4 â€” Slice Architecture**

Main tab: show first 5 items only. Existing "View All" dialogs show all items.

```vue
<!-- Before (shows ALL) -->
<v-list-item v-for="(milestone, idx) in project.milestones" ...>
<!-- After (shows max 5) -->
<v-list-item v-for="(milestone, idx) in (project.milestones || []).slice(0, 5)" ...>
```

Same pattern for work log: `workLogEntries.slice(0, 5)`.

**C.5 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KM-C1 | Slice may hide most recent entries if list is unsorted | LOW | Verify sort order before slicing |
| R-KM-C2 | Stat cards add template lines but zero API calls | NONE | Pure computed â€” no async |

---

### 2.151-D â€” Documents Tab Dual-Column Governance

**D.1 â€” Current State**

Documents window-item layout:
```
[Full-width] Attachments card (FILES section + LINKS section â€” flat list)
[Full-width] CiDocumentChecklist component
```

**D.2 â€” editSection Semantic Issue**

`detail-[id].vue` line ~1489 (Documents tab header):
```vue
<a v-if="canEditAnyTab" @click="editSection('gallery')">Manage in Edit Project Details â†—</a>
```

Calls `editSection('gallery')` inside the **Documents** tab. The `TAB_MAP` resolves both `gallery` and `documents` to edit tab `'documents'`, so this is **functionally correct** but **semantically misleading**. Fix: use `editSection('documents')` for clarity.

**D.3 â€” Dual-Column Architecture (per Operator Requirement)**

Operator: LEFT = grouped governance sections, RIGHT = file attachments.

| Column | Content | Width |
|---|---|---|
| LEFT (md=7) | `CiDocumentChecklist` â€” CPES governance checklist | 7/12 |
| RIGHT (md=5) | File Library â€” files grouped by `documentType` in accordion | 5/12 |

**D.4 â€” File Type Grouping**

`doc.documentType` field exists (used at line 1518 in chip display). Grouping strategy:
```js
const documentsByType = computed(() => {
  const grouped = new Map<string, typeof documentFiles.value>()
  for (const doc of documentFiles.value) {
    const type = doc.documentType || 'Other'
    if (!grouped.has(type)) grouped.set(type, [])
    grouped.get(type)!.push(doc)
  }
  return grouped
})
```
Render as `v-expansion-panels` accordion in the right column. `documentLinks` array preserved below.

**D.5 â€” Responsive Behavior**

| Breakpoint | Layout |
|---|---|
| xs/sm (mobile) | CiDocumentChecklist stacked above File Library (full-width, sequential) |
| md+ (desktop) | Side-by-side 7/5 split |

**D.6 â€” Risks**

| # | Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| R-KM-D1 | CiDocumentChecklist may have internal fixed-width in 7-col | LOW | Component uses fluid Vuetify layout; should adapt |
| R-KM-D2 | Files with empty documentType silently fall under 'Other' | NONE | Explicit default label |

---

### 2.151-E â€” View/Edit Governance Enforcement

**E.1 â€” Current State Audit**

| Tab | Exposed Control | Guard | Status |
|---|---|---|---|
| POW | `CiPowItemTable` | `:can-edit="false"` | âœ… READ-ONLY |
| Gallery | Navigation link only | `v-if="canEditAnyTab"` | âœ… Nav-only |
| Team | Navigation link only | `v-if="canEditAnyTab"` | âœ… Nav-only |
| Documents | Navigation link (wrong semantic target) | `v-if="canEditAnyTab"` | âœ… Nav-only (fix target) |
| Timeline | Navigation link only | `v-if="canEditCurrentProject"` | âœ… Nav-only |
| Overview | Navigation links only | `v-if="canEditCurrentProject"` | âœ… Nav-only |
| Analytics | Empty-state nav button | No guard | â„¹ï¸ Nav-only, non-critical |

**E.2 â€” Finding: Detail Page Is Already Preview-Only**

The detail page has no inline CRUD controls. The operator's concern about "POW tab exposes edit/delete" is resolved â€” current code already passes `:can-edit="false"` to `CiPowItemTable`.

**E.3 â€” Guard Consistency Issue**

Gallery and Team navigation links use `canEditAnyTab` (module-level permission). Timeline and Overview use `canEditCurrentProject` (entity-level permission). For consistency, ALL navigation links should use `canEditCurrentProject`.

**E.4 â€” Authorization Summary**

| Guard | Meaning | Correct Use |
|---|---|---|
| `canEditCurrentProject` | Entity-level: user can edit THIS project | Navigation links in detail page |
| `canEditAnyTab` | Module-level: user can edit ANY COI project | Not appropriate for entity-specific nav links |

---

### 2.151-F â€” Attachment/Document Enterprise Refactor

**F.1 â€” No Separate Attachments Tab (Confirmed)**

The detail page tab list: `overview, timeline, pow, analytics, gallery, documents, team`. No separate `attachments` tab. The `documents` tab is the attachment display surface â€” this aligns with JU-D10 (Attachments split into Gallery + Documents, COMPLETE).

**F.2 â€” Navigation Freeze Root Cause**

JT-A fix (`router.back()` â†’ `router.push()`) was completed and verified (JT-V1: âœ…). No current navigation freeze exists in `detail-[id].vue`. If freeze is reported, it may be in `edit-[id].vue` upload workflows and requires separate investigation when reproduced.

**F.3 â€” Document Categorization**

The operator requires categorized document display by type (Project Profile, Feasibility Study, POW, Certificates, Plans, Reports, Compliance, Other). The `documentType` field on the documents API response enables this grouping. See KM-D plan step for implementation.

**F.4 â€” Scope Boundary**

KM scope: Documents tab display grouping (detail page only). Categorized upload workflow in `edit-[id].vue` is deferred per YAGNI.

---

## Section 2.152 â€” Phase KN: Timeline Modal Governance, Documents Window Architecture, Attachment Synchronization

**Phase:** KN (follows KM âœ…)
**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Scope:** Timeline individual-entry modals + date filter, Documents per-file modal + overflow window, Attachment sync field-bug fixes, cross-flow continuity

---

### 2.152-A â€” Timeline Modal Architecture Assessment

#### A1. KJ-C Collection Dialogs â€” Current State

Two full-collection dialogs exist in `detail-[id].vue` (lines 1628â€“1716), implemented in Phase KJ-C:

| Dialog | Trigger Ref | Search Ref | Filter Ref | Filter Values |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| All Milestones | `milestonesDialogOpen` | `milestoneSearch` | `milestoneStatusFilter` | ALL / NOT_STARTED / IN_PROGRESS / COMPLETED / DELAYED |
| All Work Logs | `workLogDialogOpen` | `workLogSearch` | `workLogTypeFilter` | ALL / DAILY / WEEKLY / MONTHLY / QUARTERLY |

Both dialogs iterate unsliced lists via `filteredMilestones` / `filteredWorkLog` computeds. Both are `max-width="800" scrollable`.

#### A2. Individual Entry Detail Modal â€” ABSENT

No `selectedMilestone` ref exists. No `milestoneDetailDialogOpen` ref exists. Clicking a list item in either collection dialog does nothing â€” no click handler is bound. The full-detail panel for individual entries is completely absent.

**Missing from milestone dialog display:**
- `startDate`, `actualDate`, `category`, `remarks` â€” none shown
- `m.dueDate` used at line 1659 instead of `m.targetDate` â€” **field inconsistency bug** (inline list uses `m.targetDate`)

**Work log dialog** is relatively complete: shows `entryDate`, `periodLabel`, `description`, `weather`, `manpowerCount`. No critical fields missing.

#### A3. Date-Range Filter â€” ABSENT

The `workLogTypeFilter` (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY) filters by `entryType` (period classification), NOT by actual date range. No date-range input exists for either dialog. No `milestoneDate` range filter exists at all.

The `WorkLogEntry` interface has `entryDate: string` â€” available for filtering.
Milestone fields include `targetDate` â€” available for date-range filtering.

#### A4. Nested Modal Strategy

Vuetify `v-dialog` supports nesting. The recommended approach:
- Collection dialog (existing) stays at z-index base
- Detail dialog (new) is a second `v-dialog` rendered in the same root component scope, not nested inside the collection dialog markup
- `persistent=false` on the detail dialog so clicking overlay returns focus to collection
- State: `selectedMilestone ref<any>(null)` drives the detail modal; cleared on close
- No router navigation â€” all in-page dialog transitions

#### A5. Timeline Inline Date Filter Approach

For the Timeline tab itself (stat cards + inline lists), no date filter exists or is needed at that level â€” the inline lists are already sliced to 5 items. Filters belong inside the collection dialogs.

**Recommended filter row layout (inside collection dialogs):**
- Row 1: search field (md=7) + status/type filter (md=5) â€” existing
- Row 2: date-from picker (md=5) + date-to picker (md=5) + clear button (md=2) â€” new

This is a two-row filter layout inside each dialog, preserving existing filter behavior.

---

### 2.152-B â€” Documents Window Architecture Assessment

#### B1. Current Documents Tab State (post-KM-D)

The KM-D dual-column layout:
- Left col md=7: `CiDocumentChecklist` (static governance checklist)
- Right col md=5: `v-expansion-panels variant="accordion"` grouped by `documentsByType` (Map keyed by `doc.documentType || 'Other'`)

Each accordion section iterates `documentsByType.get(type)` entries and renders a list item per file with: `fileName` (link via `doc.filePath`), formatted fileSize, description. No upload date displayed. No per-file modal.

#### B2. Per-File Detail Modal â€” ABSENT

No `selectedDoc` ref. No `docDetailDialogOpen` ref. Each file is an `<a>` tag direct download link. No intermediate metadata view.

`DocumentItem` interface provides: `id`, `documentType`, `fileName`, `filePath`, `fileSize`, `mimeType`, `description`, `category`, `createdAt` â€” all sufficient for a detail modal. `formatFileSize` utility already exists.

#### B3. Overflow Window â€” ABSENT

No threshold check exists. If a project has 30+ documents, all render inline in the accordion without pagination. No "View All" trigger exists for large document counts.

**Recommended threshold:** Show "View All Documents" trigger when `documentFiles.length > 10`. The overflow window is a full-screen-width dialog (max-width="1200") with a searchable, sortable `v-list` or `v-data-table`.

#### B4. Document Date Filter

`DocumentItem.createdAt` is available for date sorting. `DocumentItem.documentType` is available for category filtering. Both are suitable for an overflow-window filter bar.

#### B5. Overflow Window Filter Architecture

| Filter | Field | Implementation |
|---|---|---|
| Category | `documentType` | v-select |
| Upload date (newest/oldest) | `createdAt` | v-select (sort direction) |
| Search | `fileName`, `description` | v-text-field |

Filters apply only inside the overflow window. Accordion grouping remains unchanged for normal (<= 10 file) views.

---

### 2.152-C â€” Attachment Synchronization Analysis

#### C1. Data Source Topology â€” Confirmed Single Source

All document consumers in `detail-[id].vue` read from `documents.value`:

| Consumer | Reads From | Status |
|---|---|---|
| Overview Key Documents panel | `findDocumentPreview(documents, ...)` | âœ… same source |
| Documents tab accordion | `documentsByType` computed â†’ `documentFiles.value` â†’ `documents.value` | âœ… same source |
| `CiDocumentChecklist` (left col) | receives `:documents="documents"` prop | âœ… same source |
| Edit page upload | writes to same `/api/.../documents` endpoint | âœ… synchronized on next fetch |

Data flow: `fetchDocuments()` â†’ `documents.value` â†’ `documentFiles` (computed) â†’ `documentsByType` (computed). Single source-of-truth is **intact**.

#### C2. BUG â€” findDocumentPreview uses `doc.originalName` (NOT in interface)

**Location:** `detail-[id].vue` line 297

```js
const haystack = `${doc.documentType || ''} ${doc.originalName || ''}`.toLowerCase()
```

`DocumentItem` interface (lines 256â€“266) declares `fileName`, NOT `originalName`. The backend API response may or may not include `originalName` as an extra field. If it does not, the match haystack is only `documentType` â€” causing the Overview Key Documents panel to miss matches where the document name (not type) contains the keyword.

**Impact:** Overview "Key Documents" panel may silently fail to surface documents unless the user typed the keyword in the `documentType` field during upload. This is a fragmentation bug â€” the interface and the match logic are out of sync.

**Fix:** Replace `doc.originalName` with `doc.fileName` in the haystack (line 297).

#### C3. BUG â€” Overview Key Documents uses `doc.fileUrl` / `doc.linkUrl` (NOT in interface)

**Location:** `detail-[id].vue` lines 1028â€“1033

```html
v-if="findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.fileUrl || findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.linkUrl"
:href="findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.fileUrl || findDocumentPreview(documents, docType.key)?.linkUrl || undefined"
```

`DocumentItem` declares `filePath`, not `fileUrl` or `linkUrl`. These field names are not in the interface. Unless the backend returns these as bonus fields, download links in the Overview Key Documents panel will always be `undefined` and the download chip will not render.

**Impact:** Overview Key Documents panel shows document names (if `originalName` bug is fixed) but the download link does not work. Critical usability regression.

**Fix:** Replace `doc.fileUrl || doc.linkUrl` with `doc.filePath` at both occurrences (lines 1028 and 1033).

#### C4. Gallery vs Documents â€” Separation Confirmed

Gallery (`/gallery` endpoint) and documents (`/documents` endpoint) are separate data stores. Gallery images do not appear in the Documents tab. This separation is correct and intentional. No synchronization issue here.

---

### 2.152-D â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

| Gap | Location | Severity | Phase KN Target |
|---|---|---|---|
| `m.dueDate` instead of `m.targetDate` in milestone dialog | `detail-[id].vue:1659` | ðŸŸ  HIGH â€” date shows blank if backend uses `targetDate` | KN-A |
| No individual-entry detail modal for milestones | Timeline collection dialog | ðŸŸ  HIGH â€” requested feature absent | KN-A |
| No individual-entry detail modal for work logs | Timeline collection dialog | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KN-B |
| No date-range filter in milestone/worklog dialogs | Both collection dialogs | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KN-C |
| `doc.originalName` instead of `doc.fileName` in find | `detail-[id].vue:297` | ðŸ”´ CRITICAL â€” match logic broken | KN-E |
| `doc.fileUrl`/`doc.linkUrl` instead of `doc.filePath` | `detail-[id].vue:1028â€“1033` | ðŸ”´ CRITICAL â€” download links broken | KN-E |
| No per-file attachment detail modal | Documents tab right col | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KN-D |
| No overflow window for >10 documents | Documents tab | ðŸŸ¢ LOW | KN-F |

**Regression risks:**
- Fixing `originalName` â†’ `fileName` in `findDocumentPreview` does NOT break anything else (other consumers use `doc.fileName` directly)
- Fixing `fileUrl` â†’ `filePath` in the download link is safe; `filePath` is the confirmed declared field
- Adding individual-entry modals is additive â€” no existing behavior removed
- Date-range filter is additive inside existing filter rows â€” `filteredMilestones`/`filteredWorkLog` computeds extended, not replaced

---

### 2.152-E â€” Priority and Execution Order

| Priority | Step | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | KN-E: Fix Overview Key Documents field bugs | Two active bugs silently break document discovery and download in the Overview panel |
| ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | KN-A: Milestone individual entry modal + `dueDate` fix | `dueDate` bug may show blank dates; individual entry modal is highest usability gap |
| ðŸŸ  HIGH | KN-B: Work log individual entry modal | Completes the nested-modal workflow for Timeline |
| ðŸŸ  HIGH | KN-C: Date-range filter for both collection dialogs | Extends existing filter capability; small scope |
| ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KN-D: Per-file attachment detail modal in Documents tab | Adds metadata visibility; additive feature |
| ðŸŸ¢ LOW | KN-F: Documents overflow window for >10 files | Scalability improvement; rare trigger for most projects |

**Execution order: KN-E â†’ KN-A â†’ KN-B â†’ KN-C â†’ KN-D â†’ KN-F**

## Section 2.153 â€” Phase KO: Attachment Synchronization, Document Governance Alignment, Timeline MOV Architecture

**Phase:** KO (follows KN â¬œ)
**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Scope:** Attachment tab governance refactor, document type taxonomy synchronization with KB-E reference table, upload form enhancement, Timeline MOV link architecture, cross-flow continuity

---

### 2.153-A â€” Attachment Tab: Current State Analysis

#### A1. Edit Page Document Upload Form â€” Current Fields

The `documents` tab in `edit-[id].vue` presents a minimal upload form with 3 fields:

| Field | Type | Values |
|---|---|---|
| File | v-file-input | PDF/DOCX/XLSX/etc. â‰¤20MB |
| Type | v-select | `attachment`, `mov`, `specification`, `contract`, `other` |
| Description | v-text-field | freetext |

**Critical gaps vs `UploadDocumentDto` capabilities:**
- `category` field available in DTO â€” **not exposed in form**
- `title` field available in DTO â€” **not exposed in form**
- `lifecycleStatus` field available in DTO â€” **not exposed in form**
- External link fields (`linkUrl`, `linkTitle`, `linkDescription`) â€” implemented in separate `submitEditLink()` function but rendered in a second card; architecture is separated from file uploads rather than unified

#### A2. `KB-E` Document Type Taxonomy â€” NOT Used in Upload Form

The `construction_document_types` reference table (KB-E, `ConstructionDocumentType` entity) contains:
- 27 seeded standard types across 6 groups
- Fields: `groupCode`, `groupLabel`, `typeCode`, `typeLabel`, `isRequired`, `sortOrder`, `isActive`
- Admin-extensible via `/api/construction-projects/document-types` endpoint

**The edit form ignores this table entirely.** The upload form's type selector uses 5 hardcoded freetext values (`attachment, mov, specification, contract, other`) rather than the structured KB-E taxonomy.

**Impact:** Documents uploaded via the edit form are not categorized using the governed taxonomy. The `CiDocumentChecklist` (KB-E) reads from `construction_document_checklist` which links checklist items to `linkedDocumentId` â€” but since uploads don't use taxonomy `typeCode` values, automatic checklist satisfaction is not possible.

#### A3. EditDocItem Interface â€” Missing Fields

`EditDocItem` in `edit-[id].vue` (line 445â€“452):
```ts
{ id, documentType, fileName, filePath, fileSize, mimeType, description }
```

Missing fields available from `listProjectDocuments` (full `Document` entity):
- `category` â€” secondary category
- `createdAt` â€” upload timestamp
- `version` â€” document version number
- `lifecycleStatus` â€” ACTIVE/ARCHIVED/DRAFT
- `uploadedBy` â€” uploader UUID

**Impact:** Upload date, version, and lifecycle status are invisible to editors. When reviewing existing documents, context is lost.

#### A4. DocumentItem Interface (Detail Page) â€” Missing Fields

`DocumentItem` in `detail-[id].vue` (line 256â€“266):
```ts
{ id, documentType, fileName, filePath, fileSize, mimeType, description, category, createdAt }
```

Missing fields available from API:
- `version` â€” could show version number in detail modal (KN-D)
- `lifecycleStatus` â€” could differentiate ACTIVE vs ARCHIVED vs DRAFT
- `uploadedBy` â€” could show who uploaded the document

#### A5. `docsByCategory` vs `documentsByType` â€” Grouping Key Mismatch

| Component | Key used | Fallback |
|---|---|---|
| Edit page `docsByCategory` | `doc.documentType \|\| 'other'` | lowercase `'other'` |
| Detail page `documentsByType` | `doc.documentType \|\| 'Other'` | capitalized `'Other'` |

Minor inconsistency. Neither uses KB-E taxonomy labels (`groupLabel`, `typeLabel`) â€” both show raw `documentType` codes as headers.

#### A6. Required Document Sections â€” Inventory Analysis

Based on KB-E entity comment ("27 standard types across 6 groups") and domain knowledge of CPES/infrastructure projects, the following represents the expected document taxonomy:

**GROUP 1 â€” Contract Foundation**
- Project Profile / Feasibility Study
- Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC)
- Bill of Quantities (BOQ)
- Contract Agreement
- Notice to Proceed (NTP)

**GROUP 2 â€” Contractor Qualifications**
- Contractor's License / PCAB
- Business Permits
- Performance Bond
- Payment Bond

**GROUP 3 â€” Technical/Engineering Documents**
- Program of Works (POW)
- Quality Control Plan (QCP)
- CPM Schedule
- As-Built Plans
- Technical Specifications

**GROUP 4 â€” Accomplishment Reports**
- Monthly Accomplishment Report
- Quarterly Progress Report
- Final Report
- Inspection Report

**GROUP 5 â€” Change Orders & Time Management**
- Change Order
- Variation Order
- Time Suspension Order
- Time Extension Order

**GROUP 6 â€” Completion & Compliance**
- Certificate of Completion
- Certificate of Acceptance
- COA Requirements
- Agency-specific compliance documents
- User-defined documents

**Conclusion:** The KB-E reference table likely mirrors this 6-group, 27-type taxonomy. The upload form should expose this taxonomy via a 2-level selector: Group â†’ Type.

---

### 2.153-B â€” Timeline MOV Architecture Analysis

#### B1. Current MOV State

**What exists:**
- Document type `mov` available in the edit form (line 1963: `{ title: 'MOV', value: 'mov' }`)
- Operation Indicators (university-operations module) have an `mov` column (migration 037) â€” unrelated to COI
- No dedicated MOV table for construction milestones or work logs
- No `milestoneId` or `timelineEntryId` foreign key on the `documents` table
- MOV documents uploaded as `documentType: 'mov'` are project-level only â€” not linked to specific milestones

**What is missing:**
- A structured MOV evidence record linked to a specific `milestoneId` or `timelineEntryId`
- MOV links (preferred: links, not files, per directive)
- Fields: `movLink`, `movTitle`, `movDescription`, `entryDate`, `evidenceCategory`, `relatedEntityId`, `relatedEntityType`, `uploadedBy`, `verificationStatus`, `remarks`

#### B2. MOV Architecture Options

**Option 1 â€” New `construction_mov_entries` table (RECOMMENDED)**
- New entity with: `id, projectId, relatedEntityId, relatedEntityType (MILESTONE|TIMELINE_ENTRY), movLink (URL), movTitle, movDescription, evidenceCategory, entryDate, uploadedBy, verificationStatus (PENDING|VERIFIED|REJECTED), remarks, createdAt`
- Backend: CRUD endpoints `/api/construction-projects/:id/milestones/:milestoneId/mov` and `/api/construction-projects/:id/timeline-entries/:entryId/mov`
- Frontend: MOV section inside milestone detail modal (KN-A) and work log detail modal (KN-B)
- New migration: `048_create_construction_mov_entries.sql`

**Option 2 â€” Reuse existing documents table with new columns**
- Add `milestone_id` and `timeline_entry_id` nullable FK columns to `documents`
- Pro: reuses existing upload/document infrastructure
- Con: conflates MOV links (URL-only, per directive) with file uploads; breaks current `documentableId` pattern
- **NOT RECOMMENDED** â€” violates SOLID (single responsibility) and directive's link-only requirement

**Option 3 â€” Store MOV links inside milestone/entry `remarks` or `metadata` as JSON**
- Pro: zero schema change
- Con: unqueryable, unstructured, not auditable, no individual CRUD
- **NOT RECOMMENDED** â€” violates governance requirements

**Decision: Option 1 â€” New `construction_mov_entries` table**

#### B3. MOV Evidence Category Taxonomy

| Category Code | Label |
|---|---|
| `photo_documentation` | Photo Documentation |
| `inspection_report` | Inspection Report |
| `completion_certificate` | Completion Certificate |
| `site_visit` | Site Visit Record |
| `progress_report` | Progress Report |
| `test_result` | Test/Quality Result |
| `meeting_minutes` | Meeting Minutes |
| `official_document` | Official Document |
| `other` | Other |

#### B4. MOV Link Validation Strategy

- Frontend: validate URL format (`https://` required) â€” same as `EDIT_EXTERNAL_URL_REGEX` pattern
- Accept: Google Drive, SharePoint, OneDrive, any `https://` URL
- No file upload for MOV â€” link-only (per directive, to avoid storage bloat)
- `verificationStatus`: `PENDING` on create; `VERIFIED`/`REJECTED` by authorized role (Admin)

#### B5. Date/Timestamp Governance

Each MOV entry carries:
- `entryDate` â€” the date the evidence was generated (user-provided)
- `createdAt` â€” the date the MOV entry was recorded in the system (auto-timestamp)
- `updatedAt` â€” last modification timestamp (auto-timestamp)

Timeline entries already have `entryDate`. Milestones have `targetDate`, `actualDate`. MOV `entryDate` adds a third temporal dimension: the evidence capture date.

#### B6. `ConstructionTimelineEntry` Fields â€” What Is Available

Entity has: `id, projectId, entryType, entryDate, periodLabel, title, description, weather, manpowerCount, equipmentUsed, workAccomplished, issuesEncountered, photosCount, createdBy, createdAt, updatedAt`

**`photosCount`** â€” exists in entity but is never populated (always 0). Once MOV entries are linked, this could auto-update to count verified photo MOV links.

Detail page `WorkLogEntry` interface maps: `id, entryType, entryDate, periodLabel, title, description, weather, manpowerCount` â€” notably missing `equipmentUsed`, `workAccomplished`, `issuesEncountered` which are in the entity. These fields should be added to the interface and displayed in the KN-B detail modal.

---

### 2.153-C â€” Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

#### C1. Synchronization Map

| Data Source | Detail Page | Edit Page | Create Form | Checklist (KB-E) |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `/documents` API | `documents.value` âœ… | `existingDocs.value` âœ… | N/A (no upload on create) | `linkedDocumentId` âœ… via KB-E |
| `/gallery` API | `galleryImages.value` | `existingGallery.value` | N/A | N/A |
| `/milestones` API | `project.milestones` | `existingMilestones.value` | N/A | N/A |
| `/timeline-entries` API | `workLogEntries.value` | `existingDiaryEntries.value` | N/A | N/A |

**Create Project:** No document upload on create â€” documents are added post-creation via Edit Project. This is by design (immediate upload requires existing `projectId`).

**No MOV endpoint exists** in the API surface. This is the primary gap.

#### C2. `WorkLogEntry` vs `EditDiaryEntry` Field Mismatch

| Field | `WorkLogEntry` (detail) | `EditDiaryEntry` (edit) | `ConstructionTimelineEntry` (entity) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `equipmentUsed` | âŒ absent | âœ… present | âœ… present |
| `workAccomplished` | âŒ absent | âœ… present | âœ… present |
| `issuesEncountered` | âŒ absent | âœ… present | âœ… present |
| `photosCount` | âŒ absent | âŒ absent | âœ… present |

The detail page `fetchWorkLog` mapper (lines 409â€“418) only maps 7 fields out of the full entity's 14. The KN-B work log detail modal (from Phase KN plan) should display the missing fields â€” but they are currently not fetched.

#### C3. Document Upload Type Taxonomy Gap

The edit form uses 5 hardcoded types. The KB-E reference system has 27 types in 6 groups. The `findDocumentPreview()` function (Overview Key Documents) matches by keyword-in-documentType â€” this works for `attachment`, `specification`, `contract` but not for the 27-type taxonomy labels.

Until the upload form adopts KB-E taxonomy codes, the checklist `linkedDocumentId` link cannot be automated. The operator must manually link each uploaded document to its checklist item.

#### C4. Risk Summary

| Risk | Severity | Affected Phase |
|---|---|---|
| Upload form ignores KB-E taxonomy â€” checklist cannot auto-link | ðŸŸ  HIGH | KO |
| `WorkLogEntry` missing 3 fields vs entity â€” KN-B modal shows incomplete data | ðŸŸ  HIGH | KO (feeds KN-B) |
| No MOV structure for milestones or work logs | ðŸ”´ CRITICAL â€” core directive | KO |
| `EditDocItem` missing `createdAt`, `version`, `lifecycleStatus` | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KO |
| New migration required (048) for MOV table | ðŸ”´ BLOCKER â€” must precede frontend MOV UI | KO |

---

### 2.153-D â€” Priority and Execution Order

| Priority | Step | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | KO-A: New `construction_mov_entries` migration | Database prerequisite for all MOV features |
| ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | KO-B: MOV backend entity + CRUD endpoints | Required before any frontend MOV UI |
| ðŸ”´ CRITICAL | KO-C: Fix `WorkLogEntry` interface â€” add missing fields | KN-B modal requires complete data |
| ðŸŸ  HIGH | KO-D: MOV UI in milestone detail modal | Frontend MOV entry creation + list |
| ðŸŸ  HIGH | KO-E: MOV UI in work log detail modal | Frontend MOV entry creation + list |
| ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KO-F: Upgrade upload form to use KB-E document types | Taxonomy synchronization |
| ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | KO-G: Extend EditDocItem + DocumentItem with missing fields | Version, createdAt, lifecycleStatus visibility |

**Execution order: KO-A â†’ KO-B â†’ KO-C â†’ KO-D â†’ KO-E â†’ KO-F â†’ KO-G**

**Files affected:**
- New migration: `database/migrations/048_create_construction_mov_entries.sql`
- New entity: `pmo-backend/src/database/entities/construction-mov-entry.entity.ts`
- New DTO files: `create-mov-entry.dto.ts`, `update-mov-entry.dto.ts`
- Service: `construction-projects.service.ts` (MOV CRUD methods)
- Controller: `construction-projects.controller.ts` (MOV endpoints)
- Frontend: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` (MOV modals)
- Frontend: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` (upload form upgrade)

---

*Section 2.153 â€” Phase KO Research complete. Phase KO plan follows in plan.md.*
