## Section 2.154 â€” Phase KP: UI Propagation Failure Audit

**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Trigger:** Operator reported that KN/KO changes are NOT observable in the rendered UI despite implementation being declared complete.
**Scope:** `detail-[id].vue` (Timeline tab, Documents tab), `edit-[id].vue` (Documents tab), related components

---

### A. Root Cause Analysis

#### ROOT CAUSE A â€” Missing `@click` handlers on inline Timeline list items (PRIMARY)

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

- **Milestone inline list** (lines 1328â€“1374): `v-list-item` elements for milestones in the main Timeline tab have **NO `@click="openMilestoneDetail(milestone)"`**. They have no `style="cursor: pointer"`. They are visually read-only and cannot be interacted with.
- **Work Log inline list** (lines 1408â€“1438): `v-list-item` elements for work log entries in the main Timeline tab have **NO `@click="openWorkLogDetail(e)"`**. They are also non-interactive.

The milestone detail modal (KN-A, lines 1869â€“1941) and work log detail modal (KN-B, lines 1943â€“2013) are correctly implemented and connected via `openMilestoneDetail()` and `openWorkLogDetail()` functions. However, they can only be triggered from within the "View All" collection dialogs, not from the inline Timeline tab view.

`CiMovEvidenceSection` (KO-D/KO-E) lives inside these modals and is therefore also unreachable from normal workflow.

**Impact:** 100% of KN-A, KN-B, KO-D, KO-E functionality is inaccessible via the primary Timeline tab view. Users must somehow reach the "View All" dialogs first, which requires crossing ROOT CAUSE B.

---

#### ROOT CAUSE B â€” Threshold gates hide "View All" dialogs for small datasets

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

- **Milestones "View All" button** (line 1320): `v-if="(project.milestones?.length ?? 0) > 5"` â€” button only appears when MORE THAN 5 milestones exist.
- **Work Log "View All" button** (line 1394): `v-if="workLogEntries.length > 5"` â€” button only appears when MORE THAN 5 entries exist.
- **Documents "View All" button** (line 1639): `v-if="documentFiles.length > 10"` â€” button only appears when MORE THAN 10 documents exist.

For any project with fewer than 6 milestones or fewer than 6 work log entries (which is the majority of test/real projects in early data entry), the "View All" dialogs are completely hidden. Since the inline list items also lack click handlers (ROOT CAUSE A), there is NO path to reach KN-A/KN-B/KO-D/KO-E/KN-F from the rendered UI.

**Impact:** Even if ROOT CAUSE A were fixed (click on inline item), the "View All" buttons for overflow content remain invisible for small datasets. KN-F (Documents overflow window with search/filter) is similarly invisible for projects with â‰¤10 documents.

---

#### ROOT CAUSE C â€” Documents tab retains accordion (`v-expansion-panels`) structure

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` lines 1665â€“1696

The Documents tab right column still uses `v-expansion-panels` with `variant="accordion"` to group files by `documentType`. The KN Phase requirement specified an enterprise-style flat list without accordion sections, using group headers instead.

Observation:
- The KN-D individual file click handler IS correctly attached: `@click="openDocDetail(doc)"` at line 1678.
- The `v-expansion-panel` wrapping still requires users to click a panel header before seeing file rows.
- The accordion structure was the legacy pre-KN architecture. KN was meant to replace it with flat grouped display.

**Impact:** The Documents tab appears visually unchanged compared to pre-KN state. The accordion structure persists. KN-D is functional if a user expands a panel, but the enterprise flat-list design is not rendered.

---

### B. Supporting Findings (Non-Blocking)

#### FINDING D â€” KO-G metadata fields ARE returned by backend

The `Document` entity (`document.entity.ts`) has:
- `version: number = 1` (line 34)
- `lifecycleStatus: string = 'ACTIVE'` (line 52)
- `uploadedBy: string` (line 55)
- `createdAt: Date` (line 67)

`listProjectDocuments()` in the service uses `this.documentRepo.find(...)`, which returns full entity instances. MikroORM serializes all properties to camelCase in the API response. Therefore `version`, `lifecycleStatus`, `createdAt` ARE included in the `/api/construction-projects/:id/documents` response.

Both `detail-[id].vue`'s `fetchDocuments()` and `edit-[id].vue`'s `fetchDocs()` use raw assignment from `res.data`, so these fields will populate correctly once the server is running.

**Status:** KO-G is likely functional. Requires server restart to verify. No code change needed.

---

#### FINDING E â€” KO-F document type taxonomy endpoint exists

The `/document-types` route is registered in the controller (verified). The `ConstructionDocumentType` entity and its repository are injected into the service. The `documentTypeOptions` computed in `edit-[id].vue` falls back to a 5-item legacy list only if the API returns an empty array.

If the KB-E taxonomy was correctly seeded in migration, the KO-F taxonomy dropdown will work.

**Status:** Requires smoke test after server restart. No code change anticipated.

---

#### FINDING F â€” Route architecture is correct (no public/private collision)

Two detail pages exist:
- `/coi/detail-[id].vue` (private, auth middleware) â†’ route `/coi/detail-${id}`
- `/coi/public/detail-[id].vue` (public, no auth) â†’ route `/coi/public/detail-${id}`

Authenticated users navigate to the private page. No route collision. No rendering divergence due to routing.

---

#### FINDING G â€” `CiProjectDiary` is correctly separated from Work Log

`CiProjectDiary` renders diary entries (`/diary-entries`) in the Timeline tab right column below Work Log. Work Log renders `workLogEntries` (`/timeline-entries`) inline in `detail-[id].vue`. These are correctly separated architecturally. Work Log inline items are affected by ROOT CAUSE A; `CiProjectDiary` is a component and is unaffected.

---

#### FINDING H â€” Component imports not a cause of failure

`CiMovEvidenceSection`, `CiDocumentChecklist`, `CiProjectDiary` are all present under `components/coi/`. Nuxt 3 auto-imports components from `components/`. No import wiring issue. Component availability is not the cause of the failure.

---

### C. Continuity Failure Root-Cause Matrix

| # | Root Cause | Type | Affected Features | Severity |
|---|-----------|------|-------------------|----------|
| A | Missing `@click` on inline milestone list items | Template omission | KN-A, KO-D | CRITICAL |
| A | Missing `@click` on inline work log list items | Template omission | KN-B, KO-E | CRITICAL |
| B | `>5` threshold hides milestone "View All" button | Logic gate | KN-A (via dialog path), KN-C | HIGH |
| B | `>5` threshold hides work log "View All" button | Logic gate | KN-B (via dialog path) | HIGH |
| B | `>10` threshold hides documents "View All" button | Logic gate | KN-F | MEDIUM |
| C | `v-expansion-panels` accordion retained in Documents tab | Template structure | KN (visual requirement) | MEDIUM |

---

### D. Synchronization Status

| Feature | Status | Root Cause |
|---------|--------|------------|
| KN-A: Milestone detail modal | Implemented but unreachable | A + B |
| KN-B: Work log detail modal | Implemented but unreachable | A + B |
| KN-C: Timeline stat cards (in "View All" milestone dialog) | Visible only with >5 milestones | B |
| KN-D: Document file detail modal | Functional (if accordion expanded) | C (visual) |
| KN-E: Timeline filter + search in "View All" dialogs | Accessible only via "View All" | A + B |
| KN-F: Documents overflow window | Accessible only with >10 documents | B |
| KO-D: MOV evidence in milestone modal | Unreachable | A + B |
| KO-E: MOV evidence in work log modal | Unreachable | A + B |
| KO-F: KB-E taxonomy dropdown | Likely functional | Pending smoke test |
| KO-G: Metadata display in edit Documents tab | Likely functional | Pending smoke test |

---

### E. Risks and Constraints

- **Regression risk:** ROOT CAUSE A and C fixes are confined to `detail-[id].vue` template only. No service, controller, composable, or entity changes needed. Regression surface is minimal.
- **Threshold lowering (ROOT CAUSE B):** Changing `>5` to `â‰¥ 1` will always render "View All" links. This improves accessibility but slightly changes layout. Since "View All" dialogs contain search/filter/stat cards, showing them at any count is desirable for governance.
- **Accordion removal (ROOT CAUSE C):** Replacing `v-expansion-panels` with flat group headers improves readability and matches the KN design intent. The KN-D click handler on `v-list-item` is retained; only the outer container structure changes.
- **No backend changes required** for ROOT CAUSES A/B/C.
- **No new components required** â€” changes are template-only in `detail-[id].vue`.

---

*Section 2.154 â€” Phase KP Research complete. Phase KP plan follows in plan.md.*

---

## Section 2.155 â€” Phase KQ: Attachment Governance Incongruency Audit and Basic Info Normalization

**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Trigger:** Operator identified (A) redundant `ideal_infrastructure_image` URL field in Basic Info that conflicts with gallery governance; (B) uploaded documents do NOT automatically synchronize with the Document Compliance Checklist; (C) Attachment Tab UX is structurally fragmented with broken upload row layout; (D) cross-flow rendering continuity requires validation.
**Scope:** `CiBasicInfoForm.vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `new.vue`, `construction-projects.service.ts`, `CiDocumentChecklist.vue`, `adapters.ts`

---

### A. Basic Info Tab â€” `ideal_infrastructure_image` Field Assessment

#### A1 â€” Field Existence and Current State

The field `ideal_infrastructure_image` (DB column) is surfaced as a URL text input in:
- `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiBasicInfoForm.vue` line 218 â€” the rendered `<v-text-field>` labeled "Proposed Design / Architectural Rendering URL"
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` line 39 â€” form state initialization: `ideal_infrastructure_image: ''`
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` line 188 â€” hydration: `form.ideal_infrastructure_image = project.idealInfrastructureImage`
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` line 370 â€” payload assembly: `ideal_infrastructure_image: form.ideal_infrastructure_image`
- `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/new.vue` lines 21, 262 â€” equivalent form state + payload
- `pmo-frontend/utils/adapters.ts` line 375 â€” `idealInfrastructureImage: backend.ideal_infrastructure_image || ''`

#### A2 â€” Rendering Status in Detail Page

Grep of `detail-[id].vue` for `ideal_infrastructure_image` and `idealInfrastructureImage` returns **NO MATCH**. The field is never rendered in the public or private detail/overview page.

**Finding:** The field is stored in the database and round-tripped through the edit form, but it has no visible presence in any overview or detail view. It is an orphaned field from the view layer's perspective.

#### A3 â€” Gallery Profile Images Already Fulfill This Role

The gallery architecture supports a `PROFILE` category (max 3 images per project). These images are:
- Uploaded via the Gallery card in the Attachment Tab of `edit-[id].vue` (lines ~2098â€“2193)
- Labeled "Project Profile Images" with a note "Shown in Overview"
- Rendered in the Overview tab of `detail-[id].vue` (already wired to the gallery fetch)

The `PROFILE` gallery images are the **already-implemented, already-rendered** authoritative source for project architectural/facade imagery. The URL text field was a pre-gallery workaround that has since been made redundant.

#### A4 â€” Governance Verdict

| Criterion | Outcome |
|---|---|
| Is `ideal_infrastructure_image` rendered in the detail/overview page? | âŒ No |
| Does the gallery PROFILE category serve the same purpose? | âœ… Yes |
| Is there any workflow that uniquely depends on the URL field? | âŒ None found |
| Is a DB migration needed to remove the column? | No â€” deprecate the UI only; retain column for backward compatibility |
| Would removal break any existing backend endpoint? | No â€” column is nullable; existing saves remain valid |

**Recommendation:** Remove the field from all frontend forms (`CiBasicInfoForm.vue`, `new.vue` form state, `edit-[id].vue` form state). Retain the backend entity property and DB column silently (no migration). Remove the adapter mapping (or mark as optional and stop emitting).

---

### B. Attachment-Checklist Governance Gap

#### B1 â€” Two Parallel, Disconnected Systems

The COI attachment ecosystem has two independent tracking systems that should be synchronized but are not:

**System 1 â€” Document Uploads (`documents` table):**
- Upload endpoint: `POST /construction-projects/:id/documents`
- `Document.documentType` â€” stores the KB-E `typeCode` string (e.g., `'BOQ'`, `'NTP'`, `'contract'`)
- `Document.category` â€” nullable freeform string (not tied to KB-E taxonomy)
- `Document.lifecycleStatus` â€” `'ACTIVE'` | `'SUPERSEDED'`
- Service method: `uploadDocument()` at `construction-projects.service.ts` lines 1852â€“1912

**System 2 â€” Document Compliance Checklist (`construction_document_checklist` table):**
- Entity: `ConstructionDocumentChecklist`
- `documentTypeId` â€” UUID FK to `construction_document_types.id` (KB-E taxonomy row)
- `linkedDocumentId` â€” UUID FK to `documents.id` (nullable â€” manually set by staff via edit dialog)
- `submissionStatus` â€” `'NOT_SUBMITTED'` | `'SUBMITTED'` | `'APPROVED'` | `'REJECTED'`
- Component: `CiDocumentChecklist.vue` line 319 â€” `linked_document_id` is a **freeform text field** where staff must manually paste a document UUID

#### B2 â€” Root Cause of Governance Disconnection

The bridge key exists but is never used in service logic:
```
documents.documentType    â†â†’    construction_document_types.typeCode
```
Both hold the same string (e.g., `'BOQ'`). A join query of the form:
```sql
SELECT cdcl.id
FROM construction_document_checklist cdcl
JOIN construction_document_types cdt ON cdcl.document_type_id = cdt.id
WHERE cdcl.project_id = :projectId AND cdt.type_code = :uploadedTypeCode
```
would identify the matching checklist item. However, `uploadDocument()` performs no such lookup after `em.persistAndFlush(doc)`.

**Result:** Every document upload requires a separate manual staff action to paste the document UUID into the checklist dialog. This is operationally fragile, duplicates effort, and defeats the purpose of the governance taxonomy.

#### B3 â€” Upload Form Category Field Mismatch

The frontend upload form in `edit-[id].vue` has a `docCategory` ref (line 630) rendered as a freeform `<v-text-field>`. This maps to `fd.append('category', docCategory.value)` â†’ `UploadDocumentDto.category` â†’ `Document.category`.

The KB-E taxonomy has `groupCode` + `groupLabel` (6 groups: `CONTRACT_FOUNDATION`, `CONTRACTOR_QUALS`, `ACCOMPLISHMENT`, `CHANGE_ORDERS`, `COMPLETION`, `CUSTOM`). There is NO frontend dropdown for these group values.

**Finding:** Even when a user selects a KB-E `typeCode` from the taxonomy dropdown, the `category` field is left to free-text entry. The system has no mechanism to auto-derive the category group from the type selection. This means the `documents.category` column carries unmapped, inconsistently entered strings.

**Recommendation:** Remove the freeform `docCategory` text input. When a `documentType` (KB-E typeCode) is selected, auto-derive and submit `category` as the matching `groupCode` from the loaded taxonomy. No manual category entry needed.

#### B4 â€” Checklist Auto-Link Strategy

After a document is uploaded and persisted, the service should:
1. Find the `ConstructionDocumentType` record where `typeCode = dto.documentType` for this project's construction type (or globally if types are shared)
2. Find the `ConstructionDocumentChecklist` record where `projectId = projectId AND documentTypeId = found.id`
3. If found AND `submissionStatus === 'NOT_SUBMITTED'`:
   - Set `linkedDocumentId = doc.id`
   - Set `submissionStatus = 'SUBMITTED'`
4. If checklist item is already `'SUBMITTED'` | `'APPROVED'` | `'REJECTED'`, do NOT overwrite â€” preserve existing governance state
5. Persist the checklist update in the same `persistAndFlush` call

**Important constraint:** If `dto.documentType` is a legacy value (`'attachment'`, `'other'`, `'specification'`, `'contract'`, `'mov'`) that does NOT match any KB-E `typeCode`, the lookup returns null and auto-link is silently skipped. No error.

**No new migration needed** â€” `construction_document_checklist.linked_document_id` is already a nullable UUID column (exists since migration 04x). This is a pure service-layer enhancement.

---

### C. Attachment Tab UX Assessment

#### C1 â€” Upload Row Layout Failure

The document upload form row in `edit-[id].vue` (lines ~1942â€“2030 in the Documents card) uses:

| Field | Column width (`sm`) | Problem |
|---|---|---|
| File input | `sm="6"` | Acceptable |
| Document type dropdown | `sm="6"` | Acceptable |
| Title field | `sm="5"` | Acceptable |
| Category (freeform) | `sm="3"` | Redundant (see B3) |
| Description | `sm="3"` | Acceptable |
| Upload button | `sm="1"` | **CRITICALLY NARROW â€” 1/12 of row** |

An `sm="1"` button column on a 12-column grid renders as approximately 82px on standard desktop. The "Upload" button text is clipped or completely invisible at this width. This is a UX-breaking layout defect.

#### C2 â€” No Governance Visibility in Attachment Tab

The Document Compliance Checklist (`CiDocumentChecklist`) renders exclusively in the Detail Page â†’ Documents tab. Staff performing uploads in Edit Project â†’ Attachments tab have **no visual feedback** on which documents are required, which are missing, or current compliance status.

This creates an inverse workflow: staff must:
1. Upload document in Edit â†’ Attachments tab
2. Navigate to Detail â†’ Documents tab to check checklist
3. Manually open a checklist item edit dialog
4. Paste the document UUID

None of these steps are in proximity to the actual upload action.

#### C3 â€” Three-Card Fragmentation

The Attachments tab consists of three separate `<v-card>` blocks:
1. **Documents** â€” compliance documents, MOVs, contracts
2. **Gallery** â€” project images (5 subcategories)
3. **External Links** â€” URL-based links

These are logically related (all are project artifacts) but are visually isolated with no shared header or structural hierarchy. The tab has no overview/summary section.

#### C4 â€” Category Section Headers Not Derived from Taxonomy

The `docsByCategory` computed in `edit-[id].vue` (lines 681â€“689) groups uploaded documents by `documentType` key (the raw typeCode string). The section headers rendered in the document list show the raw typeCode (e.g., `'BOQ'`, `'NTP'`) rather than the human-readable `typeLabel` from the taxonomy.

After KO-F, the taxonomy is loaded into `documentTypeOptions`. A `typeCodeToLabel` map could be derived from this array to display `typeLabel` as section headers instead of raw codes.

---

### D. Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

#### D1 â€” Create vs Edit Flow

| Feature | Create Project (`new.vue`) | Edit Project (`edit-[id].vue`) |
|---|---|---|
| Document upload | âŒ Not available (correct â€” project ID needed first) | âœ… Full Attachment tab |
| Gallery upload | âŒ Not available | âœ… Gallery card |
| External links | âŒ Not available | âœ… External links card |
| `ideal_infrastructure_image` | âœ… Field present (redundant â€” target for KQ-A removal) | âœ… Field present (redundant) |

The absence of upload functionality in `new.vue` is by design â€” project must be saved first to have an ID. No continuity issue here.

#### D2 â€” Edit vs Detail Page

| Feature | Edit Project | Detail Page |
|---|---|---|
| Document list source | Same `/api/construction-projects/:id/documents` | Same endpoint |
| Documents tab rendering | Cards in Attachments tab | Flat list in Documents tab (post-KP-C) |
| Compliance checklist | âŒ Not present | âœ… `CiDocumentChecklist` below document list |
| Gallery rendering | Upload/manage cards | Overview tab gallery strip |
| External links | Upload card | Not rendered in detail page |

**Continuity gap:** Checklist is detail-only. Edit page has no compliance awareness. This is a UX gap (addressed by KQ-D compliance summary widget).

#### D3 â€” Checklist vs Documents Table

| Field | `documents` table | `construction_document_checklist` table |
|---|---|---|
| Type reference | `documentType` (typeCode string) | `documentTypeId` (UUID FK to taxonomy) |
| Link to uploaded file | N/A | `linkedDocumentId` (UUID FK, manually set) |
| Status | `lifecycleStatus` (ACTIVE/SUPERSEDED) | `submissionStatus` (NOT_SUBMITTED/SUBMITTED/APPROVED/REJECTED) |
| Auto-synchronized? | âŒ Never | âŒ Never |

**Critical finding:** The two tracking systems are completely parallel with no event-driven synchronization. The only connection is the `typeCode â†” typeCode` string match â€” which exists in the data model but is unused in service logic.

---

### E. Risks and Constraints

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|
| Auto-link overwrites a manually APPROVED or REJECTED checklist item | ðŸ”´ HIGH | Gate auto-link on `submissionStatus === 'NOT_SUBMITTED'` only |
| Removing `docCategory` freeform field breaks existing saved categories in DB | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | The column and existing data remain; only the form input is removed; existing data is unaffected |
| Auto-derived category from typeCode may fail if taxonomy not loaded | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | Fallback: submit empty `category` if taxonomy not loaded |
| KQ-A removal may affect any backend validator that requires `ideal_infrastructure_image` | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | Backend already accepts it as optional (nullable column); removing from frontend payload is safe |
| Document UX redesign may break existing upload workflow | ðŸŸ  HIGH | Make incremental changes: fix layout only, preserve all upload logic |
| typeLabel as section header requires taxonomy lookup â€” adds complexity | ðŸŸ¡ LOW | Use `documentTypeOptions` array that is already loaded; derive map from it |

---

*Section 2.155 â€” Phase KQ Research complete. Phase KQ plan follows in plan.md.*

---

## Section 2.156 â€” Phase KR: Timeline Modal Architecture, Document Governance Refactor, Attachment Normalization, and Backend Constraint Failure Analysis

**Date:** 2026-05-13
**Trigger:** Operator issued post-KQ research prompt covering (A) Timeline modal governance gaps; (B) Documents Tab governance refactor; (C) Attachment Tab enterprise refactor; (D) Backend error analysis â€” FK violation on document delete, project code validation failure on PATCH, activity_logs table missing.
**Scope:** `detail-[id].vue`, `edit-[id].vue`, `construction-projects.service.ts`, `update-construction-project.dto.ts`, `database/migrations/`, `CiDocumentChecklist.vue`

---

### A. Timeline Tab â€” Enterprise Modal Governance Audit

#### A1 â€” Current Implementation State (Verified)

| Feature | Status | Code Location |
|---------|--------|---------------|
| Individual Milestone detail modal | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `detail-[id].vue` â€” `milestoneDetailDialogOpen`, KN-A |
| Individual Work Log detail modal | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `detail-[id].vue` â€” `workLogDetailDialogOpen`, KN-B |
| "View All Milestones" collection modal | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `milestonesDialogOpen`, opens at "View All" click |
| "View All Work Logs" collection modal | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `workLogDialogOpen`, opens at "View All" click |
| Nested modal navigation (collection â†’ detail) | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `@click="openMilestoneDetail(m)"` inside collection dialog |
| Milestone stat cards (Total/Completed/In Progress/Delayed) | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KM-C â€” 4 `v-card variant="tonal"` stat cards |
| Milestone date-range filter | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KN-C â€” `milestoneFilterDateFrom`/`milestoneFilterDateTo` in dialog |
| Milestone status filter | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `milestoneStatusFilter` select in dialog |
| Work log type filter (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY) | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `workLogTypeFilter` select in dialog |
| Work log date-range filter | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KN-C â€” `wlogFilterDateFrom`/`wlogFilterDateTo` in dialog |
| MOV evidence in milestone detail | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KO-D â€” `CiMovEvidenceSection` embedded |
| MOV evidence in work log detail | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KO-E â€” `CiMovEvidenceSection` embedded |

#### A2 â€” MOV Evidence Architecture (Verified)

`CiMovEvidenceSection.vue` stores the following fields per entry:
- `movLink` â€” external URL (links-only, no file storage)
- `movTitle`
- `movDescription` (optional)
- `evidenceCategory` â€” from 9-value enum (photo_documentation, inspection_report, completion_certificate, site_visit, progress_report, test_result, meeting_minutes, official_document, other)
- `entryDate` (optional)
- `verificationStatus` â€” `PENDING | VERIFIED | REJECTED`
- `remarks` (optional)

This satisfies all 7 of the 8 required MOV fields in the operator prompt. The `uploadedBy` field maps to `createdBy` stored at entity level (not surfaced as a named field in the component interface â€” cosmetic gap only, data is present via audit columns).

#### A3 â€” Identified Gaps (True Missing Features)

| Gap | Impact | Prompt Requirement |
|-----|--------|-------------------|
| No period-based filter (daily/weekly/monthly/quarterly) **on main Timeline tab** | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | Req. 3 â€” filters must also function on the main tab, not only inside dialogs |
| Work log stat cards MISSING | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | Req. 4 â€” work-log frequency/accomplishment indicators |
| No timeline visualization/charts | ðŸŸ¡ MEDIUM | Req. 4 â€” milestone counts, delay indicators, completion metrics, frequency visuals |
| Delay/completion metrics not shown outside stat cards | ðŸŸ¡ LOW | Req. 4 â€” completion metrics, delay indicators |

**Assessment:** The architectural foundation is solid. The individual detail modals (KN-A/B), collection modals (KJ-C), nested navigation, and MOV governance (KO-D/E) are all functional. The remaining gaps are:
1. Period-based granularity filter on the main Timeline tab
2. Work log companion stat cards (parallel to KM-C milestone stat cards)
3. Optional chart/visualization enhancement

#### A4 â€” Filter Architecture Recommendation

The `workLogTypeFilter` select in the "View All Work Log" dialog already filters by type (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY). The gap is a **mirrored filter row on the main Timeline tab** so users can narrow visible entries without entering the dialog.

Recommended additions to Timeline tab (above the 2-column row, after stat cards):
- Work log stat cards row: Total Work Logs, Daily Entries, Weekly Entries, Monthly/Quarterly
- Optional: Period-filter chips on the Timeline tab itself (not new dialogs)

---

### B. Documents Tab â€” Governance Refactor Audit

#### B1 â€” Current Implementation State (Verified)

| Feature | Status | Code Location |
|---------|--------|---------------|
| Dual-column layout (LEFT checklist / RIGHT files) | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KM-D â€” `md="7"` + `md="5"` columns |
| CiDocumentChecklist on left | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `<CiDocumentChecklist :project-id="projectId" />` |
| File list grouped by documentType | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `v-for="[type, docs] in documentsByType"` |
| Links section separated | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `documentLinks` array rendered separately |
| "View All" overflow modal | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | `docsWindowOpen` trigger, KN-F |
| Clickable file rows â†’ detail modal | âœ… IMPLEMENTED | KN-D â€” `openDocDetail(doc)` |

#### B2 â€” Identified Gaps

| Gap | Prompt Requirement |
|-----|-------------------|
| Right column header reads "Attachments" â€” must be "Attachments or Files" | Req. B â€” rename "attachment" â†’ "Attachments or Files" |
| No "Key Documents" section for executive documents (Project Profile, Feasibility Study, HGDG Form, Approved Floor Plan, POW, etc.) | Req. B â€” Key Documents section required |
| Key documents section should show latest uploaded versions dynamically from existing file list | Req. B â€” "Additional documents uploaded through Attachment Tab, rendered dynamically" |

#### B3 â€” Key Documents Architecture

Key documents are a **curated subset** of the existing file list, filtered by their `documentType` against known executive typeCode values. No new DB schema needed.

Strategy:
1. Define `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` = `['PROJECT_PROFILE', 'FEASIBILITY_STUDY', 'HGDG_FORM', 'FLOOR_PLAN', 'POW']` (or equivalent typeCode values from `construction_document_types` KB-E taxonomy)
2. Compute `keyDocuments = documentFiles.filter(d => KEY_DOC_TYPECODES.includes(d.documentType))`
3. Render Key Documents as a dedicated section above the grouped type list
4. Non-key documents continue to render in the grouped-by-type list below

**No migration, no backend change needed** â€” data already exists, only presentation changes.

---

### C. Attachment Tab â€” Enterprise Refactor Audit

#### C1 â€” Current Implementation State (Verified, edit-[id].vue documents window-item)

| Card | Content | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| Card 1: Documents | Clean 3-row upload form (KQ-D) + existing docs grouped by typeCodeToLabel | âœ… KQ-D CLEAN |
| Card 2: Gallery | PROFILE images section (max 3, shown in Overview) + general gallery thumbnail grid | âœ… KF-AB CLEAN |
| Card 3: External Links | URL + Title + Description + Add button | âš ï¸ HAS LAYOUT ISSUE |

#### C2 â€” External Links Layout Issue

External Links card uses column distribution `sm="5" / sm="3" / sm="3" / sm="1"` for URL/Title/Description/Add. The `sm="1"` Add button (â‰ˆ 83px) is too narrow â€” same layout bug that was fixed in the Documents upload row (KQ-D) but was NOT fixed in External Links.

**Fix required:** Restructure External Links to a 2-row layout:
- Row 1: URL (sm=6) + Title (sm=6)
- Row 2: Description (sm=8) + right-aligned "Add Link" button

#### C3 â€” Navigation Freeze Root Cause Analysis

The Attachment Tab loads three parallel data sources on mount/tab-enter:
1. `loadDocs()` â€” fetches `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/documents`
2. `loadGallery()` â€” fetches `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/gallery`
3. (External links are embedded in project detail payload â€” no separate fetch)

**Suspected causes:**
- Large document/gallery payloads on a slow connection can cause perceived freeze
- No lazy-loading per card; all three cards render simultaneously
- `watch(activeTab, ...)` may not short-circuit on re-entry after documents already loaded
- No `AbortController` / request cancellation â€” navigating away mid-load leaves dangling state

**Assessment:** No single root cause is confirmed without runtime profiling. The most reliable fix is load-on-demand gating: documents and gallery cards render a skeleton/placeholder until their own fetch completes, with no blocking of tab navigation. This is already partially implemented (each card has its own `loading*` ref), but the tab-switch watcher may be re-fetching on every entry.

---

### D. Backend Error Analysis

#### D1 â€” Document Delete FK Constraint Failure

**Error reported:** `ForeignKeyConstraintViolationException` when deleting a document.

**Root Cause Analysis:**

`removeDocument()` in `construction-projects.service.ts` line 1959:
```typescript
await this.em.removeAndFlush(doc);
```

`ConstructionDocumentChecklist` entity declares `linkedDocumentId` as:
```typescript
@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'uuid' })
linkedDocumentId?: string;
```

This is a plain `@Property` â€” NOT a `@ManyToOne`. MikroORM schema-generator does NOT create a PostgreSQL FK constraint for plain `@Property` fields, only for `@ManyToOne`/`@OneToMany`/`@ManyToMany`.

**Therefore:** If the ORM was the sole schema creator, NO FK constraint exists on `construction_document_checklist.linked_document_id`. The error must originate from elsewhere.

**Alternative explanation:** The `construction_document_checklist` table was created via `schemaGenerator.updateSchema()` at some point. If an operator manually added a FK constraint to `linked_document_id` post-creation (e.g., during debugging), the FK would not be in any migration file.

**Safest resolution strategy (regardless of FK existence):**
Before `em.removeAndFlush(doc)`, explicitly clear stale checklist references:
```sql
UPDATE construction_document_checklist
SET linked_document_id = NULL
WHERE linked_document_id = :docId;
```
This is safe in both scenarios:
- If FK exists: clearing the reference before delete prevents the violation
- If FK does not exist: the update is a no-op that restores checklist integrity (avoids dangling IDs)

This is preferred over adding an `ON DELETE SET NULL` migration because it keeps full service-layer control over checklist state and avoids a potentially disruptive schema migration.

#### D2 â€” Project Code Validation Failure on PATCH

**Error reported:** Project code must follow `CP-YYYY-NNN` on PATCH updates.

**Root Cause (Confirmed):**

`UpdateConstructionProjectDto`:
```typescript
export class UpdateConstructionProjectDto extends PartialType(
  CreateConstructionProjectDto,
)
```

`PartialType` from `@nestjs/mapped-types` wraps ALL inherited properties with `@IsOptional()`, BUT preserves all other validators. The `@Matches(/^CP-\d{4}-\d{3}$/)` decorator in `CreateConstructionProjectDto` is still active â€” it fires whenever `project_code` IS present in the PATCH body.

The frontend's edit form includes `project_code` in the PATCH payload even when the user has not changed it (because it's part of the form state that gets submitted). If the stored code value somehow has whitespace, different case, or a formatting difference, the regex fails.

**Fix:**

Override `project_code` in `UpdateConstructionProjectDto` to remove the regex constraint:
```typescript
@IsOptional()
@IsString()
project_code?: string;
```

Format validation belongs at CREATE time. PATCH should accept any string (backend reads the stored format). This prevents valid no-op PATCH requests from failing due to inherited regex.

#### D3 â€” Activity Logs Table Missing

**Error reported:** `relation "activity_logs" does not exist`

**Root Cause (Confirmed):**

Migration `044_create_activity_logs.sql` exists in `database/migrations/` and correctly creates the `activity_logs` table with all required columns. The entity `activity-log.entity.ts` declares `tableName: 'activity_logs'` which matches.

**Finding:** Migration 044 has NOT been applied on the operator's running database. This is a deployment gap, not a code gap.

**Resolution:** Run `npm run migrate` (or the project's migration runner) to apply all pending migrations including 044. No code changes needed.

Note: `ActivityLogService.logAction()` uses fire-and-forget (`void ... .catch(() => {})`) and will not crash the system even when the table is missing â€” the error is silently caught and logged as a warning. However, all audit trail data is lost until the migration is applied.

---

### E. Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

#### E1 â€” Synchronization Matrix

| Flow | Status | Gap |
|------|--------|-----|
| Upload document â†’ Checklist auto-link | âœ… KQ-B IMPLEMENTED | None |
| Delete document â†’ Checklist stale reference | âš ï¸ NOT HANDLED | `removeDocument()` does not clear `linkedDocumentId` |
| Edit â†’ Documents tab â†’ Detail â†’ Documents tab | âœ… Consistent | `documentsByType` computed from same API |
| Timeline Milestone â†’ Detail modal â†’ MOV | âœ… KO-D | Functional |
| Timeline Work Log â†’ Detail modal â†’ MOV | âœ… KO-E | Functional |
| Gallery PROFILE images â†’ Overview rendering | âœ… KF-AB | Authoritative since KQ-A |
| Documents tab file list â†’ Key Documents section | âš ï¸ NOT IMPLEMENTED | KR-B gap |
| Right column "Attachments" label | âš ï¸ WRONG LABEL | Must be "Attachments or Files" |
| External Links "Add" button layout | âš ï¸ LAYOUT BUG | sm="1" too narrow â€” KQ-D fix missed this |
| Activity log writes | âš ï¸ SILENTLY FAILING | Migration 044 not applied |
| PATCH project_code validation | âš ï¸ REGRESSION | UpdateDto inherits regex from CreateDto |

#### E2 â€” Synchronization Risks

1. **Document delete leaves stale `linkedDocumentId`** â€” After KQ-B, more checklist items will have `linkedDocumentId` set. Without pre-delete cleanup, deleting a document leaves the checklist item with a non-null `linkedDocumentId` pointing to a deleted record. This corrupts compliance governance (checklist shows SUBMITTED but the file is gone).

2. **Activity log silent failure** â€” All audit trail writes are silently dropped until migration 044 is applied. No user-visible error, but governance audit trail is incomplete.

3. **PATCH project_code regression** â€” Edit Project form submission fails for any project where the frontend re-sends the stored `project_code`. This has been failing silently or causing visible form submission errors.

---

### F. Priority Decision â€” Execution Order

| Priority | Step | Reason |
|----------|------|--------|
| 1 (CRITICAL) | KR-D1: Fix `removeDocument()` pre-delete checklist cleanup | Directly causes document management breakage |
| 2 (CRITICAL) | KR-D2: Fix UpdateDto project_code override | Causes PATCH failures on every edit with project_code in payload |
| 3 (HIGH) | KR-D3: Apply migration 044 (operator action) | Restores audit trail governance |
| 4 (HIGH) | KR-B: Documents tab â€” rename + Key Documents section | Governance clarity |
| 5 (MEDIUM) | KR-C: External Links button layout fix | UX layout bug |
| 6 (MEDIUM) | KR-A: Timeline work log stat cards | Governance completeness |

---

*Section 2.156 â€” Phase KR Research complete. Phase KR plan follows in plan.md.*

## Section 2.158 — Phase KT Research

**Research Date:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** Public COI detail page gap analysis; identify all data available but not rendered; identify data requiring new backend endpoints.

---

### A. Baseline — What `findPublicOne` Returns

`findPublicOne(id)` delegates entirely to `findOne(id)`, then filters out non-PUBLISHED records.

`findOne(id)` returns via raw SQL + ORM:
- All `construction_projects` columns (including `summary`, `scope`, `facilities`, `beneficiaries`, narrative/strategic profile fields)
- JOINed: `contractor_name`, `funding_source_name`, `created_by_name`, `submitted_by_name`, `reviewed_by_name`
- Subquery: `assigned_users` (JSON array of `{ id, name, role, department, phone, personnel_category }`)
- ORM load: `milestones` (ordered by `target_date`)
- ORM load: `financials` (ordered by `fiscal_year` desc)

**NOT included in `findOne`:**
- `pow_items` — no POW fetch in `findOne`; only accessible via `findPowItems(projectId)` (service line 1217)
- `documents` — document list not in `findOne`; fetched separately by admin detail via dedicated endpoint
- `diary_entries` — not applicable for public view
- `gallery` — fetched separately via `GET .../gallery`

---

### B. Adapter Coverage

`adaptProjectDetail()` (adapters.ts:350) maps all narrative/profile fields from the backend response:

| Backend field | UIProjectDetail key | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `summary` | `summary` | ✅ Mapped |
| `scope` | `scope` | ✅ Mapped |
| `facilities` | `facilities` | ✅ Mapped |
| `beneficiaries` | `beneficiaries` | ✅ Mapped |
| `objectives` | `objectives` | ✅ Mapped |
| `key_features` | `keyFeatures` | ✅ Mapped |
| `strategic_alignment` | `strategicAlignment` | ✅ Mapped |
| `output_indicators` | `outputIndicators` | ✅ Mapped |
| `outcome_indicators` | `outcomeIndicators` | ✅ Mapped |
| `implementing_agency` | `implementingAgency` | ✅ Mapped |
| `assigned_users` | `assignedUsers` | ✅ Mapped (via `adaptProject`) |
| `milestones` | `milestones` | ✅ Mapped |
| `financials` | `financials` | ✅ Mapped |
| POW items | _(no field)_ | ❌ Not in UIProjectDetail |

---

### C. Public Detail Page Gap Matrix

`pages/coi/public/detail-[id].vue` (290 lines) — current vs. available data:

| Section | Data Available | Rendered | Gap |
|---|---|---|---|
| Status, physical progress, contract amount | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Start/end dates, engineer/manager | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Description | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Beneficiaries | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| **Summary narrative** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-A |
| **Scope narrative** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-A |
| **Facilities narrative** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-A |
| **Objectives list** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-B |
| **Key Features list** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-B |
| **Strategic alignment** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-B |
| **Output/outcome indicators** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-B |
| Milestones table | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Gallery | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Building type/area/floors | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Contractor, fund source | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| Financial records table | ✅ | ✅ | None |
| **Team / Assigned Users** | ✅ | ❌ | KT-C |
| **POW Items** | ❌ (no backend) | ❌ | KT-D |

---

### D. Bugs Found

None in the existing public detail page logic. Specifically:
- `project.physicalAccomplishment` (line 120) — correct field; `UIProject` has `physicalAccomplishment` mapped from `physical_progress` via `adaptProject()` ✅
- `item.name` in milestones table — correct; `adaptMilestone()` maps `backend.title → name` ✅

---

### E. POW Endpoint Analysis (KT-D)

`findPowItems(projectId)` exists at service line 1217 and returns `ConstructionPowItem[]` via ORM. No public controller route exposes it.

To add a public POW endpoint:
1. Add `GET ':id/pow-items'` to `PublicConstructionController` with `@Public()` decorator
2. Call `service.findPublicOne(id)` first (publication check — throws 404 if not PUBLISHED)
3. Then call `service.findPowItems(id)` for the items
4. Frontend: add POW table section to public detail page

---

### F. Priority Decision

| Priority | Sub-phase | Work | Risk |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 (HIGH) | KT-A | Add summary, scope, facilities to public detail | Zero — frontend only |
| 2 (HIGH) | KT-B | Add objectives, key features, strategic profile | Zero — frontend only |
| 3 (MEDIUM) | KT-C | Add team/assigned-users section | Zero — frontend only |
| 4 (MEDIUM) | KT-D | Add public POW endpoint + POW table | Low — small controller + service delegate |

All KT-A through KT-C are frontend-only changes (data already fetched). KT-D requires one new controller route and one new frontend fetch.

All three are frontend-only (zero backend changes needed).

## Section 2.161 — Phase KV: COI Attachment Enterprise Refactor, Document Governance Normalization, Timeline Filter Architecture

**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** Timeline Tab unified filter, Documents Tab governance normalization, "Others" Tab creation, Attachment Tab enterprise UI refactor, cross-flow continuity analysis

---

### A. Timeline Tab — General Filter Governance

#### A.1 Current State Audit

Timeline filtering is structurally fragmented across two independent dialogs. The detail page (`detail-[id].vue`) maintains 8 separate filter refs:

| Ref | Scope | Type |
|-----|-------|------|
| `milestoneSearch` | Milestones only | String |
| `milestoneStatusFilter` | Milestones only | Enum (ALL + status values) |
| `milestoneFilterDateFrom` | Milestones only | Date string |
| `milestoneFilterDateTo` | Milestones only | Date string |
| `workLogSearch` | Work Logs only | String |
| `workLogTypeFilter` | Work Logs only | Enum (ALL + entry types) |
| `wlogFilterDateFrom` | Work Logs only | Date string |
| `wlogFilterDateTo` | Work Logs only | Date string |

Each collection lives inside its own full-screen dialog (`milestonesDialogOpen` / `workLogDialogOpen`). Users cannot simultaneously view filtered milestones and work logs against the same date range. Filter state does not persist — both reset when dialogs close.

#### A.2 Identified Gaps

1. **No shared date filter**: Milestones filter by `targetDate`; work logs filter by `entryDate`. A unified `dateFrom`/`dateTo` range should apply to both but map to each collection's relevant date field.
2. **No period/granularity filter**: Work log `entryType` (DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY) exists as a type filter. Milestones have no equivalent — but can be grouped by whether their `targetDate` falls within a selected calendar period (week/month/quarter).
3. **No "completion state" filter**: Neither collection has an explicit completion filter. For milestones: `status === 'COMPLETED'`. For work logs: no completion concept — entryType QUARTERLY is the coarsest grain.
4. **No submission date filter**: Milestones have `submittedAt` only through checklist linkage, not on the entity itself. Work logs have `entryDate` (creation date) only. "Submission date" as a filter concept maps to `entryDate` for work logs and has no direct analog for milestones.
5. **Filter state lost on dialog close**: Dialogs are `v-model` controlled — when closed, filter refs persist at page level (they are page-scope refs) but the dialog body is unmounted. Re-opening reuses the same filter values. This is actually correct behavior — the refs live at page scope, not inside the dialog. No regression here.

#### A.3 Best Unified Filter Architecture

**Recommendation: Shared date range + separate collection-specific filters**

A fully unified filter (single object controlling both collections) collapses two semantically different datasets into one filter surface, which causes UX confusion: "status" means different things for milestones vs work logs.

The correct architecture:

```ts
// Shared (applied to both):
const sharedDateFrom = ref('')
const sharedDateTo = ref('')
const sharedPeriod = ref<'ALL' | 'DAILY' | 'WEEKLY' | 'MONTHLY' | 'QUARTERLY'>('ALL')

// Collection-specific (unchanged):
const milestoneStatusFilter = ref('ALL')
const workLogTypeFilter = ref('ALL')
```

- Shared date range: filters milestones by `targetDate`, work logs by `entryDate`
- `sharedPeriod` maps WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY to dynamic date bounds computed from current date (e.g., MONTHLY = first to last day of current month). When set, overrides manual `dateFrom`/`dateTo`.
- Period quick-select renders as `v-btn-toggle` above the Timeline tab (not inside dialogs)
- Collection-specific status/type filters remain inside each dialog

**Filter-state persistence behavior**: All filter refs at page level (`detail-[id].vue` script scope). Dialog open/close does not reset state. Navigating away from Timeline tab and returning resets to defaults (acceptable — no backend persistence required).

**Rendering performance**: All filtering is purely client-side computed. Milestone data comes from `project.value?.milestones` (loaded with project). Work log data from `fetchWorkLog()` (loaded on mount). For typical project scale (≤200 milestones, ≤500 diary entries), client-side computed is suitable. No backend pagination needed.

**Scalability risk**: If a single project accumulates >1000 diary entries, the full array remains in memory. Mitigation: add `pageSize` param to `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/timeline-entries` in a future phase. Not a current concern.

#### A.4 Filter UI Placement

Current: filter controls are embedded inside the full-dialog header area of each dialog.
Recommended: add a compact filter bar to the Timeline tab body (visible before clicking "View All"), showing:
- Date range quick-select chips: `This Week | This Month | This Quarter | Custom`
- Custom date inputs (shown when "Custom" active)
- Clear all button

The milestones dialog and work log dialog each retain their search + status/type selectors. The shared date range auto-applies when opening either dialog.

---

### B. Documents Tab — Governance Normalization

#### B.1 Current State Audit

**CiDocumentChecklist component** (`components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue`) renders the compliance checklist in the detail page Documents tab. It:
- Fetches `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist`
- Groups items by `documentType.groupCode` into `v-expansion-panels`
- Accordion header chip: `X / Y approved`
- Per-item: status chip + submitted date + review notes inline
- Edit dialog: status selector, review notes textarea, expiry date, **manual UUID text field** for `linked_document_id`

**Critical issue — file not accessible**: The `linkedDocumentId` field is stored in the DB but never rendered as a clickable link/download in the checklist. Users who set it must still navigate to the Attachments tab to find the file manually. This is the primary usability gap.

**Secondary issue — accordion metric**: "0/7 approved" shows the wrong metric for operational use. Document compliance progress is better tracked by submission count ("X/Y submitted"), not approval count. Approval requires admin action; submission is the staff's responsibility. Showing submission count gives staff actionable status.

**Tertiary issue — no group-level remarks**: Each checklist item has per-item `reviewNotes`. There is no group/accordion-level remarks field. Evaluators need to communicate deficiencies at the group level (e.g., "Contract Foundation documents are incomplete — missing signed NTP").

#### B.2 Direct File Access Strategy

**Approach: Pass `documents` as a prop to `CiDocumentChecklist`**

The document list is already fetched in `detail-[id].vue` via `fetchDocuments()`. Pass `documents` as a prop of type `DocumentItem[]` to `CiDocumentChecklist`. Inside the component, when rendering a checklist item with `linkedDocumentId` set, look up the document in the passed list and render a download anchor.

```ts
// In CiDocumentChecklist, added prop:
interface Props {
  projectId: string
  canEdit: boolean
  documents: DocumentItem[]  // NEW — from parent's documentFiles
}

// Per-item computed:
function linkedDoc(item: ChecklistItem): DocumentItem | null {
  if (!item.linkedDocumentId) return null
  return props.documents.find(d => d.id === item.linkedDocumentId) ?? null
}
```

No new API calls needed. The `documents` prop is already available in `detail-[id].vue` (`documentFiles` computed). This is a zero-backend-change approach.

**Alternative: Document picker in checklist edit dialog**: Replace the UUID text field with a `v-select` populated from the `documents` prop. User selects a file from a dropdown instead of pasting a UUID. This is strictly better UX.

#### B.3 Accordion Metadata Strategy

**Submission count chip (replaces "approved" chip):**
```ts
// Per group:
const submitted = g.items.filter(i => i.submissionStatus !== 'NOT_SUBMITTED').length
// Render: `X / Y submitted`
```

**Additional accordion header metadata:**
- Latest submission date: `max(g.items.map(i => i.submittedAt)).toLocaleDateString()`
- File count: `g.items.filter(i => i.linkedDocumentId).length` items with linked files
- Missing: `g.items.filter(i => i.submissionStatus === 'NOT_SUBMITTED').length`

Display as a row of small chips below the group label, not in the chip itself (too wide for one chip).

#### B.4 Per-Group Remarks Governance

**Requirement**: Each accordion/topic needs a remarks field for evaluator comments (deficiency notes, compliance feedback, section-level observations).

**Storage options analysis:**

| Option | Schema | Complexity | Migration |
|--------|--------|-----------|-----------|
| New table `construction_document_group_remarks` | `{id, project_id, group_code, remarks, updated_by, updated_at}` | Full CRUD | Yes |
| JSONB on `construction_projects` | `document_checklist_remarks: { [groupCode]: string }` | Single-field PATCH | Yes (ALTER TABLE) |
| Aggregate from per-item `reviewNotes` | No new field | Read-only derived | None |
| Frontend-only (not persisted) | localStorage | None | None |

**Recommendation**: JSONB column `document_checklist_remarks JSONB DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb` on `construction_projects`. One `ALTER TABLE` migration. Backend: add field to entity + DTO. Frontend: emit group remarks via PATCH to the project endpoint or a dedicated sub-route `PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/document-remarks`.

The dedicated sub-route is cleaner: `PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/document-remarks` with body `{ groupCode: string, remarks: string }`. No DTO bloat on the project update endpoint.

---

### C. "Others" Tab Governance

#### C.1 Current State Audit

No "Others" tab exists in either page. The edit page has 7 tabs (Basic Info, Dates & Duration, Timeline, POW, Financial, Personnel, Attachments). Basic Info (handled by `CiBasicInfoForm`) contains three data groups that are semantically outside "Basic Info":

1. **Status Updates** (`statusUpdateRows`): `[{ date, text }]` — project milestone announcements
2. **Readiness Documents** (`readinessDocRows`): `[{ type, status, remarks }]` — pre-construction readiness checklist
3. **Signatories** (`signatoryRows`): `[{ name, position, date }]` — authorization signatures

These three groups are stored as JSONB columns (`status_updates`, `readiness_documents`, `signatories`) in `construction_projects`. They belong in an "Others" tab because they are optional supplementary information, not core project definition.

#### C.2 Recommended Architecture — KISS Compliant

**"Others" tab = move existing three groups from Basic Info to a new tab.** No new DB fields required. No new backend logic.

Edit page: 8 tabs (Basic Info, Dates & Duration, Timeline, POW, Financial, Personnel, Attachments, **Others**).
Detail page: 8 tabs (Overview, Timeline, POW, Analytics, Gallery, Documents, Team, **Others**).

In the "Others" view tab: read-only cards for Status Updates, Readiness Documents, Signatories.
In the "Others" edit tab: the existing row-management UI moved from `CiBasicInfoForm`.

**`tabCompletion.others`**: `statusUpdateRows.length > 0 || readinessDocRows.length > 0 || signatoryRows.length > 0`

**Future extensibility**: If custom fields are needed later, the Others tab is the natural extension point. For now, YAGNI — no custom field architecture needed.

---

### D. Attachment Tab — Enterprise UI Refactor

#### D.1 Current State Audit

Edit page Attachments tab (labeled "Documents" in `v-tab` but "Attachments" in tab icon) contains 3 sequentially-stacked cards:
1. **Card 1 — Documents**: Existing docs list (grouped by type) + upload form. All document types mixed (compliance + general + key docs in same list). KB-E taxonomy select controls upload type.
2. **Card 2 — Gallery**: Profile images section + general gallery thumbnails + two upload sub-forms (profile + general).
3. **Card 3** (implied from link form) — External links form.

**Issues identified:**
- No visual hierarchy between Key Documents (governance-critical) vs general attachments
- Documents and Gallery are vertically stacked — no lateral use of screen space
- Document Compliance Checklist is absent from edit page — only shown in detail page
- No MOV Evidence aggregated view in the tab
- No "Other Attachments" designated section
- No fullscreen modal per section
- Upload form is a single flat row — no indication of which section it belongs to

#### D.2 Target Layout Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ TOP: 2-COLUMN                                               │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐  ┌──────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │ KEY DOCUMENTS        │  │ GALLERY                      │ │
│  │ - upload form        │  │ - profile images (max 3)     │ │
│  │ - list: KD typecodes │  │ - general gallery grid       │ │
│  │ - expand to fullscr. │  │ - upload form                │ │
│  └──────────────────────┘  └──────────────────────────────┘ │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ MIDDLE: FULL WIDTH                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │ DOCUMENT COMPLIANCE CHECKLIST (accordion + remarks)    ││
│  │ - grouped by category                                  ││
│  │ - per-group remarks field (admin/staff editable)       ││
│  │ - linked file download chip                            ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ BOTTOM: FULL WIDTH                                          │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐│
│  │ OTHER ATTACHMENTS                                      ││
│  │ - general documents (non-key-doc types)                ││
│  │ - external links                                       ││
│  │ - MOV evidence list (read-only, links to modal)        ││
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘│
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

**Key Documents section** (top-left): Documents whose `documentType` matches `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` (same codes used in `detail-[id].vue` overview panel). Upload form pre-populates type selector from this subset.

**Gallery section** (top-right): Existing profile images + general gallery collapsed into one card with `v-tabs` (Profile | General).

**Compliance Checklist** (middle): `CiDocumentChecklist` component embedded in edit page with `canEdit` prop. Currently only shown in detail page — adding it here closes the round-trip UX gap.

**Other Attachments** (bottom): Non-key-doc document types + external links + MOV evidence list.

#### D.3 Fullscreen Modal Governance

Each major section (Key Documents, Gallery, Compliance, Other Attachments) has a "Fullscreen" `<v-btn icon="mdi-fullscreen">` in its card title. Clicking opens a `<v-dialog fullscreen>` containing only that section's content with more vertical space for browsing.

This is a frontend-only UI pattern — no backend changes needed. The dialog reuses the same refs and computed values (shared state, not duplicated).

**Implementation**: `keyDocsFullscreen` / `galleryFullscreen` / `checklistFullscreen` / `otherDocsFullscreen` boolean refs. Each fullscreen dialog renders the same content template via a shared component or named `<template>`.

#### D.4 Responsive Layout

Vuetify `v-row` + `v-col` with breakpoints:
- `cols="12" md="6"` for top 2-column layout
- On mobile (< md), key docs and gallery stack vertically — acceptable
- Compliance and Other Attachments always full width

---

### E. Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

| Flow | Current | Status |
|------|---------|--------|
| Upload in edit Attachments → appears in detail Documents tab | `fetchDocuments()` on mount | ✅ |
| Upload gallery in edit → appears in detail Overview/Gallery tab | `fetchGallery()` on mount | ✅ |
| Checklist `linkedDocumentId` → download link in Documents tab | Not rendered | ❌ BROKEN |
| Checklist group remarks → visible in Documents and Attachments | Not implemented | ❌ MISSING |
| Accordion header shows submission count | Shows "approved count" | ❌ WEAK |
| Timeline: shared date-range filter | 8 fragmented refs | ❌ FRAGMENTED |
| MOV entries aggregated in Attachments tab | Only in per-modal sections | ❌ MISSING |
| Others tab: statusUpdates/readinessDocs/signatories | Inside Basic Info | ❌ NO DEDICATED TAB |
| Key Documents upload in designated section | Mixed with all document types | ❌ NO HIERARCHY |
| Gallery upload beside key docs (2-column) | Sequential cards | ❌ FLAT LAYOUT |

**Stale rendering paths:**
- `documentsByType` computed in `detail-[id].vue` groups all docs by type but the edit page `docsByCategory` does the same — two parallel implementations of the same grouping logic (DRY violation; not urgent).
- `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` defined as `const` in `detail-[id].vue` — must be extracted to `utils/adapters.ts` or `utils/coiFormState.ts` so the edit page can share the same codes for the Key Documents section.

**Regression risks:**
1. Moving `statusUpdateRows`/`readinessDocRows`/`signatoryRows` from `CiBasicInfoForm` to Others tab requires removing them from `CiBasicInfoForm`'s props — form submit payload unchanged (arrays still bound to same refs in parent).
2. Adding `documents` prop to `CiDocumentChecklist` is additive and non-breaking.
3. Embedding `CiDocumentChecklist` in edit page requires auth: `canEdit` is already available as `computed(() => canEdit('coi'))`.
4. Splitting upload form into Key Docs vs Other Attachments requires two `v-select` instances with different item filters — same underlying `documentTypes` ref, different filter computeds.

---

### F. Priority Decision

**Execution order by dependency and governance impact:**

| Priority | Phase | Reason |
|----------|-------|--------|
| 1 | KV-A: Timeline unified filter | Foundational — shared state; downstream phases may open both dialogs simultaneously |
| 2 | KV-B: Checklist file access (linked document download link + document picker) | Highest usability impact; zero backend changes |
| 3 | KV-C: Accordion metadata (submission count + latest date + file count) | Pairs with KV-B in CiDocumentChecklist |
| 4 | KV-D: Per-group remarks (JSONB migration + sub-route + UI) | Requires backend migration — isolate as own phase |
| 5 | KV-E: Attachment tab layout refactor (2-column + compliance embed + other section) | Highest scope — restructures edit page |
| 6 | KV-F: Others tab creation (move statusUpdates/readinessDocs/signatories) | Additive — safe to isolate |
| 7 | KV-G: Fullscreen modal governance (per-section fullscreen dialogs) | Pure UI — zero logic dependencies |

KV-D (remarks migration) is the only phase requiring a DB migration. It must be tested separately from pure frontend phases.

---

*Section 2.161 — Phase KV Research complete. Phase KV plan follows in plan.md.*

---

### A. Timeline Tab — Enterprise Governance Model

#### A.1 Conceptual Role of the Timeline Tab

The Timeline tab is the **operational transparency ledger** for the construction project. It is not a milestone tracker — it is the primary audit surface for:
- Construction progress (contractor self-reporting)
- Independent monitoring (evaluators, ECO, PMO)
- Executive visibility (directors, OVPEO, OVPAF)
- Audit accountability (auditors, oversight, read-only)

Two distinct record types coexist:

| Type | Entity | Who Creates | Frequency |
|------|--------|-------------|-----------|
| **Project Milestones** | `construction_milestones` | Admin, Staff (project managers, PMO) | Phase-driven (contractual) |
| **Work Log / Project Diary** | `construction_timeline_entries` | Staff, contractors, monitoring personnel | Daily/Weekly/Monthly/Quarterly |

#### A.2 Role-Based Timeline Access Matrix

| Role | Create Milestone | Create Work Log | View All | MOV Entry | Approve/Verify MOV |
|------|-----------------|-----------------|----------|-----------|-------------------|
| SuperAdmin | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Admin | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Staff (assigned) | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ |
| Staff (not assigned) | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ (published) | ❌ | ❌ |
| Viewer | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ (published) | ❌ | ❌ |
| Auditor | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ (all) | ❌ | ❌ |

**Constructor/Contractor:** Maps to Staff role with project assignment. Can create Work Log entries (diary) only. Cannot create or modify milestones.
**Evaluator/ECO/Monitoring:** Maps to Staff (assigned) — can create Work Log entries with `entryType = 'EVALUATION'` or `'MONITORING'`. MOV entries are their primary accountability trail.
**PMO/OVPEO/OVPAF/Directors:** Maps to Admin or Viewer — can view all timeline entries, approve MOV verification.
**Division Chiefs/Oversight:** Maps to Auditor or Viewer — read-only access to full timeline.

#### A.3 MOV/Evidence Architecture

**Link-only approach** (validated recommendation):
- `construction_mov_entries` table already exists (Phase KO/KS migration)
- Fields: `movLink`, `movTitle`, `movDescription`, `evidenceCategory`, `entryDate`, `uploadedBy`, `verificationStatus`, `remarks`
- Architecture is correct — no file storage
- Missing: `verificationStatus` workflow UI (currently stored but never rendered for admin approval)

**Gap**: MOV entries are only accessible inside per-milestone and per-work-log modals. No aggregated MOV view exists at the project level or Attachments tab level.

#### A.4 Filter Architecture (Current State + Gaps)

**Implemented (KV-A):** Shared `sharedPeriod` / `sharedDateFrom` / `sharedDateTo` + `periodBounds` computed applying to both `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog`.

**Gap — Filter visibility**: The period filter bar is in the Timeline tab body. When users click "View All Milestones" or "View All Work Logs", the dialogs open over the tab. The filter bar is now hidden. Users cannot change the period filter from within the dialog.

**Fix needed**: Either (a) duplicate filter controls inside each dialog header, or (b) move the period bar into the dialog itself (better UX — filter is contextual to the collection being browsed). Shared state remains at page level; the dialogs render the same refs.

#### A.5 Timeline Visualization

Current: stat cards (total milestones, completed, in-progress, delayed; total work logs, DAILY/WEEKLY counts, MONTHLY/QUARTERLY counts). Both sets implemented in KM-C and KR-A.

Enhancement needed: Add per-period accomplishment summary — "This month: N work logs, M milestones progressed." Links to the shared filter so clicking a stat card auto-applies the corresponding period filter.

---

### B. Overview Image Normalization

#### B.1 Current State

`detail-[id].vue` renders two separate gallery sections in the Overview tab:
1. **Profile Images strip** (`profileImages` computed, `category === 'PROFILE'`, max 3) — `detail-[id].vue:773`
2. **Recent Gallery strip** (`recentGalleryImages` computed, non-PROFILE, max 6 thumbnails) — `detail-[id].vue:809`

This creates a double-image surface in Overview. The Recent Gallery duplicates content from the Gallery tab.

#### B.2 Recommendation

Remove the "Recent Gallery" card from Overview entirely. Keep only the Profile Images strip (max 3, shown only when `profileImages.length > 0`).

The Profile strip compact layout is already correct: responsive column count by `profileImages.length` (1→12, 2→6, 3→4). No restructuring needed — only removal of the `recentGalleryImages` card.

**Fullscreen preview**: Add a `<v-dialog>` on profile image click for fullscreen preview. Currently images are not clickable. A simple click handler opens a dialog with the full-size image.

**Impact**: `recentGalleryImages` computed can stay in script for potential future reuse — only the template card is removed.

---

### C. Timeline Filter Failure Audit

#### C.1 Root Cause Analysis

The filter-failure report refers to the state of timeline dialog filters. Three stacked issues:

**Issue 1 — Filter bar invisible in dialog context:**
The `sharedPeriod` `v-btn-toggle` renders in `<v-window-item value="timeline">` body at `detail-[id].vue:1321`. When `milestonesDialogOpen = true`, the `<v-dialog>` opens over the entire viewport — the filter bar is behind the dialog overlay and inaccessible. The filter still applies (shared refs), but users cannot see or change it.

**Issue 2 — No filter state indicator inside dialog:**
The dialog header (`All Milestones`) does not show the current active filter (e.g., "Showing: Monthly (May 2026)"). Users have no feedback that filtering is active.

**Issue 3 — Gallery inner tabs unconnected:**
`<v-tabs>` at `edit-[id].vue:2045` has no `v-model`. `<v-window>` at `edit-[id].vue:2049` has no `v-model`. Both are rendered but are not linked. Clicking "Profile" / "General" tab has zero effect — the window doesn't switch. All content renders simultaneously (not tabbed).

#### C.2 Fix Strategy

- Issue 1: Add compact filter chips inside each dialog's `v-card-title` area showing active period + a "Change" button that closes the dialog, lets user change filter, then user re-opens dialog. OR: embed the `v-btn-toggle` inside the dialog directly (reusing same `sharedPeriod` ref — zero duplication).
- Issue 2: Add a dismissible chip in dialog title: `Active filter: {{ sharedPeriod !== 'ALL' ? sharedPeriod : 'All dates' }}`.
- Issue 3: Add `const galleryActiveTab = ref('profile')` and bind `v-model="galleryActiveTab"` to both `<v-tabs>` and `<v-window>`.

---

### D. "Others" Tab — Governance Rework

#### D.1 Current State

Phase KV-F moved three administrative JSONB arrays (statusUpdateRows, readinessDocRows, signatoryRows) from CiBasicInfoForm into the Others tab. These are supplementary administrative records — not an operational monitoring space.

#### D.2 Recommended Architecture — High-Priority Project Monitoring Space

The "Others" tab should be renamed and restructured into a **Project Monitoring & Governance Log** surface:

**Section 1 — Incident & Special Concerns Log**
- Free-text entries with: date, severity (Low/Medium/High/Critical), description, reported_by, resolution_status
- Stored as JSONB `incident_log` on `construction_projects` or as a new table
- No new backend required if JSONB; a new table if searchable/exportable

**Section 2 — Risk Register**
- risk_description, likelihood, impact, mitigation, owner, status
- Same storage decision (JSONB vs table)

**Section 3 — Escalation Records**
- escalated_to (person), escalation_date, issue, resolution
- JSONB adequate for MVP

**Section 4 — Administrative Records (relocated from Basic Info)**
- Status Updates, Readiness Documents, Signatories (current content)
- Demote to a collapsed sub-section at bottom of Others tab

**Storage recommendation (KISS):** JSONB columns per category on `construction_projects`. No new tables needed for MVP — one `ALTER TABLE` migration adds 3 JSONB columns. Can be migrated to separate tables when search/reporting requirements emerge.

---

### E. Attachment Tab — Architecture Findings

#### E.1 Current Gallery Tab Bug

The inner gallery tabs (Profile/General) in the Attachments tab are broken: `<v-tabs>` and `<v-window>` have no shared `v-model`. This is the highest-priority fix in the Attachments tab.

**Fix:** Add `const galleryActiveTab = ref<'profile' | 'general'>('profile')` and bind to both elements.

#### E.2 Manual Document Naming

**Current:** Key Documents upload uses `<v-select>` bound to `docType` from `keyDocTypeOptions`. This forces staff to pick from a predefined taxonomy.

**Requirement:** Replace the document type select with a **free-text title field** (`docTitle` already exists) as the primary identifier, with document type as optional classification (still sent to backend but not required for upload).

**Implementation:** Change the Key Documents upload form to:
- Required: file input + `docTitle` (free text, becomes the document name)
- Optional: `docType` select (still sends taxonomy code if selected, defaults to `'other'` if blank)

This keeps backend compatibility while allowing flexible naming.

#### E.3 Shared `docFile`/`docType` Ref Collision

Both Key Documents and Other Attachments upload forms bind to the same `docFile` and `docType` refs. Uploading from one section leaves the other section's type selection reset to the default.

**Fix:** Add separate refs: `keyDocFile`, `keyDocType`, `keyDocTitle` for the Key Documents section. The existing `docFile`/`docType`/`docTitle` remain for Other Attachments.

#### E.4 MOV Evidence Aggregation

MOV entries exist per-milestone and per-work-log (accessed from modals). No project-level MOV summary exists in the Attachments tab. The bottom "Other Attachments" section should include a read-only "MOV Evidence" sub-section: a flat list of all `construction_mov_entries` for the project (fetched from `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries`).

---

### F. Step-Save Governance

#### F.1 Current State

`edit-[id].vue` has one `handleSubmit()` function that submits the entire form. The "Save Changes" button exists only in the Others tab (moved there in KV). The Previous/Next footer navigation exists across all tabs. No per-tab save exists.

#### F.2 Architecture Options

| Option | Complexity | UX | Risk |
|--------|-----------|-----|------|
| **PATCH per-tab** — each tab has its own Save button that sends only that tab's fields | Medium | Best | Low (idempotent PATCH) |
| **Auto-save on tab change** — blur/leave triggers PATCH | High | Seamless | Medium (race conditions) |
| **Keep single-submit, add Save button to each tab** — all tabs share the same `handleSubmit()` | Low | Acceptable | None |

**Recommendation (KISS):** Option 3 — add a "Save Changes" button to each tab's window-item. All call the same `handleSubmit()`. No architecture change to backend. Users can save from any tab without navigating to Others. The existing "Step: X of 8" progress bar already communicates state.

The "Save Changes" button currently lives only inside the Others `v-window-item`. Moving it to a sticky footer or duplicating it per tab is the implementation path.

---

### G. Personnel Authorization Architecture

#### G.1 Current State

`useCoiAccess(project)` composable returns: `canEditCurrentProject` (owner/assigned or Admin), `isOwnerOrAssigned`, `canEditAnyTab`. All tabs share the same edit gate. No per-tab permission exists.

Staff users who are assigned to a project can edit all tabs — including Milestones, Financial, Documents, Personnel. This is too permissive for roles like "Read-only Auditor" or "Monitoring Personnel."

#### G.2 Role Hierarchy Recommendation

Proposed tab-level permissions:

| Tab | Admin | Staff (assigned) | Viewer | Auditor |
|-----|-------|-----------------|--------|---------|
| Basic Info | Edit | Edit | View | View |
| Schedule | Edit | Edit | View | View |
| Timeline (Milestones) | Edit | View | View | View |
| Timeline (Work Log) | Edit | Edit | View | View |
| POW | Edit | View | View | View |
| Financial | Edit | View | View | View |
| Attachments | Edit | Edit | View | View |
| Personnel | Edit | View | View | View |
| Others | Edit | Edit | View | View |

**Implementation path:** Extend `useCoiAccess()` to return per-tab edit flags. `canEditMilestones` = isAdmin. `canEditWorkLog` = isAdmin || isOwnerOrAssigned. `canEditFinancial` = isAdmin. These flags are passed to sub-components.

#### G.3 Integration with User Management

The Administration/User Management page manages system-level roles (SuperAdmin, Admin, Staff, Viewer, Auditor). COI project-level assignment is separate — managed via `record_assignments` table.

Gap: There is no UI in the project Personnel tab that allows directly granting/revoking edit access per-tab. The current Personnel tab manages assignment (who is on the project) but not capability (what they can do). These should be linked — the Personnel tab shows assigned users with their system role and derived tab-level capabilities as a read-only capability matrix.

---

### I. Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

| Sync Path | State |
|-----------|-------|
| Upload in edit Attachments → detail Documents | ✅ Synchronized (same API endpoint) |
| Gallery upload in edit → detail Overview Profile strip | ✅ Synchronized (PROFILE category) |
| Gallery upload in edit → detail Gallery tab | ✅ Synchronized (non-PROFILE category) |
| Recent gallery in Overview → Gallery tab | ❌ BROKEN — detail Overview still shows `recentGalleryImages` card (should be removed) |
| Checklist file link → download chip in detail Documents | ✅ Implemented (KV-B) |
| Per-group remarks → persisted via PATCH | ✅ Implemented (KV-D), pending DB migration |
| Shared timeline filter → milestones + work log dialogs | ⚠️ Filter active but invisible inside dialogs |
| Gallery inner tabs in edit Attachments | ❌ BROKEN — `v-tabs` / `v-window` unlinked |
| Separate upload refs (Key Docs vs Other Docs) | ❌ BROKEN — single `docFile`/`docType` shared between both sections |
| MOV evidence aggregated view in Attachments | ❌ Not implemented |
| Others tab → operational monitoring | ⚠️ Contains admin records, not monitoring content |
| Per-tab save capability | ❌ Not implemented |
| Personnel tab-level authorization | ❌ Not implemented |

---

### K. Priority Decision

Execution order for Phase KW:

| Priority | Sub-phase | Scope |
|----------|-----------|-------|
| 1 | KW-A: Bug fixes (gallery tabs + docFile collision) | 30-min fixes, prevents broken UX |
| 2 | KW-B: Overview Recent Gallery removal | Single card removal from detail page |
| 3 | KW-C: Filter visibility in dialogs | Add period indicator + mini-filter inside dialogs |
| 4 | KW-D: Manual document naming (Key Docs) | Replace select with free-text in Key Docs upload |
| 5 | KW-E: Per-tab Save button | Duplicate Save Changes button per window-item |
| 6 | KW-F: Others tab rework (monitoring space + admin records) | Add 3 monitoring sections; admin records demoted |
| 7 | KW-G: MOV evidence aggregated view in Attachments | Read-only list fetched from existing endpoint |
| 8 | KW-H: Personnel tab-level authorization | Extend `useCoiAccess()` + per-component prop |

KW-A is prerequisite for everything else. KW-B and KW-C are independent and parallelizable. KW-D through KW-H are sequential by dependency.

---
