## Section 2.163 — Phase KX: Timeline Filter Stabilization, Gallery Rendering Fix, DTO Governance Normalization

**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** VERIFY_STEP KW + Timeline filter root-cause analysis, Gallery image loading failure, DTO validation failure (Others tab), Project image normalization, MOV governance in milestone/diary dialogs

---

### F. VERIFY_STEP KW — Implementation Audit

#### F.1 Verified Working

| Feature | Evidence |
|---------|----------|
| Gallery inner tabs linked (galleryActiveTab v-model) | edit-[id].vue — galleryActiveTab ref bound to v-tabs and v-window |
| Key Documents: separate keyDocFile/keyDocType/keyDocTitle refs | uploadKeyDoc() function present |
| Key Documents: free-text title as primary field | v-text-field v-model="keyDocTitle" in upload form |
| Per-tab Save Changes button on every window-item | 7 + 1 save buttons confirmed |
| Overview "Recent Gallery" card removed | detail-[id].vue — recentGalleryImages card entirely absent |
| Profile image fullscreen preview dialog | profilePreviewOpen ref + dialog at page level |
| Filter indicator chips in both dialog titles | sharedPeriod chip in milestone + work log dialog titles |
| Mini period toggle inside both dialogs | v-btn-toggle v-model="sharedPeriod" in both dialog areas |
| Others tab: Incident Log, Risk Register, Escalation Records cards | edit-[id].vue — all 3 monitoring sections present |
| Others tab: Administrative Records in collapsed panel | v-expansion-panels wrapping existing rows |
| Detail Others: 4 read-only monitoring sections | Incident, Risk, Escalation, Administrative all present |
| MOV evidence aggregated view in Attachments tab | fetchMovEntries(), movEntries ref, MOV sub-section present |
| useCoiAccess.ts per-tab flags | canEditMilestones, canEditWorkLog, canEditFinancial, canEditPow, canEditPersonnel all exported |
| Add Milestone gated by canEditMilestones | v-if="canEditMilestones" on button |
| Add Record (financial) gated by canEditFinancial | v-if="canEditFinancial" on button |
| Personnel v-autocomplete gated by canEditPersonnel | v-if="canEditPersonnel" on assignment control |
| MikroORM migration 070000 created | Migration20260514070000_AddProjectMonitoringFields.ts file exists |
| Entity fields incidentLog, riskRegister, escalationRecords | construction-project.entity.ts — 3 new JSONB properties |
| Adapter UIProjectDetail includes monitoring fields | adapters.ts interface + mapping updated |

#### F.2 Broken — Critical

| Feature | Root Cause | Severity |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| DTO validation failure on PATCH | incident_log, risk_register, escalation_records NOT in CreateConstructionProjectDto or UpdateConstructionProjectDto. Backend ValidationPipe uses forbidNonWhitelisted: true — unknown properties throw HTTP 400. The entity and migration were added but the DTO was never updated. Every handleSubmit() call from Others tab fails. | CRITICAL |
| Gallery images stuck in loading state | edit-[id].vue EditGalleryItem interface has filePath: string but backend ConstructionGallery entity returns imageUrl. All gallery v-img elements bind :src="img.filePath" — img.filePath is undefined. Images produce broken/loading state permanently. | CRITICAL |

#### F.3 Working But Needs Rework

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| Timeline filter: date boundary errors | filteredMilestones and filteredWorkLog compare ISO datetime strings ("2026-05-14T00:00:00.000Z") against plain date strings ("2026-05-14"). String comparison fails for boundary dates: "2026-05-31T00:00:00.000Z" <= "2026-05-31" is false because "T" > "" at position 10. Last-day entries are incorrectly excluded. Fix: .slice(0, 10) on date fields before comparison. |
| Timeline filter: v-btn-toggle deselection | Vuetify 3 v-btn-toggle allows deselection by default — clicking the active button sets the model to undefined. When sharedPeriod becomes undefined: undefined !== 'ALL' is true, periodBounds returns { from:'', to:'' }, filter uses empty strings. Fix: add :mandatory="true" to all three v-btn-toggle instances. |
| Project image space dominance | Profile images use aspect-ratio="16/9" full-column — with 1 image this is very tall. Fix: fixed height="160" compact horizontal strip. |

#### F.4 Not Implemented

| Feature | Note |
|---------|------|
| MOV links in Add Milestone dialog | milestoneDialog has no MOV section — staff cannot add MOV when creating milestones |
| MOV links in Add Diary Entry dialog | diaryDialog has no MOV section |
| Document Compliance Checklist title deduplication | CiDocumentChecklist renders its own title card; edit/detail pages wrap it in another card with same title |

---

### E. DTO Validation Failure — Root Cause

Backend main.ts ValidationPipe: `{ whitelist: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true, transform: true }`.

forbidNonWhitelisted causes HTTP 400 for any property NOT in the DTO. CreateConstructionProjectDto ends at signatories (line 212). incident_log, risk_register, escalation_records are in the entity and adapter but NOT the DTO.

UpdateConstructionProjectDto extends PartialType(CreateConstructionProjectDto) — only inherits declared fields.

Fix: Add three @IsOptional() @IsArray() properties to CreateConstructionProjectDto.

Also: the service update() method must map these DTO fields to the entity. Verify that construction-projects.service.ts update() reads and sets incidentLog, riskRegister, escalationRecords from dto fields.

---

### C. Gallery Image Loading — Root Cause

EditGalleryItem interface in edit-[id].vue:
```ts
interface EditGalleryItem { id: string; filePath: string; caption?: string; category?: string }
```

Backend ConstructionGallery entity field: `imageUrl!: string` (construction-gallery.entity.ts:12).

Gallery template uses: `:src="img.filePath"` → img.filePath is undefined → v-img placeholder never resolves.

detail-[id].vue is CORRECT: uses adaptGalleryItem() from adapters.ts which maps imageUrl correctly.

Fix: Change EditGalleryItem.filePath to imageUrl throughout edit-[id].vue.

---

### B. Timeline Filter Root Cause

Two distinct issues:

1. Date comparison: ISO datetime vs plain date string. Last-day boundary fails.
   Fix: Apply .slice(0, 10) to m.targetDate and e.entryDate before comparison.

2. v-btn-toggle deselection: clicking active button → sharedPeriod = undefined → filter appears broken.
   Fix: :mandatory="true" on all three v-btn-toggle instances.

---

### A. Overview Project Image Normalization

Current: aspect-ratio="16/9" per image, responsive column width (1→full, 2→half, 3→4-col).
Issue: 1 image = full width 16:9 = ~360px tall on typical viewport.

Recommendation: Replace aspect-ratio with height="160" fixed. Keep 3 images always in horizontal strip (cols="4" always). Card max-height capped. Fullscreen preview already implemented (KW-B2).

---

### D. MOV in Milestone/Diary Dialogs

CiMovEvidenceSection component exists (Phase KO). Currently mounted in detail page modals only.
Edit page milestone and diary dialogs need a post-save MOV section.

Architecture: After saveMilestone() succeeds, show CiMovEvidenceSection with relatedEntityType="MILESTONE" and :related-entity-id="milestoneEditingId". Close button becomes "Done". Same pattern for diary entries.

Constraint: MOV section requires entity ID — cannot be shown before save. The dialog must remain open post-save with a Done button, or show a secondary "Add Evidence" step.

---

### I. Priority Decision

| Priority | Phase | Issue | Type |
|----------|-------|-------|------|
| 1 | KX-A | Gallery imageUrl fix | Frontend critical |
| 2 | KX-B | DTO + service update for monitoring fields | Backend critical |
| 3 | KX-C | Timeline filter: slice(0,10) + mandatory toggle | Frontend |
| 4 | KX-D | Project image compact layout | Frontend UX |
| 5 | KX-E | Checklist title deduplication | Frontend cosmetic |
| 6 | KX-F | MOV in milestone/diary edit dialogs | Frontend |

---

*Section 2.163 — Phase KX Research complete. Phase KX plan follows in plan.md.*

## Section 2.164 — Phase KY: File Serving Architecture, JSONB Storage Fix, Others Tab Sync, External Link Normalization

**Date:** 2026-05-14
**Scope:** VERIFY_STEP KX + File 404 root cause, gallery rendering root cause, JSONB double-wrapping `[[]]` root cause, Others tab synchronization failure, external link validation normalization

---

### VERIFY_STEP KX — Implementation Audit

#### Verified Working

| Feature | Evidence |
|---------|----------|
| EditGalleryItem interface uses imageUrl | edit-[id].vue interface corrected |
| 4 img.imageUrl bindings in template | all :src="img.filePath" replaced |
| incident_log / risk_register / escalation_records in CreateConstructionProjectDto | @IsOptional @IsArray fields added |
| All three monitoring fields added to jsonFields in service update() | 'incident_log', 'risk_register', 'escalation_records', 'document_checklist_remarks' in jsonFields array |
| .slice(0, 10) on date comparisons in filteredMilestones and filteredWorkLog | normalization applied to both computeds |
| :mandatory="true" on all 3 v-btn-toggle instances | 3 occurrences confirmed |
| Profile images height="160" compact strip | aspect-ratio removed, fixed height applied |
| CiDocumentChecklist outer card title removed | replaced with simple div wrapper + fullscreen button |
| CiMovEvidenceSection in milestone dialog post-save | milestoneJustSavedId ref + component mounted |
| CiMovEvidenceSection in diary dialog post-save | diaryJustSavedId ref + component mounted |

#### Broken — Critical

| Feature | Root Cause | Severity |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| File/document 404 on download | No static file serving endpoint. Backend stores files on disk (./uploads/). Stored filePath is a RELATIVE filesystem path (e.g., construction_gallery/projectId/uuid_name.pdf). No app.useStaticAssets() in main.ts. No /uploads proxy in nuxt.config.ts. Browser resolves relative path against current page URL (/coi/*), producing /coi/uuid_name.pdf — not served by anything. | CRITICAL |
| Gallery images stuck in loading state | SAME root cause as file 404. Even with imageUrl field fix (KX-A), the imageUrl value is the same relative path stored in DB (e.g., construction_gallery/projectId/uuid.jpg). v-img :src="construction_gallery/..." resolves to /coi/construction_gallery/... → no server handler → image loads indefinitely. | CRITICAL |
| JSONB double-wrapping '[[]]' in monitoring fields | Confirmed from UPDATE query log. status_updates = '[[]]', incident_log = '[[]]' etc. Root cause: When frontend sends empty arrays [] for these fields, the NestJS service includes them in fields ([] !== undefined is TRUE if the DTO property is initialized as [] by class-transformer). JSON.stringify([]) = '[]'. But the stored result is '[[]]'. Most likely: class-transformer with enableImplicitConversion: true is wrapping each array element, and for empty arrays creates [[]] when the form sends status_updates as a one-element array containing an empty object/array due to Vue reactivity initialization. Fix: explicitly exclude empty arrays from the payload using `|| undefined` pattern, AND fix class-transformer behavior by adding @Type decorator for nested DTOs. | HIGH |
| Others tab data not visible in Project Details | Caused by JSONB corruption above — data IS stored but as [[]] (one row of empty object). The adapter maps incidentLog: [[]] — the detail page iterates [[]], gets r=[] (empty array), renders one empty row with no visible text. Appears as "nothing" but is actually corrupt data rendering with undefined fields. | HIGH |

#### Working But Needs Rework

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| External link validation | Current validation in submitEditLink() uses EDIT_EXTERNAL_URL_REGEX = /^https?:\/\/.+/i — this is correct and already generic. BUT the hint text says "Google Drive, SharePoint, or any https:// URL" which implies it's restricted. The regex itself is fine; the UX hint needs updating to clarify ALL https:// URLs are accepted. |
| Timeline filter: mandatory toggle | Implemented. But period 'DAILY' button exists in tab-level toggle but NOT in in-dialog mini toggles. Daily period may be useful. Minor inconsistency — non-breaking. |

---

### B. File Serving Architecture — Root Cause Analysis

**StorageService.saveFile() returns:**
```ts
filePath: path.relative(this.uploadDir, filePath)
// Example: 'construction_gallery/projectId/uuid_name.pdf'
```

This relative path is stored in the DB as the document/gallery URL and passed directly to the frontend.

**main.ts has NO static file serving:**
```ts
// No app.useStaticAssets() or ServeStaticModule
// Only: app.setGlobalPrefix('api')
```

**nuxt.config.ts Nitro devProxy:**
```ts
'/api': { target: 'http://localhost:3000/api', changeOrigin: true }
// No /uploads proxy
```

**Result**: When frontend renders `<a href="construction_gallery/projectId/uuid.pdf">` on page `/coi/detail-x`, browser resolves to `/coi/construction_gallery/projectId/uuid.pdf`. Nuxt router has no matching page. 404.

The specific 404 URL `/coi/{uuid}_{filename}.pdf` indicates files were uploaded WITHOUT entityType/entityId (saved to root ./uploads/), making filePath = just `{uuid}_{filename}.pdf`. On page `/coi/*`, relative URL = `/coi/{uuid}_{filename}.pdf`.

**Required fix — two-part:**

1. **Backend**: Add `app.useStaticAssets(uploadDir, { prefix: '/uploads' })` to serve files from the uploads directory. This makes files accessible at `http://localhost:3000/uploads/relative_path`.

2. **Frontend Nuxt proxy**: Add `/uploads` proxy in `nitro.devProxy`:
   ```ts
   '/uploads': { target: 'http://localhost:3000/uploads', changeOrigin: true }
   ```

3. **StorageService**: Change returned filePath to INCLUDE the `/uploads/` prefix:
   ```ts
   filePath: `/uploads/${path.relative(this.uploadDir, filePath)}`
   ```
   This makes stored paths absolute relative to server root, e.g., `/uploads/construction_gallery/projectId/uuid.pdf`. Frontend uses these as `href="/uploads/..."` — resolved to `http://frontend:3001/uploads/...` → proxied to `http://localhost:3000/uploads/...` → NestJS serves the file.

**Note**: Existing files in DB have paths WITHOUT the `/uploads/` prefix. A one-time migration or a frontend guard that prepends `/uploads/` when `filePath` doesn't start with `http` or `/uploads` would handle legacy data.

---

### D. Gallery Rendering — Root Cause

Gallery images store `imageUrl: uploadResult.filePath`. With the KX-A fix, edit-[id].vue now uses `img.imageUrl`. But `img.imageUrl` = relative path like `construction_gallery/projectId/uuid.jpg`. Without the file serving fix (KY-A), `<v-img :src="construction_gallery/...">` fails to load.

The fix is the SAME as B — once `/uploads/` prefix is included in stored paths and the Nuxt proxy routes `/uploads` to the backend, gallery images will resolve and render correctly.

---

### E. JSONB Double-Wrapping — Root Cause

**From the SQL UPDATE log:**
```sql
status_updates = '[[]]', incident_log = '[[]]' ...
```

`'[[]]'` = `JSON.stringify([[]])` = the service received `dto.field = [[]]` (array containing empty array).

**Chain of events traced:**

1. Frontend initializes `statusUpdateRows.value = []` (empty)
2. Frontend sends `status_updates: statusUpdateRows.value.length > 0 ? statusUpdateRows.value : undefined`
3. With empty rows: sends `status_updates: undefined` → JSON strips it → NOT in request body
4. NestJS DTO: `dto.status_updates = undefined`
5. Service: `fields = Object.keys(dto).filter(k => dto[k] !== undefined)` → `status_updates` excluded
6. NOT in UPDATE

This chain should work. But the UPDATE DOES include `[[]]`. Contradiction.

**Most likely root cause**: The `enableImplicitConversion: true` option in ValidationPipe causes class-transformer to process `@IsArray()` fields. For `Record<string, any>[]` WITHOUT `@Type()` decorator, when class-transformer encounters a non-undefined value, it may call `plainToClass` logic that wraps scalar-or-array values. If the frontend is sending `[]` (not `undefined`), class-transformer might transform `[]` → `[[]]` via `Array.from` or similar internal behavior for complex generic types.

**Simpler root cause**: The frontend IS sending `[]` (not `undefined`) because Vue's `computed()` or `ref()` proxies return `[]` as a truthy object even when length is 0. In JavaScript, `[].length > 0` = false. BUT if `statusUpdateRows.value` is a Vue reactive proxy of `[]`, `Proxy([]).length` still equals `0`. No difference.

**Most actionable fix**: Change the service's `fields` filter to also exclude empty arrays:
```ts
const fields = Object.keys(dto).filter(
  (k) =>
    dto[k] !== undefined &&
    !(Array.isArray(dto[k]) && (dto[k] as any[]).length === 0) &&
    k !== 'assigned_user_ids' &&
    k !== 'assignments',
);
```

This prevents empty arrays from being written to the DB as `'[]'` or `'[[]]'`, preserving whatever was previously stored.

**Alternative fix**: In `StorageService`, ensure the filePath is consistent. But for JSONB corruption, the service fix is the right lever.

**Additionally**: Existing corrupted `[[]]` data in DB needs to be cleaned. Either via a SQL migration (`UPDATE construction_projects SET incident_log = '[]'::jsonb WHERE incident_log = '[[]]'::jsonb`) or by the adapter providing safe mapping with normalization.

---

### F. Others Tab Synchronization

The Others tab data (incidentLog, riskRegister, escalationRecords) IS being saved (UPDATE succeeds per log). But when the detail page renders, data appears empty.

Root cause: JSONB corruption (`[[]]`). The adapter maps `incidentLog: backend.incident_log || []` = `[[]]`. Detail page template: `v-for="(r, i) in project.incidentLog"` → `r = []` (empty array), `r.severity = undefined` → empty chip, empty description text. Visually appears as nothing meaningful.

Fix: Normalize in adapter — filter out non-object items from monitoring arrays:
```ts
incidentLog: (backend.incident_log || []).filter((r: any) => r && typeof r === 'object' && !Array.isArray(r)),
```

This prevents corrupt `[[]]` from rendering empty rows in the detail view.

---

### A. Project Image Normalization — Verification

KX-D implemented `height="160"` with `cols="4"` always. This is verified working. The profile images strip is now compact. Fullscreen preview works (KW-B2). This is confirmed working.

---

### G. Cross-Flow Continuity

| Sync Path | State |
|-----------|-------|
| Document upload → download URL | ❌ BROKEN — relative path, no file server |
| Gallery upload → image render (edit + detail) | ❌ BROKEN — relative path, no file server |
| Others tab save → detail page render | ⚠️ Partial — data saves but [[]] corrupt |
| Timeline filter: date normalization | ✅ Fixed in KX-C |
| Timeline filter: toggle deselection | ✅ Fixed in KX-C |
| Profile image compact layout | ✅ Fixed in KX-D |
| Gallery inner tabs switching | ✅ Fixed in KW-A1 |
| DTO forbidNonWhitelisted fix | ✅ Fixed in KX-B1 |
| External link validation | ✅ Working (https:// accepted) |
| MOV in milestone/diary dialogs | ✅ Fixed in KX-F |

---

### I. Priority Decision

| Priority | Phase | Scope | Type |
|----------|-------|-------|------|
| 1 | KY-A | File serving: main.ts useStaticAssets + StorageService /uploads prefix + Nuxt proxy | Backend + Frontend config |
| 2 | KY-B | JSONB empty-array filter in service update() + adapter normalization | Backend + Frontend |
| 3 | KY-C | External link UX: update hint text, rename "Google Drive" references to "External Link" | Frontend |
| 4 | KY-D | Existing [[]] data cleanup migration | Backend DB |

KY-A is prerequisite for KY-D (can't verify data until files render). KY-A and KY-B are independent.

---

*Section 2.164 — Phase KY Research complete. Phase KY plan follows in plan.md.*

## Section 2.165 — Phase LA: Timeline View Governance, Project Image Optimization, Others Tab Synchronization Audit

**Date:** 2026-05-18
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE — VERIFY_STEP KZ confirmed operational (migrations applied). Research covers: A) Project Image Preview, B) Timeline Filter Layout, C) Timeline Table/Calendar Views, D) Key Document Field Normalization, E) MOV/Progress Summary, F) Others Tab Sync, G) KC Continuity Audit, H) Cross-Flow Validation.
**Baseline:** KZ ✅ complete. File serving operational (useStaticAssets + /uploads proxy). Path migration run. Migrations 060000–090000 applied.

---

### VERIFY_STEP KZ — Operator Confirmed

KZ declared ✅ verified and functional per operator confirmation. Migration 090000 applied. Migrations 060000–080000 applied.

---

### A. Project Image Preview — Current State Audit

**Current implementation (`detail-[id].vue`):**
```
profileImages (computed) → gallery.filter(g => g.category === 'PROFILE')
Rendered: v-row > v-col cols="4" > v-img height="160" cover + caption text
Fullscreen: profilePreviewImg/profilePreviewOpen refs → v-dialog with single v-img
```

**Gaps identified:**
| Gap | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| No image title | Only `img.caption` shown below image. No structured label/title field |
| No upload date | `UIGalleryItem.uploadedAt` is available but never surfaced in Overview strip |
| No structured metadata | Caption text below image is plain text — no chip, no icon, no hierarchy |
| Fullscreen shows image only | `profilePreviewOpen` dialog has just `v-img` — no metadata, no caption, no date |
| No edit-page parity | Edit page profile strip (in documents tab) uses same basic card pattern |

**Available data:** `UIGalleryItem` has `id`, `imageUrl`, `caption`, `category`, `isFeatured`, `uploadedAt` — all usable.

**Architecture recommendation:**
- Replace the basic `v-col > v-img + caption` with a card-based structure per image:
  - Image renders at fixed height (160px, cover)
  - Card footer: caption as title, `uploadedAt` chip using `formatDate()`
  - Per-image fullscreen button (zoom icon overlay on hover)
- Fullscreen dialog: show image at max width + footer card with caption, upload date
- No new API call needed — all data already in `gallery.value`

---

### B. Timeline Filter — Layout & Grouping Governance

**Current implementation (`detail-[id].vue` timeline tab):**
```
Single d-flex flex-wrap ga-2 row:
  [Filter Period:] [v-btn-toggle: ALL DAILY WEEKLY MONTHLY QUARTERLY]
  [if ALL: v-text-field From] [v-text-field To] [clear btn]
  [else: chip showing periodBounds.from — periodBounds.to]
```

**Period semantics (current, verified operational):**
- ALL → no period restriction (manual date range active)
- DAILY → from=today, to=today
- WEEKLY → from=Monday of current week, to=Sunday of current week
- MONTHLY → from=first day of current month, to=last day of current month
- QUARTERLY → from=first day of current quarter, to=last day of current quarter

**Layout gaps:**
- Date range fields appear inline with toggle without visual separation
- No label distinguishing "Date Range" from period toggle
- On small screens, flex-wrap causes visual fragmentation
- Clear button has no label — icon-only may be missed

**Grouping requirement analysis:**
The prompt requests period filters show "grouped entries" (all entries grouped by day/week/month/quarter) rather than "entries in current period only". This is a **semantic change** from the current implementation:
- Current: DAILY = entries ON today
- Required: DAILY = all entries, GROUPED/ORDERED by day (all dates visible, organized into day buckets)

This requires a different rendering architecture:
- **Option 1 (Recommended):** Add a separate `groupByMode` toggle. When ON, entries are grouped into collapsible sections by the selected period unit. The date range filter remains unchanged.
- **Option 2:** Repurpose DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY to mean "group all entries by this unit" — changes existing verified behavior.

**Decision:** Option 1 preserves verified behavior and adds grouping as additive. Implement `groupByMode` boolean ref.

---

### C. Timeline — Table & Calendar View Architecture

**Current state:** LIST view only. No table columns, no calendar grid.

**Table view requirements:**
| Column | Source | Notes |
|--------|--------|-------|
| Date | `targetDate` / `entryDate` | Formatted |
| Title | `title` / `periodLabel` | Milestone or diary entry |
| Type | `status` / `entryType` | Chip |
| Category | `category` | Small chip |
| Progress | `progress` | For milestones only |
| Actions | — | View detail button |

Vuetify `v-data-table` is available (Vuetify 3 — `v-data-table` with `items`, `headers`, `item-value`). This is the correct component for table view.

**Calendar view requirements:**
- Vuetify 3 does NOT ship a `v-calendar` component by default in Vuetify 3.x (it was removed; `vuetify/labs` has it but is experimental).
- Alternative: Custom month-grid calendar using CSS grid and date arithmetic.
- RECOMMENDATION: Defer calendar view to a separate phase. Implement table view in LA. Calendar view is complex and dependency-risky.

**View-mode toggle architecture:**
```ts
const timelineViewMode = ref<'list' | 'table'>('list')
```
- Single toggle in timeline tab header (List | Table)
- Both views share `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` computeds
- Table view: separate `v-data-table` instances for milestones and work log

---

### D. Key Document Classification — Field Normalization

**Current implementation (`edit-[id].vue` line 2131–2132):**
```html
<v-select v-model="keyDocType" label="Classification (optional)" :items="keyDocTypeOptions" clearable ... />
```

**`keyDocTypeOptions` derivation:**
```ts
const keyDocTypeOptions = computed(() => documentTypeOptions.value.filter(o => keyDocTypeSet.has(o.value)))
```
Where `keyDocTypeSet = new Set(KEY_DOC_TYPECODES)` = `['PROJECT_PROFILE', 'FEASIBILITY_STUDY', 'HGDG_FORM', 'FLOOR_PLAN', 'POW']`.

**Problem:**
- If KB-E taxonomy is not seeded in DB (`documentTypes.value` is empty), `documentTypeOptions` falls back to hardcoded list (Attachment, MOV, Specification, Contract, Other). None of these match KEY_DOC_TYPECODES → `keyDocTypeOptions` is **empty** → v-select shows no options.
- User cannot classify a key document unless taxonomy is seeded.

**Field change requirement:** Replace `v-select` with free-text input.

**Risk of plain v-text-field:**
- If user types free-text (e.g., "My Custom Type"), `documentType` stored in DB will be that free-text string.
- The compliance checklist auto-link (`addDocumentToProject` service) checks `docChecklistRepo` for matching typeCode — free-text won't match any typeCode → no auto-link.
- The `keyDocs` computed (`filter(d => keyDocTypeSet.has(d.documentType ?? ''))`) won't match free-text → document won't appear in Key Documents list.

**Recommended fix:** `v-combobox` — allows both taxonomy selection (from `keyDocTypeOptions`) AND free-text input. When taxonomy is loaded, user gets suggestions. When typing, free-text is accepted. This satisfies the governance requirement while providing flexibility.

**Additional fix:** If taxonomy not seeded, `keyDocTypeOptions` is empty and combobox provides no suggestions. Fallback: add static KEY_DOC label options as a hardcoded list when taxonomy empty.

---

### E. MOV & Progress Summary — Implementation State

**MOV Evidence — Current State:**

| Location | State | Gap |
|----------|-------|-----|
| `detail-[id].vue` milestone detail dialog (KO-D) | ✅ Implemented | MOV shown for any selected milestone |
| `detail-[id].vue` work log detail dialog (KO-E) | ✅ Implemented | MOV shown for any selected diary entry |
| `edit-[id].vue` milestone dialog — CREATE mode (KX-F) | ✅ Implemented | MOV shown AFTER create (`milestoneJustSavedId` set) |
| `edit-[id].vue` milestone dialog — EDIT mode | ❌ NOT shown | When `milestoneDialogMode === 'edit'`, MOV section is hidden (milestoneJustSavedId remains null) |
| `edit-[id].vue` diary dialog — CREATE mode | ✅ Implemented | MOV shown AFTER create (`diaryJustSavedId` set) |
| `edit-[id].vue` diary dialog — EDIT mode | ❌ NOT shown | When editing existing diary, MOV not accessible |

**Fix for edit mode:** When `milestoneDialogMode === 'edit'` and `milestoneEditingId` is set, render `CiMovEvidenceSection` with `milestoneEditingId` as `relatedEntityId`. Same for diary edit mode.

**Progress Summary — Current State:**
- No dedicated Progress Summary card in Timeline tab or Overview tab.
- Physical/financial progress bars exist in the hero card (Overview tab).
- Timeline KPI stat cards (`Total Milestones`, `Completed`, `In Progress`, `Delayed`) exist.
- No cross-field summary combining milestones + physical_progress + days remaining.

**Progress Summary required:** A timeline-level summary card showing:
1. Physical Progress: actual vs target (progress bar)
2. Financial Progress: actual vs target (progress bar)
3. Milestone Completion Rate: X/Y milestones (progress bar)
4. Days Remaining: `targetCompletionDate - today` (chip: green/warning/error)
5. Overall Health Indicator (derived from above)

This is additive to existing KPI cards — not replacing them.

---

### F. Others Tab Synchronization — Root Cause Analysis

**Save flow (edit page):**
1. `incidentLogRows.value` → `handleSubmit()` → `incident_log: [...rows]` in payload
2. Service `update()` → `jsonFields` includes `'incident_log'` → `JSON.stringify(dto.incident_log)` → UPDATE SQL
3. DB stores correct JSONB array ✅

**Load flow (detail page) — identified gaps:**

| Step | Issue | Severity |
|------|-------|----------|
| `BackendProjectDetail` interface missing `incident_log`, `risk_register`, `escalation_records`, `document_checklist_remarks` | TypeScript type gap — runtime works (JS accesses property from raw API response) but IDE will show errors and strict builds may fail | MEDIUM |
| Adapter `backend.incident_log` access | Works at runtime — not blocked. TypeScript may warn but doesn't prevent execution in Nuxt dev mode | LOW |
| Migration 080000 cleaned `[[]]` → `[]` | Any data saved BEFORE migration is now `[]` in DB. User must re-enter monitoring data. | INFORMATIONAL |
| New saves (after migration) should work correctly | The empty-array filter in service + adapter normalization prevent `[[]]` re-corruption | ✅ |

**Conclusion:** The Others tab DOES work correctly for NEW data entered after migrations. The perceived "synchronization failure" is one of:
1. User's prior data was cleaned to `[]` by migration 080000 (data loss — must re-enter)
2. TypeScript interface gap causing confusion but NOT a runtime failure

**Required fix:** Add missing fields to `BackendProjectDetail` interface in `adapters.ts` — resolves TypeScript errors and prevents future regressions.

---

### H. Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

| Cross-flow path | State | Gap |
|----------------|-------|-----|
| Create → Edit → Detail (monitoring data) | Create has no Others tab → correct; Edit saves → Detail renders | ✅ after re-entry |
| Edit milestone → MOV (CREATE) | Shown post-save | ✅ |
| Edit milestone → MOV (EDIT existing) | NOT shown | ❌ Gap |
| Detail milestone click → MOV | Shown in detail dialog | ✅ |
| Gallery upload (Edit) → render (Detail) | /uploads/ prefix — now works with migrations | ✅ |
| Document upload (Edit) → download (Detail) | /uploads/ prefix — now works | ✅ |
| keyDocType taxonomy empty | v-select shows no options | ❌ Gap |
| BackendProjectDetail interface | Missing monitoring fields | ❌ TypeScript gap |
| new.vue Others tab | Not implemented — correct (create-only page) | ✅ by design |
| Timeline filter Apply to preview cards | filteredMilestones.slice(0,5) used in preview | ✅ KZ-B confirmed |
| sharedPeriod filter shared between milestone+worklog dialogs | Same ref, updates both | ✅ |

**Synchronization risks:**
1. `keyDocType` v-select with empty taxonomy → key document classification becomes impossible
2. `BackendProjectDetail` interface gap → any future adapter change touching monitoring fields may silently break
3. MOV edit-mode gap → users editing milestones cannot manage MOV without going to detail page

## Section 2.166 — Phase LB: Timeline View Normalization, Overview Image Governance, Attachment Modal Stabilization, Others-Tab Synchronization

**Date:** 2026-05-18
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE — Confirmed [[]] corruption in PATCH payload (`incident_log: [[]]`). Comprehensive audit of LA deliverables + new governance scope: attachment modal, gallery metadata, grouped filter, calendar view.

### VERIFY_STEP LA — Implementation Audit

#### VERIFIED WORKING

| Feature | Evidence |

| Timeline table view toggle (LA-F) | `timelineViewMode` ref; `v-data-table` for milestones + work log |
| Progress Summary card (LA-G) | `progressSummary` computed; Physical/Financial/Milestone bars + days-remaining chip |

#### BROKEN — CRITICAL (Confirmed by network inspection)

| Feature | Root Cause | Evidence |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| Others tab synchronization | `fetchData()` in `edit-[id].vue` does NOT apply normalization filter on monitoring arrays. `incidentLogRows.value = [[]]` because DB has `[[]]` and raw assignment perpetuates it. On submit, `incident_log: [[]]` (length=1) passes the empty-array guard → written back to DB → endless cycle. Detail page adapter correctly filters `[[]]` → `[]` → shows "No data". | PATCH payload confirmed: `incident_log: [[]]` |

#### WORKING BUT NEEDS REWORK

| Feature | Issue |
|---------|-------|
| Attachment section label | Line 2143 in `detail-[id].vue`: "Attachments or Files" — should be "Attachments" |
| Document detail modal (attachment preview) | `v-card-title` shows `selectedDoc.fileName` — long filenames overflow/overlap. No title-wrap class. `max-width: 560` clips content. Missing: uploaderName, tag label fix |
| Overview image panel | LA-E implemented, but "Project Images" card only shows when `profileImages.length > 0` — if gallery empty, no visual affordance. `sm="4"` columns → on mobile all 3 images stack vertically. Compact layout acceptable but `cursor-zoom-in` CSS class is not a Vuetify utility |

#### NOT IMPLEMENTED

| Grouped filter mode (Daily/Weekly/Monthly/Quarterly show ALL entries grouped) | Current behavior: filter to current period only |
| Gallery `imageTakenDate` metadata | Entity has `uploadedAt` only; no `imageTakenDate` field |
| MOV before saving milestone (pre-save) | Conceptually impossible — MOV requires `relatedEntityId` which only exists after save |

### A. Overview Image Governance — Current State

**Current implementation (LA-E):**
- Card shows profile images at `height=160, cover` inside `v-col cols="12" sm="4"`
- Each card: image + caption title (font-weight-medium) + upload date chip below
- Fullscreen: `profilePreviewImg`, `profilePreviewTitle`, `profilePreviewDate` → dialog with image + metadata footer

**Remaining gaps:**
| Gap | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| No visual affordance when gallery empty | Card has `v-if="profileImages.length > 0"` → nothing shown when empty |
| No "image taken date" | Entity only has `uploadedAt` (server timestamp). No user-editable `imageTakenDate` |
| Caption = title (dual-purpose) | Caption field used both as title overlay and as metadata — no separate description |
| `cursor-zoom-in` is non-Vuetify | Should be `style="cursor: pointer"` |
| Fullscreen lacks navigation | Can't scroll between profile images in fullscreen |
| Edit-page profile strip minimal | In `edit-[id].vue`, profile images show as small 100px cards — not synchronized with detail layout |

**Architecture recommendation:**
- Add empty state card (visible even with 0 images): "Upload up to 3 profile images via Edit Project → Attachments tab"
- Add `imageTakenDate` to entity/DTO/migration as optional DATE field
- In fullscreen dialog: show caption (renamed "Description"), imageTakenDate + uploadedAt
- Keep LA-E card layout — it is functionally sound; only fix the cursor utility class

---

### B. Timeline Filter & View Normalization — Current State

**Filter bar (LA-D — verified working):**
- 2-column layout with labeled sections (Filter Period | Date Range / Active Window) ✅
- Remaining gap: grouped-entry rendering NOT implemented

**Grouped filter analysis:**
Current semantics: DAILY = entries ON today. WEEKLY = entries IN current week. etc.
Required semantics (from prompt): DAILY = ALL entries, organized into DAY buckets.

These are fundamentally different architectures:
- **Current**: `filteredMilestones` is a flat filtered array
- **Required**: a `groupedMilestones` computed that groups ALL items by their date unit

**Implementation approach for grouped mode:**
Add a `groupByPeriod` boolean ref. When ON, render milestone/work-log entries grouped into collapsible sections:
```
Group: 2026-05-01 (Monday)
  - Milestone: Foundation Complete
  - Work Log: Weekly report W18
Group: 2026-05-15
  - Milestone: Structural Frame
```
Grouping key for DAILY = `entryDate.slice(0,10)`. WEEKLY = ISO week. MONTHLY = `YYYY-MM`. QUARTERLY = `YYYY-Q`.

The existing `timelineViewMode` toggle (list/table) remains. Grouping is orthogonal to view mode.

**Calendar view analysis:**
- Vuetify 3 does NOT ship a production-ready `v-calendar` in Vuetify 3.x stable. `vuetify/labs/VCalendar` is experimental and not stable for production use.
- Alternative: Custom month-grid calendar using CSS grid + date arithmetic. Each month cell shows badges for milestones/entries on that date.
- Complexity: HIGH. Requires date navigation (prev/next month), event positioning, responsive scaling.
- **Recommendation**: Implement a lightweight custom calendar — a 7-column CSS grid month view with colored event dots per day. Click a date to see entries for that day in a panel.

---

### C. Others Tab Synchronization — Root Cause (Confirmed)

**Confirmed by network inspection:**
```
PATCH payload: incident_log: [[]]
```

**Complete cycle:**
```
1. DB: incident_log = [[]] (historical corruption)
2. fetchData(): incidentLogRows.value = [[]]  ← NO filter applied
3. incidentLogRows.value.length = 1 (not 0) → passes empty-array guard
4. handleSubmit: incident_log: [[]] sent in PATCH
5. service.update(): JSON.stringify([[]])='[[]]' → DB stays [[]]
6. fetchProject() in detail: backend.incident_log = [[]] → adapter filter removes inner []
   → incidentLog = [] → "No incidents recorded"
```

**The single-file fix:**
In `edit-[id].vue` `fetchData()`, lines 274-276:
```ts
// CURRENT (broken):
incidentLogRows.value  = Array.isArray((p as any).incident_log)       ? (p as any).incident_log       : []
riskRegisterRows.value = Array.isArray((p as any).risk_register)      ? (p as any).risk_register      : []
escalationRows.value   = Array.isArray((p as any).escalation_records) ? (p as any).escalation_records : []

// FIXED:
const monNorm = (v: any) => Array.isArray(v) ? v.filter((r: any) => r && typeof r === 'object' && !Array.isArray(r)) : []
incidentLogRows.value  = monNorm((p as any).incident_log)
riskRegisterRows.value = monNorm((p as any).risk_register)
escalationRows.value   = monNorm((p as any).escalation_records)
```

After this fix: `[[]]` → filtered to `[]` → `incidentLogRows.value = []` → user adds valid rows → saves → DB corrected.
No migration needed — DB self-corrects on next valid save.

---

### D. Attachment Modal — Current State

**"Attachments or Files" label** — `detail-[id].vue` line 2143:
```html
<v-icon icon="mdi-file-multiple" size="small" />
Attachments or Files
```
Must be renamed to "Attachments".

**Document detail modal (`docDetailDialogOpen`):**
```html
<v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2 pa-4">
  <v-icon icon="mdi-file-document" color="primary" />
  {{ selectedDoc.fileName }}   ← OVERFLOW RISK: long filenames break layout
  <v-spacer />
  <v-btn icon="mdi-close" variant="text" ... />
</v-card-title>
```

**Gaps identified:**
| Gap | Detail |
|-----|--------|
| fileName overflow in title | Long filenames push close button off-screen; no `text-wrap` or `word-break` |
| No title hierarchy | `documentType` is a chip below — no prominent document name |
| No uploader name | `uploadedBy` field exists on `DocumentItem` but not displayed in modal |
| No download button label | Just icon "mdi-download" button — not enterprise-grade |
| Card width 560 clips on mobile | Should be responsive `max-width: 640` with full-width on mobile |

**Fix architecture:**
- Move `selectedDoc.fileName` from `v-card-title` to a `v-card-text` section with `word-break: break-word`
- `v-card-title` becomes: icon + "Document Preview" + close btn (stable, no overflow)
- First section in card body: prominent filename with `text-h6`, then chips row
- Keep existing metadata (size, date, description, version, lifecycle)

---

### E. Gallery Governance — Current State

**Entity `ConstructionGallery`:** `imageUrl`, `caption`, `category`, `isFeatured`, `uploadedAt` — **no `imageTakenDate`**.

**Category governance:**
| Value | Meaning | Usage |
|-------|---------|-------|
| PROFILE | Project identity image | Overview strip (max 3) |
| BEFORE | Pre-construction photo | General gallery |
| IN_PROGRESS | During construction | General gallery |
| COMPLETED | Post-construction | General gallery |
| DEFECT | Defect documentation | General gallery |

**Gap:** No `imageTakenDate` field. Gallery entries only track server-side `uploadedAt`. A photo taken in 2024 but uploaded in 2026 would show `uploadedAt: 2026` with no indication of when it was captured.

**Required additions:**
1. Entity: `@Property({ nullable: true, columnType: 'date' }) imageTakenDate?: Date`
2. Migration: `ALTER TABLE construction_gallery ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS image_taken_date DATE`
3. DTO: `@IsOptional() @IsDateString() image_taken_date?: string` in gallery upload endpoint
4. Frontend upload form: add `v-text-field type="date"` for `imageTakenDate`
5. Adapter: add `imageTakenDate` to `UIGalleryItem`
6. Display: show in edit gallery card + detail fullscreen dialog

---

### F. MOV & Progress Summary — Current State

**MOV (Means of Verification) — current coverage:**

| Location | Status | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Detail page: milestone detail dialog | ✅ | `CiMovEvidenceSection` with `MILESTONE` type |
| Detail page: work log detail dialog | ✅ | `CiMovEvidenceSection` with `TIMELINE_ENTRY` type |

| Edit page: diary dialog EDIT mode | ✅ | LA-C: `diaryEditingId` triggers section |
| MOV BEFORE create (pre-save) | ❌ Impossible | MOV requires `relatedEntityId` — entity must exist first |

**Conclusion on MOV "before add":** The prompt requirement for MOV in "Add Milestone dialog" cannot be satisfied pre-save because `CiMovEvidenceSection` requires a `relatedEntityId`. The current post-create approach (dialog stays open, shows MOV section) is architecturally correct. The UX gap is that users must click "Add" then immediately get prompted — which IS implemented.

| "Attachments or Files" | ❌ Stale label | NAMING GAP |
| Progress Summary → detail | ✅ | |
| MOV create/edit | ✅ | |
| Filter period → table view | ✅ filteredMilestones shared | |

## Section 2.167 — Phase LC: Timeline Enterprise Refactor — Filter Governance, Calendar Navigation, MOV File Upload, Schema Normalization

**Date:** 2026-05-18
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE — Timeline governance audit. Audit of line 2690+ in edit-[id].vue, MOV file upload gap, advanced filter parameterization, calendar navigation gaps, schema normalization.
**Baseline:** LB ✅ complete. List/table/calendar views implemented. Basic grouped filter (LB-D). Calendar month grid (LB-E). MOV URL-only links via CiMovEvidenceSection.

---

### Critical Audit — Line 2690+ in edit-[id].vue

**Line 2690:** `</v-form>` — end of the main project form.
**Lines 2692–2871:** Milestone Add/Edit dialog (`v-dialog`).
**Lines 3030+:** Diary Add/Edit dialog (`v-dialog`).

**WHAT IS IMPLEMENTED (verified by reading):**

| Feature | State | Evidence |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Milestone dialog standard fields | ✅ | title, status, category, start_date, target_date, actual_start_date, actual_date, progress, description, remarks |
| MOV post-create (milestone) | ✅ KX-F | `milestoneJustSavedId` triggers `CiMovEvidenceSection` |
| MOV edit mode (milestone) | ✅ LA-C | `milestoneEditingId` triggers `CiMovEvidenceSection` |
| Diary dialog standard fields | ✅ | entry_type, entry_date, period_label, title, description, weather, manpower_count, equipment_used, work_accomplished, issues_encountered |
| MOV post-create (diary) | ✅ KX-F | `diaryJustSavedId` triggers `CiMovEvidenceSection` |
| MOV edit mode (diary) | ✅ LA-C | `diaryEditingId` triggers `CiMovEvidenceSection` |

**WHAT IS NOT IMPLEMENTED:**

| Feature | Root Cause | Impact |
|---------|-----------|--------|
| MOV file upload | `CiMovEvidenceSection` only accepts URLs. `MOV_URL_REGEX = /^https?:\/\/.+/i` — no file input. `ConstructionMovEntry` entity lacks `filePath`, `fileSize`, `mimeType` fields. | HIGH — prompt requires "uploaded files/images ≤ 15 MB" |
| MOV before first save (add dialog) | Architecturally impossible — `CiMovEvidenceSection` requires `relatedEntityId` which only exists after the entity is created | Design constraint — post-save MOV is the correct pattern |
| Advanced filter (year/quarter/month/sort) | `periodBounds` computed uses `new Date()` (today) — always anchors to the CURRENT period. Cannot navigate to Q1 2025. | HIGH — filters only work for the current period |
| Calendar jump (year/month picker) | Only prev/next month buttons implemented. No direct year or month selection. | MEDIUM |
| Calendar uses unfiltered data | `calendarDays` computed uses raw `project.value?.milestones` and `workLogEntries.value` — ignores `filteredMilestones`/`filteredWorkLog`. | MEDIUM — grouped filter has no effect on calendar |

---

### A. Timeline View Architecture — Current State

**List view:** flat sorted list with preview (5 items) + "View All" dialog — ✅ operational
**Table view:** `v-data-table` with columns + sortable headers — ✅ operational
**Calendar view (LB-E):** month grid, prev/next month, colored chips — ✅ structural, gaps below

**Calendar view gaps:**
1. Uses `project.value?.milestones` (raw unfiltered) not `filteredMilestones`
2. Uses `workLogEntries.value` (raw) not `filteredWorkLog`  
3. No year/month picker — only sequential prev/next navigation
4. Calendar month/year not synced to `sharedPeriod` filter selection

**Recommended fix:**
- `calendarDays` reads from `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` (already computed)
- Add `calendarYear` selector (v-select: range of available years from 2020-2030)
- Add `calendarMonth` selector (v-select: Jan-Dec)
- Keep prev/next as shortcuts

---

### B. Filter Governance — Critical Gap

**Current `periodBounds` computed:**
```ts
// DAILY → from=today, to=today
// WEEKLY → from=this Monday, to=this Sunday
// MONTHLY → from=first day of this month, to=last day of this month
// QUARTERLY → from=first day of this quarter, to=last day of this quarter
```

The reference `now = new Date()` is hardcoded to today. Cannot show entries from Q1 2025 without manually entering date range.

**Required advanced filter architecture:**

Add 3 secondary filter refs that narrow the period bounds when period is set:
- `filterYear` ref (number | null, default null = current year)
- `filterQuarter` ref (1|2|3|4 | null, default null)
- `filterMonth` ref (1–12 | null, default null)
- `filterSortDir` ref ('asc' | 'desc', default 'asc')

**Revised `periodBounds` logic:**
```
DAILY + filterMonth=1 + filterYear=2025 → from=2025-01-01, to=2025-01-31
DAILY + filterQuarter=1 + filterYear=2025 → from=2025-01-01, to=2025-03-31
WEEKLY + filterQuarter=1 + filterYear=2025 → from=2025-01-01, to=2025-03-31
MONTHLY + filterYear=2025 → from=2025-01-01, to=2025-12-31
QUARTERLY + filterYear=2025 → from=2025-01-01, to=2025-12-31
```

The `filterMonth` only applies when `filterQuarter` is null; `filterQuarter` narrows the quarter boundary within the year.

**Sort direction:**
`filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` return items in whatever order the server returns them. Add sort direction to both computeds based on `filterSortDir`:
- 'asc': sort by `targetDate`/`entryDate` ascending
- 'desc': sort descending

**Filter UI layout:**
The current 2-column filter bar has Period + Date Range. Need a 3rd row for Year/Quarter/Month/Sort when period ≠ ALL:
```
[Period Toggle: ALL | DAILY | WEEKLY | MONTHLY | QUARTERLY]
[if period ≠ ALL: Year dropdown | Quarter dropdown | Month dropdown | Sort direction]
[if period = ALL: From date | To date | Clear]
```

---

### C. MOV File Upload — Architecture

**Current entity (`ConstructionMovEntry`):**
```ts
movLink: string        // required URL
movTitle: string       // required
movDescription?: text  // optional
evidenceCategory: string
entryDate?: date
verificationStatus: 'PENDING'|'VERIFIED'|'REJECTED'
remarks?: text
```

**Gap:** No `filePath`, `fileSize`, `mimeType`, `fileName` fields. Entity is URL-only by design.

**Required additions for file upload support:**

Entity changes:
```ts
movLink?: string       // NOW OPTIONAL (either link or file, not both required)
filePath?: string      // server file path (from StorageService, /uploads/... prefix)
fileName?: string      // original filename for display
fileSize?: number      // bytes
mimeType?: string      // MIME type
```

The `movLink` becomes optional — a MOV entry has EITHER a link OR a file (or both for redundancy).

**Backend changes:**
1. Migration: add `file_path VARCHAR(500)`, `file_name VARCHAR(255)`, `file_size INTEGER`, `mime_type VARCHAR(100)` columns (nullable)
2. Entity: add 4 nullable properties
3. DTO: add optional `@IsOptional() @IsString()` fields
4. New upload endpoint: `POST /api/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries/:entryId/upload-file` via `FileInterceptor`
5. Service: `uploadMovFile()` method — uses `StorageService.uploadFile()` in `construction_mov` subdirectory; updates entity

**Frontend changes (`CiMovEvidenceSection.vue`):**
- Add a mode toggle: "Link" | "File"
- In "File" mode: show `v-file-input` (accept: `image/*,application/pdf,.doc,.docx`, max 15MB) + title field
- In "Link" mode: existing URL form
- POST to upload endpoint after file selection
- Display: distinguish file entries (show filename + download chip) from link entries (show URL chip)

**Validation:**
- File: ≤ 15MB, image or document types
- Link: existing `MOV_URL_REGEX` (https://)
- At least one of (movLink, filePath) must be present at service level

---

### D. Timeline Schema Assessment

**`ConstructionTimelineEntry` current fields:**
`entryType`, `entryDate`, `periodLabel`, `title`, `description`, `weather`, `manpowerCount`, `equipmentUsed`, `workAccomplished`, `issuesEncountered`, `photosCount`, `createdBy`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`

**Gap for operational reporting:**
- No `reporterType` enum (constructor/evaluator/inspector/admin) — cannot distinguish who filed the entry
- No `linkedMilestoneId` (UUID, nullable) — cannot link a work log to a specific milestone for progress attribution
- No `reportingPeriod` explicit field (quarter/year) — must be derived from `entryDate`

**YAGNI assessment:**
- `reporterType`: HIGH value — enables evaluator vs constructor log filtering. IMPLEMENT.
- `linkedMilestoneId`: MEDIUM value — enables milestone-drill-down. DEFER (complex join queries).
- `reportingPeriod`: YAGNI — derivable from `entryDate`.

**`ConstructionMilestone` current fields:**
`id`, `projectId`, `title`, `description`, `targetDate`, `actualDate`, `status`, `remarks`, `startDate`, `actualStartDate`, `progress`, `category`, `createdAt`

**Gap:**
- No `weightPercentage` — all milestones treated as equal. DEFER (speculative).
- No `milestoneType` (major/minor/administrative). DEFER.
- These are YAGNI per current data-entry scope.

---

### E. MOV Pre-Save Constraint Analysis

**Prompt states:** "missing in Add Milestone / Add Work Log dialogs"

**Root cause finding:** MOV is NOT missing. It IS implemented but only visible in two specific states:
1. **Post-create state** (`milestoneJustSavedId` or `diaryJustSavedId` set): dialog stays open after save, MOV section shown
2. **Edit state** (`milestoneDialogMode === 'edit'` + `milestoneEditingId` set): MOV shown when editing

**Why pre-save MOV is architecturally impossible:**
`CiMovEvidenceSection` requires `relatedEntityId` to POST `/api/construction-projects/:id/mov-entries`. The milestone/diary must exist in DB before MOV can be linked. Pre-save = no ID exists.

**Resolution for prompt requirement:** The post-save UX (dialog stays open, MOV section appears immediately) IS the correct pattern for "Add Milestone → then add MOV." The user flow is: fill milestone form → click "Add" → MOV section appears immediately in the same dialog → add evidence links/files → click "Done."

This flow is fully implemented for both URL MOV and will include file MOV in Phase LC.

---

## Section 2.168 — Phase LD Research: COI Continuity Audit, Others Tab Sync Fix, Overview Image Governance, Gallery Metadata, Attachment Modal Normalization

**Date:** 2026-05-19
**Prompt Origin:** META_DISCUSSION — CSU CORE: COI Timeline View Normalization, Overview Image Governance, Attachment Modal Stabilization, Others Tab Synchronization
**ACE Phase:** 1 — Research only. No implementation.

---

### Context

The operator issued a META_DISCUSSION prompt covering subsystems A–J (Overview, Timeline, Others, Attachments, Gallery, MOV/Progress, KC continuity audit, and cross-flow validation). The prior phases LA/LB/LC were marked COMPLETE in plan.md. This section audits actual code state vs. declared completion status, identifies confirmed bugs, and documents the true implementation state for each subsystem.

---

### A. Overview Tab — Project Image Governance: Current State Audit

**Current implementation (confirmed from detail-[id].vue lines 966–1023):**
- 3-column card grid per profile image
- Each image: 160px height cover image with fullscreen button overlay
- Below image: caption as `text-body-2 font-weight-medium` (truncated), taken-date chip, upload-date chip
- Fullscreen: `v-dialog` at line 2603, shows `v-img` contain + caption + uploadedAt chip

**Gaps identified:**

1. **`profilePreviewDate` passes `img.uploadedAt`, NOT `img.imageTakenDate`** (line 989, 1002). The fullscreen dialog therefore shows "Upload Date" as the date chip — the imageTakenDate is visible in the card thumbnail but NOT passed to the fullscreen. This is an inconsistency.

2. **Profile image upload in edit-[id].vue has NO `imageTakenDate` input** (lines 2197–2207). The general gallery upload at line 2244 has `galleryImageTakenDate` but profile upload form does not. Result: profile images will always have `imageTakenDate === null` unless set via another path.

3. **"Executive-grade" layout gap** per operator's standard: The current layout is functional but lacks structured metadata display (no image title separate from caption, no description field, no concise executive card hierarchy). The card is a simple thumbnail grid — it does not act as an "infrastructure preview / project identity visual."

4. **Missing from fullscreen**: No "taken date" chip, no category label, no description metadata — only caption + uploadedAt.

**Verdict:** Partially implemented. Core functionality present. Gaps: profile upload lacks imageTakenDate capture; fullscreen metadata incomplete; executive-grade layout not yet achieved.

---

### B. Timeline Tab — Filter & View Normalization: Current State Audit

**Current implementation (confirmed from detail-[id].vue lines 513–689):**
- `filterYear`, `filterQuarter`, `filterMonth`, `filterSortDir` refs: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LC-A)
- `periodBounds` parameterized: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LC-A)
- `calendarDays` reads `filteredMilestones.value` and `filteredWorkLog.value`: ✅ FIXED (LC-B)
- `calendarYear`/`calendarMonth` dropdowns: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LC-B, lines 1773–1784)
- `groupByPeriod` + `groupedMilestones` + `groupedWorkLog`: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LB-D)
- Filter bar UI — Period toggle + Date Range row + Narrow-to row: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LA-D, LC-A, lines 1519–1602)
- Sort direction toggle: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LC-A, line 1586–1598)
- Table view: ✅ IMPLEMENTED (LA-F)

**Gaps identified:**
1. **Edit page timeline tab has NO filter bar** — `edit-[id].vue` timeline tab contains only Progress Summary fields + milestone/diary editors. This is architecturally correct (editing ≠ viewing) but should be documented as an intentional design boundary.
2. **`groupByPeriod` stays enabled when ALL is selected** — the grouped display requires `sharedPeriod !== 'ALL'` (`groupedMilestones` returns `null` if `ALL` or `!groupByPeriod`). The group toggle itself is not hidden/disabled when `sharedPeriod === 'ALL'`. Minor UX gap.
3. **Grouped display label format**: `getGroupKey()` produces ISO-formatted strings (e.g., `2025-W14` for weekly). These are machine-readable but not user-friendly in the group headers.

**Verdict:** Timeline filter and views are substantially complete. Remaining gaps are UX-level: group label formatting, groupByPeriod toggle disable-when-ALL. These are cosmetic.

---

### C. Others Tab — Synchronization Failure: Root Cause Confirmed

**CONFIRMED BUG** from audit of `edit-[id].vue` lines 448–454:

```typescript
incident_log: incidentLogRows.value.length > 0 ? incidentLogRows.value : undefined,
risk_register: riskRegisterRows.value.length > 0 ? riskRegisterRows.value : undefined,
escalation_records: escalationRows.value.length > 0 ? escalationRows.value : undefined,
```

**Root cause:** When a user **clears all rows** from any Others section (incident log, risk register, or escalation records), the corresponding ref becomes an empty array. The save payload then sends `undefined` for that field (the `length > 0 ? x : undefined` pattern). The backend PATCH (`PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id`) ignores undefined fields — it only updates fields present in the request body. The backend never receives the instruction to clear the array, so the JSONB column retains its old value.

**Impact:**
- User adds 3 incidents → saves → detail shows 3 incidents ✅
- User deletes all 3 incidents in edit → saves → payload sends `incident_log: undefined` → backend no-op → detail STILL shows 3 incidents ❌
- This is the "saves successfully BUT data still does NOT render/display correctly" observation from the operator

**Correct fix:** Send explicit `[]` (empty array) when rows are empty:
```typescript
incident_log: incidentLogRows.value,          // always send (empty [] clears the field)
risk_register: riskRegisterRows.value,
escalation_records: escalationRows.value,
status_updates: statusUpdateRows.value,
readiness_documents: readinessDocRows.value,
signatories: signatoryRows.value,
```

**Same pattern affects status_updates, readiness_documents, signatories** (lines 448–450) — all use `length > 0 ? x : undefined`. All must be corrected.

**Backend behavior to verify:** `construction-projects.service.ts` PATCH handler must accept `[]` as a valid "clear" value and update the JSONB column. The `jsonFields` array (line 686) handles JSON parse on read — the write path must not reject empty arrays.

---

### D. Attachment Tab — Preview Modal: Current State Audit

**Current implementation (confirmed from detail-[id].vue lines 2906–2966):**
- `v-dialog` max-width 640, scrollable
- Title bar: icon + "Document" label + close button ← stable title (LB-B fix)
- Body section 1: `text-h6` fileName with `word-break:break-word` ← overflow fix (LB-B)
- Body section 2: chips (documentType, category, version, lifecycleStatus)
- Body section 3: uploaded date, file size, description
- Actions: Close + Download button (if filePath present)

**Gaps identified:**
1. **No "latest version" prominence** — version chip exists (`v{{ selectedDoc.version }}`) but is visually equal to other chips. No indication of whether the current version is the latest.
2. **No fullscreen preview** — For image attachments (PDFs, images), there is no preview/render in the modal. The user can only download.
3. **No metadata section for "image taken date"** — document modal has no metadata for photos/images.
4. **Tab label is already "Attachments"** (confirmed edit page line 1494, detail page search). No rename needed.
5. **Detail page attachment card title is "Attachments"** (confirmed line 2484). No rename needed.
6. **Structural adequacy**: Current modal is functional for basic document metadata display. The "overlapping file title" bug was fixed by LB-B. The current structure meets enterprise-basic standard.

**Gap summary:** Modal is stable. Remaining gap is the absence of inline file preview (PDF/image embed) and latest-version emphasis. These are enhancement requests, not bugs.

---

### E. Gallery Governance — Metadata: Current State Audit

**Current implementation:**
- General gallery upload (edit-[id].vue line 2244): `galleryImageTakenDate` text-field → `image_taken_date` in FormData (line 1354) ✅
- General gallery display (edit-[id].vue line 2224): `imageTakenDate` chip shown ✅
- Detail page general gallery: needs verification
- Profile image upload (edit-[id].vue lines 2197–2207): **NO `imageTakenDate` input** ❌
- Profile image display (detail-[id].vue line 1009): `imageTakenDate` chip shown ✅ (will be null for existing profile images)
- Fullscreen preview: passes `img.uploadedAt` not `img.imageTakenDate` ❌ inconsistency

**Category governance (per operator requirement):**
- "Profile" category = `category === 'PROFILE'` → Overview images ✅ (line 280 detail, line 1174 edit)
- "General" category = `category !== 'PROFILE'` → Gallery Tab entries ✅
- Category-aware upload form in general gallery (BEFORE/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/DEFECT options) ✅
- Profile upload form has no category selector (always uploads as PROFILE) ✅

**Gaps:**
1. Profile image upload form lacks `imageTakenDate` input — all profile images will have null `imageTakenDate`
2. Fullscreen preview inconsistency: shows `uploadedAt` where `imageTakenDate` would be more appropriate
3. Fullscreen gallery fullscreen modal (galleryFullscreen dialog at line 2417): profile section (line 2426–2435) shows NO imageTakenDate chip, no date at all — inconsistent with the inline thumbnail which shows it

**Verdict:** Gallery governance is partially implemented. Core category distinction is sound. Date metadata is present for general images but absent from profile image capture path.

---

### F. MOV & Progress Summary: Current State Audit

**MOV post-create (KX-F pattern):**
- Add Milestone → save → `milestoneJustSavedId` set → MOV section revealed in same dialog: ✅ CONFIRMED (lines 2817–2832)
- Add Diary Entry → save → `diaryJustSavedId` set → MOV section revealed: ✅ CONFIRMED (lines 3171–3186)
- Edit Milestone → MOV section shown (LA-C): ✅ CONFIRMED (lines 2842–2856)
- Edit Diary Entry → MOV section shown (LA-C): ✅ CONFIRMED (lines 3196–3210)

**Architectural note on pre-save MOV constraint:** MOV entries require `relatedEntityId` (FK). Pre-save, no entity ID exists. The post-save reveal pattern IS the architecturally correct implementation. "MOV missing in Add dialogs" as stated in the operator prompt is factually resolved by the KX-F post-save pattern. The user sees MOV immediately after clicking "Add" — functionally equivalent to having it in the Add dialog.

**MOV file upload (LC-C):** Implemented per plan.md. Status: pending operator verification and migration run.
**Reporter Type (LC-D):** Implemented per plan.md. Status: pending operator verification and migration run.

**Progress Summary (LA-G):**
- `progressSummary` computed (line 778): physical %, financial %, MS completion rate, days remaining ✅ CONFIRMED
- Detail page timeline tab renders at lines 1701–1742 ✅ CONFIRMED

**Gap:** MOV `CiMovEvidenceSection` used in `detail-[id].vue` — need to verify if it also shows MOV entries on the detail timeline view (not just edit). Reading from the search above, `CiMovEvidenceSection` is imported in `edit-[id].vue`. Need to confirm it's also present in `detail-[id].vue` milestone/diary detail modals.

---

| Gallery fullscreen — date metadata | LD target | ❌ MISSING | galleryFullscreen profile section shows no dates |
| Document modal rename (Attachments) | LB | ✅ ALREADY CORRECT | Tab says "Attachments", card says "Attachments" |

### H. Cross-Flow Continuity Validation

| Cross-flow path | Status | Risk / Notes |
|----------------|--------|-------------|
| Edit Others → PATCH → Detail Others render | ❌ BROKEN for clear-to-empty | Root cause: `undefined` payload |
| Edit Others → PATCH → Detail Others (add new rows) | ✅ WORKS | Rows present → `length > 0 → array sent` |
| Edit profile image upload → detail overview Images | ✅ WORKS | category=PROFILE filter correct |
| Edit general gallery → detail Gallery tab | ✅ WORKS | category filter correct |

| filteredMilestones → table view | ✅ WORKS | shared computed |

| MOV: Edit milestone → MOV section | ✅ WORKS | LA-C |

| Profile image `imageTakenDate` → fullscreen | ❌ BROKEN | Passes `uploadedAt` instead |
| Others save with empty arrays | ❌ BROKEN | Sends `undefined` instead of `[]` |

**Critical synchronization risk:** Others Tab clear-to-empty is the highest-priority bug — affects data integrity and operator trust. A user who "clears" their incident log and saves will see the old data persist, creating confusion and loss of confidence in the save operation.

## Section 2.169 — Phase LF Research: Timeline Date Governance, Calendar Filter Normalization, MOV Dialog Consistency Refactor

**Date:** 2026-05-19
**Prompt Origin:** META_DISCUSSION — CSU CORE: Timeline Date Governance, Calendar Filter Normalization, and MOV Dialog Consistency Refactor
**ACE Phase:** 1 — Research only. No implementation.

---

### Context

This section completes the LF research audit deferred from the interrupted prior session. The audit covers the full Timeline ecosystem in `detail-[id].vue` and `edit-[id].vue`, with specific focus on: (A) Edit vs Detail page timeline architecture divergence, (B) hardcoded `availableYears` calendar range bug, (C) `periodBounds` time-window/grouping conflation, (D) filter-to-calendar synchronization status, (E) MOV dialog consistency post-Phase LE, (F) LC-C/LC-D migration readiness, and (G) cross-flow continuity.

---

### A. Timeline Architecture — Edit Page vs Detail Page Divergence

**Edit Project `edit-[id].vue` Timeline Tab:**
- Contains: Progress Summary input cards (physical/financial % data entry), 2-column CRUD layout (milestones left, diary entries right), Add/Edit/Delete dialogs with MOV section (Phase LE unified these)
- NO filter bar, NO view mode toggle (List/Table/Calendar), NO calendar grid, NO grouped view
- Purpose: data entry and management

**Detail/View Page `detail-[id].vue` Timeline Tab:**
- Contains: Filter bar (Period / Date Range / Year / Quarter / Month / Sort), Progress Summary display cards, View toggle (List/Table/Calendar), Calendar grid, Grouped list view, Milestone detail modals
- READ-ONLY: milestones and diary displayed but not editable
- Purpose: visualization and governance review

**Design boundary is architecturally correct.** Edit = CRUD tool. Detail = visualization tool. However, the operator's prompt (Section B, Requirement 3) explicitly states: "Timeline must support Table and Calendar View for BOTH Project Details/View AND Edit Project." This means the Edit page lacks feature parity with the Detail page for visualization — adding calendar/filter/view-mode to `edit-[id].vue` would be a significant scope expansion.

**Assessment:** Before implementing, operator must confirm this is still required. Adding full filter/calendar machinery to the Edit page risks UX confusion (simultaneous CRUD + visualization in one form), doubles the maintenance surface for timeline filter logic, and would require extracting filter state into a shared composable. Defer LF-E pending operator decision.

---

### B. Calendar Year Range — Critical Governance Bug

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` line 518

```javascript
const availableYears = Array.from({ length: 11 }, (_, i) => 2020 + i)
// Generates: [2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026, 2027, 2028, 2029, 2030]
```

**Used in TWO places:**
1. Line 1585: Year dropdown in the "Narrow to:" filter row → `<v-select v-model="filterYear" :items="availableYears" />`
2. Line 1818: Calendar year selector → `<v-select v-model="calendarYear" :items="availableYears" />`

**Impact:**
- Infrastructure projects with milestones dated before 2020 (e.g., 2015 groundbreaking, 2018 design phase) cannot be filtered to by year — those years don't appear in the dropdown
- Projects with target completion beyond 2030 cannot be navigated to in the calendar
- CSU infrastructure portfolio spans decades; this is a governance failure

**Fix strategy:** Replace the hardcoded array with a computed that generates a wide range. Two options:

Option 1 (Simple, preferred by KISS): Static wide range from 1990 to `currentYear + 15`
```javascript
const thisYear = new Date().getFullYear()
const availableYears = Array.from({ length: thisYear + 15 - 1990 + 1 }, (_, i) => 1990 + i)
```

Option 2 (Data-aware): Derive range from actual milestone dates in the project. Min = `min(milestone.targetDate year, timeline entry year) - 1` clamped to 2000. Max = `max(project.targetCompletionDate year, currentYear) + 5`.

**Recommendation:** Option 1. Simpler, one-line fix, no dependency on loaded project data. 1990–(currentYear+15) covers all realistic CSU infrastructure timelines.

---

### C. `periodBounds` Time-Window Conflation — Architecture Gap

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue` lines 540–588

**Current behavior when period is set WITHOUT any Year/Quarter/Month narrowing:**

| Period | Default `periodBounds` result | Milestones/entries shown |
|--------|------------------------------|--------------------------|
| DAILY  | `{ from: '2025-05-19', to: '2025-05-19' }` (today only) | Only entries dated today |
| WEEKLY | `{ from: '2025-05-19', to: '2025-05-25' }` (this week) | Only entries this week |
| MONTHLY | `{ from: '2025-05-01', to: '2025-05-31' }` (this month) | Only entries this month |
| QUARTERLY | `{ from: '2025-04-01', to: '2025-06-30' }` (current quarter) | Only entries current quarter |
| ALL | `{ from: '', to: '' }` | All entries |

**Root cause:** The period selector conflates two independent concerns:
1. **Grouping granularity** — how to group and label entries (by day / by week / by month / by quarter)
2. **Time window** — which date range to include in the result

The year/quarter/month narrowing controls were added to override the default fallback, but the fallback itself (today / this week / this month / this quarter) is counter-intuitive for operators who expect "Daily" to mean "show all entries, grouped by day."

**Observed failure mode:** Operator selects "Daily" → sees 0 or 1 entries (only today's data) → reports filter as broken. The filter IS working correctly by the current logic — but the logic is semantically wrong. The period selector should be a GROUPING control, not a DATE WINDOW control.

**Fix strategy:** Modify `periodBounds` so that when NO narrowing is set (`filterYear = null AND filterQuarter = null AND filterMonth = null`), all period values return `{ from: '', to: '' }`. The period selector then only affects `getGroupKey()` (used in `groupedMilestones` / `groupedWorkLog`).

When narrowing IS set, apply the appropriate date window:
- Year only → full year window
- Year + Quarter → that quarter within that year
- Year + Month → that month within that year
- Quarter (no year) → that quarter in current year (rarely used; Year should be required)

Revised `periodBounds` logic:
```typescript
const periodBounds = computed((): { from: string; to: string } => {
  if (sharedPeriod.value === 'ALL') return { from: '', to: '' }
  // No narrowing → show all entries; period only affects grouping
  if (!filterYear.value && !filterQuarter.value && !filterMonth.value) return { from: '', to: '' }

  const year = filterYear.value ?? new Date().getFullYear()
  const pad = (n: number) => String(n).padStart(2, '0')
  const fmt = (d: Date) => `${d.getFullYear()}-${pad(d.getMonth() + 1)}-${pad(d.getDate())}`

  if (filterQuarter.value) {
    const q = filterQuarter.value
    return { from: fmt(new Date(year, (q - 1) * 3, 1)), to: fmt(new Date(year, q * 3, 0)) }
  }
  if (filterMonth.value) {
    const m = filterMonth.value - 1
    return { from: fmt(new Date(year, m, 1)), to: fmt(new Date(year, m + 1, 0)) }
  }
  // Year only → full year
  return { from: fmt(new Date(year, 0, 1)), to: fmt(new Date(year, 11, 31)) }
})
```

**Note on `sharedPeriod` watcher:** Line 537 resets `filterYear/Quarter/Month` when period changes. This is correct — when switching period granularity, stale narrowing should not persist.

**Regression risk:** LOW. The only change in behavior is: DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY with no narrowing now shows ALL entries instead of today/this week/this month/this quarter. Since the "Active Window" chip was showing those dates (line 1575), it will now show an empty range `" — "` when no narrowing is set. This chip should be updated to show "All dates" or hidden when bounds are empty.

---

### D. Filter ↔ Calendar Synchronization — Confirmed Working (LC-B)

**Status: ✅ VERIFIED CORRECT**

The calendar's `calendarDays` computed (lines 674–690) reads:
```typescript
milestones: (filteredMilestones.value as any[]).filter(m => (m.targetDate ?? '').slice(0,10) === dateStr),
entries:    filteredWorkLog.value.filter(e => (e.entryDate ?? '').slice(0,10) === dateStr),
```

Both `filteredMilestones` and `filteredWorkLog` are reactive computeds that apply `periodBounds` and all narrowing filters. When filters change → both computeds recompute → `calendarDays` recomputes → calendar re-renders with only filtered entries.

**Remaining UX gap (not a bug):** Calendar month/year navigation (`calendarMonth`, `calendarYear`) is independent of the filter window. A user can:
- Set filter Year=2024/Q1 → calendar shows Q1 2024 entries ✅
- Then manually navigate calendar to December 2025 → correct (nothing from Q1 2024 shows) but user may be confused why calendar is now empty without realizing they navigated outside the filter window

**Enhancement LF-D:** When period filter is active and `periodBounds.from` changes, auto-navigate calendar to the filter window start (set `calendarYear` + `calendarMonth` from `periodBounds.from`). This is a watcher: `watch(periodBounds, (bounds) => { if (bounds.from) { calendarYear.value = parseInt(bounds.from.slice(0,4)); calendarMonth.value = parseInt(bounds.from.slice(5,7)) - 1 } })`.

---

### E. MOV Dialog Consistency — Resolved (Phase LE)

**Status: ✅ RESOLVED**

Phase LE (implemented in this session) unified the Add/Edit dialog behavior:
- Before: Add → save → "Done" button (only MOV section, no form); Edit → form + MOV together
- After: Add → save → dialog transitions to Edit mode (form + MOV + Cancel/Save) — identical to Edit flow

Directives LE-D1 through LE-D4 govern this behavior. The `milestoneJustSavedId` and `diaryJustSavedId` refs have been removed from `edit-[id].vue`.

- **Risk:** If migration not run, reporter_type filter in frontend will always be null/ignored (no DB error, just no data)

**Action required:** Operator must confirm migration status by running `SELECT name FROM mikro_orm_migrations ORDER BY executed_at` and verifying both `Migration20260518020000` and `Migration20260518030000` appear as executed. If not: `npx mikro-orm migration:up` in `pmo-backend/`.

## Section 2.170 — A: Add vs Edit Milestone Dialog Inconsistency — Root Cause Analysis

**Phase:** LG Research | **File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 2714–2864

### Finding

The Add/Edit Milestone dialog is a single `<v-dialog v-model="milestoneDialog">`. MOV section visibility is gated by a conditional at **line 2834**:

```vue
<template v-if="milestoneDialogMode === 'edit' && milestoneEditingId">
  <!-- Full MOV Evidence section + Save button -->
</template>
<template v-else>
  <!-- Cancel + Add button only — NO MOV section -->
</template>
```

**In CREATE mode:** `milestoneEditingId` is `null` → MOV gate fails → MOV section invisible → user sees form fields only.

**In EDIT mode:** `milestoneEditingId` holds the existing UUID → MOV gate passes → `CiMovEvidenceSection` renders with that ID.

### Current Post-Save Transition (Existing Design Intent)

`saveMilestone()` (lines 950–990) — after a successful POST in create mode:
```typescript
milestoneEditingId.value = (created as any)?.id ?? null
milestoneDialogMode.value = 'edit'
// → dialog stays open; mode flips to 'edit'; MOV section now appears
```

The design intent was: Create → Save → dialog transitions to edit mode with MOV section visible. This technically works but creates a **UX discontinuity**: user in Add mode sees NO indication that MOV exists or will appear. The visual layout differs between Add and Edit modes, confirming the screenshot findings.

### Root Cause Summary

1. MOV section visibility is gated on an existing entity UUID — this is architecturally correct (MOV requires entity ID)
2. The `v-else` branch provides NO MOV placeholder, hint, or container in create mode
3. The result is a visually inconsistent dialog that violates the DRY/unified-dialog principle
4. After save, the dialog stays open in edit mode (correct behavior), but user never sees the transition purpose

### Required Fix Direction

- Always render the MOV section container in the dialog — in BOTH create and edit modes
- In create mode: show an informational placeholder ("Save the milestone first to attach MOV evidence")
- In edit mode: show the full `CiMovEvidenceSection` (current behavior)
- Dialog button text: "Add" (create) / "Save" (edit) — preserved from current implementation
- The post-save edit-mode transition is retained — after clicking "Add", dialog stays open in edit mode with full MOV available

---

### Finding

The Diary/Work Log Add/Edit dialog follows the **identical pattern** as the Milestone dialog:
- MOV gated by `diaryDialogMode === 'edit' && diaryEditingId`
- `saveDiary()` transitions to edit mode after create: `diaryEditingId.value = created.id; diaryDialogMode.value = 'edit'`
- Same UX gap in Add mode

**Required Fix:** Identical to Section 2.170 — add informational MOV placeholder in the `v-else` branch.
