- Controller: remove all `pow-items` routes (GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE)
- Module: remove `ConstructionPowItem` from entity list

**Frontend removals:**
- `CiPowItemTable.vue` — delete
- `detail-[id].vue` — remove `import CiPowItemTable`, POW tab window item, POW tab header, `pow` from tab list, `canEditPow` references
- `edit-[id].vue` — remove POW tab, `pow` from `tabOrder`, related `tabRequired`/`tabCompletion` entries

---

### 2.209-J: Unified Tab Architecture

Target tab structure (synchronized across all 3 pages):

| Tab | Key | new.vue | edit-[id].vue | detail-[id].vue |
|-----|-----|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| Basic Info | `basic` | YES | YES | YES (read-only) |
| Dates and Duration | `schedule` | YES | YES | YES (read-only) |
| Progress and Financial | `progress` | YES | YES | YES (read-only) |
| Personnel | `personnel` | YES | YES | YES (read-only) |
| Timeline | `timeline` | NO (N/A) | YES (PAUSED) | YES (PAUSED) |
| Documents | `documents` | YES | YES | YES |
| Analytics | `analytics` | NO | NO | YES (existing) |
| Others | `others` | NO | YES (existing) | NO |

---

### 2.209-K: Migration Dependency Chain

Last existing migration: `Migration20260520010000_AddMilestoneAuditFields.ts`

New migration sequence:
1. `Migration20260521010000_AddProjectNewFields` — all new columns on `construction_projects`
2. `Migration20260521020000_AddFundingSourceType` — `type` on `funding_sources` + predefined seed updates
3. `Migration20260521030000_ArchivePowItemsTable` — rename `construction_pow_items`
4. `Migration20260521040000_NormalizeProjectStatus` — UPDATE status PLANNING->PROPOSAL, COMPLETED->COMPLETE

All use `class extends Migration` with `this.addSql()`. All ADD COLUMN operations are nullable — near-instant on PostgreSQL.

---

### 2.209-L: Risks and Continuity Safeguards

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| Status migration breaks existing projects | Run UPDATE in migration; keep TS enum backward compat during transition |
| contractorId FK removal breaks data | Keep `contractorId` nullable forever; new text field takes display precedence |
| POW removal breaks active users | Archive table (RENAME not DROP); remove UI feature flags cleanly |
| funding_source_id in analytics queries | Analytics reads `fundingSourceId` unchanged; JSONB is purely additive |
| CiBasicInfoForm refactor breaks new.vue | Update coiFormState.ts interface simultaneously; compile-check before Phase 3 |
| Timeline tab regression | PAUSE_STEP strictly enforced; zero changes to timeline tab logic |
| Large migration locking construction_projects | ADD COLUMN nullable is near-instant on PostgreSQL; acceptable |

---

## §2.212 — Progress Report, Revision Orders & Three-Page Synchronization Research

*Research Phase — RUN_ACE directive received 2026-05-13 (Progress Report + Revision Orders + Strategic Alignment + Three-page sync). Findings captured below. Supersedes prior session context summary.*

---

### 2.212-A: Current Backend State

**Entities — fully implemented (no migration work required):**

| Entity | Table | Phase |
|--------|-------|-------|
| `ConstructionProgressReport` | `construction_progress_reports` | NE-A (2026-05-21) |
| `ConstructionRevisionOrder` | `construction_revision_orders` | ND-A (2026-05-21) |

**`ConstructionProgressReport` columns:**
`id`, `projectId`, `reportType` (MONTHLY/QUARTERLY/AD_HOC/WEEKLY), `reportDate`, `reportNumber`, `percentageCompletion`, `plannedAccomplishment`, `slippage`, `costIncurredToDate`, `costIncurredThisPeriod`, `calendarDaysElapsed`, `percentTimeElapsed`, `remarks`, `issuesEncountered`, `mitigationActions`, `movDocumentId`, `movLink`, `createdBy`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `updatedBy`

**`ConstructionRevisionOrder` columns:**
`id`, `projectId`, `revisionNumber`, `revisionType` (VOR/CTE/WSO/WRO/OTHER), `revisionDate`, `newStartDate`, `newCompletionDate`, `newDuration`, `costAdjustment`, `justification`, `approvalStatus` (DRAFT/APPROVED/REJECTED), `movDocumentId`, `movLink`, `createdBy`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`, `updatedBy`

**Backend routes — all confirmed in controller:**
- `GET/POST /api/construction-projects/:id/progress-reports`
- `PATCH/DELETE /api/construction-projects/:id/progress-reports/:reportId`
- `GET/POST /api/construction-projects/:id/revision-orders`
- `PATCH/DELETE /api/construction-projects/:id/revision-orders/:roId`

**Mirror logic confirmed:**
- `mirrorLatestReportToProject()` → syncs `physical_progress`, `cost_incurred_to_date`, `as_of_date` from latest progress report
- `mirrorLatestApprovedRevisionToProject()` → syncs `revised_start_date`, `revised_completion_date`, `revised_project_duration` from latest APPROVED revision

---

### 2.212-B: Current Frontend State — CiProgressReportTab.vue

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProgressReportTab.vue` (466 lines, Phase NG)

**Props:** `projectId: string`, `readOnly?: boolean` (default false)

**Composable:** `useCoiProgressReports(projectIdRef)` — returns `items`, `loading`, `create`, `update`, `remove` — no pagination

**View modes:** `card` | `table` | `comparison` (toggled via v-btn-toggle)

**Gaps identified:**

| Gap | Location | Impact |
|-----|----------|--------|
| Card view has NO pagination | `v-for` over all `items` | Can grow unbounded with many reports |
| Table view uses `hide-default-footer` + `items-per-page=50` | `<v-data-table>` | No real pagination — effectively shows all |
| No List view mode | Component | Directive requires Table + List dual view |
| No Project Date summary header | Component | Directive requires Duration/Start/Target synchronized from parent |
| Project Date fields come from parent context | Not passed as props | Must be added as optional props |

---

### 2.212-C: Current Frontend State — CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue` (342 lines, Phase NF-B)

**Props:** `projectId: string`, `readOnly?: boolean`

**Composable:** `useCoiRevisionOrders(projectIdRef)`

**Current state:**
- Single view: Table only (v-data-table with client-side pagination at 8 per page)
- Add/Edit dialog: full field set (revisionType, revisionDate, revisionNumber, dates, costAdjustment, justification, approvalStatus, movLink)

**Gap:** No List view mode. Directive requires dual view (Table ↔ List).

---

### 2.212-D: Three-Page Synchronization Audit

**Confirmed divergence:**

| Feature | new.vue | edit-[id].vue | detail-[id].vue |
|---------|---------|---------------|-----------------|
| Progress tab exists | YES | YES | NO (missing) |
| Progress tab uses `CiProgressReportTab` | NO (inline flat fields) | YES | N/A |
| Revision Orders uses `CiRevisionOrdersTable` | NO | YES (schedule tab) | NO |
| `CiRemarksLog` component | NO (inline addRemark pattern) | YES (OE-B) | NO |

**Root cause for new.vue divergence:**
`CiProgressReportTab` requires `projectId` for CRUD API calls. A project being created has no ID yet. Therefore `CiProgressReportTab` cannot be mounted in new.vue during creation. The inline flat fields (`physical_progress`, `as_of_date`, `cost_incurred_to_date`) write to project entity columns directly — this is architecturally correct for the initial snapshot. Resolution: keep flat fields for new.vue initial state, add instructional note: "Full progress tracking is available after saving the project."

**Root cause for detail-[id].vue missing progress tab:**
The detail page was built before `CiProgressReportTab` existed (Phase JR predates Phase NG). The detail page reads `project.value.physicalProgress` etc. for the Timeline tab progress summary, but no dedicated Progress Reports tab was ever added. The service's `findOne()` already embeds `progress_reports` in the response (returned as `progressReports` array). This data is available on `project.value` — can be used to provide `projectId` to `CiProgressReportTab`.

**Root cause for CiRemarksLog missing in new.vue:**
`CiRemarksLog` was extracted as a component only in Phase OE (this session). new.vue still uses inline `newRemarkText` / `addRemark` logic. This must be synchronized as part of the OG+ work.

---

### 2.212-E: Strategic Alignment Chip Text Overflow

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiHierarchicalSelectorTrigger.vue` (147 lines, Phase OC)

**Current state after OC:**
- `maxVisibleChips` prop with show-more/fewer pattern — implemented ✅
- Chip container: `d-flex flex-wrap ga-1` — chips DO wrap correctly ✅
- Individual chips: `v-chip size="small" variant="tonal"` — **no max-width, no word-break CSS**

**Problem:** Long RDP 2023–2028 attribute names (e.g., "Accelerate Strategic Industries and Employment") overflow horizontally within the chip itself without wrapping. Chip container wraps to next line correctly, but the chip text does not wrap internally.

**Fix:** Add `style="white-space: normal; word-break: break-word; max-width: 260px;"` to each `v-chip`. This is CSS-only, no logic change.

---

### 2.212-F: new.vue Current Progress Tab (Architecture)

**Lines 946–1100:** Two `v-card` blocks side-by-side:
1. Physical Status card — `physical_progress` (slider/number), `as_of_date` (date picker)
2. Financial Status card — `cost_incurred_to_date` (currency), `date_completed` (date picker)
Plus inline remarks log with `newRemarkText` / `addRemark` / `remarks_log` array

These write to project entity columns (flat fields on `construction_projects`). This is NOT the same as `construction_progress_reports` multi-entry table.

**Design decision:** new.vue progress tab serves as "initial project state snapshot." Multi-entry progress reporting belongs in edit-[id].vue and detail-[id].vue post-creation. This is architecturally correct; the flat fields on `construction_projects` (`physical_progress`, `cost_incurred_to_date`, `as_of_date`) are the source for overview cards and analytics. The `CiProgressReportTab` multi-entry system targets the `construction_progress_reports` table separately.

**Alignment needed:**
- Replace inline `newRemarkText/addRemark` with `<CiRemarksLog>` component
- Add instructional banner: "Full progress report history (monthly/quarterly entries) is available after project creation."

---

### 2.212-G: detail-[id].vue Tab Structure (Current)

Tabs: `overview`, `timeline`, `analytics`, `gallery`, `documents`, `team`, `others`

Service `findOne()` already returns `progress_reports` embedded. Frontend `adaptProjectDetail()` maps them. However, the detail page has no `<CiProgressReportTab>` usage and no `progress` window-item.

**Proposed addition:** Insert `<v-tab value="progress">` and `<v-window-item value="progress">` containing:
- `<CiProgressReportTab :project-id="projectId" :read-only="!canEditCurrentProject" />`
- `<CiRevisionOrdersTable :project-id="projectId" :read-only="!canEditCurrentProject" />` (within the progress tab or in a dedicated expandable section)

Tab order after insertion: `overview`, `progress`, `timeline`, `analytics`, `gallery`, `documents`, `team`, `others`

---

### 2.212-H: Risk and Continuity Assessment

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|-----------|
| CiProgressReportTab crashes in detail page if projectId is undefined | Guard: only mount if `projectId` is truthy string |
| new.vue `CiRemarksLog` migration breaks existing remarks data | `CiRemarksLog` uses same JSONB array contract — v-model compatible |
| Pagination added to card view breaks scroll position | Use `v-pagination` with `page` ref; no router change needed |
| detail-[id].vue tab order breaks bookmarked URLs | No hash-based URLs; `v-tabs` uses value strings not indices — safe |
| new.vue flat-field progress still writes to project entity | Intentional — initial snapshot design; no change to backend contract |
| List view in CiRevisionOrdersTable adds UI surface area | Local view toggle — no backend change; client-side slice |

---


---

## §2.213 — Toggle Controls + List View Layout Refactor (Phases OM–OP)
**Research Date:** 2026-05-21
**Scope:** CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue · CiProgressReportTab.vue

---

### §2.213-A — Root Cause: Toggle Icon Compression

**Component: CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue (line 180–183)**
`html
<v-btn-toggle v-model="viewMode" mandatory density="compact" variant="outlined" divided>
  <v-btn value="table" size="x-small" icon="mdi-table" title="Table View" />
  <v-btn value="list" size="x-small" icon="mdi-format-list-bulleted" title="List View" />
</v-btn-toggle>
`
**Component: CiProgressReportTab.vue (line 191–196)**
`html
<v-btn-toggle v-model="viewMode" mandatory density="compact" color="info" variant="outlined" divided>
  <v-btn value="list" size="x-small" icon="mdi-format-list-bulleted" title="List view" />
  <v-btn value="card" size="x-small" icon="mdi-view-grid-outline" title="Card view" />
  <v-btn value="table" size="x-small" icon="mdi-table" title="Table view" />
  <v-btn value="comparison" size="x-small" icon="mdi-compare-horizontal" title="Comparison view" />
</v-btn-toggle>
`

**Root cause:** density="compact" on the -btn-toggle group AND size="x-small" on each -btn = double-compression. This causes icons to clip, reducing tap target size below HCI minimums (below 24px effective hit area). On the 4-button progress group, the visual crowding is more severe. Vuetify -btn-toggle does not propagate density to child buttons automatically, but the combination of x-small + the group's compact density removes all internal padding.

**Fix:** Remove density="compact" from the toggle group (let it default to default). Change child button size from x-small → small. Add explicit min-width: 36px via class. This gives 36px height, comfortable icon padding, and proper active-state fill for the selected mode.

**Active-state:** -btn-toggle with mandatory prop already applies selected background via Vuetify's internal color logic. The color prop on the toggle group (or matched ariant="tonal" on active item) drives the fill. Current ariant="outlined" on the group + divided gives border separation — this is correct. Only the size/density combination needs correction.

**Responsive:** The toggle + Add button share a d-flex align-center ga-2 wrapper. On small screens this can overflow. Fix: allow the wrapper to lex-wrap and use ga-2 with mt-1 fallback. No breakpoint logic needed — lex-wrap handles it gracefully.

---

### §2.213-B — Root Cause: List View Card Layout Compression

**CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue list card (lines 256–332):**

Current structure:
`
[Card header: d-flex justify-space-between]
  LEFT: Rev# chip | Type chip | Status chip | Date     RIGHT: MOV | Expand | Edit | Delete
[Card body: d-flex flex-wrap ga-4]
  New Start | New Completion | Duration | Cost Adjustment (v-if populated)
`

**Problems:**
1. The LEFT area holds 4 items (3 chips + date) while RIGHT holds 4 icon buttons. The entire center of the card (~50% width) is empty whitespace.
2. The body row uses -if per field — if no date changes are present, body is effectively empty and confusing.
3. The chips-only header makes it hard to quickly scan key scheduling data (new dates, cost) without expanding.
4. On mobile, chips + date wrap unpredictably, pushing actions off-screen.

**Fix:** Replace the current 2-zone (header left + header right) pattern with a 3-column -row covering the full card body:
- LEFT col (sm=3): Rev # (bold) + Type chip (color-coded) + Status chip + Revision Date
- CENTER col (sm=6): New Start / New Completion / Duration / Cost Adjustment in a 2×2 stat grid
- RIGHT col (sm=3): Actions aligned right (MOV link · Expand · Edit · Delete)

The expandable area (justification text) remains as a -expand-transition below the row. This eliminates whitespace and keeps all key data scannable without expanding.

---

### §2.213-C — Root Cause: Progress Report List View Layout Compression

**CiProgressReportTab.vue list card (lines 257–344):**

Current structure:
`
[Card header: d-flex justify-space-between]
  LEFT: Type chip | Report#/Date      RIGHT: MOV | Expand | Edit | Delete
[Inline summary: d-flex flex-wrap ga-4]
  Completion% | Planned% | Slippage% | Cost to Date
[Expandable:]
  Cost This Period | Calendar Days | % Time Elapsed | Remarks | Issues | Mitigation
`

**Problems:**
1. Same center-void pattern: left has 2 items, right has actions, center is empty.
2. The statistics row below (Completion, Planned, Slippage, Cost) is the **primary data** — it should be the center column of the card, not a secondary row beneath.
3. Left section has insufficient identification: only Type chip + report number/date — no "Reporting Period" or submission date concept.
4. On narrow screens, stat grid wraps chaotically.

**Fix:** Same 3-column pattern:
- LEFT col (sm=3): Report Type chip (color-coded) + Report # + Report Date + 	ext-caption "Reporting Period" label
- CENTER col (sm=6): % Completion (large/bold) + Planned% + Slippage% (colored red if negative) + Cost to Date — in a 2×2 stat grid
- RIGHT col (sm=3): MOV link · Expand · Edit · Delete actions

Expandable area retains: Cost This Period, Calendar Days, % Time Elapsed, Remarks, Issues, Mitigation.

---

### §2.213-D — Rendering Consistency Between Components

Both components share the same structural pattern. Phase OM can apply the toggle fix to both in a single pass (they are in separate files so both need updating). Phases ON and OO are separate because the data models and field layouts differ.

| Component | Toggle fix | List layout fix |
|-----------|-----------|----------------|
| CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue | Phase OM | Phase ON |
| CiProgressReportTab.vue | Phase OM | Phase OO |

No backend changes, no migrations, no new composables required.

**Synchronization impact:** Both components are reused across detail-[id].vue, edit-[id].vue, and 
ew.vue (via the CiProgressReportTab). Changes to the component propagate automatically to all three pages. No page-level changes needed.

---

### §2.213-E — Risk Register

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|-----------|
| v-btn-toggle divided border renders incorrectly with size="small" | Low | Test; fall back to custom border CSS if needed |
| 3-col v-row wraps to single column on xs screens | Low | Use lex-column on xs + lex-row on sm+ via Vuetify breakpoint cols |
| Double-SFC concatenation bug (seen in OH/OI) | Medium | Use targeted edit tool replacements, not full rewrites |

---

## §2.214 — Toggle Structure + List Distribution + Per-Report Remarks (Phases OQ–OT)

**Date:** 2026-05-22
**Scope:** Follow-up to OM–OP. ECO feedback indicates the toggle controls still look compressed despite OM's density/size fix, list-view 3-column distribution from ON/OO still presents as right-aligned with whitespace-on-left, and remarks must live inside each Progress Report as a nested chronological list (not a standalone card).

---

### §2.214-A — Toggle structure root cause

OM dropped `density="compact"` + bumped `size="x-small"` → `size="small"` — visible padding improved but `<v-btn icon="mdi-foo" size="small" />` shorthand still renders Vuetify's default min-width that hugs the icon tightly. `<v-btn-toggle variant="outlined" divided>` draws thin dividers that visually overlap small-size icons; active-state contrast weak.

**OQ fix strategy:**
- Replace `<v-btn icon="mdi-x">` shorthand with `<v-btn><v-icon size="20">mdi-x</v-icon></v-btn>` — explicit icon child = consistent 20px sizing
- Drop `divided` attribute; let solid background carry separation
- Apply `color="primary|info|warning"` on the wrapper toggle so active button gets accent-tinted background
- Wrap with `class="border rounded"` for unified segmented outline
- `min-width: 44px` (touch-target compliant) via inline style or class

---

### §2.214-B — List view 3-column distribution diagnosis

Likely causes of the "content stuck right" perception after ON/OO:
1. Center column (`md="7"`) uses a nested `v-row no-gutters` with `cols="6"` stat cells. Each cell has `pa-1` — no `flex-grow`, so short stat values ("—") leave the cell right-anchored.
2. Left + Right have `bg-grey-lighten-5` while Center is plain — creates a "U-shape" perception where the center reads as empty whitespace.
3. On narrow viewports, `flex-md-column flex-row` on Right column may not collapse cleanly.

**OR fix strategy:**
- Rebalance: `cols="12" md="3 | 6 | 3"` — actions column gets more breathing room
- Replace nested `v-row no-gutters` in Center with CSS grid: `display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 12px 16px;` — guarantees equal split regardless of value lengths
- Each stat cell: remove `pa-1`, let grid `gap` handle spacing; explicit `text-align: left`
- Strip `bg-grey-lighten-5` from Left+Right OR apply matching tint to Center so the card feels visually continuous
- Hairline vertical dividers via `border-right: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` for crisp section separation

---

### §2.214-C — Per-Progress-Report remarks architecture decision

ECO directive: standalone Remarks Log card on Progress Report tab is redundant. Remarks belong to each Progress Report entry — independently managed per report.

**Data model evaluated:**

| Option | Pros | Cons |
|--------|------|------|
| **(A) Add `remarksList: jsonb` to ConstructionProgressReport** | Per-report ownership. Independent CRUD via existing endpoints. Single migration. No new entity. | Bumps row size on the reports table (negligible). |
| **(B) New entity `ConstructionProgressReportRemark`** | True audit isolation. Independent reviewer permissions per remark. | Migration + entity + DTO + service + controller + UI complexity. |
| **(C) Reuse project-level `remarks_log` keyed by `progress_report_id`** | No new schema. | Foreign-key-by-convention fragile; orphan cleanup on report delete is manual. |

**Recommendation: (A)** — lowest friction, matches report lifetime (delete report → remarks delete with it), doesn't block (B) as future option.

**Schema change:**
```typescript
@Property({ columnType: 'jsonb', default: '[]' })
remarksList: Array<{ text: string; author?: string; createdAt: string; updatedAt?: string }> = [];
```

**Migration `Migration20260522040000_AddProgressReportRemarksList`:**
```sql
ALTER TABLE construction_progress_reports
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS remarks_list JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]';
```

**DTO:** `CreateProgressReportDto` + `UpdateProgressReportDto` add `remarks_list?: Array<...>` (class-validator `@IsArray() @IsOptional()`).

**Service stamping (same Hybrid Light pattern as §2.211-D):** on report create/update, pre-process `dto.remarks_list` — entries missing `created_at` stamped NOW; missing `author` resolved from JWT.

**Frontend (CiProgressReportTab.vue dialog):**
- Add a "Remarks" section inside the existing add/edit dialog (NOT a separate dialog)
- `<CiBulletListInput v-model="form.remarks_list_texts" />` — operator gets the simple add/remove list UX
- On save: wrap each plain text into `{ text, createdAt }` with timestamps preserved for existing entries on edit
- Per-entry author + timestamp display surfaces in expanded card row (existing expansion area in list view)

**Standalone Remarks Log card removal:** delete the existing card body from edit-[id].vue Progress Report tab. The project-level `construction_projects.remarks_log` JSONB column remains in schema for back-compat; UI no longer renders it; data preserved.

---

### §2.214-D — Three-page synchronization invariant — still upheld

All changes target reusable components (`CiRevisionOrdersTable`, `CiProgressReportTab`) and one entity (`ConstructionProgressReport`). Effect propagates to edit (current consumer) and any future detail-page consumer with no per-page divergence.

---

### §2.214-E — Phase boundaries

| Phase | Scope | Blocks |
|-------|-------|--------|
| OQ | Toggle button group structural refactor (both components) | None |
| OR | List view 3-column container distribution rebalance + CSS grid stat layout | None |
| OS | Backend `remarksList` JSONB + migration + DTO + service stamping; frontend dialog UI + standalone Remarks Log card removal | OT |
| OT | Verification + closure | All above |

**Implementation strategy:** OQ + OR are pure frontend, independent — any order. OS spans backend + frontend — backend first (entity + migration + DTO + service), then frontend dialog wiring, then card removal.

---

## §2.215 — Detail Page Redesign, Compact Controls, List-Type Progress Report Fields

**Phase:** OR (amended) + OS (expanded) + OU (new) + OT (verification)
**Scope:** detail-[id].vue overview tab + edit-[id].vue toggle/card layout + CiProgressReportTab form list-type fields
**Date:** 2026-05-22

---

### §2.215-A — detail-[id].vue Overview Tab: Current Structure Analysis

Current overview tab rendering order (inside `<v-window-item value="overview">`):
1. `<CiProjectHeroCard>` — physical/financial progress, days remaining, contract amount
2. Profile images card (PROFILE-category gallery images, max 3)
3. Publication status strip — pub status chip, approval/rejection metadata, implementing agency
4. KPI stat row — Financial Progress% + Milestones completed/total (2 tonal cards)
5. Section divider ("Project Details")
6. 2-column `v-expansion-panels` (LEFT: Info/Location/Contract/Financial/Approval | RIGHT: Key Docs/Strategic/Indicators/Signatories)

The existing page header (ABOVE the tabs) already contains: project name, project code, and action buttons (Submit/Withdraw/Publish/Reject/Edit). The tabs are directly after `<CiProjectGuideCard>`.

**Problem:** The `CiProjectHeroCard` is inside the overview tab, not above the tabs. Project-critical summary data (contractor, PM, PE, funding source, start date, target completion) are buried inside expansion panels — not visible at a glance.

**Proposed layout:**
```
[PAGE HEADER: title + code + action buttons — existing, unchanged]
[ENTERPRISE SUMMARY CARD — NEW, inserted between guide card and v-tabs]
  TOP ROW: Title chip | Code chip | Status chip | Category chip
  LEFT COL (md=6): Contractor / Funding Source / Start Date / Target Completion
  RIGHT COL (md=6): Project Manager / Project Engineer / Physical% / Financial% / Days Remaining
[V-TABS — existing, unchanged]
[V-WINDOW > overview tab]
  → 8 collapsible v-expansion-panels (see §2.215-B)
```

**Field availability on `UIProjectDetail`** (all resolved, no new endpoints needed):
| Display | Source field |
|---------|-------------|
| Contractor | `project.contractor` (adapts `contractor_name`) |
| Funding Source | `project.fundSource` (adapts `funding_source_name`) |
| Start Date | `project.startDate` (adapts `start_date`) |
| Target Completion | `project.targetCompletionDate` (adapts `target_completion_date`) |
| Project Manager | `project.projectManager` (adapts `project_manager`) |
| Project Engineer | `project.projectEngineer` (adapts `project_engineer`) |
| Physical % | `project.physicalProgress` |
| Financial % | `project.financialProgress` |
| Days Remaining | `progressSummary.daysRemaining` (existing computed) |

CiProjectHeroCard may be REMOVED from the overview tab body since its data is now in the summary card — or kept as a compact progress-only card. Decision: remove `CiProjectHeroCard` from overview tab body; the summary card covers its data plus more. The profile images card stays (visual aid).

---

### §2.215-B — Overview Tab: Collapsible Container Architecture

Replace the current ad-hoc layout (hero card + status strip + KPI row + expansion panels) with a unified `v-expansion-panels multiple` container. 8 sections:

| # | Panel | Content Source |
|---|-------|---------------|
| 1 | Project Profile | Summary, description, scope, objectives (array), key features (chips), beneficiaries |
| 2 | Dates & Duration | Start date, target completion, actual completion, project duration, original contract duration, contract number |
| 3 | Progress & Reports | Physical%, Financial%, milestones count, Days Remaining; link/button to Progress Reports tab |
| 4 | Strategic Alignment | RDP chapters (rdpAlignment), 0+8 agenda (socioeconomicAgenda), LIKHA goals (csuLikhaGoals) — see §2.215-E |
| 5 | Project Indicators | Output indicators (array), outcome indicators (array) |
| 6 | Key Documents | Document checklist (existing keyDocuments computed); drive links |
| 7 | Financial Summary | Financials from `project.financials` (existing financialSummary computed) |
| 8 | Approval History | Publication status, approval metadata, audit trail preview (auditLogs) |

Default open: panels 1 (Project Profile) and 3 (Progress & Reports). All others collapsed.

**State change:** replace `overviewPanelsLeft` + `overviewPanelsRight` (separate left/right column panels) with single `overviewPanels: number[]` controlling the unified single-column panel set. `[0, 2]` = panels 1 and 3 open by default.

---

### §2.215-C — Action Buttons: Horizontal vs Vertical in List Cards

**Current state** (both `CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue` and `CiProgressReportTab.vue`):
```html
<v-col cols="12" md="2" class="d-flex flex-md-column flex-row align-center justify-center ga-1 ...">
```

`flex-md-column` forces vertical stacking on md+ breakpoints. On desktop this creates a tall right column (typically 4 stacked icon buttons). Operator directive: "compact horizontal grouping, not vertical stacks."

**Fix:** Remove `flex-md-column` — keep `flex-row` always:
```html
<v-col cols="12" md="2" class="d-flex flex-row flex-wrap align-center justify-center ga-1 ...">
```

`flex-wrap` allows overflow to a second row on very narrow columns without losing horizontal character. Column width `md="2"` is sufficient for 4 x 28px buttons in a row (4 × 28 = 112px < typical md="2" = ~180px at 12-col 1200px container).

For Revision Orders: MOV icon + expand toggle + edit + delete = 4 buttons horizontal.
For Progress Reports: same 4-button pattern.

No column width changes needed for this fix — `md="2"` already allocates enough space for horizontal layout.

---

### §2.215-D — OR Scope Clarification: Center Column CSS Grid

From §2.214-B, the center column in both list-view cards uses nested `v-row no-gutters` with `cols="6"` stat cells. Short values right-anchor and create "U-shape" with `bg-grey-lighten-5` on left/right cols but plain center. Fix already prescribed:

```css
display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 12px 16px;
```

Combined with horizontal actions (§2.215-C), the OR phase touches:
1. Center col: CSS grid div replacing nested `v-row`
2. Right col: `flex-md-column` → `flex-row flex-wrap`
3. Optional: add `border-right: 1px solid rgba(0,0,0,0.08)` hairlines to Left and Center cols

No column width rebalancing needed — `3 | 7 | 2` already works; `flex-row` action layout fits within `md="2"`.

---

### §2.215-E — OS Expanded Scope: 4 JSONB List Fields

**Trigger:** Operator directive: convert single-text fields in the Progress Report form to multi-entry list-type (CiBulletListInput pattern) for: Narrative, Remarks, Issues/Risks Encountered, Mitigation Actions.

**Current entity fields on `ConstructionProgressReport`:**
| Field | Type | Disposition |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `remarks` | `text`, nullable | KEEP for back-compat; supplemented by `remarksList` |
| `issuesEncountered` | `text`, nullable | KEEP for back-compat; supplemented by `issuesEncounteredList` |
| `mitigationActions` | `text`, nullable | KEEP for back-compat; supplemented by `mitigationActionsList` |
| `narrative` | — | DOES NOT EXIST — new field required |

**4 new JSONB columns to add:**

| New Column | ORM Property | Default |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| `narrative_list` | `narrativeList` | `'[]'` |
| `remarks_list` | `remarksList` | `'[]'` |
| `issues_encountered_list` | `issuesEncounteredList` | `'[]'` |
| `mitigation_actions_list` | `mitigationActionsList` | `'[]'` |

**Entry shape per item:** `{ text: string; createdAt: string; author?: string }`

**Migration:** extend `Migration20260522040000_AddProgressReportRemarksList` to add all 4 columns:
```sql
ALTER TABLE construction_progress_reports
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS narrative_list JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS remarks_list JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS issues_encountered_list JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS mitigation_actions_list JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '[]';
```

**DTO additions** (both Create + Update via PartialType):
```typescript
@IsOptional() @IsArray() narrative_list?: Array<{ text: string; author?: string; created_at?: string }>;
@IsOptional() @IsArray() remarks_list?: Array<{ text: string; author?: string; created_at?: string }>;
@IsOptional() @IsArray() issues_encountered_list?: Array<{ text: string; author?: string; created_at?: string }>;
@IsOptional() @IsArray() mitigation_actions_list?: Array<{ text: string; author?: string; created_at?: string }>;
```

**Service stamping:** on create/update, for each list field — entries missing `created_at` stamped with `new Date().toISOString()`; missing `author` resolved from calling user's name (best-effort, no hard failure).

**Composable `ProgressReport` interface additions:**
```typescript
narrativeList: Array<{ text: string; createdAt: string; author?: string }>;
remarksList: Array<{ text: string; createdAt: string; author?: string }>;
issuesEncounteredList: Array<{ text: string; createdAt: string; author?: string }>;
mitigationActionsList: Array<{ text: string; createdAt: string; author?: string }>;
```
Normalize maps `r.narrativeList ?? r.narrative_list ?? []` for each.

**Dialog form state additions (`CiProgressReportTab.vue`):**
- `narrative_items: string[]` (simple text array for bullet input)
- `remarks_items: string[]`
- `issues_items: string[]`
- `mitigation_items: string[]`

`openEdit(r)`: hydrate from `r.narrativeList.map(x => x.text)` etc.
`save()`: convert back to `{ text, createdAt }` — preserve existing entries' timestamps on edit (match by index).

`<CiBulletListInput>` wires up 4 times in the dialog form, one per field.

**List-view expanded detail:** render each list as a numbered bullet list under section headers ("Narrative", "Remarks", "Issues Encountered", "Mitigation Actions"). Fallback to old text field if list is empty (back-compat).

---

### §2.215-F — Adapter Gap: RDP/0+8/LIKHA Not Yet in UIProjectDetail

`findOne` returns `cp.*` which includes `rdp_alignment`, `socioeconomic_agenda`, `csu_likha_goals` as JSONB columns. The backend service correctly parses these in `jsonFields`. However, `UIProjectDetail` in `adapters.ts` does not yet include these fields — they are not in the interface definition nor in `adaptProjectDetail`.

**Fix required for OU phase:**
1. Add to `UIProjectDetail` interface:
   ```typescript
   rdpAlignment: any[];
   socioeconomicAgenda: any[];
   csuLikhaGoals: any[];
   ```
2. Add to `adaptProjectDetail`:
   ```typescript
   rdpAlignment: backend.rdp_alignment || [],
   socioeconomicAgenda: backend.socioeconomic_agenda || [],
   csuLikhaGoals: backend.csu_likha_goals || [],
   ```
   
No backend changes. `BackendProjectDetail` already receives these fields via `cp.*` in the SELECT.

---

### §2.215-G — Phase Boundaries

| Phase | Scope | Risk |
|-------|-------|------|
| OR | Center col CSS grid + right col `flex-row` horizontal actions (both components) | Low — template-only |
| OS | 4 JSONB list fields + migration + DTOs + service + composable + dialog (CiProgressReportTab) | Low — all additive |
| OU | Adapter gap fix (rdpAlignment etc.) + enterprise summary card above tabs + overview tab collapsible refactor | Medium — detail-[id].vue template restructure |
| OT | Verification of OR + OS + OU | None |

**Synchronization invariant:** OR and OS target reusable components — effect propagates to all consumers (edit page, detail page). OU targets only detail-[id].vue. No cross-dependency between OR/OS and OU.

---

## §2.216 — Phase OV/OW Research (2026-05-22)
### Scope: Toggle control standardization, Comparison View removal, Narrative dialog cleanup, card visual refinement

---

### §2.216-A: CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue — Toggle & Default View Audit

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue` (481 lines)

**Current toggle (lines 182–195):**
```html
<v-btn-toggle v-model="viewMode" mandatory color="warning" variant="outlined" class="border rounded">
  <v-btn value="table" title="Table View" style="min-width: 44px">
    <v-icon size="20">mdi-table</v-icon>
  </v-btn>
  <v-btn value="list" title="List View" style="min-width: 44px">
    <v-icon size="20">mdi-format-list-bulleted</v-icon>
  </v-btn>
</v-btn-toggle>
```
- Icon-only: no text labels, no `density="compact"`, no `divided` attribute
- Active state relies on icon colour change only — fails HCI minimum contrast requirement

**Timeline reference (detail-[id].vue line 1912):**
```html
<v-btn-toggle v-model="timelineViewMode" density="compact" variant="outlined" divided :mandatory="true">
  <v-btn value="list" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-format-list-bulleted">List</v-btn>
  <v-btn value="table" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-table">Table</v-btn>
  <v-btn value="calendar" size="small" prepend-icon="mdi-calendar-month">Calendar</v-btn>
</v-btn-toggle>
```
- Text labels + prepend-icon + density="compact" + divided = enterprise-standard

**Default viewMode:** `ref<'table' | 'list'>('table')` at line 20 → directive requires LIST VIEW as default.

**Card styling (lines 270–335):**
- `elevation="1"` — minimal shadow, visually flat
- No left-border accent — identity column merges visually with center
- `bg-grey-lighten-5` on left col is subtle but insufficient for enterprise distinction
- Spacing wrapper `d-flex flex-column ga-2` (line 268) is adequate

**Fix strategy:**
- OV-A: Change default viewMode from `'table'` → `'list'`
- OV-B: Replace toggle with Timeline-style (`density="compact"`, `divided`, `:mandatory="true"`, `size="small"`, `prepend-icon` + text labels; keep `color="warning"`)
- OV-C: Add `elevation="2"` + `style="border-left: 3px solid rgba(var(--v-theme-warning), 0.6)"` on each list card; add `class="mb-2"` outer gap

---

### §2.216-B: CiProgressReportTab.vue — Toggle Audit

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiProgressReportTab.vue` (697 lines)

**Current toggle (lines 210–228):**
```html
<v-btn-toggle v-model="viewMode" mandatory color="info" variant="outlined" class="border rounded">
  <v-btn value="list" ...><v-icon size="20">mdi-format-list-bulleted</v-icon></v-btn>
  <v-btn value="card" ...><v-icon size="20">mdi-view-grid-outline</v-icon></v-btn>
  <v-btn value="table" ...><v-icon size="20">mdi-table</v-icon></v-btn>
  <v-btn value="comparison" ...><v-icon size="20">mdi-compare-horizontal</v-icon></v-btn>
</v-btn-toggle>
```
- Same issues: icon-only, no text labels, no density/divided attributes
- Comparison btn triggers dead view (script has NO backing refs)

**Fix strategy:** Replace with Timeline-style 3-button toggle (list/card/table) — drop comparison.

---

### §2.216-C: CiProgressReportTab.vue — Comparison View Dead Code

**Script** (lines 1–196): `viewMode = ref<'list'|'card'|'table'|'comparison'>('list')` — type includes `'comparison'`.
`compareSelected`, `comparedReports`, `toggleCompare` are NEVER defined anywhere in script.

**Template** (lines 467–526): Full comparison view references `comparedReports.length`, `compareSelected.has(r.id)`, `toggleCompare(r.id)` — all undefined → runtime ReferenceError if user clicks "comparison".

**Removal scope:** Lines 467–526 in template + `'comparison'` from viewMode type literal.

---

### §2.216-D: CiProgressReportTab.vue — Narrative Dialog Redundancy

**Two overlapping sections in dialog:**

| Section | Label | Fields | Lines |
|---------|-------|--------|-------|
| Legacy | "Narrative" | `remarks` textarea, `issues_encountered` textarea, `mitigation_actions` textarea | 656–667 |
| New | "Structured Narrative Lists" | `narrative_items` CiBulletListInput, `remarks_items`, `issues_items`, `mitigation_items` | 669–682 |

- Both sections populated from `blank()` and `openEdit()` — double entry complexity
- Legacy textareas are SUPERSEDED by CiBulletListInput fields
- Section labels "Narrative" / "Structured Narrative Lists" are ambiguous
- `save()` function correctly strips `_items` arrays and converts to `_list` payloads
- Legacy `remarks/issues_encountered/mitigation_actions` from `rest` spread also reach backend (backward compat OK)

**Removal scope:** Remove 3 legacy `v-textarea` fields from dialog template AND from `blank()` AND from `openEdit()`. Retain in `save()`'s `rest` spread as empty strings will be filtered out (filter: `v !== ''`).

**New dialog section label:** Replace "Narrative" + "Structured Narrative Lists" → single "Narrative Details" section with 4 labeled subsections:
1. **[Remarks]** → `remarks_items` CiBulletListInput
2. **[Issues / Risks Encountered]** → `issues_items` CiBulletListInput
3. **[Mitigation Actions]** → `mitigation_items` CiBulletListInput
4. **[General Narrative Notes]** → `narrative_items` CiBulletListInput

**Expanded render (lines 344–368):** Keep the backward-compat fallback pattern (shows list if available, falls back to text field). No changes needed here.

---

### §2.216-E: Card Visual Refinement — Both Components

**Pattern confirmed from OR-A/OR-B cards:**
- Both list-view cards use `elevation="1"` — OK baseline
- No colored left-border accent distinguishing record type
- Light background on outer wrapper (`pa-3 d-flex flex-column ga-2`) is neutral grey

**Suggested improvements (matching enterprise PMO pattern):**
- `elevation="2"` on each card (soft shadow)
- Left border accent: `style="border-left: 3px solid rgba(...)"` using brand color
  - Revision Orders → warning color accent
  - Progress Reports → info color accent
- Card hover: no change needed (Vuetify default)
- Wrapper gap: already `ga-2` — increase to `ga-3` for better card breathing room
- Add `class="pa-3"` to outer wrapper (already `pa-3` — verify)

---

## §2.217 — Phase OY/OZ/PA Research (2026-05-22)
### Scope: MOV (Means of Verification) safe-open refactor — validation, feedback, reusable guard

---

### §2.217-A: MOV Render Point Inventory

All current MOV link render points use raw `<a :href="..." target="_blank">` tags — zero validation before redirect.

| # | File | Line(s) | Pattern | Risk |
|---|------|---------|---------|------|
| 1 | `CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue` | ~232 (table) | `<a v-if="item.movLink" :href="item.movLink">` | Opens URL without format check |
| 2 | `CiRevisionOrdersTable.vue` | ~313 (list) | `<a v-if="r.movLink" :href="r.movLink">` | Same |
| 3 | `CiProgressReportTab.vue` | ~350 (list) | `<a v-if="r.movLink" :href="r.movLink">` | Same |
| 4 | `CiProgressReportTab.vue` | ~402 (card) | `<a v-if="r.movLink" :href="r.movLink">` | Same |
| 5 | `CiProgressReportTab.vue` | ~432 (table) | `<a v-if="item.movLink" :href="item.movLink">` | Same |
| 6 | `CiMovEvidenceSection.vue` | ~239 (file) | `<a :href="e.filePath" target="_blank">` | No null guard on filePath |
| 7 | `CiMovEvidenceSection.vue` | ~245 (url) | `<a :href="e.movLink ?? '#'" target="_blank">` | Falls back to '#' — takes user to top of page with no feedback |
| 8 | `edit-[id].vue` | ~2841 | `<a :href="mov.movLink" target="_blank">` | No `v-if` guard — `mov.movLink` may be undefined |

**Summary:** 8 unsafe render points across 4 files. No validation, no user feedback on failure, no empty-state messaging.

---

### §2.217-B: Current Validation Coverage

**CiMovEvidenceSection.vue `submitLink()`** (line 112): Validates URL format with `MOV_URL_REGEX = /^https?:\/\/.+/i` on SAVE — but NOT on VIEW. Once stored, links are rendered raw.

**CiMovPendingSection.vue**: No URL validation — URL field is optional, saved as-is.

**Backend DTO**: `mov_link` is `@IsOptional() @IsUrl()` in `create-mov-entry.dto.ts` — server-side format validation exists but does not prevent broken/expired URLs from being stored.

**Conclusion:** Validation exists at submission time only. There is no view-time guard. Broken, expired, or manually-typed invalid URLs bypass all checks.

---

### §2.217-C: MOV State Classification

| State | Condition | Required Feedback |
|-------|-----------|-------------------|
| `empty` | url is null / undefined / '' | "No MOV link has been provided for this record." |
| `invalid` | fails `https?://` regex | "The MOV link appears to be invalid or malformed." |
| `valid` | passes regex | `window.open(url, '_blank', 'noopener,noreferrer')` |

File entries (filePath) are stored as server-side paths. They may return 403/404 if deleted. Frontend cannot pre-check server file availability — fail gracefully with toast on network error.

---

### §2.217-D: Composable Design — `useMovSafe`

**File:** `pmo-frontend/composables/useMovSafe.ts`

```ts
const URL_REGEX = /^https?:\/\/.+\..+/i
type MovState = 'valid' | 'empty' | 'invalid'

function resolveMovState(url?: string | null): MovState
function openMovSafely(url?: string | null): void   // validates + opens or shows toast
function openFileSafely(path?: string | null): void  // same for filePath entries
```

- Zero external dependencies beyond `useToast`
- Pure frontend: no backend round-trip for URL validation
- `openMovSafely` replaces all `<a :href>` click targets
- `openFileSafely` replaces `e.filePath` direct links in CiMovEvidenceSection

---

### §2.217-E: Component Design — `CiMovLinkBtn.vue`

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiMovLinkBtn.vue`

Props:
```ts
url?: string | null    // the MOV link or file path
iconOnly?: boolean     // default true — shows mdi-link-variant icon button
size?: string          // default 'x-small'
color?: string         // default 'primary'
label?: string         // tooltip label, default 'View MOV'
```

Behavior:
- Renders a `v-btn icon` (iconOnly=true) or `v-btn prepend-icon` (iconOnly=false)
- Empty/invalid URL → `color="grey"` + tooltip "No MOV provided" + calls `openMovSafely` (which shows toast)
- Valid URL → `color={color}` + tooltip with url preview + opens on click
- Always renders (no v-if on url) — consistent column width in tables/cards

Replaces ALL 8 render points above.

---

### §2.217-F: edit-[id].vue MOV render audit

**Line 2841:** `<a :href="mov.movLink" target="_blank">` — no `v-if="mov.movLink"` guard. If `movLink` is null/undefined, the anchor has no href, and clicking opens a blank tab or navigates to the page root. Replace with `CiMovLinkBtn :url="mov.movLink"`.

---

### §2.217-G: Scope Boundary (YAGNI)

- Backend URL re-validation on GET is **out of scope** — adds latency, no institutional requirement
- File availability server-check is **out of scope** — filePath is already served via NestJS static or storage service; 404s are handled by browser
- MOV "verified" workflow changes are **out of scope** — existing PENDING/VERIFIED/REJECTED status chips remain unchanged
- CiMovPendingSection.vue URL validation on add **out of scope** (field is optional, saved as-is — server DTO validates)

---

## §2.218 — Phase PC/PD/PE Research (2026-05-22)
### Scope: Timeline Tab — Milestone + Diary Card Treatment (edit-[id].vue)

---

### §2.218-A: Current State — Timeline Tab in edit-[id].vue

**Location:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`, lines ~2030–2410 (v-window-item value="timeline")

**Structure:**
1. **Progress Summary card** (lines ~2033–2091) — `v-card class="mb-4"` with unstyled `v-card-title`. No icon, no accent color, no polished header consistent with the schedule tab's Primary Project Dates card.
2. **2-column row** — Left: Milestones card. Right: Project Diary card.
   - Both are `v-card class="mb-4 h-100"` with plain `v-card-title` (no `bg-grey-lighten-4`, no icon color, no subtitle-1 class).
3. **Milestones list** (lines ~2179–2250) — Uses `v-list lines="three" density="comfortable"` with `v-list-item v-for class="border-b"`. Each item:
   - Prepend: color avatar
   - Title: milestone title + status chip + category chip
   - Subtitle: date range + progress bar + description + remarks
   - Append: edit + delete buttons
   - **No card container per item** — flat divider-separated list, visually inconsistent with OR/OW treatment.
4. **Diary list** (lines ~2340–2403) — Same flat `v-list density="comfortable" class="pa-0"` with `v-list-item class="border-b"`. Each item:
   - Prepend: color avatar
   - Title: entry title + type chip
   - Subtitle: date + period label + description + weather/manpower
   - Append: edit + delete buttons
   - **No card container per item** — same flat pattern.

---

### §2.218-B: Inconsistency with Completed Tabs

| Tab | List Item Rendering | Card Treatment | Status |
|-----|---------------------|----------------|--------|
| Dates & Duration | CiRevisionOrdersTable | `elevation="1" border` + warning left accent | ✅ Done (OR/OV) |
| Progress Report | CiProgressReportTab | `elevation="1" border` + info left accent | ✅ Done (OW) |
| Timeline (Milestones) | flat `v-list-item border-b` | **None** | ⬜ Next |
| Timeline (Diary) | flat `v-list-item border-b` | **None** | ⬜ Next |

---

### §2.218-C: Card Header Inconsistency

The schedule tab (Dates & Duration) uses a polished card header:
```html
<v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2 py-2 px-4 bg-grey-lighten-4">
  <v-icon size="small" icon="mdi-calendar-clock" color="primary" />
  <span class="text-subtitle-1 font-weight-medium">Primary Project Dates</span>
</v-card-title>
```

The timeline tab uses a bare header:
```html
<v-card-title>
  <v-icon start>mdi-trending-up</v-icon>
  Progress Summary
</v-card-title>
```
Same issue on Milestones and Project Diary card titles — no `bg-grey-lighten-4`, no `text-subtitle-1 font-weight-medium`, no `py-2 px-4`.

---

### §2.218-D: Proposed Card Treatment per Item

**Milestones** — replace `v-list-item border-b` with:
```html
<v-card elevation="1" border rounded="lg" class="overflow-hidden"
  style="border-left: 3px solid rgba(var(--v-theme-primary), 0.7)">
```
Keep existing content structure (no major restructuring needed — the milestone item is simpler and the existing prepend/subtitle layout is appropriate).

Use dynamic accent color matching milestone status:
- COMPLETED → `--v-theme-success`
- IN_PROGRESS → `--v-theme-primary`
- NOT_STARTED → `--v-theme-grey` (or no accent)
- DELAYED → `--v-theme-error`

**Diary entries** — same card treatment with info/teal accent:
```html
<v-card elevation="1" border rounded="lg" class="overflow-hidden"
  style="border-left: 3px solid rgba(var(--v-theme-info), 0.7)">
```

---

### §2.218-E: Card Header Polish

Replace bare `v-card-title` headers in timeline tab with consistent headers:
```html
<v-card-title class="d-flex align-center ga-2 py-2 px-4 bg-grey-lighten-4">
  <v-icon size="small" icon="mdi-flag-checkered" color="primary" />
  <span class="text-subtitle-1 font-weight-medium">Project Milestones</span>
</v-card-title>
```
Same for Progress Summary (icon: `mdi-trending-up`, color: `success`) and Project Diary (icon: `mdi-notebook-outline`, color: `info`).

---

### §2.218-F: Item Content Restructure Strategy

**KISS principle:** Do NOT apply full 3-column CSS grid to milestone/diary items. The existing prepend-avatar + content + append-actions layout is appropriate and already conveys hierarchy. The bordered card wrapper alone (with accent + elevation + rounded corners) provides sufficient visual improvement and consistency.

Gap the list wrapper: replace the `v-list` wrapper with a plain `div class="d-flex flex-column ga-2"` to render cards with spacing instead of a list with dividers.

---

### §2.218-G: Scope Boundary

- **In scope:** Timeline tab card treatment (edit-[id].vue milestones + diary items) + card header polish
- **Out of scope:** Personnel tab (already uses `v-card variant="tonal"` per group — visually adequate), Documents tab (upload-form pattern, not a list-of-records pattern), Others tab (row-based form entries, not list items)
- **Out of scope:** Adding view toggle to edit-page timeline (YAGNI — edit page is a data entry form, not a display surface; view toggles belong on the detail page where they already exist)


---
