## §2.236 — External Personnel RBAC Synchronization (SA–SD)

**Directive:** Contractor user assigned "Contributor" (tabTimeline + tabProgressReport + tabOthers = true; tabProjectProfile + tabDatesDuration + tabPersonnel + tabAttachments = false) still sees all tabs in both edit-[id].vue and detail-[id].vue.

### Root Cause 1 — edit-[id].vue:279 Hardcoded Bypass

File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue`

`visibleTabOrder` (line 275–287) filters `tabOrder` against `effectivePermissions`. However line 279 contains:

```typescript
if (tab === 'basic' || tab === 'progress') return true
```

This unconditionally admits `basic` (Project Profile) and `progress` (Progress Report) for ALL users, including contractors with `tabProjectProfile: false` and `tabProgressReport: false`. The tab permission matrix is bypassed before the permission check runs.

Additionally, `useCoiAccess(rawProject)` destructure (lines 34–44) does NOT include `isOwnerOrAssigned` or `myAssignment`, both of which are required to distinguish "assigned user with explicit permissions" from "non-assigned staff with default access".

### Root Cause 2 — detail-[id].vue: Zero Tab Filtering

File: `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/detail-[id].vue`

Line 20:
```typescript
const { canEditCurrentProject, isOwnerOrAssigned, canEditAnyTab } = useCoiAccess(project)
```

`effectivePermissions`, `myAssignment` are NOT destructured. No `v-if` guards exist on any `<v-tab>` elements (lines 1053–1081). All 8 tabs are always rendered for all users regardless of role or assignment.

### Detail Tab → permKey Map

| detail-[id] tab value | permKey (COI_PROJECT_TABS) | Notes |
|----------------------|---------------------------|-------|
| `overview`           | `tabProjectProfile`       | |
| `progress`           | `tabProgressReport`       | |
| `timeline`           | `tabTimeline`             | |
| `analytics`          | — (always visible)        | Read-only tab, no restriction |
| `gallery`            | `tabAttachments`          | Shared perm with documents |
| `documents`          | `tabAttachments`          | Shared perm with gallery |
| `team`               | `tabPersonnel`            | |
| `others`             | `tabOthers`               | |

### Correct visibleTabOrder Logic (SA Fix)

Current behavior: admin always pass → hardcoded bypass for basic+progress → remaining tabs gated by ep.

Correct behavior: admin always pass → for assigned users with permissions, use ep[permKey] for ALL tabs → for non-assigned non-admin, current defaults apply (all tabs visible, edit-page access itself is guarded separately).

```
isOwnerOrAssigned.value && myAssignment.value?.permissions
  ? ep[permKey] ?? false       ← assigned user: respect override
  : true                       ← non-assigned non-admin: show all (edit guard handles access)
```

### activeTab Correction (SC)

Both pages: when `visibleTabOrder` (edit) or equivalent (detail) changes after project load, if `activeTab` is not in the visible set, redirect to first visible tab. Prevents "Insufficient permissions" error from staying on a now-hidden tab.

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| SA | edit-[id].vue visibleTabOrder: remove hardcoded bypass, add isOwnerOrAssigned+myAssignment to destructure | pages/coi/edit-[id].vue |
| SB | detail-[id].vue: add effectivePermissions+myAssignment+isOwnerOrAssigned, add v-if to all 8 tabs | pages/coi/detail-[id].vue |
| SC | activeTab correction watcher (both pages) | pages/coi/edit-[id].vue, pages/coi/detail-[id].vue |
| SD | Verification | — |

---

*Phase SA–SD research complete (§2.236). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*


---

## §2.237 — RBAC Synchronization: Edit-Page Template Gap + Contractor Creation Block (TA–TD)

**Directive:** Full RBAC synchronization between institutional and external personnel. SA–SC implementation was partially correct — visibleTabOrder computed is accurate but the edit-[id].vue TEMPLATE does not use it for tab rendering.

### Root Cause A — edit-[id].vue Template (lines 1944–1978): Stale v-if Conditions

SA-B correctly rewrote `visibleTabOrder` (the computed) but the template's `<v-tab>` elements were NOT updated:

| Tab | Template condition | Problem |
|-----|--------------------|---------|
| `basic` | `<v-tab value="basic">` (no v-if) | ALWAYS renders — ignores tabProjectProfile |
| `schedule` | `v-if="isAdmin \|\| effectivePermissions.tabDatesDuration"` | Skips isOwnerOrAssigned/hasExplicitPerms gate |
| `timeline` | `v-if="isAdmin \|\| effectivePermissions.tabTimeline"` | Same skip |
| `progress` | `<v-tab value="progress">` (no v-if) | ALWAYS renders — ignores tabProgressReport |
| `personnel` | `v-if="isAdmin \|\| effectivePermissions.tabPersonnel"` | Same skip |
| `documents` | `v-if="isAdmin \|\| effectivePermissions.tabAttachments"` | Same skip |
| `others` | `v-if="isAdmin \|\| effectivePermissions.tabOthers"` | Same skip |

`visibleTabOrder` is used ONLY for: step counter, completion percentage, nextTab/prevTab nav, Prev/Next button visibility. It is NOT connected to the `<v-tab>` render visibility.

Consequence: contractor with tabProjectProfile=false and tabProgressReport=false still sees both tabs. When they navigate to Project Profile, tab content runs permission assertions → "Insufficient permissions" error.

Correct fix: replace each `<v-tab>` condition with `v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('tabValue')"` — uses the already-correct centralized computed as the single source of truth.

### Root Cause B — Middleware: /coi/new Not Blocked for Contractors

`middleware/permission.ts` contractor block (lines 14–22):
```typescript
if (isContractor.value) {
  const allowedPrefixes = ['/dashboard', '/coi', '/login', '/contractor']
  const allowed = allowedPrefixes.some(
    prefix => to.path === prefix || to.path.startsWith(prefix + '/')
  )
  if (!allowed) return navigateTo('/dashboard')
  return  // ← exits before canAdd check runs
}
```

`/coi/new` matches `to.path.startsWith('/coi/')` → contractor passes through. The subsequent `canAdd` guard at lines 81–97 never executes because of the early `return`. Contractors can navigate directly to `/coi/new` via URL.

### Root Cause C — new.vue: No Contractor Guard

`new.vue` uses `middleware: ['auth', 'permission']` but relies on the permission middleware — which (per Root Cause B) does not block contractors from `/coi/new`. No in-page guard exists.

### Backend Status (VERIFIED SECURE)

`@Post() @Roles('Admin', 'Staff')` — Contractor role NOT listed → RolesGuard returns 403 automatically. All write endpoints (PATCH, POST milestones/timeline/diary) also require 'Admin' or 'Staff'. Backend is fully secured against contractor project creation.

### detail-[id].vue Status (VERIFIED CORRECT)

SB-C (previous phase) correctly added `v-if="isTabVisible('X')"` to all 8 `<v-tab>` elements. `isTabVisible` computed uses `hasExplicitPerms` check identical to `visibleTabOrder`. Detail page tab filtering is working as intended.

### index.vue New Project Button (VERIFIED CORRECT)

`v-if="canAdd('coi')"` — Contractor role resolves to `ROLE_PERMISSIONS.Viewer` (no matching key in matrix) → `canAdd: false` → button hidden. Correct.

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| TA | edit-[id].vue template: add `v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('X')"` to all 7 `<v-tab>` elements | pages/coi/edit-[id].vue |
| TB | middleware: block `/coi/new` for contractors before early return | middleware/permission.ts |
| TC | new.vue: add in-page contractor redirect guard | pages/coi/new.vue |
| TD | Verification | — |

---

*Phase TA–TD research complete (§2.237). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*


---

## §2.238 — Root Cause: saveAssignments() Wrong Payload Key (UA–UE)

**Directive:** External personnel RBAC still not synchronized — contractor still sees all tabs despite TA–TC fixes.

### Root Cause — saveAssignments() Sends Wrong Key

`CiPersonnelAccessCard.saveAssignments()` (line 217–237):
```typescript
await api.patch(`/api/construction-projects/${props.projectId}`, {
  assigned_users: draft.value.map(d => ({   // ← WRONG: 'assigned_users' not in DTO
    id: d.id,                                // ← WRONG: should be 'user_id'
    ...
    permissions: d.permissions,
  })),
})
```

Backend `UpdateConstructionProjectDto` extends `CreateConstructionProjectDto` which has:
```typescript
@IsOptional()
@IsArray()
@ValidateNested({ each: true })
@Type(() => AssignmentMetadataDto)
assignments?: AssignmentMetadataDto[];  // ← correct key
```

NestJS `ValidationPipe` is configured with `whitelist: true`. Fields not in the DTO are stripped silently. `assigned_users` → stripped. Backend receives `dto.assignments = undefined` → `updateRecordAssignmentsWithMetadata()` never called → nothing written to `record_assignments`.

The `record_assignments` table remains empty for the project. When the contractor fetches the project, `assigned_users` from the SQL subquery returns `[]`. `myAssignment = null` → `hasExplicitPerms = false` → `return true` fallback → ALL tabs visible.

### Two-Path Asymmetry (Root Cause Localized)

| Path | Payload | Assignments saved? |
|------|---------|-------------------|
| "Save Personnel" button → `saveAssignments()` | `{ assigned_users: [...] }` | NO — wrong key, stripped |
| "Save Changes" button → `handleSubmit → getDraftAssignments()` | `{ assignments: [{ user_id, ... }] }` | YES — correct key |

Admin testing likely used "Save Personnel" button → appeared to work (toast fires on HTTP 200) → assignment never persisted.

### Secondary Issue — AssignmentMetadataDto.permissions Schema

`AssignmentMetadataDto.permissions` (backend DTO, line 42–50) declares old fields only:
```typescript
permissions?: { canEdit?, canUpload?, canApprove?, canAccessFinancials?, canAccessOthers?, canAccessPersonnel?, tabs? };
```

New fields (`tabProjectProfile`, `tabDatesDuration`, `tabTimeline`, `tabProgressReport`, `tabPersonnel`, `tabAttachments`, `tabOthers`, `accessLevel`, `canCreate`, `canDelete`, `canReview`) are NOT declared. Since `permissions` lacks `@ValidateNested()` + `@Type()`, NestJS does NOT perform field-level stripping on the nested object — new fields DO pass through. However, the DTO type definition is stale and misleading.

### Secondary Issue — UIProject.assignedUsers.permissions Type (adapters.ts)

`UIProject.assignedUsers.permissions` interface (adapters.ts line 106–114) declares old permission fields only. At runtime this doesn't strip anything (TypeScript interfaces have no runtime effect), but TypeScript warns on access to new fields and `useCoiAccess` must use `(u as any).permissions` workarounds.

### Secondary Issue — useCoiAccess.ts: No Contractor Fail-Safe

If permissions JSONB is somehow null (e.g., old record before UA fix), `hasExplicitPerms = false` → `return true` → all tabs open for contractor. There is no role-based fail-safe in the fallback.

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| UA | Fix saveAssignments(): `assigned_users` → `assignments`, `id` → `user_id` | CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue |
| UB | Update AssignmentMetadataDto.permissions to `Record<string, any>` | assignment-metadata.dto.ts |
| UC | Add isContractor fail-safe fallback to useCoiAccess tab filters | composables/useCoiAccess.ts |
| UD | Update UIProject.assignedUsers.permissions type to `Record<string, any>` | utils/adapters.ts |
| UE | Verification | — |

---

*Phase UA–UE research complete (§2.238). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*


---

## §2.239 — Root Cause: CiPersonnelAccessCard Draft Defaults Grant-by-Default to Contractors (VA–VH)

**Directive:** External personnel RBAC still not synchronized after UA–UD fixes. Contractor still sees unauthorized tabs.

### Root Cause — Draft Initialization Uses Grant-by-Default Fallback for All Users

`CiPersonnelAccessCard` `watch(() => props.assignedUsers, ...)` (lines 114–138):

```typescript
const p = u.permissions || {}  // p = {} when permissions is null
permissions: {
  tabProjectProfile: p.tabProjectProfile ?? true,   // null → true (GRANT)
  tabDatesDuration:  p.tabDatesDuration  ?? true,   // null → true (GRANT)
  tabTimeline:       p.tabTimeline       ?? true,   // null → true (GRANT)
  tabProgressReport: p.tabProgressReport ?? true,   // null → true (GRANT)
  tabPersonnel:      p.tabPersonnel      ?? false,  // null → false (deny)
  tabAttachments:    p.tabAttachments    ?? true,   // null → true (GRANT)
  tabOthers:         p.tabOthers         ?? false,  // null → false (deny)
  ...
}
```

When a contractor is assigned to a project with `record_assignments.permissions = NULL` (old row, or row inserted via legacy `assigned_user_ids` path), `p = {}`. Every `?? true` fallback fires. Admin sees all tab checkboxes as "checked" in the Personnel Access Card UI.

If admin clicks "Save Personnel" without inspecting the checkboxes, `saveAssignments()` sends:
```typescript
permissions: { tabProjectProfile: true, tabTimeline: true, tabProgressReport: true, ... }
```

Backend saves this all-true JSONB to `record_assignments.permissions`.

When contractor views the project:
- `myAssignment.value?.permissions = { tabProjectProfile: true, ... }` (truthy)
- `hasExplicitPerms = true`
- `effectivePermissions.tabProjectProfile = true`, etc.
- UC fail-safe never fires (it only activates when `hasExplicitPerms = false`)
- ALL tabs visible — contractor sees everything

**The UC isContractor fail-safe is bypassed** because the all-true permissions JSONB makes `hasExplicitPerms = true`.

### Failure Chain

```
record_assignments.permissions = NULL (old assignment)
    ↓
CiPersonnelAccessCard watch: p = {} → tabProjectProfile ?? true = true
    ↓
Admin saves without review → permissions = {all: true} saved
    ↓
Contractor fetches project → myAssignment.permissions = {all: true}
    ↓
hasExplicitPerms = true → UC fail-safe bypassed
    ↓
effectivePermissions.tabProjectProfile = true → tab visible
    ↓
ALL unauthorized tabs rendered
```

### Secondary Issue — BackendProject.assigned_users TypeScript Type Missing Fields

`BackendProject.assigned_users` interface (adapters.ts line 57):
```typescript
assigned_users?: { id: string; name: string; role?: string | null; department?: string | null; phone?: string | null }[]
```

Missing: `permissions`, `email`, `personnelCategory` / `personnel_category`, `projectRole` / `project_role`, `user_role`. Runtime data passes through (TypeScript does not strip JSON), but:
- TypeScript may warn on `backend.assigned_users[].permissions` access
- Stale type misleads future developers
- `adaptProject` cannot be statically typed for permission access

### Secondary Issue — RecordAssignment Entity Type Stale

`RecordAssignment.permissions` entity (record-assignment.entity.ts lines 42–51) still typed as the old 6-field schema. MikroORM stores JSONB as-is regardless, but the TypeScript mismatch causes confusion.

### Architectural Issue — Runtime-Stateful Permission Model

Contractor permissions require a four-step runtime chain to resolve:
1. Admin saves assignment via PATCH (correct key required)
2. Backend writes to `record_assignments.permissions` JSONB
3. Frontend fetches project → assignedUsers populated
4. `useCoiAccess` matches user ID → reads permissions

If ANY step yields unexpected data (null permissions, wrong defaults, slow fetch), the filter silently fails open or closed. Institutional users never have this problem — their permissions are login-time stateless (ROLE_PERMISSIONS matrix, auth store).

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| VA | Fix CiPersonnelAccessCard draft initialization: deny-by-default for external users with null permissions | CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue |
| VB | Fix BackendProject.assigned\_users TypeScript type: add all fields | utils/adapters.ts |
| VC | Fix RecordAssignment entity permissions type: Record<string, any> | record-assignment.entity.ts |
| VD | Backend: return deny-by-default permissions for contractor null permissions in findOne response | construction-projects.service.ts |
| VE | Backend: add GET /api/me/project-permissions endpoint | construction-projects.controller.ts + service |
| VF | Frontend: add useProjectPermissionsStore Pinia store | stores/projectPermissions.ts |
| VG | Frontend: update useCoiAccess to read from store; fetch at auth init | composables/useCoiAccess.ts, stores/auth.ts |
| VH | Verification | — |

---

*Phase VA–VH research complete (§2.239). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*


---

## §2.240 — Root Cause: Fail-Open Tab Default + Hydration Race + Duplicated Resolver (WA–WG)

**Directive:** External personnel RBAC still leaks tabs on the Edit Project Page. Content correctly blocked, but unauthorized **tab chips still render**. Required: filter-before-render, ONE centralized resolver, edit/detail/new synchronization.

### Confirmed: Content guard works, tab rendering does not

After VA–VH, the permission *content* guards work (unauthorized content does not load). But the `<v-tab>` chips still render on `edit-[id].vue`. Investigation of `visibleTabOrder` (edit-[id].vue lines 288–300) and `isTabVisible` (detail-[id].vue) reveals the decision logic:

```typescript
if (admin) return true
const permKey = EDIT_TAB_PERM_MAP[tab]
if (!permKey) return true
if (hasExplicitPerms) return !!(ep as any)[permKey]
if (isContractorUser.value) return false
return true   // ← FAIL OPEN
```

### Root Cause A — Fail-Open Default During Auth Hydration Window

`hasExplicitPerms = isOwnerOrAssigned.value && !!myAssignment.value?.permissions`.
Both `isOwnerOrAssigned` and `isContractor` depend on `authStore.user`. On a hard reload the sequence is:

1. Page mounts → `loading = true` (skeleton shown)
2. `fetchData()` fetches the project (fast) → `loading = false` → **tabs render**
3. Auth `initialize()` → `fetchCurrentUser()` → `/api/auth/me` + permission-store fetch (slower; races step 2)

If step 2 finishes before step 3, at render time `authStore.user === null`:
- `isContractor` → false (no role string)
- `isOwnerOrAssigned` → false (no userId)
- `hasExplicitPerms` → false
- `isContractorUser` → false
- **fallback `return true` fires → ALL 7 tabs render**

This is the "route hydration before filtering" / "permission flickering" the directive describes. The tab list is computed and rendered *before* RBAC inputs are known, and the default is to **show**.

### Root Cause B — No accessResolved Gate

Tabs render the instant `loading === false` (project fetched). There is no flag that asserts "RBAC inputs are ready." `loading` tracks the *project fetch*, not the *permission resolution*. The template gates on `v-if="loading"`/`v-else` only — never on whether auth/permissions are known.

### Root Cause C — Duplicated Resolver (No Central Engine)

The same authorization decision is implemented twice:
- **edit-[id].vue**: `EDIT_TAB_PERM_MAP` + `visibleTabOrder` computed
- **detail-[id].vue**: `DETAIL_TAB_PERM_MAP` + `isTabVisible` computed

Two copies of the filter logic → drift risk, inconsistent behavior, and exactly the "fragmented authorization" the directive prohibits. The canonical tab source `COI_PROJECT_TABS` (coiFormState.ts) exists but only the edit page's `tabOrder` aligns to its `tabValue`s; the detail page maintains its own map.

### Root Cause D — Contractor Detection Only in Fallback Branch

`isContractor` deny is the *last* branch, reached only when `hasExplicitPerms` is false. It is a patch over the fail-open default, not a first-class gate. The correct model evaluates membership + permissions BEFORE deciding, and renders nothing until resolved.

### Backend Posture (verified, no gap)

`findOne` already throws `ForbiddenException` for contractors not assigned to a project (service lines ~393). `assertProjectPermission()` enforces canCreate/canEdit/canDelete/canUpload server-side before every sub-resource CUD. Backend authorization is sound — the defect is purely frontend render timing + duplication.

### Canonical Tab Structure (single source: COI_PROJECT_TABS)

| permKey | edit tabValue | detail tabValue | label |
|---------|---------------|-----------------|-------|
| tabProjectProfile | basic | overview | Project Profile |
| tabDatesDuration | schedule | (folded into overview) | Dates & Duration |
| tabTimeline | timeline | timeline | Timeline |
| tabProgressReport | progress | progress | Progress Report |
| tabPersonnel | personnel | team | Personnel |
| tabAttachments | documents | gallery, documents | Attachments |
| tabOthers | others | others | Others |
| — | — | analytics (always visible) | Analytics |

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| WA | Centralized resolver in useCoiAccess: `canViewTab(permKey)`, `accessResolved`. Fail-CLOSED until resolved. | composables/useCoiAccess.ts |
| WB | edit-[id].vue: consume central resolver; gate <v-tabs>/<v-window> on accessResolved; derive map from COI_PROJECT_TABS | pages/coi/edit-[id].vue |
| WC | detail-[id].vue: consume central resolver; gate tabs on accessResolved | pages/coi/detail-[id].vue |
| WD | new.vue: confirm creation-only (contractors blocked); align tabs to COI_PROJECT_TABS | pages/coi/new.vue |
| WE | Backend enforcement audit (findOne 403 + assertProjectPermission) — verify, document | construction-projects.service.ts |
| WF | Action-button gating audit (Create/Edit/Delete/Upload/Review/Approve via effectivePermissions) | edit-[id].vue, detail-[id].vue |
| WG | Verification | — |

---

*Phase WA–WG research complete (§2.240). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*

---

## §2.241 — Root Cause: v-window-item Renders Without Guard + SC-A Watcher Empty-Tab Blind Spot (XA–XD)

**Branch:** `pmo-coi` | **Date:** 2026-05-26

### Why Detail Page Works / Why Edit Page Does Not

The detail page (`detail-[id].vue`) has an `analytics` tab that is unconditionally visible (`isTabVisible('analytics') = true`). Consequence: `visibleTabs` (filtered tab list) is **never empty** for any user. The SC-B watcher's `visibleTabs.length > 0 &&` guard is always satisfied, so `activeTab` is always redirected to the first visible tab (at minimum, `analytics`). Contractors with deny-all permissions see only the analytics panel — correct behavior by accident.

The edit page (`edit-[id].vue`) has **no unconditionally visible tab**. All 7 tabs pass through `canViewTab(permKey)`. For a contractor whose permissions deny all tabs, `visibleTabOrder` = `[]`.

---

### Root Cause A — SC-A Watcher Does Not Handle Empty visibleTabOrder

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 295–299

```typescript
watch(visibleTabOrder, (tabs) => {
  if (tabs.length > 0 && !tabs.includes(activeTab.value)) {
    activeTab.value = tabs[0]
  }
}, { immediate: false })
```

**Failure chain:**
1. Contractor opens edit page. `initialTab = 'basic'`. `activeTab = 'basic'`.
2. `fetchData()` completes → `rawProject` populated → `accessResolved = true`.
3. `visibleTabOrder` recomputes for contractor with deny-all permissions → `[]`.
4. SC-A watcher fires with `tabs = []`.
5. Condition: `tabs.length > 0 && ...` → `0 > 0` = **false** → watcher body does NOT run.
6. `activeTab` remains `'basic'`.
7. `<v-window v-model="activeTab">` renders `<v-window-item value="basic">` because `activeTab === 'basic'`.
8. **Result:** Full Basic Info form content is visible. No tab chip is shown (chip has `v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('basic')"` = false). The content panel renders without a navigation handle — the unauthorized form content is fully visible.

**Fix:** Remove `tabs.length > 0 &&` condition. Use `tabs[0] ?? ''` fallback so `activeTab` becomes empty string when no tabs exist, preventing any window-item from matching.

---

### Root Cause B — `<v-window-item>` Elements Lack v-if Guards

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` lines 1982–end of window section

`<v-tab v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes('basic')">` gates the tab chip. But `<v-window-item value="basic">` has no parallel `v-if`. Vuetify renders the active window-item based solely on `v-model="activeTab"`. When `activeTab = 'basic'` (from Root Cause A), the full form content mounts and renders regardless of whether the chip is visible.

**Fix (defense-in-depth):** Add `v-if="visibleTabOrder.includes(tab)"` to each `<v-window-item>` mirroring the `<v-tab>` guard. This ensures that even if the activeTab watcher lags or is overridden, no unauthorized content can mount. Additionally gate `<v-window>` with `v-if="visibleTabOrder.length > 0"` to prevent the component from mounting at all when no tabs are visible.

---

### Root Cause C — Middleware Contractor Branch Early-Returns Before Edit Route Guard

**File:** `pmo-frontend/middleware/permission.ts` lines 14–84

```typescript
if (isContractor.value) {
  // ...prefix allowlist...
  return // ← line 27: exits before edit route guard
}

// lines 68-84: NEVER reached for contractors
for (const { pattern, module } of editRoutePatterns) {
  if (pattern.test(to.path)) {       // /^\/coi\/edit-/
    if (!canEdit(module)) { return navigateTo('/dashboard') }
  }
}
```

The contractor branch's `return` exits middleware before the `canEdit('coi')` guard at lines 68–84 is evaluated. Contractors navigate freely to `/coi/edit-[id]` even though they have no edit permission.

**Fix:** In the contractor branch, before the allowlist check, explicitly test for edit route patterns and redirect to the corresponding detail page instead of allowing edit page access.

---

### Root Cause D — No Page-Level Guard in edit-[id].vue

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` setup block

`edit-[id].vue` has no setup-time redirect for contractor users. Even with middleware fixes, direct URL access or stale middleware state leaves the page accessible. Defense-in-depth requires a `watchEffect` at setup that redirects contractors to `detail-[id]`.

---

### Why the fix is minimal and safe

The two code changes (RC-A watcher + RC-B window-item guards) are **contained to `edit-[id].vue`** and do not touch `useCoiAccess.ts`, `permission.ts`, or any backend code. They correct the Vue rendering state machine's handling of the zero-tabs case. Institutional users (admin, staff with full access) always have `visibleTabOrder.length > 0`, so the empty-string fallback never fires for them. The fix is invisible in the normal (authorized) flow.

The middleware fix (RC-C) is a separate, clean architectural improvement.

---

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| XA | Fix SC-A watcher: remove `tabs.length > 0` guard; use `tabs[0] ?? ''` fallback | `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` |
| XB | Add `v-if` guards on `<v-window>` and all 7 `<v-window-item>` elements | `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` |
| XC | Middleware: redirect contractors from `/coi/edit-*` to `/coi/detail-*` | `middleware/permission.ts` |
| XD | Edit page defense-in-depth: `watchEffect` redirect for contractors | `pages/coi/edit-[id].vue` |

---

*Phase XA–XD research complete (§2.241). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*

---

## §2.242 — CiPersonnelAccessCard: External Personnel Refactor to Unified Panel Architecture (YA–YD)

**Branch:** `pmo-coi` | **Date:** 2026-05-26

### Current Architecture — Two Independent Personnel Systems

`CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` currently has **three separate data flows** for external personnel, causing RBAC fragmentation:

| Data Source | API | State | Save Mechanism | Feeds useCoiAccess? |
|-------------|-----|-------|----------------|---------------------|
| `draft[]` (external slice) | `eligible-contractors` → `commitAddContractor` | `contractorUsers`, `addContractorId` | `getDraftAssignments()` → `record_assignments` | ✅ YES |
| `contractors[]` | `contractor-assignments` (invite-based) | `contractors`, `inviteLoading` | `removeContractor()` → separate API | ❌ NO |
| `invites[]` | `contractor-invites` | `invites`, `inviteGenerating` | `revokeInvite()` / `deleteInvite()` | ❌ NO |

Only the first flow (`draft[] → record_assignments`) is visible to `useCoiAccess`. The invite-based `contractors[]` and `invites[]` bypasses the RBAC system entirely. This is the source of the "contractor appears assigned but Edit Project doesn't render properly" symptom.

---

### Left Panel — CSU/Institutional Users (CORRECT REFERENCE)

```
staffUsers (eligible-for-assignment)
  └─ commitAdd() / commitBatch()
       └─ draft[] (filtered: !isExternalUser)
            └─ institutionalDraft → rendered as user cards
                 └─ permission panel (Section C + A + B)
                      └─ getDraftAssignments() → saveAssignments()
                           └─ record_assignments (JSONB permissions)
                                └─ useCoiAccess → tab/action gates
```

Single data source. Single save path. Single RBAC pipeline. No separate state.

---

### Right Panel — External Personnel (FRAGMENTED — TO REWRITE)

```
contractorUsers (eligible-contractors)        ← SEPARATE list
  └─ commitAddContractor()
       └─ draft[] (filtered: isExternalUser)  ← only CONTRACTOR category
            └─ externalDraft → user cards
                 └─ permission panel (duplicated code, orange theme)

contractor-assignments (invite-accepted)      ← COMPLETELY SEPARATE
  └─ contractors[] → "ASSIGNED CONTRACTORS" section
       └─ removeContractor() → separate DELETE

contractor-invites                            ← COMPLETELY SEPARATE
  └─ invites[] → "ACTIVE INVITE LINKS" section
       └─ generateInvite / revokeInvite / deleteInvite
```

Three independent data paths. None of the invite path feeds RBAC. The `isExternalUser()` classifier only checks `'CONTRACTOR'` — excludes Constructor, Supplier, Consultant, External Auditor, External Partner.

---

### Invite Infrastructure — Complete Inventory for Removal

**Reactive state to remove:**
- `invites`, `contractors`, `inviteLoading`, `inviteGenerating`, `inviteError`, `copiedToken`
- `showInviteHistory`, `pendingInvites`, `historicInvites`
- `revokeConfirmDialog`, `revokeTargetId`, `deleteConfirmDialog`, `deleteTargetId`

**Functions to remove:**
- `loadContractorData()`, `generateInvite()`, `revokeInvite()`, `deleteInvite()`
- `removeContractor()`, `copyInviteLink()`, `inviteStatusColor()`
- `promptRevoke()`, `confirmRevoke()`, `promptDelete()`, `confirmDelete()`

**Separate contractor search state to remove (replaced by unified search):**
- `contractorUsers`, `contractorLoading`, `addContractorId`, `addContractorRole`
- `loadContractors()`, `commitAddContractor()`

**Watcher to remove:**
- `watch(() => props.projectId, (id) => { if (id && isAdmin.value) loadContractorData() })`

**Template blocks to remove:**
- "Generate Invite" button (lines 620–630)
- "QUICK ASSIGN EXISTING CONTRACTOR" panel (QF section, lines 634–680)
- "ASSIGNED CONTRACTORS" section (lines 769–792)
- `v-else-if="!inviteLoading"` empty state (line 793)
- "ACTIVE INVITE LINKS" section (lines 796–828)
- Invite history (lines 831–865)
- Non-admin invite note (lines 866–868)
- Revoke Invite dialog (lines 940–958)
- Delete Invite dialog (lines 960–979)
- `inviteError` alert (lines 764–766)

---

### New Architecture — Unified External Panel

```
staffUsers (eligible-for-assignment)          ← SAME list as left panel
  └─ commitAddExternal()
       └─ draft[] (filtered: isExternalUser)  ← all 6 external categories
            └─ externalDraft → user cards     ← SAME card structure as left
                 └─ permission panel          ← SAME 3-section structure
                      └─ getDraftAssignments() → saveAssignments()
                           └─ record_assignments (JSONB permissions)
                                └─ useCoiAccess → tab/action gates
```

One data source. One save path. One RBAC pipeline. Zero invite infrastructure.

---

### External Category Set (6 types)

```typescript
const EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES = new Set([
  'CONTRACTOR', 'CONSTRUCTOR', 'SUPPLIER',
  'CONSULTANT', 'EXTERNAL_AUDITOR', 'EXTERNAL_PARTNER',
])

const EXTERNAL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS = [
  { title: 'Contractor',                    value: 'CONTRACTOR' },
  { title: 'Constructor',                   value: 'CONSTRUCTOR' },
  { title: 'Supplier',                      value: 'SUPPLIER' },
  { title: 'Consultant',                    value: 'CONSULTANT' },
  { title: 'External Auditor',              value: 'EXTERNAL_AUDITOR' },
  { title: 'External Implementing Partner', value: 'EXTERNAL_PARTNER' },
]
```

`isExternalUser()` becomes: `return EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES.has(u.personnelCategory ?? '')`

---

### DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS (deny-by-default, per VA-A)

External personnel must start with all tabs false (deny-by-default). A new `DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS()` function is used when adding external users. Admin explicitly grants tabs.

```typescript
const DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS = (): FullPermissions => ({
  canCreate: false, canEdit: false, canDelete: false,
  canUpload: false, canReview: false, canApprove: false,
  tabProjectProfile: false, tabDatesDuration: false, tabTimeline: false,
  tabProgressReport: false, tabPersonnel: false,
  tabAttachments: false, tabOthers: false,
  accessLevel: 'Viewer',
})
```

---

### Batch Dialog — Mode Parameterization

The existing batch dialog is extended with a `batchMode: 'institutional' | 'external'` ref. `openBatchDialog(mode)` sets the mode and pre-loads the appropriate default category. `commitBatch()` uses `DEFAULT_PERMS()` for institutional and `DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS()` for external. Category dropdown shows `PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS` or `EXTERNAL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS` based on mode.

---

### Draft Watch — VA-A expanded classification

The `isExternal` check in the `watch(() => props.assignedUsers)` handler must use the full set:
```typescript
const isExternal = EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES.has((u as any).personnel_category ?? '')
  || EXTERNAL_CATEGORIES.has((u as any).personnelCategory ?? '')
  || (u as any).user_role === 'Contractor'
```

---

### Backend Dependency

The existing `/api/users/eligible-for-assignment` endpoint is reused for both panels. No new backend endpoint is needed. The endpoint must return users of all roles (institutional and external). If the current implementation filters to Staff-only, that is a backend concern outside this refactor scope — the frontend architecture is correct as long as the endpoint contract is met.

The `eligible-contractors` endpoint (`/api/users/eligible-contractors`) becomes unused by the frontend after this refactor.

---

### Visual Parity Requirements

| Element | Institutional (Left) | External (Right) |
|---------|---------------------|-----------------|
| Panel color | `primary` (blue) | `warning` (orange/amber) |
| Avatar color | `categoryColor(personnelCategory)` | `categoryColor(personnelCategory)` or orange for external |
| Add panel | search + PERSONNEL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS + project role | search + EXTERNAL_CATEGORY_OPTIONS + project role |
| "Add Multiple" button | ✅ opens batch dialog (institutional mode) | ✅ opens batch dialog (external mode) |
| User card structure | identical | identical (orange accent) |
| Category chip | institutional label | external label |
| Permission panel | Section C + A + B | Section C + A + B (identical) |
| Read-only badge | accessLevel chip | accessLevel chip |
| Empty state | "No institutional personnel assigned yet." | "No external personnel assigned yet." |

---

### Scope Summary

| Phase | Description | Files |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| YA | Script: remove invite infrastructure; add external category set + DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS; unify search/add; update classification | `CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` |
| YB | Template: rewrite right panel — mirror left panel structure; remove all invite UI | `CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` |
| YC | Batch dialog: parameterize for institutional vs external mode | `CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` |
| YD | Verification | — |

---

*Phase YA–YD research complete (§2.242). Awaiting Phase 2 plan.*

---

## §2.243 — Auth System Audit: Login Blocker, Assignment Search, OAuth Auto-Create (ZA–ZF)

**Status:** Phase 1 Research Complete
**Trigger:** RUN_ACE — Primary complaint: newly registered users cannot login even with correct credentials and after password reset. Secondary requirements: Google OAuth auto-registration, unified external personnel search, registration page Google button.
**Audit scope:** `auth.service.ts`, `auth.controller.ts`, `google.strategy.ts`, `jwt.strategy.ts`, `auth.module.ts`, `users.service.ts`, `user.entity.ts`, `stores/auth.ts`, `pages/login.vue`, `pages/register.vue`, `pages/auth/callback.vue`, `composables/useApi.ts`, `contractor-auth/*`, `app.module.ts`
**Continuity:** Extends §2.242. Auth + RBAC pipeline confirmed correct at the cryptographic and JWT layers. Bugs are in error signaling, endpoint filtering, and OAuth strategy logic.

---

### 2.243-A: Primary Login Blocker — Misleading Error for Inactive Accounts

**Root cause confirmed:**

`auth.service.ts:register()` (lines 125–127) creates every self-registered user with:
```typescript
status: 'PENDING',
isActive: false,
```

`auth.service.ts:validateUser()` (lines 52–57) short-circuits on `!user.isActive`:
```typescript
if (!user.isActive) {
  this.logger.warn(`LOGIN_FAILURE: user_id=${user.id}, reason=ACCOUNT_INACTIVE`);
  return null;  // ← indistinguishable from wrong password
}
```

`auth.service.ts:login()` (line 153–155) converts ANY null return to:
```typescript
throw new UnauthorizedException('INVALID_CREDENTIALS');
```

Frontend `login.vue` shows "Invalid credentials" for **all** UnauthorizedException cases.

**Effect:** A user who self-registered and has the correct password sees "Invalid credentials" — indistinguishable from a wrong password. They reset their password (which does nothing since `isActive` is still false), still can't login. The system is behaving correctly by design (PENDING accounts require admin activation) but the error is completely misleading.

**bcrypt is NOT broken.** `bcrypt.hash(10 rounds)` at registration vs `bcrypt.hash(12 rounds)` in UsersService for admin-created users — both compare correctly via `bcrypt.compare()`. Not a bug.

**Fix direction:** In `login()`, run a pre-query to detect `isActive: false` and throw `UnauthorizedException('ACCOUNT_INACTIVE')` BEFORE calling `validateUser`. Frontend maps error code to a specific message.

---

### 2.243-B: Critical YA-YD Regression — `eligible-for-assignment` Excludes External Roles

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts:198–215`

`findEligibleForAssignment()` SQL (lines 200–210):
```sql
SELECT u.id, u.first_name, u.last_name, u.campus
FROM users u
WHERE u.deleted_at IS NULL
  AND u.is_active = true
  AND EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM user_roles ur
    JOIN roles r ON ur.role_id = r.id
    WHERE ur.user_id = u.id
      AND r.name IN ('Staff', 'Admin', 'SuperAdmin')  ← excludes ALL external roles
  )
ORDER BY u.last_name, u.first_name
```

**YA-YD refactor impact:** `CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` external panel uses `staffUsers` (from this endpoint) for BOTH institutional and external panel searches. External users assigned the Contractor/Consultant/Supplier/etc. role are excluded from search results → external panel can never find and add these users.

**Two additional gaps:**
1. `email` field is missing from the query return — the autocomplete subtitle shows email but the field isn't returned
2. `findEligibleContractors()` (line 218–233) is now stale — it was the old contractor-specific search; YA-YD removed the UI that called it, but the method remains

**Fix:** Remove role filter entirely. Return all active non-deleted users. Add `email` field. The caller (`CiPersonnelAccessCard`) already knows the context (institutional vs external panel); the endpoint doesn't need to filter by role.

---

### 2.243-C: Google OAuth Strategy Blocks New Account Auto-Creation

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts:63–69`

```typescript
if (result.length === 0) {
  return done(
    new UnauthorizedException('No account found. Contact your administrator.'),
    false,
  );
}
```

Comment at line 53: `// Look up user by google_id OR email (Directive 202 — no self-registration)`

**Current behavior:** Google OAuth ONLY works for accounts pre-created by an admin. Any first-time Google sign-in from an unregistered email fails with 401.

**Required behavior (per prompt):** Auto-create a new user when Google identity is verified but no account exists. Set `isActive: true`, `status: 'ACTIVE'` (OAuth-verified → no admin approval needed). Preserve email uniqueness (link if email already exists → Directive 203 handles this).

**Directive 203 (line 84–88) is CORRECT and must be preserved:**
```typescript
if (!user.google_id) {
  // Link google_id if email already exists
  await this.em.getConnection().execute(...)
}
```

**New flow when user not found:**
```
Google verified email not in DB → create User (firstName, lastName, email, googleId, isActive=true)
                                  → return done(null, newUser)
```

**Role assignment:** Do NOT auto-assign a role for Google-created users. Admin assigns roles via User Management after first login. Until role is assigned, user can access the system but sees minimal sidebar.

---

### 2.243-D: Registration Page Missing Google OAuth Button

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/register.vue`

- `login.vue` has `handleGoogleLogin()` → `window.location.href = '/api/auth/google'` ✅
- `register.vue` has NO Google OAuth button ❌

The registration page must add a "Continue with Google" button. Position: above the stepper form (prominent, before account/password setup). The same `window.location.href = '/api/auth/google'` function handles both login and registration flows — Google handles the distinction.

---

### 2.243-E: ContractorAuthModule — Fragmented Auth System Still Active

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/app.module.ts:102` — `ContractorAuthModule` still imported and active.

The `contractor-auth/` module contains:
- `ContractorAuthController` — exposes `/api/contractor-auth/*` endpoints
- `ContractorAuthService` — contractor-specific login, invite acceptance, etc.
- Legacy entities: `ContractorUser`, `ContractorInviteToken`, `ProjectContractorAssignment`

This is the OLD pipeline. The YA-YD refactor removed all contractor-specific auth from the **frontend**, but the backend still exposes these endpoints. The `project_contractor_assignments` table still exists (confirmed by `Migration20260525010000_UnifyContractorUsers` which migrated it to use `user_id` instead of `contractor_user_id`).

**Scope decision for ZA-ZF phases:** The `ContractorAuthModule` backend cleanup is OUT OF SCOPE for this phase — it's a multi-migration removal with potential data impact. The YA-YD refactor already unified the frontend. The backend legacy endpoints are inactive from the frontend's perspective. **Flag for future phase.**

---

### 2.243-F: No Default Role After Admin Activation

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts:1063–1073`

`activateUser()` updates `status='ACTIVE'` and `is_active=true` only. No role assignment.

An activated self-registered user has `roles: []` in their JWT. The frontend `usePermissions` composable treats empty roles as viewer-only (no sidebar modules, no CRUD). The user can technically log in but sees a nearly empty dashboard.

**Fix option A:** Auto-assign 'Staff' role during activation (safest default for institutional users).
**Fix option B:** Admin must manually assign role in User Management (current behavior — may be intentional).

**Decision:** Implement option A as the default. Staff is the lowest-privilege named role. Admin can change it afterward. This prevents the "I can login but nothing works" confusion.

---

### 2.243-G: Auth Architecture Status (All Other Systems: CORRECT)

| Component | Status | Notes |
|-----------|--------|-------|
| bcrypt hashing | ✅ Correct | 10 rounds (register) vs 12 (admin create) — both compare correctly |
| JWT signing/verification | ✅ Correct | `AUTH_JWT_SECRET` used in both `JwtModule` and `JwtStrategy` |
| JWT expiry | ✅ 8h | Configurable via `AUTH_JWT_EXPIRES_IN` |
| Account lockout | ✅ Working | 5 failures → 15-min lock; `unlockAccount()` endpoint in UsersService |
| Rate limiting | ✅ Working | ThrottlerGuard: 5 login attempts/min, 3 register attempts/min |
| Google callback page | ✅ Correct | `pages/auth/callback.vue` reads `?token=`, calls `loginWithToken()` |
| RBAC aggregation | ✅ Correct | `UserRole → Role → Permission` via ORM in `login()` and `getProfile()` |
| `useApi` composable | ✅ Correct | Sends `Authorization: Bearer <token>` from localStorage |
| Self-registration form | ✅ Correct | Lowercases email; validates password strength client-side |
| `activateUser` SQL | ✅ Correct | `rowCount` check works; passwordHash preserved through activation |

---

| Phase | Scope | Files |
|-------|-------|-------|
| ZA | Backend: distinguish ACCOUNT_INACTIVE from INVALID_CREDENTIALS in `login()` | `auth.service.ts` |
| ZB | Backend: expand `findEligibleForAssignment()` to include all active users + email | `users.service.ts` |
| ZC | Backend: Google Strategy auto-create user on first OAuth login | `google.strategy.ts` |

## Section 2.244 — Remove PENDING Registration Flow, Google OAuth Role Assignment, register.vue UI Compression (ZG–ZI)

**Date:** 2026-05-26
**Scope:** Auth service `register()` bypass of PENDING state; Google OAuth Staff role assignment; register.vue vertical space compression and letter-spacing fix

---

### 2.244-A: Current `register()` Flow (Broken)

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/auth.service.ts`

Current flow creates user with `isActive: false` and `status: 'PENDING'` — user cannot log in immediately after registration.

```typescript
// CURRENT (lines ~218–252):
const user = this.em.create(User, {
  ...
  isActive: false,           // ← blocks login
  status: 'PENDING',         // ← blocks login (ZA pre-check)
  metadata: { registrationSource: 'self-registration', registeredAt: new Date().toISOString() },
});
await this.em.persistAndFlush(user);
// No user_roles entry created → roles: [] in JWT
return { message: 'Registration submitted. An administrator will review and activate your account.' };
```

**ZG target changes:**
1. Change `isActive: false` → `isActive: true`
2. Change `status: 'PENDING'` → `status: 'ACTIVE'`
3. After `persistAndFlush(user)`, query Staff role and insert `user_roles` record via ORM
4. Update return message to `'Your account has been created. You can now sign in with your credentials.'`

**UserRole entity fields (from ZE research):** `id (uuid), userId, roleId, isSuperadmin, assignedBy, createdAt, updatedAt`
- Use ORM `em.create(UserRole, {...})` + `em.persistAndFlush()` for consistency with CRUD pattern
- `assignedBy` = `null` (self-registration — no admin actor)
- `isSuperadmin` = `false`

---

### 2.244-B: Google OAuth Missing Staff Role (Residual Gap from ZC)

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts`

ZC (Phase ZA–ZF) auto-creates the user with `isActive: true` but does NOT create a `user_roles` entry. Result: new Google OAuth users get JWT with `roles: []`, sidebar is empty, all RBAC checks fail.

**ZH target:** Immediately after `this.em.persistAndFlush(newUser)` in the auto-create block, query the Staff role by name and insert into `user_roles`.

Pattern mirrors ZE (`activateUser`) but uses ORM instead of raw SQL (CRUD context):
```typescript
const staffRole = await this.em.findOne(Role, { name: 'Staff' });
if (staffRole) {
  const userRole = this.em.create(UserRole, {
    userId: newUser.id,
    roleId: staffRole.id,
    isSuperadmin: false,
    assignedBy: null,
  });
  await this.em.persistAndFlush(userRole);
}
```

**Required imports:** `Role` entity, `UserRole` entity — must be added to `google.strategy.ts` imports.

---

### 2.244-C: register.vue Vertical Space Budget Analysis

**File:** `pmo-frontend/pages/register.vue`
**Target viewport:** 1366×768 (available form-panel height ≈ 704px after 2rem top+bottom padding = 64px)

**Step 1 content stack (current measurements):**
| Element | Height |
|---------|--------|
| CSU official seal (`width=70`) | ~80px |
| `mb-3` below seal | 12px |
| Form title + subtitle | ~60px |
| `mb-4` below subtitle | 16px |
| Google button row outer div (`mb-4`) | 16px |
| Google button (`size="large"`) | ~44px |
| `mb-3` below Google button | 12px |
| Stepper bar (`mb-4`) | 16px |
| Form card (Step 1 content) | ~410px |
| `form-footer` (mt-1 after links) | ~30px |

**Total estimated: ~696px** — borderline, but with form card internal padding at `1.25rem` (20px top+bottom = 40px overhead) and `input-group mb: 0.875rem` (14px × 3 fields), Step 1 fits if margins are tightened.

**Step 3 specific issue:** `<v-alert type="info">` saying "Your account will be reviewed by an administrator before access is granted." — this must be **removed entirely** (ZG removes PENDING flow, so the message is factually wrong).

### 2.244-D: register.vue Letter-Spacing and Divider Issues

**Letter-spacing issue:**
- Vuetify `v-btn` default: `letter-spacing: 0.0892857em` (Material Design spec)
- `text-transform: none` does NOT reset letter-spacing
- Fix: add `letter-spacing: normal` to `.google-btn` class in `<style scoped>`

**"or register with email" wrapping issue:**
- The divider row is: `<v-divider /> <span class="...">or register with email</span> <v-divider />`
- In narrow flex containers, the span text wraps
- Fix: add `white-space: nowrap` to the span, or add it as an inline style

---

All changes are purely cosmetic (CSS class changes and `width` attribute on `<v-img>`). No logic changes.

| Element | Current | Target | Savings |
|---------|---------|--------|---------|
| Seal `width` | `70` | `52` | ~18px |
| Seal `mb-X` | `mb-3` | `mb-2` | 4px |
| Form subtitle `mb` (CSS `.form-subtitle`) | `margin-bottom: 1rem` | `0.375rem` | ~10px |
| Google btn outer div | `mb-4` | `mb-2` | 8px |
| Google btn | `mb-3` | `mb-2` | 4px |
| Stepper bar | `mb-4` | `mb-2` | 8px |
| Form card padding (CSS `.form-card`) | `padding: 1.25rem` | `0.875rem` | ~8px |
| Step title mb (CSS `.step-title`) | `margin-bottom: 0.875rem` | `0.5rem` | ~6px |
| Input group mb (CSS `.input-group`) | `margin-bottom: 0.875rem` | `0.5rem` | ~10px × fields |
| Form wrapper padding (CSS `.form-wrapper`) | `padding: 1rem 0` | `0.25rem 0` | ~12px |
| Form footer mt (CSS `.form-footer`) | `margin-top: 1rem` | `0.5rem` | 8px |

**Total estimated savings: ~96–110px** — sufficient to eliminate scroll at 1366×768.

- `successMessage.value` set to: `'Registration submitted. An administrator will review and activate your account.'`
- Step 3 `<v-alert type="info">`: "Your account will be reviewed by an administrator before access is granted."

**Target:**

## Section 2.245 — Auth Stabilization Pass 2: activateUser SQL Bug, Google OAuth Domain + PasswordHash, Partial Unique Index (ZJ–ZL)

**Date:** 2026-05-26
**Scope:** Four root-cause defects still active after ZG–ZI deployment; traced via entity schema, DB snapshot, and strategy code

---

### 2.245-A: Issue A Root Cause — ZE SQL Bug in `activateUser()` Blocks Admin Activation

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/users/users.service.ts:1059–1091`

ZE inserted code that references columns that do NOT exist in the `user_roles` table:

```sql
-- Line 1073 (broken):
SELECT id FROM user_roles WHERE user_id = ? LIMIT 1
-- Error: column "id" does not exist

-- Lines 1083–1085 (broken):
INSERT INTO user_roles (id, user_id, role_id, is_superadmin, assigned_by, created_at, updated_at)
VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), ?, ?, false, ?, NOW(), NOW()) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
-- Error: column "id" does not exist; column "updated_at" does not exist
```

**Actual `user_roles` schema (confirmed from MikroORM snapshot):**

| Column | Type | Notes |
|--------|------|-------|
| `user_id` | uuid | PRIMARY KEY (composite) |
| `role_id` | uuid | PRIMARY KEY (composite) |
| `is_superadmin` | bool | default false |
| `assigned_by` | uuid | nullable |
| `assigned_at` | timestamptz | nullable, default now() |
| `created_at` | timestamptz | NOT NULL, default now() |
| `created_by` | uuid | nullable |

There is NO `id` column and NO `updated_at` column. The primary key is composite `(user_id, role_id)`.

**Impact:** `activateUser()` throws a PostgreSQL error (`column "id" does not exist`) every time admin attempts to activate a PENDING user. The user stays PENDING forever. No admin-level activation is possible through the API.

**Correct replacement SQL:**
```sql
-- Check (line 1073 fix):
SELECT user_id FROM user_roles WHERE user_id = ? LIMIT 1

-- Insert (lines 1083-1085 fix):
INSERT INTO user_roles (user_id, role_id, is_superadmin, assigned_by, created_at)
VALUES (?, ?, false, ?, NOW())
ON CONFLICT (user_id, role_id) DO NOTHING
```

---

### 2.245-B: Issue B Root Cause 1 — Google OAuth Domain Restriction Blocks External Emails

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts:39–54`
**Config:** `pmo-backend/.env` line 25: `GOOGLE_ALLOWED_DOMAINS=carsu.edu.ph`

```typescript
const allowedDomains = this.configService.get<string>('GOOGLE_ALLOWED_DOMAINS', '');
if (allowedDomains) {  // ← truthy because 'carsu.edu.ph' is set
  const domains = allowedDomains.split(',').map((d) => d.trim());
  const emailDomain = email.split('@')[1];
  if (!domains.includes(emailDomain)) {
    return done(new UnauthorizedException('Email domain not permitted for this system'), false);
  }
}
```

Any Google account that is not `@carsu.edu.ph` is rejected before user lookup or creation. This blocks:
- Gmail (`gmail.com`)
- Contractors (any corporate domain)
- Suppliers
- External personnel

**Fix:** Remove the entire `allowedDomains` block. Google's OAuth already validates email ownership. Role-based onboarding controls access after login.

---

### 2.245-C: Issue B Root Cause 2 — Google OAuth Auto-Create Fails with NOT NULL Violation

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/auth/strategies/google.strategy.ts:73–98` (ZC-A block)
**DB constraint:** `password_hash varchar(255) NOT NULL` (confirmed from snapshot, line 42852)

Google OAuth auto-creates users without setting `passwordHash`:
```typescript
const newUser = this.em.create(User, {
  email, username: email, googleId: profile.id,
  firstName, lastName, avatarUrl: ...,
  isActive: true, status: 'ACTIVE',
  metadata: { ... }
  // ← NO passwordHash set
});
await this.em.persistAndFlush(newUser);
// ↑ Fails: "null value in column password_hash violates not-null constraint" (PG error 23502)
```

The `catch (err)` block only handles error code `23505` (unique violation). Error `23502` falls through to `return done(err as Error, false)`, aborting OAuth.

**Fix:** Set `passwordHash: ''` (empty string sentinel) when creating Google OAuth users. This satisfies the NOT NULL constraint. The `validateUser()` sentinel at line 59–65 already checks:
```typescript
if (user.googleId && (!user.passwordHash || user.passwordHash === '')) {
  return null; // SSO-only account — blocks password login
}
```
No migration needed; the existing sentinel handles this case.

---

### 2.245-D: Issue C Root Cause — Full-Table UNIQUE Constraints Block Re-registration of Soft-Deleted Emails

**DB constraints confirmed from snapshot (lines 43314–43351):**

| Constraint | Column | Partial? |
|------------|--------|----------|
| `users_email_key` | `email` | NO — full table |
| `users_username_unique` | `username` | NO — full table |
| `users_google_id_key` | `google_id` | NO — full table |

**Failure sequence for soft-deleted user re-registration:**
1. Admin soft-deletes user → `deleted_at` set, `email` still in UNIQUE index
2. New user registers same email
3. App check: `SELECT id WHERE email = ? AND deleted_at IS NULL` → returns 0 rows → app proceeds
4. ORM `persistAndFlush(user)` → PostgreSQL attempts INSERT
5. UNIQUE constraint `users_email_key` fires: row with that email exists (deleted user) → 23505
6. App `catch` block: `throw new ConflictException('An account with this email already exists')`
7. User sees conflict error even though their registration should succeed

**Fix:** Replace full-table UNIQUE constraints with partial UNIQUE indexes that exclude soft-deleted rows. Requires a new MikroORM migration:
```sql
ALTER TABLE users DROP CONSTRAINT users_email_key;
ALTER TABLE users DROP CONSTRAINT users_username_unique;
ALTER TABLE users DROP CONSTRAINT users_google_id_key;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_email_active_unique   ON users(email)     WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_username_active_unique ON users(username)  WHERE deleted_at IS NULL;
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_google_id_active_unique ON users(google_id) WHERE deleted_at IS NULL AND google_id IS NOT NULL;
```

The ORM `23505` catch block in `register()` and `google.strategy.ts` remains valid — the partial indexes still enforce uniqueness for active users.

---

### 2.245-E: Snapshot vs Entity Discrepancies (Non-blocking, documented)

| Field | Entity declaration | DB column | Impact |
|-------|--------------------|-----------|--------|
| `email` | `nullable: true` | `NOT NULL` | No functional issue; register/OAuth always set it |
| `password_hash` | `columnType: 'text'` | `varchar(255)` | No issue; bcrypt hashes are 60 chars; PostgreSQL transparent coercion |
| `username` | `length: 255` | `varchar(100)` | Edge case: emails >100 chars fail INSERT; out of scope |
| `firstName/lastName` | `nullable: true, columnType: 'text'` | `varchar(100) NOT NULL` | OAuth sets both; no issue |

These mismatches are pre-existing schema debt. Not addressed in current phases (YAGNI).

---

### 2.245-F: ZJ–ZL Phase Execution Plan

| Phase | Scope | Files |
|-------|-------|-------|
| ZJ | Backend: Fix `activateUser()` SQL — correct `user_roles` column names | `users.service.ts` |
| ZK | Backend: Remove Google OAuth domain restriction + fix `passwordHash` sentinel for auto-created users | `google.strategy.ts` |
| ZL | DB migration: Replace full-table UNIQUE constraints with partial UNIQUE indexes on `email`, `username`, `google_id` | new MikroORM migration |

---

## §2.246 — COI Attachment Tab Major Refactor: Audit Logs, Masterlist Documents, Gallery, Per-item Remarks (ZM–ZR)

### 2.246-A: Current Architecture Inventory

#### Edit Page (edit-[id].vue) — "Attachments" tab (tab key: `documents`)

The edit page already implements the KV-E enterprise layout introduced in a previous phase:

| Section | Location | Implementation |
|---------|----------|----------------|
| Key Documents | Top-left column (md=6) | File upload + list; classified by `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` = `['PROJECT_PROFILE','FEASIBILITY_STUDY','HGDG_FORM','FLOOR_PLAN','POW']`; POST `/api/construction-projects/:id/documents` with `documentType` |
| Gallery | Top-right column (md=6) | `v-tabs` with Profile (max 3) / General; POST `/api/construction-projects/:id/gallery`; delete via `api.del(...)` |
| Compliance Checklist | Full-width middle | `<CiDocumentChecklist>` component; per-GROUP remarks (KV-D3); edit dialog opens on pencil icon |
| Other Attachments | Full-width bottom | Remaining docs (`otherDocs`); grouped by `documentType` via `docsByCategory` |

Fullscreen dialogs: `keyDocsFullscreen`, `galleryFullscreen`, `checklistFullscreen`, `otherDocsFullscreen` (boolean refs).

RBAC in edit page: `canEditCurrentProject` gates ALL write operations. Finer-grained flags (`canUploadDocuments`, `canDeleteResources` from `useCoiAccess`) are imported but NOT applied to the attachment tab — only used elsewhere.

#### Detail Page (detail-[id].vue) — "Gallery" tab + "Documents" tab (separate tabs)

- **Gallery tab** (tab key: `gallery`): Simple `v-img` grid over ALL gallery items (no Profile/General split). No upload capability. "Manage in Edit Project Details ↗" link.
- **Documents tab** (tab key: `documents`): 7-col `<CiDocumentChecklist>` + 5-col file attachment list (Key Documents section + grouped-by-type remaining files + Links section). Old 2-column layout — does NOT match edit-page enterprise layout.

Gap: Detail page renders gallery and documents as **separate tabs**; edit page renders them as **sub-sections of one tab**. The user request requires the detail page to also show the 4-section enterprise layout inside its documents tab (with read-only Gallery embedded, not a separate tab).

#### CiDocumentChecklist Component

- Fetches from `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist`
- Groups items by `groupCode` into an accordion
- Per-GROUP remarks: `groupRemarks?: Record<string, string>` prop; editable if `canEditRemarks=true`; emits `remarksUpdate` on blur → parent PATCHes `/api/construction-projects/:id/document-remarks`
- Per-ITEM remarks: **NOT IMPLEMENTED** — `ChecklistItem` interface has `reviewNotes` (stored field) but no free-text `remarks` field visible outside the edit dialog
- Edit dialog: Opens via pencil icon; sets `submission_status`, `review_notes`, `expiry_date`, `linked_document_id`
- User request requires per-item remarks **always visible inline** (not hidden behind edit dialog button)

#### Backend Document/Gallery Endpoints

| Method | Route | Auth | Notes |
|--------|-------|------|-------|
| GET | `/api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist` | Admin/Staff/Viewer/Auditor | Lazy-initializes checklist on first call |
| PATCH | `/api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist/:itemId` | Admin/Staff | `UpdateDocumentChecklistDto`: submission_status, review_notes, expiry_date, linked_document_id |
| PATCH | `/api/construction-projects/:id/document-remarks` | Admin/Staff | Body: `{ groupCode, remarks }` |
| GET | `/api/construction-projects/:id/gallery` | Admin/Staff/Viewer/Auditor | Returns all gallery items |
| POST | `/api/construction-projects/:id/gallery` | Admin/Staff | `FileInterceptor` 10MB; form fields: caption, category, image_taken_date |
| DELETE | `/api/construction-projects/:id/gallery/:galleryId` | Admin/Staff | |
| POST | `/api/construction-projects/:id/documents` | Admin/Staff | `FileInterceptor` or JSON link; fields: documentType, title |
| DELETE | `/api/construction-projects/:id/documents/:docId` | Admin/Staff | |

#### Existing Audit Log System

`activity_logs` table (`ActivityLog` entity, `ActivityLogService`):
- Fields: `id`, `user_id`, `userEmail`, `userName`, `action` (enum), `entityType`, `entityId`, `metadata` (JSONB), `createdAt`
- Actions: `CREATE`, `UPDATE`, `DELETE`, `SUBMIT`, `PUBLISH`, `REJECT`, `WITHDRAW`, `UPLOAD`, `REMOVE_ATTACHMENT`
- Controller: `GET /api/activity-logs/:entityType/:entityId` — guarded by `SuperAdmin|Admin|Auditor|Staff`
- COI service already calls `activityLog.log(...)` for: gallery upload, gallery delete, document delete, checklist update, document remarks update, revision orders, etc.
- Detail page fetches from `/api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/${projectId}?pageSize=25`

---

### 2.246-B: Gap Analysis — What Needs to Be Built

| # | Gap | Location | Severity |
|---|-----|----------|----------|
| B1 | Per-item remarks field on checklist items — inline, not buried in dialog | Backend entity + DTO + frontend component | High |
| B2 | Detail page documents tab uses old 2-column layout; gallery is a separate tab | detail-[id].vue | High |
| B3 | RBAC for attachments: all write ops use `canEditCurrentProject`; `canUploadDocuments`/`canDeleteResources` not applied | edit-[id].vue | Medium |
| B4 | No global search/filter bar across attachment sections | edit-[id].vue + detail-[id].vue | Medium |
| B5 | No batch upload / drag-and-drop | edit-[id].vue | Medium |
| B6 | Preview limit: Key Docs / Gallery / Other Attachments show ALL items; no "5 items + View All" pattern | edit-[id].vue + detail-[id].vue | Low |
| B7 | Dedicated COI audit log display — currently only rendered inside "others" tab at bottom of detail page | detail-[id].vue | Low |
| B8 | Document Masterlist ECO taxonomy — current `document_types` seeded with what codes? Need verification | Backend DB query | Medium |

**Out-of-scope per YAGNI:**
- A separate `coi_audit_logs` DB table — existing `activity_logs` already captures all COI events with sufficient fidelity
- new.vue changes — no project ID at creation time, document upload not applicable

---

### 2.246-C: Document Masterlist ECO Taxonomy

From the reference image (`docs/references/ChatGPT Image May 26, 2026, 09_12_59 PM.png`), the masterlist has 4 groups and the following type codes:

| Group | Group Code | Type Codes | Type Labels |
|-------|-----------|------------|-------------|
| ECO Forms | ECO_FORMS | F-ECO-001 | Data Request Form (DBR) |
| | | F-ECO-002 | Service Request Form (SRF) |
| | | F-ECO-003 | Infrastructure Project Proposal Form (IPP) |
| | | F-ECO-004 | Concrete Floating Period Authorization (CFA) |
| | | F-ECO-005 | Request for Payment (RPT) |
| | | F-ECO-006 | Work Suspension (WS) |
| | | F-ECO-007 | Work Suspension Request (WSR) |
| | | F-ECO-008 | Contract Flow Computation (CFP) |
| Support Documents | SUPPORT_DOCS | SD-ECO-001 | Work Suspension Order |
| | | SD-ECO-002 | Work Resumption Order |
| | | SD-ECO-003 | Notice of Commencement |
| | | SD-ECO-004 | Show Cause Order |
| | | SD-ECO-005 | (additional support docs as seen in sheet tabs) |
| Reports & Monitoring | REPORTS | RM-ECO-001 | Monthly Accomplishment Report |
| | | RM-ECO-002 | Site Inspection Report |
| | | RM-ECO-003 | Weekly Inspection Report |
| | | RM-ECO-004 | Quality Assessment Report |
| | | RM-ECO-005 | Punch List Report |
| Certifications | CERTIFICATIONS | CERT-001 | Certificate of Site Inspection (CIS) |
| | | CERT-002 | Certificate of Final Acceptance (CFA) |

**Critical finding:** The `ConstructionDocumentType` taxonomy is managed via `GET /api/construction-projects/document-types` and seeded in a prior migration. The `KEY_DOC_TYPECODES` are separate (`PROJECT_PROFILE`, `FEASIBILITY_STUDY`, `HGDG_FORM`, `FLOOR_PLAN`, `POW`) and are not the same as the ECO masterlist codes. The ECO masterlist taxonomy is for the **Compliance Checklist** grouping — these are `groupCode` values in the `construction_document_types` table. If the ECO masterlist codes are not yet seeded, a MikroORM migration must insert them.

**Verification needed before Phase 3:** Query `SELECT group_code, type_code, type_label FROM construction_document_types ORDER BY group_code, sort_order;` to confirm whether ECO codes already exist.

---

### 2.246-D: Per-item Remarks — Schema Decision

**Current state:** `construction_document_checklist` table has: `id`, `project_id`, `document_type_id`, `submission_status`, `submitted_by`, `submitted_at`, `reviewed_by`, `reviewed_at`, `review_notes`, `current_version`, `expiry_date`, `linked_document_id`.

**Needed:** A `remarks` column (free-text, nullable) for per-item evaluator notes — distinct from `review_notes` (which is structured review feedback inside the edit dialog).

**Decision:** Add `remarks TEXT NULL` column to `construction_document_checklist`. This is a single-column additive migration. Update `ConstructionDocumentChecklist` entity, `UpdateDocumentChecklistDto`, and `updateDocumentChecklistItem()` service method.

The `CiDocumentChecklist` component will render `remarks` inline beneath each checklist item:
- If `canEditRemarks=true`: editable `<v-textarea>` that auto-saves on blur via PATCH to checklist item endpoint
- If `canEditRemarks=false`: read-only text or em-dash if empty

This replaces the per-item reliance on the dialog for adding notes — the user explicitly requested remarks NOT tied to the edit button.

---

### 2.246-E: Audit Log Strategy Decision

**Option A (Recommended) — Enrich existing `activity_logs`:**
- No new DB table or migration
- Add a `section` key to the `metadata` JSONB for COI events: `{ section: 'DOCUMENT'|'GALLERY'|'CHECKLIST'|'KEY_DOC', fileName?, typeCode?, action? }`
- Add a dedicated frontend `CiAuditLogPanel.vue` component that renders `/api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/:id` with enriched metadata display
- Move audit log out of the "others" tab — embed it as a collapsible section at the bottom of the Attachments tab, or as a dedicated `Audit Log` tab

**Option B — New `coi_audit_logs` table:** Separate table, separate entity, separate service method, new migration. Richer schema (fieldDeltas JSONB, section enum, itemType). More code, same data already captured.

**Decision: Option A.** Existing `activity_logs` table is authoritative. The COI service already logs all significant operations. Adding `section` to metadata at call sites is minimal risk. A dedicated frontend panel with enriched display satisfies the requirement without schema proliferation. YAGNI governs.

**Backend change (ZN):** Audit no new endpoint needed — existing `/api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/:id` already works. Only add `section` metadata to document/gallery/checklist log calls in `construction-projects.service.ts`.

**Frontend change (ZR):** New `CiAuditLogPanel.vue` component; embedded in detail-[id].vue Attachments tab (bottom section) or as a new sibling tab.

---

| Phase | Scope | Primary Files |
|-------|-------|---------------|
| ZM | DB migration: Add `remarks` column to `construction_document_checklist` | New MikroORM migration |
| ZN | Backend: Update entity, DTO, service to persist `remarks`; add `section` metadata to audit log calls | `construction-projects.service.ts`, entity, DTO |
| ZO | Frontend: `CiDocumentChecklist` — per-item inline remarks (replaces group-only remarks model) | `components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue` |

| ZR | Frontend: New `CiAuditLogPanel.vue`; embed in detail-[id].vue Attachments tab | New component + detail page integration |
