## Section 2.249 — Enterprise Attachment Hub v2: Request Analysis (Phase AAA)

**Updated:** 2026-05-27 | Phase AAA Phase 1

### 2.249-A: Scope Summary

Major enterprise-level refactor of the Attachment Tab ecosystem requested. Key items:
- Compliance Checklist repositioned as first landing page (currently tab #5)
- Enterprise repository cards for Key Documents (premade + custom containers per project)
- Gallery with Open Repository modal, lightbox preview, grid/list toggle
- CPES Document Repository — new section based on 22-item checklist from `docs/references/Construction Documents for CSUCC Projects.xlsx`
- Supporting Documents modal enterprise upgrade (search, filter, sort, drag-drop, pagination)
- Audit Log fix (404 on `/api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/:id`)
- Uploader tracking on all document items
- Batch upload support

---

### 2.249-B: CPES Documentary Requirements (Extracted from Excel)

Source: `docs/references/Construction Documents for CSUCC Projects.xlsx` — Sheet: "CSU-Main Ladies and Gents Dorm"
Title: "CPES DOCUMENTARY REQUIREMENTS FOR CSUCC CTHM AND ACADEMIC BUILDING COMPLEX"

22 required documents:

| No. | Document Name | Proposed type_code | Sort |
|-----|---------------|--------------------|------|
| 1 | Approved Statement of Time Elapsed (STE) | CPES_STE | 10 |
| 2 | Approved Statement of Work Accomplished (SWA) | CPES_SWA | 20 |
| 3 | All Approved Variation Orders (if any) | CPES_VAR_ORDERS | 30 |
| 4 | All Approved Time Extensions (if any) | CPES_TIME_EXT | 40 |
| 5 | Time Suspension/Resume Orders and Monthly Suspension Reports | CPES_SUSPENSION | 50 |
| 6 | Contractor Valid License (or JV Special License + Agreement) | CPES_CONTRACTOR_LIC | 60 |
| 7 | Contract of Agreement and Bill of Quantities (BOQ) | CPES_BOQ | 70 |
| 8 | Notice to Proceed (NTP) | CPES_NTP | 80 |
| 9 | Materials Engineers Accreditation (projects above Php 150M) | CPES_MATERIALS_ENG | 90 |
| 10 | Final Completion Inspection Report | CPES_COMPLETION_INSP | 100 |
| 11 | Certificate of Completion | CPES_CERT_COMPLETION | 110 |
| 12 | Straight Line Diagram (Roads and Flood Control) | CPES_STRAIGHT_LINE | 120 |
| 13 | As-Built Plan | CPES_AS_BUILT | 130 |
| 14 | Program of Works (POW) | CPES_POW | 140 |
| 15 | Materials Test Reports/Results | CPES_MATERIALS_TEST | 150 |
| 16 | Original and Revised Quality Control Program (QCP) | CPES_QCP | 160 |
| 17 | Approved Plans | CPES_APPROVED_PLANS | 170 |
| 18 | CPM Work Schedule or Bar Chart / Manpower and Equipment Schedule | CPES_CPM_SCHEDULE | 180 |
| 19 | Organizational Chart and Financial Chart (S-curve or cash flow) | CPES_ORG_CHART | 190 |
| 20 | General Layout of Project Facilities | CPES_LAYOUT | 200 |
| 21 | List of Subcontractors or Suppliers | CPES_SUBCONTRACTORS | 210 |
| 22 | Cover Sheet (Annex-8) | CPES_COVER_SHEET | 220 |

group_code = `CPES_DOCS`, group_label = `CPES Documentary Requirements`, isRequired = true for all.

Existing group_code CHECK constraint (post-ZW migration) allows: `GROUP_1..6`, `ECO_FORMS`, `SD_ORDERS`, `SD_REPORTS`, `SD_CERTS`. Must expand to add `CPES_DOCS`.

---

### 2.249-C: Audit Log 404 Root Cause

**File:** `pmo-backend/src/activity-logs/activity-log.controller.ts:8`
**Defect:** `@Controller('api/activity-logs')` — includes `api/` in controller prefix.
**Cause:** `main.ts:43` calls `app.setGlobalPrefix('api', { exclude: ['health'] })`. NestJS stacks global prefix before controller prefix. Result: route registered at `/api/api/activity-logs`.
**Frontend call:** `GET /api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/{projectId}` → 404.
**Fix:** Change `@Controller('api/activity-logs')` → `@Controller('activity-logs')`. One-line fix.
**Pattern:** All other controllers use bare paths (`@Controller('construction-projects')`) — this is the only controller with the erroneous `api/` prefix.

---

### 2.249-D: Document Entity Uploader Gap

`Document.uploadedBy` (UUID field) exists in DB but is not joined or surfaced in the hub document list response. The hub `HubDoc` interface lacks `uploadedByName`. Fix: modify `getDocumentsForProject()` raw SQL to LEFT JOIN with `users` table on `d.uploaded_by = u.id` and return `u.full_name` (or fallback to `u.email`) as `uploaded_by_name`.

---

### 2.249-E: Current Hub Architecture Audit (Post-ZW–ZZ)

Section order in current `CiAttachmentHub.vue`:

| # | Tab | Label | Gated by hasProject |
|---|-----|-------|---------------------|
| 1 | `key` | Key Documents | No |
| 2 | `gallery` | Gallery | No |
| 3 | `eco` | ECO Forms | No |
| 4 | `supporting` | Supporting Documents | No |
| 5 | `checklist` | Compliance Checklist | Yes |
| 6 | `other` | Other Attachments | No |
| 7 | `audit` | Audit Log | Yes |

Required new order: `checklist → key → gallery → eco → supporting → cpes → other → audit`.

**Key Documents current state:** Flat list (first 5 shown, overflow chip opens modal) + single expandable inline upload form. No premade cards per doc type.

**Gallery current state:** Inline Profile/General sub-tabs. No "Open Repository" fullscreen modal. No lightbox.

**CiDocumentChecklist:** Uses `v-expansion-panels` per group (accordion). User requires responsive grid dashboard, non-collapsible.

**CPES Repository:** Does NOT exist. No `CPES_DOCS` group in taxonomy. Must add via migration.

**CiRepositoryModal:** Exists but missing: search field, filter controls, sort, drag-drop upload zone, pagination, uploader info, recent activity section, audit trail.

**CiAuditLogPanel:** Renders but all fetches return 404 (see 2.249-C).

---

### 2.249-F: Gap Analysis

| Requirement | Current | Gap | Priority |
|-------------|---------|-----|----------|
| Checklist first | Tab #5 | Reorder sections; default activeSection | HIGH |
| Checklist grid | Accordion | Refactor to v-row cards | HIGH |
| CPES Repository | Missing | New migration + new hub section | HIGH |
| Audit Log 404 | Broken | @Controller prefix fix (1 line) | HIGH |
| Key Docs cards | Flat list | 3 CiRepositoryCard + "Other Key Docs" card | HIGH |
| Key Docs custom containers | Missing | Per-project customSection JSONB + backend PATCH | MEDIUM |
| Gallery Open Repository | Missing | New CiGalleryModal.vue | HIGH |
| Gallery lightbox | Missing | v-dialog with v-img | MEDIUM |
| Modal enterprise upgrade | Basic | search/filter/sort/drag-drop/pagination/uploader | HIGH |
| Uploader tracking | UUID only | LEFT JOIN users in service, add to HubDoc | MEDIUM |
| Batch upload | Missing | multiple file input + frontend upload loop | MEDIUM |
| Audit Log table view | Expansion panel | Prominent v-data-table with filter | MEDIUM |

---

### 2.249-G: Architecture Decisions for Phase AAA

**D1 — CPES taxonomy:** Seed 22 CPES docs as `CPES_DOCS` group in `construction_document_types`. They auto-appear in `CiDocumentChecklist` (handles all groups) AND in a new dedicated hub section. No new tables needed.

**D2 — Key Docs custom containers:** Store per-project custom key sections as JSONB field `customKeySections: [{id, label, typeCode}]` on `construction_projects`. typeCode = `CUSTOM_{sectionId}`. Avoids global taxonomy pollution. Backend: new PATCH endpoint `/api/construction-projects/:id/custom-sections`. Frontend: reads `customKeySections` from project and renders dynamic cards.

**D3 — Gallery modal:** New `CiGalleryModal.vue` (gallery-mode fullscreen dialog). Separate from `CiRepositoryModal` (document-focused). Receives gallery items + controls as props from hub.

**D4 — Batch upload:** Frontend loops over selected files calling single-file upload endpoint for each. No new backend endpoint. Show per-file progress state array.

**D5 — Uploader name:** Modify `getDocumentsForProject()` raw SQL to LEFT JOIN users. Return `uploadedByName`. Zero new endpoints.

**D6 — Modal search/filter/pagination:** All client-side within modal. No new API calls. Filter is a computed ref on the `documents` prop. Pagination is "show first 20, load more" on client array slice.

**D7 — Recent activity in modal:** Modal receives `projectId`. Fetches `GET /api/activity-logs/CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT/{projectId}?pageSize=5` on open. Renders as compact list at modal bottom (requires AAA-A audit log fix first).

---

## §2.250 — Enterprise Attachment Ecosystem Refactor v3: Compliance Repository, ECO Merge, Gallery Simplification, Modal UX, Checklist Collapsibility (Phase BBB)

**Last Updated:** 2026-05-27
**Status:** ✅ Research complete — Plan written to `docs/plan.md` Phase BBB

---

### 2.250-A: Scope

Major refactor of the Attachment Tab ecosystem following Phase AAA completion. Key operator requirements:

1. **Compliance Repository** — CPES tab currently shows a single `CiRepositoryCard` that opens `CiRepositoryModal` with an empty flat file list. No per-item text fields, no manual input workspace. Must be replaced with a dedicated per-item entry workspace.
2. **ECO Forms removal** — Dedicated ECO tab to be removed. ECO forms merged into Supporting Documents as 4th category.
3. **Gallery simplification** — Inline gallery section (profile/general tabs + inline upload forms) is too heavy. Must become a lightweight thumbnail strip + "Open Gallery" CTA that delegates all management to `CiGalleryModal`.
4. **Checklist collapsibility** — AAA-E changed to always-visible grid cards. User requests collapsible accordion sections to reduce scroll fatigue.
5. **Modal size reduction** — `fullscreen` dialogs (CiRepositoryModal, CiGalleryModal) are too large. Must become max-width bounded dialogs (~1100px, max-height 85vh).

---

### 2.250-B: Architecture Trace — Frontend → API → DB

**Attachment Data Flow:**
```
Hub (CiAttachmentHub) → fetchDocuments() → GET /api/construction-projects/:id/documents
                       → fetchGallery()   → GET /api/construction-projects/:id/gallery
                       → fetchDocTypes()  → GET /api/construction-projects/document-types/grouped
Checklist             → fetchChecklist() → GET /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist
                                           ↳ returns ALL checklist rows (all groups including CPES_DOCS)
Per-item update       → PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist/:itemId
Per-item submission   → POST (upload) /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions
```

**DB Tables involved:**
- `documents` — uploaded files; `documentable_type=CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT`, `document_type` = typeCode
- `construction_document_types` — taxonomy; groups: GROUP_1..6, ECO_FORMS, SD_ORDERS, SD_REPORTS, SD_CERTS, CPES_DOCS
- `construction_document_checklist` — per-project, per-typeId compliance tracking; has `remarks`, `linked_document_id`, `submission_status`
- `construction_document_submissions` — immutable submission history (file + version)

**CPES Gap Root Cause:** The CPES tab renders a single `CiRepositoryCard` → opens `CiRepositoryModal`. The modal shows documents WHERE `documentType IN (cpesCodes)`. Since no files have been uploaded via CPES type codes, the list is empty. The per-item workspace (remarks textarea, file upload, status) exists in `CiDocumentChecklist` but not in the CPES section. The user needs to enter notes and upload files per-CPES-item, not just bulk-upload with a type code.

---

### 2.250-C: Gap Analysis

| Gap | Current | Required | Priority |
|-----|---------|----------|----------|
| CPES per-item entry | Single repository card, empty | Dedicated per-item workspace (CiComplianceRepository) | CRITICAL |
| CPES text field input | None | Per-item remarks textarea, auto-save | CRITICAL |
| CPES file upload | Generic repo upload (no item link) | Per-item file upload linked to checklist item | CRITICAL |
| ECO Forms tab | Dedicated tab (download-only list) | Merged into Supporting Docs as 4th card | HIGH |
| Gallery inline panel | Profile/general tabs + inline forms (heavy) | Thumbnail strip + Open Gallery CTA | HIGH |
| Checklist scroll | All groups visible (grid), high scroll | Collapsible accordion per group | HIGH |
| Modal fullscreen | fullscreen dialogs (too large) | max-width="1100" + max-height 85vh | MEDIUM |
| Supporting Docs | 3 cards (Orders, Reports, Certs) | 4 cards (+ ECO/Forms) | MEDIUM |

---

### 2.250-D: CPES Per-Item Workspace Architecture Decision

**Decision:** Create `CiComplianceRepository.vue` — a dedicated component for the Compliance Repository tab.

**Data source:** Reuse existing `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist` endpoint. Filter client-side to `item.documentType?.groupCode === 'CPES_DOCS'`. No new backend endpoint needed.

**Per-item fields:**
- Type label + required indicator (from `documentType.typeLabel`, `documentType.isRequired`)
- Submission status chip (from `item.submissionStatus`)
- Per-item remarks textarea (maps to `item.remarks`; auto-save on blur via PATCH)
- Linked file display (chip linking to `linkedDoc()`)
- Upload/Resubmit button (opens inline file picker → POST to submissions endpoint)
- History button (opens navigation drawer via existing pattern)

**Integration:** Hub replaces the single `CiRepositoryCard` in the `v-window-item value="cpes"` with `<CiComplianceRepository :project-id="projectId" :can-edit="canUpload" :documents="documents" :can-edit-remarks="canEditRemarks" />`.

---

### 2.250-E: ECO Forms Merge Architecture Decision

**Decision:** Keep `ECO_FORMS` group code in DB unchanged. Add ECO forms as 4th `CiRepositoryCard` in Supporting Documents section. Remove dedicated `eco` window-item from hub.

**Changes required:**
- Hub `sections` computed: remove `{ value: 'eco', ... }` entry
- Hub: add `ecoFormsStats = computed(() => repoStats(codesForGroup('ECO_FORMS')))`
- Supporting Documents template: add 4th `CiRepositoryCard` with `ecoFormsStats` and `ecoFormsCodes`
- `managedCodes` already includes ECO codes (via `codesForGroup('ECO_FORMS')`) — no change

**Supporting tab layout:** 4 cards in 2×2 grid (`cols="12" md="6"`) instead of current 3 cards in 3-column row.

---

### 2.250-F: Gallery Simplification

**Decision:** The `v-window-item value="gallery"` inline section replaced with a compact summary card:
- First row: 6 recent gallery thumbnails (clickable → opens CiGalleryModal)
- Second row: count summary + "Open Gallery" primary button + "Upload" secondary button (→ opens modal with upload expanded)
- Remove all inline form controls (profileImageFile, galleryImageFile, galleryImageCategory, etc.)
- Gallery management fully delegated to CiGalleryModal

**Staging mode gallery:** CiGalleryModal already handles staging mode via `persistGallery` emit which pushes to `queue.images`. The staging preview (list of queued images) can be shown in a compact format within the simplified gallery section.

---

### 2.250-G: Checklist Accordion Restoration

**Decision:** Revert AAA-E responsive grid cards back to collapsible accordion (`v-expansion-panels`). Rationale: Always-visible grid causes excessive vertical scrolling when all groups are expanded simultaneously. Accordion shows group summaries in headers (collapsed) → user drills into specific group. Reduces initial view from ~1500px scroll to ~400px.

**Accordion panel header structure:**
- Group label + progress chip (submitted/total) + completion percentage
- Thin progress bar strip within header

**Default state:** First group open, rest collapsed. User can open multiple.

---

### 2.250-H: Modal Dimension Architecture

**Decision:** Change both CiRepositoryModal and CiGalleryModal from `fullscreen` to bounded dialogs.

**CiRepositoryModal:** `max-width="1100"` + `scrollable` + card-text max-height via CSS
**CiGalleryModal:** `max-width="1200"` + keep `fullscreen` prop removed + internal scroll

**Impact:** Modals now float over content rather than replacing the entire viewport. User can see context (project name in page title etc.) via modal backdrop.

---

### 2.250-I: Architecture Decisions D1–D8

**D1 — CiComplianceRepository:** New component replacing CPES CiRepositoryCard. Fetches checklist, filters to CPES_DOCS, renders per-item workspace. Reuses existing PATCH + submission upload endpoints. No new backend.

**D2 — ECO merge:** Keep `ECO_FORMS` group in DB unchanged. Add 4th card to Supporting Docs. Remove ECO tab. Zero backend changes.

**D3 — Gallery hub simplification:** Remove inline forms from Gallery window-item. Thumbnail strip + modal CTA only. Staging images shown as compact queued list.

**D4 — Checklist accordion:** Revert AAA-E grid to v-expansion-panels with summary headers. Collapse all but first by default.

**D5 — Modal dimensions:** Remove `fullscreen` from CiRepositoryModal + CiGalleryModal. Use `max-width` + CSS max-height for contained scroll.

**D6 — CPES rename:** Hub tab label: "Compliance Repository". Component name: CiComplianceRepository. Internal text references "Compliance & Evaluation Documents".

**D7 — Supporting Docs layout:** 4 cards in 2×2 grid. ECO card: title "ECO & PMO Forms", icon "mdi-form-select", color "purple".

**D8 — Compliance Repository view mode:** In view mode (`canEdit=false`, `canEditRemarks=false`), CiComplianceRepository renders read-only: status chips, file download links, remarks text (no textarea). Upload/history buttons visible in edit mode only.

---

## §2.251 — Phase CCC Research: Attachment Ecosystem Enterprise Refactor (Root-Cause Analysis)

**Date:** 2026-05-27 · **Trigger:** Operator RUN_ACE Phase 1+2 (enterprise attachment-tab refactor prompt) · **Predecessor:** Phase BBB (implemented, code-verified)

### 2.251-A: Scope reconciliation vs. already-shipped work

Several prompt requirements were already delivered in Phases AAA/BBB and must NOT be re-implemented (avoid churn — KISS/YAGNI). Confirmed present in current code:

| Prompt requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Identical rendering across detail/new/edit | ✅ DONE | All 3 pages mount the single `CiAttachmentHub` (ZY-D1). `new.vue:1115` mode=staging; `detail` mode=view; `edit` mode=edit. |
| Enterprise repository modal (search/filter/sort/pagination/uploader/recent activity) | ✅ DONE (AAA-H) | `CiRepositoryModal.vue` has `modalSearch/modalFilterType/modalSort`, `MODAL_PAGE_SIZE`, `recentActivity`, uploader subtitle. |
| Modal responsive dimensions (not fullscreen) | ✅ DONE (BBB-E) | `max-width="1100"/"1200"` + internal scroll. |
| Compliance repository per-item workspace | ✅ DONE (BBB-B) | `CiComplianceRepository.vue`. |
| Supporting Docs categories Orders/Reports/Certifications | ✅ DONE | `SD_ORDERS`/`SD_REPORTS`/`SD_CERTS` groups + 3 cards + ECO 4th (BBB-F). |
| Checklist collapsible (scroll fatigue) | ✅ DONE (BBB-D) | accordion. |

**Net-new Phase CCC work** is therefore the *non-overlapping remainder*: (1) download lifecycle hardening + DOWNLOAD audit, (2) checklist→repository navigation, (3) compliance repo 2-column grid + Annex-8 removal, (4) "Miscellaneous/Uncategorized" 4th supporting category, (5) filter-icon rendering audit, (6) modal interaction-label polish.

### 2.251-B: Download lifecycle — ROOT CAUSE (critical gap)

Trace: `StorageService.saveFile` (`storage.service.ts:58-78`) writes to `./uploads/<entityType>/<entityId>/<uuid><ext>` and returns `filePath = /uploads/<rel>`. `main.ts:19` serves it via `app.useStaticAssets(absoluteUploadDir, { prefix: '/uploads' })`. Nuxt dev proxies `/uploads` → `localhost:3000/uploads` (`nuxt.config.ts:64`). Frontend renders the raw `filePath` as `<a :href="doc.filePath">`.

**Findings / gaps:**
1. **Filename loss:** the on-disk file is UUID-named; the static server sets `Content-Disposition` from the UUID, so a plain `<a href>` click downloads `e7b1…c2.pdf`, not the original name. The original name lives only in `documents.file_name` (DB). Browsers honor an `<a download="originalName">` attribute *only same-origin* — true under the dev proxy, fragile in prod if `apiBase` points cross-origin.
2. **No DOWNLOAD audit:** `ActivityAction.DOWNLOAD` exists in the enum but is **never fired** — `grep DOWNLOAD service.ts` → 0 `fireLog` calls. Downloads are completely untracked, violating the audit requirement.
3. **No download endpoint:** there is no `GET :id/documents/:docId/download` that streams with a correct `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<original>"`. MIME is stored (`documents.mime_type`) but never re-asserted on serve.
4. **Reupload path is already version-safe** for checklist items (`submitDocumentVersion` → `construction_document_submissions` ledger, `getDocumentSubmissions` orders by version DESC). For non-checklist repository docs, "replace" = soft-delete old + upload new (`removeDocument` preserves physical file + history). This satisfies the "download→edit→reupload" requirement *for checklist/CPES items*; generic repository docs have no in-place replace, only add+delete.

**Decision D-CCC-1:** Add a dedicated authenticated download endpoint `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/documents/:docId/download` that streams via `StreamableFile` with `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<original>"` + `Content-Type: <mimeType>`, and fires `ActivityAction.DOWNLOAD`. Frontend download controls switch from raw `filePath` href to this endpoint. External-link docs (`mimeType = application/x-google-drive-link`) keep the raw href (open in new tab, no streaming).

### 2.251-C: Checklist → repository navigation — ROOT CAUSE

`CiDocumentChecklist.vue` has no scroll/focus/redirect logic (`grep scrollIntoView|focus` → 0). Checklist items and repository cards live in sibling `v-window-item`s inside `CiAttachmentHub`; the checklist cannot switch `activeSection` because it does not own that state.

**Decision D-CCC-2:** Add a `navigate` emit from `CiDocumentChecklist` → caught by `CiAttachmentHub`, which maps a checklist item's `groupCode` → hub section value and sets `activeSection`, then sets a transient `highlightTypeCode` consumed by the target section / opens the matching `CiRepositoryModal`. Mapping table (groupCode → hub tab): `SD_ORDERS|SD_REPORTS|SD_CERTS|ECO_FORMS → 'supporting'`; `CPES_DOCS → 'cpes'`; key-doc codes → `'key'`. For supporting/key, open the relevant `CiRepositoryModal` filtered to that type so the user lands directly on the file. No backend change.

### 2.251-D: Compliance repository grid + Annex-8 removal

`CiComplianceRepository.vue` (BBB-B) renders one full-width `v-card` per item → 22 stacked cards = scroll fatigue. The prompt wants a **two-column responsive grid** and removal of the **Cover Sheet (Annex-8)** item (`CPES_COVER_SHEET`, seeded `Migration20260527030000:48`).

**Decision D-CCC-3:** Wrap item cards in `<v-row><v-col cols="12" md="6">` (2-col on ≥md). **Decision D-CCC-4:** Filter `CPES_COVER_SHEET` out of `cpesItems` client-side (KISS — no destructive migration; the seeded row stays but is hidden). A reversible down-migration is *not* warranted since the taxonomy row is harmless when unrendered; document the client-side exclusion constant `EXCLUDED_CPES_CODES = ['CPES_COVER_SHEET']`.

### 2.251-E: Supporting Documents 4th category (Miscellaneous/Uncategorized)

Current groups: `SD_ORDERS`, `SD_REPORTS`, `SD_CERTS`, `ECO_FORMS`. The prompt's category D ("Miscellaneous / Uncategorized Forms") maps to the existing **"Other Attachments"** section (docs whose `documentType ∉ managedCodes`). This already exists in the hub as the `other` window-item.

**Decision D-CCC-5:** No new DB group. Rename the Supporting Docs "Certifications" card label to "Certifications & Other Documents" (per prompt category C) and treat the existing **Other Attachments** tab as category D. No taxonomy migration.

### 2.251-F: Filter-icon rendering audit

The global filter bar (`CiAttachmentHub.vue` top) uses `v-text-field prepend-inner-icon="mdi-magnify"` + a `Clear (n)` `v-btn prepend-icon="mdi-filter-off"`. Modals use `v-btn-toggle` with `mdi-sort-*` icons and `v-select` clearable (default clear icon). No explicit sizing on several icon buttons → inconsistent visual weight vs. the `size="small"` icons elsewhere. The "broken filter icon" most likely refers to (a) the `v-btn-toggle` sort icons rendering at default (large) size in the now-smaller bounded modal, and (b) `clearable` clear-icons on dense fields overlapping text.

**Decision D-CCC-6:** Standardize: all toolbar action icons `size="small"`; all `v-btn-toggle` buttons `size="small"` with `density="compact"`; ensure `hide-details` + `single-line` on dense filter fields. Apply uniformly across `CiRepositoryModal`, `CiGalleryModal`, `CiComplianceRepository`, and the hub filter bar. Purely presentational; no logic.

### 2.251-G: Audit coverage matrix (current vs. required)

| Action | Fired today? | Where |
|---|---|---|
| UPLOAD (doc) | ✅ | `service.ts:2002` |
| BATCH_UPLOAD | ⚠️ partial | modal emits N single UPLOADs; no distinct BATCH_UPLOAD log |
| REMOVE_ATTACHMENT | ✅ | `service.ts:2076` |
| SUBMIT (version) | ✅ | `service.ts:2155` |
| DOWNLOAD | ❌ MISSING | — (D-CCC-1 adds it) |
| REMARKS_UPDATE | ⚠️ | PATCH document-checklist fires generic UPDATE, not REMARKS_UPDATE |
| TEMPLATE_UPLOAD | ⚠️ | `uploadDocumentTypeTemplate` fires CREATE, not TEMPLATE_UPLOAD |

**Decision D-CCC-7:** Add DOWNLOAD logging (D-CCC-1). Optionally align REMARKS_UPDATE / TEMPLATE_UPLOAD enum usage — **deferred** (low value, the panel already labels them; YAGNI unless operator insists).

### 2.251-H: RBAC continuity

`@Roles` on document routes: list = Admin/Staff/Viewer/Auditor; upload/submit = Admin/Staff; delete = Admin. New download endpoint must mirror **read** roles (Admin/Staff/Viewer/Auditor) and the `@Public()` public controller must NOT expose it (downloads of governance docs stay authenticated). Frontend `canUpload/canDelete` props already gate UI; download is available to all who can view.

### 2.251-I: Architecture Decisions summary (D-CCC-1 … D-CCC-7)

D-CCC-1 Download endpoint + StreamableFile + Content-Disposition + DOWNLOAD audit (backend + frontend) ·
D-CCC-2 Checklist→repository navigation via emit + activeSection mapping ·
D-CCC-3 Compliance repo 2-column grid ·
D-CCC-4 Hide CPES_COVER_SHEET (Annex-8) client-side ·
D-CCC-5 Rename Certifications card; reuse Other Attachments as category D ·
D-CCC-6 Filter/toolbar icon sizing standardization ·
D-CCC-7 DOWNLOAD audit (with -1); REMARKS_UPDATE/TEMPLATE_UPLOAD alignment deferred.

**Out of scope / rejected:** new storage backend, S3, MIME re-encoding, duplicate-hash detection (no requirement signal; YAGNI), destructive Annex-8 migration, new Supporting Docs DB group.

---

## §2.252 — Phase DDD Research: Landing + Detail + Toolbar + SD Template Library (Root-Cause Analysis)

**Date:** 2026-05-27 · **Trigger:** RUN_ACE Phase 1+2 (id `coi-r9x41`) — landing/detail HCI refactor + toolbar overflow + real SD template files · **Predecessors:** AAA/BBB/CCC (implemented)

### 2.252-A: Scope reconciliation (avoid re-doing shipped work)

Much of the prompt's Part 3 (modals, download lifecycle, filter-icon sizing) is already delivered: AAA-H (enterprise modal: search/filter/sort/pagination/uploader/recent-activity), BBB-E (bounded modals), CCC-A (download streaming + filename + MIME + DOWNLOAD audit), CCC-E (filter icon/clear-icon standardization). **Net-new Phase DDD work:** (1) SD template library wiring with the *real* `.docx` files, (2) attachment toolbar overflow fix (the From/To date-field clipping the prompt's image shows), (3) landing-page dashboard enrichment using existing analytics endpoints, (4) detail-page Overview densification.

### 2.252-B: Supporting-Documents template library — the concrete deliverable (Part 4)

Operator supplied real shareable forms under `docs/references/SHAREABLE SUPPORT DOCUMENTS-…/{ORDERS,REPORTS AND MONITORING,CERTIFICATIONS AND OTHER DOCUMENTS}`. Filenames embed the code: `SD-ECO-ECO-0NN` ↔ DB `type_code = SD_ECO_0NN` (taxonomy seeded in `Migration20260527020000`). Confirmed exact mapping:

| File | type_code | group |
|---|---|---|
| SD-ECO-ECO-001 Variation Order | SD_ECO_001 | SD_ORDERS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-002 Work Suspension Order | SD_ECO_002 | SD_ORDERS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-003 Work Resumption Order | SD_ECO_003 | SD_ORDERS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-004 Contract Time Extension Order | SD_ECO_004 | SD_ORDERS |
| (DRAFT) 005 Notice of Non-Compliance | SD_ECO_005 | SD_ORDERS |
| (DRAFT) 006 Show Cause Order | SD_ECO_006 | SD_ORDERS |
| (DRAFT) 007 Notice of Termination | SD_ECO_007 | SD_ORDERS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-008 Construction Logbook | SD_ECO_008 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-009 Weekly Accomplishment Report | SD_ECO_009 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-010 Monthly Progress Report | SD_ECO_010 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-011 Site Instruction | SD_ECO_011 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-012 Site Inspection Report | SD_ECO_012 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-013 Project Inspection Report | SD_ECO_013 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-014 Quality Assessment Report | SD_ECO_014 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-015 Safety Compliance Report | SD_ECO_015 | SD_REPORTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-016 Certificate of Site Inspection | SD_ECO_016 | SD_CERTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-017 Certificate of Completion | SD_ECO_017 | SD_CERTS |
| SD-ECO-ECO-018 Certificate of Final Acceptance | SD_ECO_018 | SD_CERTS |
| Mandatory Certification - LUDIP | SD_ECO_019 | SD_CERTS |

**Mechanism (existing):** `construction_document_types.template_url` is the blank-form pointer; `CiRepositoryModal` renders a "Blank forms:" download row from `relevantTypes.filter(t => t.templateUrl)`; `uploadDocumentTypeTemplate` (admin) sets it to `/uploads/coi-templates/<uuid>.docx` served statically. So templates ride the same `/uploads` static pipe.

**Root cause "CPES/SD empty":** no `template_url` is populated for any SD type → no blank-form buttons appear → users can't download→edit→reupload. We must **seed `template_url`** for SD_ECO_001–019.

**Decision D-DDD-1 (seeding strategy):** Add a one-time, idempotent **seed script** `pmo-backend/scripts/seed-sd-templates.ts` (run via `ts-node`) that: copies each shareable `.docx` from `docs/references/SHAREABLE…/<folder>/` into the served uploads dir (`<UPLOAD_DIR>/coi-templates/SD_ECO_0NN.docx`), then `UPDATE construction_document_types SET template_url = '/uploads/coi-templates/SD_ECO_0NN.docx' WHERE type_code = 'SD_ECO_0NN'`. Idempotent (overwrite-safe; re-runnable). NOT a MikroORM migration — migrations must not perform filesystem copies and must stay environment-portable. The script is the enterprise-correct, reviewable artifact. DRAFT forms (005–007) are seeded too (operator may swap finals later via the existing admin template-upload route). LUDIP file has no `SD-ECO` prefix → mapped explicitly by name.
**Open question for operator:** confirm `UPLOAD_DIR` is a persistent volume in deployment (templates must survive redeploys). If uploads are ephemeral, re-run the seed script post-deploy (documented as an operator step).

### 2.252-C: Attachment toolbar overflow (Part 3, the image bug)

`CiAttachmentHub` global filter bar is a single `d-flex flex-wrap ga-2` row containing: search (`flex:1 1 280px`), From (`type=date label="From"` `max-width:160px`), To (same), Clear btn. With Vuetify `density="compact"` + `variant="outlined"` + a floating `label`, native date inputs need ≥168px or the label collides with the `dd/mm/yyyy` placeholder → the reported overflow/overlap. On mid widths the search's `flex-grow` starves the date fields.

**Decision D-DDD-2:** Restructure the filter bar into a responsive `v-row dense` grid: search `cols=12 sm=6 md=5`, From `cols=6 sm=3 md=3`, To `cols=6 sm=3 md=3`, Clear in a trailing auto col. Use `single-line` (already added in CCC-E for search) and ensure date fields have their own `min-width` so labels never overlap. This guarantees wrapping without clipping at all breakpoints. Same pattern applied to any modal toolbar that mixes labelled date/select fields.

### 2.252-D: Landing page dashboard enrichment (Part 1)

Current `index.vue`: Projects tab (KPI cards: Total/Completed/Ongoing/Pending + filter bar + data-table) and Analytics tab (donut by_status, bar by_campus). Available without new backend: `analytics/summary` → `{ total, total_contract_value, by_status[], by_campus[], by_publication_status[] }`; `analytics/financial-summary`; module-wide recent activity via `GET /api/activity-logs?entityType=CONSTRUCTION_PROJECT&pageSize=N` (**SuperAdmin/Admin/Auditor only** — must role-gate the widget); per-project progress already on each `UIProject` (`physicalAccomplishment`).

**Decision D-DDD-3 (bounded, no new backend):** Enrich the Projects tab top band with: (a) a **Budget Overview** card (`total_contract_value` via `formatCurrencyShort`, already present), (b) a **Publication breakdown** mini-stat (Draft/Pending/Published/Rejected from `by_publication_status`), (c) a **Delayed Projects** card computed client-side (`status===ONGOING && physicalAccomplishment < threshold`, threshold configurable, default behind-schedule heuristic), (d) a **Recent Activity** panel (role-gated) from the activity-logs list endpoint. No speculative widgets that need endpoints we don't have (audit summaries beyond recent activity, compliance % aggregation) — those are **deferred** pending a dedicated analytics endpoint (YAGNI now).

### 2.252-E: Detail page Overview densification (Part 2)

`detail-[id].vue`: enterprise summary card above 7 tabs (overview/progress/timeline/analytics/documents/team/others); Overview tab has low information density / whitespace. Edit page shares the same field set (single source: `adapters.ts` `UIProjectDetail`). 

**Decision D-DDD-4:** Restructure Overview into a responsive 2–3 column card grid grouping: Project Identity, Financials (contract/funding), Progress (physical/financial/milestone bars — already computed in `progressSummary`), Schedule (dates), Attachment & Compliance quick-summary (counts + "open documents" CTA). Collapsible advanced sections to cut scroll. Presentation-only; reuses existing computeds; no field renames (adapters untouched).

### 2.252-F: RBAC / governance continuity

No backend route changes except the template seed (data only, via script). Activity-log list endpoint is admin-gated → the Recent Activity widget renders only for those roles (use `usePermissions`/role check). All three pages keep the single `CiAttachmentHub` (ZY-D1). Adapters remain the only interface source (JB-D3).

### 2.252-G: Architecture Decisions (D-DDD-1 … D-DDD-4)

D-DDD-1 SD template seed script (real .docx → template_url, idempotent, not a migration) ·
D-DDD-2 Filter toolbar → responsive v-row grid (no overflow/label collision) ·
D-DDD-3 Landing dashboard enrichment using existing endpoints only (budget, publication breakdown, delayed, role-gated recent activity) ·
D-DDD-4 Detail Overview densification into grouped responsive card grid (presentation-only).

**Out of scope / rejected:** new analytics endpoints, compliance-% aggregation service, redesign of edit/new forms (already synchronized via adapters + CiAttachmentHub), moving templates to a DB blob, any change to immutable routes or taxonomy.

---

## §2.253 — Phase EEE Research: Landing Tier 1 Analytics, Project Details Dashboard, Modal Improvements, SD Per-Form Grid (Root-Cause Analysis)

**Date:** 2026-05-27 · **Trigger:** RUN_ACE Phase 1+2 — COI landing structural refactor, project details enterprise dashboard, CiRepositoryModal improvements, Supporting Documents per-form container grid · **Predecessors:** AAA–DDD (all implemented)

### 2.253-A: Scope reconciliation (what DDD delivered vs. what EEE must add)

DDD-C added a 6-card KPI band, publication breakdown row, and role-gated recent activity panel to the landing page. DDD-D added a quick-access strip to the detail OU-B card. DDD-B fixed the global filter bar overflow. CCC-E standardized modal icon sizing.

**Net-new Phase EEE scope:**
- **Landing (Part 1):** Remove the DDD-C publication breakdown row (user-directed REMOVE); move recent activity below the project table; add Tier 1 analytics directly in the Projects tab top (hero strip, portfolio progress bar, budget gauge, campus ranked bars, needs-attention list) — all from existing endpoints.
- **Detail (Part 2):** Add a collapsible dashboard summary block between the OU-B card and the accordion — financial bar, milestone progress, and quick-action strip; additive only, no accordion rewrite (KISS on 3,548-line file).
- **Attachment (Part 3):** Restructure CiRepositoryModal search/filter toolbar into `v-row dense` grid; improve metadata display in doc list; enhance upload guidance text.
- **Supporting Docs (Part 4, CRITICAL):** Create `CiSupportingDocsRepository.vue` (per-type checklist workspace, groupCode-parameterized adaptation of CiComplianceRepository); wire SD_ORDERS / SD_REPORTS / SD_CERTS sections in CiAttachmentHub to use it inline instead of the modal-card approach.

### 2.253-B: Landing page — structural refactor and Tier 1 analytics

**Current Projects tab top stack (index.vue):**
1. 6-card KPI band (Total / Completed / Ongoing / Delayed / Pending Review / Total Budget) — rows 458–513
2. DDD-C publication breakdown row + recent activity panel — rows 516–549
3. Filter bar — rows 552–584
4. Data table card — rows 586–771

**User directive:**
- REMOVE the publication breakdown row (lines 516–549 as a unit — the `v-row dense class="mb-4"` containing publication chips + activity panel)
- MOVE recent activity BELOW the data table (after `</v-card>` closing the table)
- ADD hero strip + Tier 1 analytics widgets between KPI band and filter bar

**Tier 1 analytics data sources (no new backend):**

| Widget | Source | Already fetched? |
|---|---|---|
| Hero strip (focal metric: total contract value) | `stats.totalContractValue` + `stats.avgProgress` | ✅ from `fetchProjects` |
| Portfolio stacked progress (by_status distribution) | `analyticsSummary.by_status[]` | ⚠️ Analytics tab only — needs lift to `onMounted` |
| Budget utilization gauge | `financialSummary.utilization_rate`, `financial_summary.total_appropriation/obligation/disbursement` | ⚠️ Analytics tab only — needs lift to `onMounted` |
| Campus ranked bars | `analyticsSummary.by_campus[]` | ⚠️ Analytics tab only — needs lift to `onMounted` |
| Needs-attention list | computed from `projects.value` (delayed + pending + rejected) | ✅ from `fetchProjects` |

**Decision D-EEE-1:** Lift `fetchAnalytics()` call into `onMounted` (alongside `fetchProjects`; keep lazy guard in `watch(activeTab…)` for re-fetch). Add an `analyticsReady` computed (`!!analyticsSummary.value`). Widgets show skeleton or compact fallback while loading — no blocking splash.

**Decision D-EEE-2 (Hero strip):** A single full-width card with: left focal stat (`total_contract_value` large text + "Portfolio Budget"), center portfolio-wide average progress bar (from `stats.avgProgress`), right mini status-distribution pips (a horizontal grouped bar or colored chip row from `by_status`). Purely from already-computed refs; no new ApexChart instance (avoid render overhead on first load).

**Decision D-EEE-3 (Budget gauge):** A compact card with three `v-progress-linear` stacked: Appropriation (baseline), Obligation (obligation/appropriation), Disbursement (disbursement/appropriation). Colored `teal/info/success`. Avoids a full radialBar ApexChart widget on the landing page (page already has ApexCharts in Analytics tab; KISS).

**Decision D-EEE-4 (Campus ranked bars):** A compact `v-list` of campuses sorted descending by count, each with a `v-progress-linear` sized relative to max campus count. No ApexChart — pure Vuetify. Responsive-col alongside the budget gauge.

**Decision D-EEE-5 (Needs-attention list):** A compact card listing projects needing action: delayed (`ONGOING && physicalAccomplishment < DELAY_THRESHOLD`) + pending review (`PENDING_REVIEW`) + rejected (`REJECTED`). Each item: name chip + status badge + router-push to detail. Max 5 items with "Show all" toggle. Role-agnostic (all roles can see their attention items).

**Decision D-EEE-6 (Recent activity relocation):** Move the role-gated `recentActivity` `v-col` block to after the data table `</v-card>` — render as a standalone card at the bottom of the Projects tab. Same data/fetch, same role gate, just repositioned.

**Layout target (Projects tab, top-down):**
1. 6-card KPI band (unchanged — just `md="2"` per card)
2. Hero strip card (full-width, 1 row)
3. `v-row`: Budget gauge `md=5` + Campus ranked bars `md=4` + Needs-attention `md=3`
4. Filter bar (unchanged)
5. Data table
6. Recent Activity card (admin-gated, below table)

### 2.253-C: Project Details enterprise summary enhancements

**Current detail-[id].vue structure:** OU-B hero card (6 stats + DDD-D quick-access strip) → 8-panel `v-expansion-panels` accordion (Overview / Scope & Description / Location & Classification / Financials / Funding Source / Progress Summary / Assigned Personnel / Notes & Status History).

**What the user wants:** "enterprise project dashboard with project summary hero, grouped information cards, progress/timeline/personnel/repository/attachment/compliance/financial summaries, quick access actions."

**Root cause of information deficit:** The accordion panels render form-field displays (labels + values), not visual summaries. Milestones, compliance status, and attachment counts require navigating to separate tabs.

**Decision D-EEE-7 (bounded, additive-only):** Insert a collapsible `v-expansion-panels` block between the OU-B card and the accordion (`variant="accordion"` with a single "Dashboard Summary" panel, initially open). Content: (a) financial bar row (appropriation/obligation/disbursement 3-level `v-progress-linear`), (b) milestone progress chip + bar (`milestones.filter(COMPLETED).length / milestones.length`), (c) gallery thumbnail strip (first 4 images), (d) quick-action buttons (Edit, Submit for Review, Open Documents). All data already fetched in `fetchProject()`. No new API calls. ZERO accordion panel changes — the existing 8 panels remain untouched.

**Decision D-EEE-8:** If `project.financials` (array per fiscal year) exists, sum to get portfolio totals for the financial bar; fallback to `project.totalContractAmount` as appropriation if financials are empty. Use existing `formatCurrencyShort` util.

**Out-of-scope for EEE-C:** Redesign of the 8 accordion panels, moving/removing tabs, adding new API calls.

### 2.253-D: CiRepositoryModal toolbar improvements

**Current toolbar (lines 218–248 of CiRepositoryModal.vue):** `d-flex align-center ga-2 flex-wrap px-4 pt-3` containing: search text-field (flex:1), type select (max-width:220px), `v-btn-toggle` (3 sort icon-buttons), `v-spacer`, upload toggle btn.

**Root causes of visual issues:**
1. `flex-wrap` causes the `v-btn-toggle` to wrap to a second line on mid-widths, detaching it from the search.
2. The `v-btn-toggle` `density="compact"` sets toggle height, but inner `v-btn` icon-only renders at `size="small"` which may conflict with the toggle's density, producing inconsistent heights vs. adjacent `v-text-field density="compact"`.
3. No visual grouping between search+filter section and the sort+upload action section.

**Decision D-EEE-9:** Convert modal toolbar to `v-row dense class="px-4 pt-3"`: search `cols=12 sm=6 md=5`, type select `cols=12 sm=4 md=4`, sort-toggle + upload btn in `cols=12 sm=2 md=3 class="d-flex justify-end align-center ga-2"`. This prevents wrapping and aligns all controls in one row at ≥sm.

**Decision D-EEE-10 (upload guidance):** Add a `v-alert type="info" density="compact" variant="tonal"` inside the upload zone explaining accepted formats and max size — renders only when `showUpload`. Replaces the single grey text line currently in the drag zone.

**Decision D-EEE-11 (metadata enrichment):** In the flat doc list, add `doc.fileSize` display (formatted as KB/MB) and `doc.lifecycleStatus` chip to `v-list-item-subtitle`. Already on the `RepoDoc` interface; just not rendered. Purely presentational.

### 2.253-E: Supporting Documents per-form container grid (CRITICAL)

**Current SD implementation in CiAttachmentHub:**
- `Supporting Documents` tab renders 4 `CiRepositoryCard` tiles (Orders / Reports / Certs / ECO Forms)
- Clicking opens `CiRepositoryModal` with a flat list of all documents for that group
- This is a "browse uploaded files" UX — no per-type status, no blank-form awareness per item

**What the user wants:** "per-form repository container grid" — each specific form type gets its own card showing submission status, blank-form download, upload/resubmit, history. This is the **CiComplianceRepository pattern applied to SD groups**.

**Root cause of gap:** `CiComplianceRepository` hard-codes `groupCode = 'CPES_DOCS'` and `EXCLUDED_CPES_CODES`. It cannot be reused as-is for SD_ORDERS, SD_REPORTS, SD_CERTS. A new group-parameterized component is needed.

**Decision D-EEE-12 (New component: `CiSupportingDocsRepository.vue`):**
- Props: `projectId: string`, `groupCode: string` (SD_ORDERS | SD_REPORTS | SD_CERTS), `groupLabel: string`, `color: string`, `canEdit?: boolean`, `canEditRemarks?: boolean`
- Shares same fetch endpoint: `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist`
- Filters items: `item.documentType?.groupCode === groupCode` (no exclusions for SD groups)
- Summary bar: same pattern as CiComplianceRepository but with `groupLabel` + submitted/approved counts
- Per-type cards: identical template to CiComplianceRepository (`statusIcon`, `statusLabel`, blank form download, submit/resubmit, history, remarks)
- Upload/submission: same endpoint `POST /api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions`
- History: same endpoint `GET .../submissions`
- Authenticated download: same `api.download()` helper

**Justification for new component over generalization:** Modifying CiComplianceRepository risks CPES regression (the most critical compliance section). A new component with identical structure but `groupCode` prop is safer, reviewable, and meets DRY threshold (4 uses: CPES + 3 SD groups, but CPES stays as-is).

**Decision D-EEE-13 (CiAttachmentHub wiring):** In the `Supporting Documents` `v-window-item`:
- Replace `CiRepositoryCard` + `@open` pattern for SD_ORDERS, SD_REPORTS, SD_CERTS with `<CiSupportingDocsRepository>` inline (no modal trigger).
- ECO Forms card stays as `CiRepositoryCard` → `openRepo` modal (ECO forms are flat-upload, not checklist-based workflow).
- Remove `ordersStats`, `reportsStats`, `certsStats` computeds (no longer needed; stats now come from CiSupportingDocsRepository's own summary bar).
- Keep `ecoStats` for the remaining ECO Forms card.
- Layout: each `<CiSupportingDocsRepository>` in its own `v-col cols=12` (full-width, stacked) — the per-type grid inside the component already gives 2-column layout, so wrapping in `cols=12` avoids nested cramping.

**Decision D-EEE-14 (Blank-form download in SD cards):** When `templateUrl` is populated (after DDD-A seed), each SD type card shows a "Download Blank Form" button (same pattern as CiComplianceRepository). This creates the seamless download→edit→reupload lifecycle the user specified.

**No backend changes for EEE-E:** The checklist endpoint, document submissions endpoint, and taxonomy endpoint are all unchanged. SD checklist items were seeded in the migration. The only prerequisite is the DDD-A seed script having been run to populate `template_url`.

### 2.253-F: RBAC / governance continuity

- No new backend routes; no new entities; no adapter changes (JB-D3 preserved)
- `CiAttachmentHub` remains the ONLY attachment renderer (ZY-D1 — new component lives inside hub's `v-window-item`)
- `api.del()` not `api.delete()` (JB-D3)
- Analytics endpoint calls: `analytics/summary` + `analytics/financial-summary` (immutable paths, just new call site in `onMounted`)
- Recent activity on landing: same admin-gate (`canViewActivity = isAdmin || isSuperAdmin`)
- SD per-form checklist actions: `canEdit` prop controls submit/resubmit buttons; same guards as CPES section

### 2.253-G: Architecture Decisions Summary (D-EEE-1 … D-EEE-14)

D-EEE-1 Lift `fetchAnalytics` to `onMounted`; add `analyticsReady` guard for conditional rendering ·
D-EEE-2 Hero strip: focal stat + avg progress bar + status distribution pips (no new ApexChart on landing) ·
D-EEE-3 Budget gauge: 3-level stacked `v-progress-linear` (appropriation/obligation/disbursement) ·
D-EEE-4 Campus ranked bars: `v-list` + per-row `v-progress-linear` (no ApexChart) ·
D-EEE-5 Needs-attention list: computed from `projects.value`; max 5 items + show-all ·
D-EEE-6 Recent activity: relocate below table; same data/gate; additive-only ·
D-EEE-7 Detail page dashboard summary: collapsible panel inserted between OU-B and accordion; financial bar + milestone chip + gallery thumbnails + quick-actions ·
D-EEE-8 Financial bar totals: sum financials array or fallback to `totalContractAmount` ·
D-EEE-9 Modal toolbar: `v-row dense` grid (prevents wrap/misalignment) ·
D-EEE-10 Upload guidance: `v-alert` in upload zone ·
D-EEE-11 Metadata: add fileSize + lifecycleStatus to doc list subtitle ·
D-EEE-12 New `CiSupportingDocsRepository.vue`: groupCode-parameterized per-type checklist workspace ·
D-EEE-13 CiAttachmentHub SD wiring: SD_ORDERS/SD_REPORTS/SD_CERTS → inline CiSupportingDocsRepository; ECO Forms stays modal ·
D-EEE-14 Blank-form download in SD cards via `templateUrl` (populated by DDD-A seed)

**Out of scope / rejected:** new backend analytics aggregation endpoints, redesign of existing accordion panels, migration changes, ApexChart sparkline on landing (performance concern on first-paint), full rewrite of detail-[id].vue, CPES exclusion list changes.

---

## §2.254 — Phase FFF Research: COI Enterprise Refactor — Detail Grid Layout, Nested Folder Repository, Checklist-as-Tracker, Timeline Removal, Timelogs (Root-Cause Analysis)

> ACE Phase 1 artifact. Scope: detail/new/edit page sync, attachment repository nested-folder architecture, document checklist refactor, supporting-documents workspace, timeline tab removal, progress-report readability + timelogs. Operator-confirmed decisions in §2.254-K.

### 2.254-A: Current data model (traced)

- `documents` (polymorphic, soft-delete): `documentableType/Id`, `documentType` (string code), free-text `category`, `lifecycleStatus`, `version`, `metadata jsonb`, `uploadedBy`. **No `parent_id` / folder concept.** Flat store behind every repository surface.
- `construction_document_types` (reference taxonomy): `groupCode`, `groupLabel`, `typeCode` (unique), `typeLabel`, `isRequired`, `sortOrder`, `templateUrl`. 6 groups / 27 types. Seeded; admin-extensible. SD templates seeded into `templateUrl` (Phase DDD-A/EEE-F, 19/19).
- `construction_document_checklist` (per-project per-type tracking row): `submissionStatus`, `linkedDocumentId`, `currentVersion`, `remarks`, review fields. Lazy-initialised on first GET.
- `construction_document_submissions` (versioned): `checklistItemId` to `documentId`, `version`, `submittedBy/At`, `submissionNotes`.
- **ROOT CAUSE (nested folders):** taxonomy is fixed and flat; "folders" today are derived groupings (groupCode) rendered as cards. No mechanism for operator-created containers or arbitrary nesting. Template/Submission pairing exists only implicitly via checklist/submissions for seeded types.

### 2.254-B: Attachment Hub architecture (CiAttachmentHub.vue, 906 ln)

- Single attachment renderer (directive ZY-D1). Mode-aware: view | edit | staging. Owns documents, gallery, grouped doc-types.
- 7 internal sections: Compliance Checklist, Key Documents, Gallery, Supporting Documents, Compliance Repository (CPES), Miscellaneous & Uncategorized, Audit Log.
- Supporting Documents already renders 3 inline CiSupportingDocsRepository (SD_ORDERS/SD_REPORTS/SD_CERTS per-type workspaces) + ECO Forms as modal card (CiRepositoryCard to CiRepositoryModal).
- Upload lifecycle: persistDoc() to api.upload(/documents) (multipart, 20 MB) or staging-queue push in new.vue; links via POST /documents documentType:link. Delete: api.del(/documents/:id) (Admin only). Download: api.download(/documents/:id/download).
- Checklist-to-repository nav already plumbed: onChecklistNavigate({groupCode,typeCode}) maps group to section + opens modal. handleRemarksUpdate(groupCode,remarks) to PATCH /document-remarks.
- Custom Key sections stored via PATCH /custom-key-sections in project metadata (typeCode CKS_*) — precedent for operator-defined containers, but flat (no nesting).

### 2.254-C: Document Checklist (CiDocumentChecklist.vue, 676 ln) — Part 3 gap

- **Currently performs direct uploads**: submit/resubmit dialog (L80–110) does api.upload(.../document-checklist/:itemId/submissions). Part 3 forbids direct upload/edit/delete in the checklist.
- Emits navigate and remarksUpdate already (redirect + remarks plumbing exists in the hub).
- Remarks today = single free-text per group (PATCH /document-remarks, string). Part 3 wants **list-based** remarks (text + author + timestamp + chronological). Note construction_progress_reports already uses the jsonb {text,createdAt,author}[] list pattern (reusable) via CiBulletListInput / CiRemarksLog.
- **FFF-D scope:** strip submit/resubmit + delete affordances from checklist; convert each row to status + counters + remarks-indicator + "Go to repository" button (reuse navigate emit); upgrade remarks to list shape; re-style as readable grid.

### 2.254-D: Detail page layout (detail-[id].vue, 3630 ln) — Part 1 gap

- Tab order: overview, progress, timeline, analytics, documents, team, others. Gated by isTabVisible() (RBAC tabXxx flags; tabTimeline defaulted false in service L174/490).
- Overview today: profile images card (<=3) + publication strip + (EEE-C) collapsible Dashboard Summary panel + accordion sections. **No grid/column system** — sections stack vertically (scroll fatigue, weak hierarchy = the Part 1 complaint).
- Gallery data already fetched; profileImages computed exists; a carousel can reuse gallery images.
- **FFF-A scope:** Row-1 grid — LEFT carousel gallery, RIGHT always-expanded Project Profile (status/timeline/budget/agencies/quick-stats, non-collapsible). Remaining sections to collapsible 2-per-row v-row/v-col md=6 grid with responsive collapse. Pure presentation; no data/endpoint changes.

### 2.254-E: Progress Reports + Timelogs (CiProgressReportTab.vue 644 ln; entities) — Parts 5/7

- construction_progress_reports: reportType (MONTHLY/WEEKLY/QUARTERLY/AD_HOC), reportDate, percentageCompletion, plannedAccomplishment, slippage, cost fields, percentTimeElapsed, 4 jsonb narrative lists, movLink. **Maps 1:1 to the MPR form** (SD-ECO-010).
- construction_timeline_entries: entryType (WEEKLY default), entryDate, periodLabel, title, description, weather, manpowerCount, equipmentUsed, workAccomplished, issuesEncountered, photosCount, reporterType. **Maps to the WAR site-log** (SD-ECO-009). MOV entries link via relatedEntityType=TIMELINE_ENTRY (service L1458/1501) — **must not be dropped**.
- **Reference forms (extracted):** WAR-009 = WAR No., reporting period, project details, MAJOR ACCOMPLISHMENTS FOR THE WEEK, Issues/Risk, Personnel/Equipment Constraints, Mitigation/Contingency, Look-ahead, Photo documentation, sign-offs. MPR-010 = MPR No., period as-of, statement of time elapsed and work accomplished (contract amounts, time extensions, % time elapsed, planned accomplishment, slippage, remarks), project concerns, statement of work accomplished (line-item % table), photo docs, sign-offs.
- **Tiny-font complaint:** CiProgressReportTab leans on text-caption/text-body-2/x-small throughout (L218, L279–302, list/card views). FFF-B raises primary data text to text-body-2/text-body-1, enlarges stat values, increases chip legibility.

### 2.254-F: Timeline tab (Part 6) — removal inventory

Frontend (detail-[id].vue): tab def L1302–1304; DETAIL_TAB_ORDER L50; tab-key maps L32/L194; quick-action button L1201–1205. Work-Log fetch L490–553 (/timeline-entries); milestone + work-log detail dialogs L623–717; calendar view state L718–818; view-mode toggle L819–820; progress summary L900; full timeline v-window-item L1957+ with the advanced **Period/Date-range/Year/Quarter/Month/sort filter bar** (L1958–2098) — *this filter UI is the reusable basis for Timelogs*. Components: milestones table, CiCalendarLogPanel/diary, CiBatchMilestoneDialog, CiBatchDiaryDialog.

Backend (preserve entity + endpoints per D-FFF-2/3; remove only UI tab): controller timeline-entries CRUD L299–355 (+batch); service L1316–1451; entity + repo in module L11/L30; tabTimeline flags L174/490; unused timelineData jsonb stub on project (retain). **Decision:** keep table/endpoints (back Timelogs + MOV); remove tab; relocate per D-FFF-3; deprecate tabTimeline in tab config.

### 2.254-G: new.vue (1337 ln) / edit-[id].vue (3366 ln) sync — global requirement

- All three pages already share CiAttachmentHub (staging/edit/view) — repository ecosystem centralised, so nested-folder work lands once in the hub and propagates.
- Field parity verified at FFF-G; utils/adapters.ts remains the only interface source (JB-D3); detail Row-1 profile fields must mirror edit/new form field set.

### 2.254-H: Nested folder architecture (Parts 2/4/5) — True Schema (operator-chosen)

- New entity construction_document_folders: id, projectId, parentId (nullable self-FK, ON DELETE CASCADE), name, kind ENUM(ROOT,FOLDER,FORM,TEMPLATE,SUBMISSION), groupCode (anchors tree to a surface: KEY/SD_*/ECO_FORMS/MISC/CPES_DOCS), sortOrder, createdBy, timestamps. Index (projectId, parentId).
- Add nullable folderId uuid to documents (FK to folders, ON DELETE SET NULL). Existing flat docs keep folderId=null (back-compat; render under surface root).
- Endpoints (additive sub-resources of immutable /construction-projects/:id): GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE :id/document-folders; recursive tree assembled server-side; uploads accept optional folderId.
- **Template/Submission convention:** creating a FORM folder auto-creates child TEMPLATE + SUBMISSION folders. Seeded templateUrl blank forms surface inside the relevant TEMPLATE folder (link, do not move — no data loss). Checklist/submissions remain authoritative for seeded SD/ECO compliance; folders are an additive organisational layer.
- Rendering: recursive component (breadcrumbs + tree/grid toggle, drag-drop upload into a folder), applied uniformly to Key Documents, Supporting Documents, Other Forms, Miscellaneous, Compliance Repository.
- **Risk:** highest blast radius (schema + migration + all surfaces). Sequenced last, own Phase-3 gate.

### 2.254-I: Label changes (Part 4)

- "ECO Forms" to "Other Forms" (CiAttachmentHub SD section card + label, taxonomy groupLabel display only — do NOT mutate seeded groupCode/data).
- "Blank Forms" to "Download Template" (CiSupportingDocsRepository + repository surfaces).

### 2.254-J: Checklist synchronisation (Parts 2/3)

- Existing auto-sync: uploading a doc with a recognised documentType updates the checklist (server-side link). Nested uploads must continue to set documentType so checklist stays in sync regardless of folder placement. Folder placement is organisational; compliance status remains type-driven.

### 2.254-K: Operator-confirmed decisions (2026-05-28)

- **D-FFF-1** Nested folders = TRUE SCHEMA (construction_document_folders + documents.folderId). Seeded templates preserved by linking into TEMPLATE folders.
- **D-FFF-2** Timelogs BACKED BY construction_timeline_entries (reuse; no new table; MOV links preserved; relocate existing Period/Year/Quarter/Month filter UI into a Timelogs container under Progress Reports).
- **D-FFF-3** Timeline tab removal = RELOCATE: Milestones to Progress tab ("Progress & Milestones"); Diary/Work-Log to Timelogs container; drop standalone Calendar.
- **D-FFF-4** Delivery = PHASED, each sub-phase independently Phase-3-authorized. Group 1: FFF-A (detail grid) + FFF-B (progress fonts + Timelogs). Group 2: FFF-C (timeline removal). Group 3: FFF-D (checklist) + FFF-E/F (nested folders) + FFF-G (new/edit sync).

**Out of scope / rejected:** dropping construction_timeline_entries; mutating seeded taxonomy groupCode/typeCode/data; deleting seeded templateUrl blank forms; new route on the immutable /api/construction-projects base beyond additive sub-resources; rewriting CiAttachmentHub as anything other than the single renderer (ZY-D1); quarter-scoping COI (JQ-D8).

---

---

## §2.255 — Phase GGG: COI Critical Architecture Refactor
**Date:** 2026-05-28  
**Branch:** pmo-coi  
**Status:** Phase 1 Research ✅ Complete — Phase 2 Plan ✅ Frozen

---

### 2.255-A: Scope & Context

Phase GGG is a comprehensive corrective refactor of the COI module, superseding the incorrect/incomplete FFF-C through FFF-G. The operator identified that FFF-C was declared complete but had critical implementation gaps, and that the entire architectural direction needed correction.

**Operator mandate (verbatim summary):**
- Project Details Page MUST be strictly PRESENTATION / VIEW-ONLY / ANALYTICS / MONITORING — no CRUD, no add/upload/edit/delete buttons
- All CRUD ONLY in Edit Project Page and authorized modals
- Timeline tab: COMPLETE removal from all locations
- Nested folder/container system for all repository sections
- Checklist: tracking-only (no upload/CRUD), redirects to repositories, list-based remarks
- ECO Forms → Other Forms; Blank Forms → Download Template
- Timelogs container preserved (FFF-B is good); Progress Report preserved
- Part 7: Add Timelogs container below Progress Report (already done in FFF-B ✅)

---

### 2.255-B: FFF-C Gap Analysis (Root Cause)

**Gap 1 — COI_PROJECT_TABS not cleaned (CRITICAL)**
- File: `pmo-frontend/utils/coiFormState.ts` line 126
- Entry `{ permKey: 'tabTimeline', tabValue: 'timeline', label: 'Timeline', defaultOn: true }` still present
- This drives `CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` permission checkbox loop (iterates COI_PROJECT_TABS) — Timeline checkbox still appears in Personnel tab editor
- Also drives `EDIT_TAB_PERM_MAP` in `edit-[id].vue` (harmless since `timeline` is not in `tabOrder`)

**Gap 2 — Milestone CRUD orphaned (CRITICAL)**
- `edit-[id].vue`: Full milestone state machine exists in script (lines 770–940, 1334–1497)
  - `existingMilestones`, `milestoneDialog`, `milestoneForm`, `milestoneDialogMode`, `milestoneEditingId`
  - `editFilteredMilestones`, `pagedEditMilestones`, `openCreateMilestone()`, `openEditMilestone()`, `saveMilestone()`, `executeDeleteMilestone()`
  - `batchMilestoneOpen`, `CiBatchMilestoneDialog`
- But the TEMPLATE section for milestone management was removed when Timeline tab was deleted — milestone editing is UNREACHABLE from the edit page UI
- Progress tab (line 2147–2164) has only `CiProgressReportTab` + divider + `CiTimelogsContainer`

**Gap 3 — editSection('timeline') stale reference**
- `detail-[id].vue` line 1993: `@click="editSection('timeline')"` navigates to `?tab=timeline`
- Timeline tab does not exist in `tabOrder` → router silently falls back to `basic` tab
- Should navigate to `?tab=progress`

**Gap 4 — tabTimeline in FullPermissions + ACCESS_PRESETS**
- `CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` lines 41, 58–90: `tabTimeline: boolean` still in interface and all 5 presets
- `useCoiAccess.ts` line 90: `tabTimeline: adminAll` still in effectivePermissions base object

### 2.255-C: Architecture Violation Audit — Detail Page

3. **Progress tab (line 1993):** `"Manage in Edit ↗"` link calls `editSection('timeline')` — wrong tab, harmless but broken. NOT a CRUD violation; just a broken navigation link.

**Documents tab (line 2086-2091):** `CiAttachmentHub mode="view" :can-upload="false" :can-delete="false"` ✅ — correctly read-only.

**Orphaned timeline code (lines 498–880, ~382 lines) — DEAD CODE:**

### 2.255-D: CiAttachmentHub.vue Architecture Trace

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiAttachmentHub.vue`

Props:
- `projectId: string`
- `mode: 'view' | 'edit' | 'staging'`
- `canUpload: boolean` (default false)
- `canDelete: boolean` (default false)
- `canEditRemarks: boolean` (default false)
- `modelValue: StagedQueue` (staging mode only)
- `customKeySections: CustomKeySection[]`

Internal sections (derived from `docTypeGroups`):
- `keyCodes` = KEY_DOC_TYPECODES (hardcoded in coiFormState.ts)
- `ecoFormCodes` = group 'ECO_FORMS'
- `sdOrdersCodes` = group 'SD_ORDERS'
- `sdReportsCodes` = group 'SD_REPORTS'
- `sdCertsCodes` = group 'SD_CERTS'
- `cpesTypes/cpesCodes` = group 'CPES_DOCS'
- `otherDocs` = docs not in any managed group

Renders (ZY-D1): Key Docs, Gallery, ECO Forms, Supporting Docs (Orders/Reports/Certs), Compliance Checklist, Other Attachments, Audit Log.

**Label override points:**
- "ECO Forms" → displayed via `groupLabel` from API response (DB seeded in migration)
- Operator requires rename to "Other Forms"
- Safe approach: frontend label override map in CiAttachmentHub (avoid touching seeded taxonomy data)
- "Blank Forms" / "Download Template": button label in `CiSupportingDocsRepository.vue` — needs grep verification

### 2.255-E: CiDocumentChecklist.vue Architecture Trace

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiDocumentChecklist.vue`

Current capabilities (violations per GGG):
- `submitDialog` — inline file upload for checklist item submission
- `saveSubmission()` — POSTs file to `/api/construction-projects/:id/document-checklist/:itemId/submissions`
- `dialog` — edit checklist item status (review/approve/reject)
- Both expose CRUD buttons to users with `canEdit=true`

`navigate` emit: `emit('navigate', { groupCode?, typeCode? })` — already exists, good foundation for GGG-D.

`groupRemarks` prop: `Record<string, string>` — simple string per group.  
GGG-D upgrade: `Record<string, { text: string; author?: string; timestamp: string }[]>` as list of structured remarks.

`summary` computed: total, submitted, approved, required, requiredApproved — keep for header stats.
`groupedItems` computed: items grouped by `documentType.groupCode` — keep.

**GGG-D Changes:**
1. Remove `submitDialog`, `submitDialogItem`, `submitFile`, `submitNotes`, `submittingVersion`, `saveSubmission()`, `openSubmitDialog()`
2. Remove upload buttons from template; add "View in Repository" button per item that emits `navigate`
3. Upgrade `groupRemarks` prop type from `Record<string, string>` to `Record<string, RemarkEntry[]>` where `RemarkEntry = { text: string; author?: string; timestamp: string }`
4. Add `remarksUpdate` emit signature upgrade to pass array
5. Template: render remarks as chronological list with author + timestamp per entry

### 2.255-G: CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue Architecture Trace

**File:** `pmo-frontend/components/coi/CiPersonnelAccessCard.vue` (~36.5 KB)

Timeline-related fields:
- `FullPermissions` interface line 41: `tabTimeline: boolean`
- `DEFAULT_PERMS()` line 58: `tabTimeline: true`
- `DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS()` line 68: `tabTimeline: false`
- `ACCESS_PRESETS` lines 75–90: all 5 presets (Viewer/Contributor/Editor/Manager/Admin) include `tabTimeline: true`
- `normalizePermissions()` line 158–159: `tabDatesDuration: p.tabDatesDuration ?? p.tabTimeline ?? defaultTab`  ← this merge pattern is a MIGRATION HELPER for old DB records; MUST be kept
- Line 159: `tabTimeline: p.tabTimeline ?? defaultTab` ← this sets `tabTimeline` in normalized output; should remain for backward compat with old saved JSONB data until all records are migrated
- Template loop: iterates `COI_PROJECT_TABS` to render permission checkboxes — once `timeline` removed from `COI_PROJECT_TABS`, the checkbox auto-disappears

**Conclusion:** 
- Remove `tabTimeline: boolean` from `FullPermissions` interface
- Remove from `DEFAULT_PERMS`, `DEFAULT_EXTERNAL_PERMS`, all `ACCESS_PRESETS`
- Keep `normalizePermissions()` lines 158–159 (backward compat with old JSONB)
- Template loop auto-fixes (driven by COI_PROJECT_TABS)

### 2.255-K: Nested Folder System — Backend Analysis

**Current state:** No `construction_document_folders` table or entity exists (planned in FFF-E but not implemented).

**Required schema:**
```sql
CREATE TABLE construction_document_folders (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  project_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES construction_projects(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  parent_folder_id UUID REFERENCES construction_document_folders(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  folder_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
  folder_type VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'GENERAL', -- GENERAL | TEMPLATE | SUBMISSIONS
  group_code VARCHAR(100), -- ties folder to a document type group (ECO_FORMS, SD_ORDERS, etc.)
  sort_order INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT NOW()
);
-- Optional: construction_documents.folder_id FK (nullable)
ALTER TABLE construction_documents ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS folder_id UUID REFERENCES construction_document_folders(id) ON DELETE SET NULL;
```

**MikroORM approach:** New entity class + migration file (migration 048 or next available).

**Endpoints (additive, sub-resource):**
- `GET /api/construction-projects/:id/document-folders` — tree or flat list
- `POST /api/construction-projects/:id/document-folders` — create folder
- `PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/document-folders/:folderId` — rename/reorder
- `DELETE /api/construction-projects/:id/document-folders/:folderId` — delete (cascade)
- `PATCH /api/construction-projects/:id/documents/:docId` — update `folderId` (assign to folder)

**FFF-D2 compliance:** `construction_timeline_entries` table NOT dropped. Folder system is additive.
**FFF-D3 compliance:** Seeded templates (templateUrl in document_types) NOT modified. Template folders are new containers that can optionally link to seeded content.

---

### 2.255-L: Nested Folder System — Frontend Architecture

**New component:** `CiFolderRepository.vue`

Props:
- `projectId: string`
- `folders: FolderNode[]` (recursive tree)
- `groupCode: string` (ties to repository section)
- `canManage: boolean` (create/rename/delete folders)
- `canUpload: boolean` (upload into folders)
- `canDelete: boolean` (delete files)
- `mode: 'view' | 'edit'`

FolderNode interface (in `utils/adapters.ts`):
```ts
export interface CiDocumentFolder {
  id: string
  projectId: string
  parentFolderId: string | null
  folderName: string
  folderType: 'GENERAL' | 'TEMPLATE' | 'SUBMISSIONS'
  groupCode?: string | null
  sortOrder?: number
  children?: CiDocumentFolder[]
  documents?: CiDocument[]
}
```

**Integration into CiAttachmentHub.vue:**
- Replace flat section renders for ECO_FORMS, SD_ORDERS, SD_REPORTS, SD_CERTS with `CiFolderRepository` per group
- Keep `mode="view"` pass-through; folder management only available in edit mode
- Fetch folders alongside documents in `fetchDocuments()`

**Label overrides (no DB mutation):**
In `CiAttachmentHub.vue` add:
```ts
const GROUP_LABEL_OVERRIDES: Record<string, string> = {
  ECO_FORMS: 'Other Forms',
}
```
Use when rendering section headers: `GROUP_LABEL_OVERRIDES[g.groupCode] ?? g.groupLabel`

"Blank Forms" → "Download Template": locate in `CiSupportingDocsRepository.vue` or `CiAttachmentHub.vue` template and update button/link label text.

### 2.255-M: Acceptance Criteria Mapping (GGG)

| Criterion | Sub-phase | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Project Details Page strictly view-only | GGG-A | Remove all CRUD; fix CiProgressReportTab/CiRevisionOrdersTable read-only |
| No CRUD in detail page | GGG-A | CiMovEvidenceSection :can-edit→false |
| Orphaned timeline script removed | GGG-A | ~380 lines |
| editSection('timeline') fixed | GGG-A | → 'progress' |
| COI_PROJECT_TABS timeline entry removed | GGG-B | coiFormState.ts |
| FullPermissions tabTimeline removed | GGG-B | CiPersonnelAccessCard |
| Personnel tab no longer shows Timeline checkbox | GGG-B | auto-fixed by COI_PROJECT_TABS |
| Milestone CRUD restored to edit page | GGG-C | Progress tab window-item |
| Milestone create/edit/delete dialogs accessible | GGG-C | State machine already exists |
| Checklist tracking-only | GGG-D | Remove upload/submit dialogs |
| Checklist redirects to repository | GGG-D | navigate emit per item |
| Remarks = list {text, author, timestamp}[] | GGG-D | upgrade groupRemarks |
| ECO Forms → Other Forms | GGG-E | Frontend label override |
| Blank Forms → Download Template | GGG-E | Button label update |
| new.vue timeline guide text fixed | GGG-B | Lines 644, 1129 |
| Nested folder backend | GGG-F | Entity + migration + endpoints |
| Nested folder frontend CiFolderRepository | GGG-G | New component |
| Nested folders in ECO/SD/CPES sections | GGG-G | CiAttachmentHub integration |
| Template/Submissions folder pattern | GGG-G | folderType in UI |
| Repository sync preserved | all | ZY-D1 compliance |
| Seeded templates preserved | GGG-F | additive only |
| Audit logs preserved | all | no deletions |
